### Hope — 2013-06-10T18:28:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-87925>

Hi Inger,

what a beautiful post and decision and experience with this new man....I am way out of the loop (I just got back) from everything but so excited you are meeting new people. I wouldn't worry about what he thinks of you though I know it's impossible to do it....I totally understand how you would feel not good enough.....it's so easy to think that lie after the way you have been brought up. And you are truly changing those thoughts and transforming who you are. You are on the way....also remember he is divorced, too. Why? What has he done to heal from that? What has he learned? You know all the incredible ways you have grown from all you have gone through. It's easy to have fun and play pretend in dating....someone said, people put their best foot forward in dating, and then when you marry and hit real life, the other foot comes out.

I encourage you to take things slow with this man....just have fun. It is a little strange that he brings you bread and milk etc but if he eats the CW way he may not realize the importance of this.....for you, until he gets to know you better. I think also that a little milk in the coffee probably won't kill you. If love is the greatest power, then the positive energy you get from this man will far outweigh a bit of milk??

Anyway I am so so excited for you. You will have an amazing partnership eventually, there is no doubt. You are so pretty, inside and out, that the men will line up at your door. And, I think when you get to the point of wanting to be with him, you don't have to do it overnight if you still live at home then. And eventually, you will marry or just live with someone where you don't need to live with the legalism at home. I can't wait for that day to come for you!!!!

I have been wondering how you are doing and couldn't be more delighted for you!!!!! And I am so jealous you get to live in Europe!! I came home this weekend, and back to this wooden hut (it's nothing more) that smells inside....from all the humidity going into the air from the wood, instead of stone like in Europe. The air inside there is so much better....ugh.

You deserve nothing but the very best, Inger....I know it's hard to believe that.....when you have gone through what you have, but you're getting it more and more. I can see you now, in my mind's eye, creating a beautiful life of freedom, of love....and joy, and health, and ecstasy

(((((((((((HUGS)))))))))))))

I must say your eating habits are great! I did not drink the coffee he brought me with milk, I just took a sip of it to try how he's tasted. He was not sad because of it at all and apoplogised. I am so very very curious to hear he's story. But he has lovely parents that own a own island in the Baltic ocean. he spend every other weekend there.. he showed me pics. So beautiful. Not everyone are called to be in heaven.  there is no way around but to go the narrow path dad.. and if you want not, your suffering will continue (I say it like a joke, you know) And he has to laugh.. and then he tells the other.. oh man Inger she just laugh of me now.. she says I must go through the narrow path.. oh man. ### Inger — 2013-06-10T21:09:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-87936>

My appetite has decreased since I met that guy Sunday.. how weird is that? But not bad! Now I need less food, finally!   Hope this continues!

Today I had max 1/2 pound raw ground beef as burger for breakfast, with coconut oil.. some celery and linden leaves, 1 red grapefruit, for lunch only 1 coffee with coconut oil and 1 egg yolk from own hens (I had to have that cup because I had lots of work to do and I had not slept almost anything! But I sure felt after it did me no good..) and for dinner 3 mackerels as a tartar, raw + a wild greens smoothie. That was it. Very little as I slept almost nothing and that normally increases my appetite very much.

Tonight I will sleep well. Lambs are in the 1st pasture.. I cut off the mums wool today she had a bit much wool still. and soon I am ready for bed.

### Inger — 2013-06-15T21:40:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88124>

Hope, the animals are doing great! The lambs are so big already. I need to take a pic for you! So many chicken babies to take care of.. and now.. all of the sudden 5 other moms are laying on their eggs too as they want babies too.. OMG. I am going to drown in those tiny chicks!

I am struggeling a bit with my soul because of this man I met..huh. He is just so kind and nice. It brings up some painful stuff. I feel so not ready for this. But still I want to meet him so badly! On our last date we went to a nice cafe by the sea and had some amazing shrimp sandwiches. I left the bread for sure and ate only the shrimps and eggs. IDK why this feels also painful for me. But I need to relax and take one day at time. He asked if we could meet Tuesday and I agreed.. so looks like he wants to see me again. We have only kissed twice.. but that made me almost pass out..huh. Why am I so sensible. It is kind of too much! I am learning so much about me.. and it is not always easy either. I do am confident what ever happen it is going to be good. I need to cool my head and not want this man so badly.. because he sure is a testosterone bomb for sure. I can feel it with all my senses. He is so kind, so very kind. Ok I am not going to tell more now because I need to see where this goes first.... weird thing is, we met the day before yesterday, and yesterday I had to work.. I had slept only 5 hours I could not sleep. Anyway I had crazy much energy at work and I just flied.. I felt like I weighed maybe.. 100 grams or so? Very weird! It was so fun.

I got little sleep also because hubbys brother was here for a few days. he has turned into a vegetarian.. kind of a buddhist or so? And he wanted to tell about it all the time, huh! lol But he is very nice guy. I made nettle smoothies for him and showed him what to eat from the nature. He thought it was so nice!

Tonight I slept 9 hours though, and man I feel so good. No desire for coffee at all. I am going to drink it only on occasion, that's it.

Breakfast was 1/2 pound raw salmon thin sliced on a bed of raw organic tomatoes and cucumbers.. with some EVOO, apple cider vinegar and salt. So yummy. 1 tablespoonful coconut oil.

For lunch I had 1 big nettle and wild edible smoothie, 6 oysters au naturel, and about one cup of a colostrum + lingonberries smoothie for dessert, and a big slice of organic pineapple.

Dinner was 1 big mackerel raw, only the filet as tartar with fresh ginger, onion, vinegar, salt and a little raw honey and celery sticks. Then some linden leaves, some organic cucumber and 1/4 of a fresh coconut.

Now I am ready for bed. I am going to put my salt socks on again tonight, I love the cold wet socks on my feet in summer, as it is so warm in my bedroom in comparison to winter time. The river has now only 58 degree F again, as it has been cold and rainy. So very nice to swim in there again. I play I am a frog when I swim my 3 rounds.. it is so much fun. That water is quite muddy too, so it kind of fits. I did it twice today, so 6 rounds all togehter. Each is only 1,5 minutes so it is not really much but to me it is perfect

Now if we meet again I am going to ask him a lot instead..ha. So I do not need to tell my sad stories. He is a very intelligent man BTW. ### caroline — 2013-06-17T11:20:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88223>

Inger - I think I need a tutorial in dehydrating stuff! So - I would guess that it is important to have different heat settings?
A timer would be important?

When you dehydrate your elk meat - you are making very thin ones? .... like jerky?

If I did hamburger patties in the oven - what temperature would I use ..... 80 degrees? .... for how long? I realize it would depend on the thickness.

I have been looking at dehydrators for a week now...... don't know which one to get .... or just use my oven???

### Inger — 2013-06-17T11:57:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88225>

Audrey a temp controller is important, a timer not at all. You do not want to dehydrate on more than 45 degree C. I usually do 40 d. C. (104 F) You can lose lower too, as low as you like. But I like when the burgers are a bit warmish.

Oven is not so good because it has no fan.. it needs some air to blow to get it dry out faster or it will take very long. For jerky it might work but for burgers not so...

I do quite thin burgers, today I had 4 burgers made from 250 gram elk meat. I make them like a sandwich, you know? If I make jerky I slice the meat as thin as I can, that way it dries faster. For my burgers they are not ment to dry through, only on the surface and inside they are still a bit moist, but they get harder so they are easy to eat as a "sandwich". I always put them in before going to bed, and take them right out for breakfast. They are in around 10 hours maybe.

Hope this helps!

### caroline — 2013-06-17T12:18:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88226>

One more question - are your burgers cooked all the way thru or are they pink or rare? I have done them in the oven for a couple of hours and they were a little rare - and I love them like that.
Thanks Inger!

But still I want to meet him so badly! On our last date we went to a nice cafe by the sea and had some amazing shrimp sandwiches. I left the bread for sure and ate only the shrimps and eggs. IDK why this feels also painful for me. But I need to relax and take one day at time. He asked if we could meet Tuesday and I agreed.. so looks like he wants to see me again. We have only kissed twice.. but that made me almost pass out..huh. Why am I so sensible. It is kind of too much! I am learning so much about me.. and it is not always easy either. I do am confident what ever happen it is going to be good. I need to cool my head and not want this man so badly.. because he sure is a testosterone bomb for sure. I can feel it with all my senses. He is so kind, so very kind. Ok I am not going to tell more now because I need to see where this goes first.... weird thing is, we met the day before yesterday, and yesterday I had to work.. I had slept only 5 hours I could not sleep. Anyway I had crazy much energy at work and I just flied.. I felt like I weighed maybe.. 100 grams or so? Very weird! It was so fun.
>
> I got little sleep also because hubbys brother was here for a few days. he has turned into a vegetarian.. kind of a buddhist or so? And he wanted to tell about it all the time, huh! lol But he is very nice guy. I made nettle smoothies for him and showed him what to eat from the nature. He thought it was so nice!
>
> Tonight I slept 9 hours though, and man I feel so good. No desire for coffee at all. I am going to drink it only on occasion, that's it.
>
> Breakfast was 1/2 pound raw salmon thin sliced on a bed of raw organic tomatoes and cucumbers.. with some EVOO, apple cider vinegar and salt. So yummy. 1 tablespoonful coconut oil.
>
> For lunch I had 1 big nettle and wild edible smoothie, 6 oysters au naturel, and about one cup of a colostrum + lingonberries smoothie for dessert, and a big slice of organic pineapple.
>
> Dinner was 1 big mackerel raw, only the filet as tartar with fresh ginger, onion, vinegar, salt and a little raw honey and celery sticks. Then some linden leaves, some organic cucumber and 1/4 of a fresh coconut.
>
> Now I am ready for bed. I am going to put my salt socks on again tonight, I love the cold wet socks on my feet in summer, as it is so warm in my bedroom in comparison to winter time. The river has now only 58 degree F again, as it has been cold and rainy. So very nice to swim in there again. I play I am a frog when I swim my 3 rounds.. it is so much fun. That water is quite muddy too, so it kind of fits. I did it twice today, so 6 rounds all togehter. Each is only 1,5 minutes so it is not really much but to me it is perfect

Dear Inger,

oh how I wish I could be face to face with you and listen to your story and also encourage you to step back, relax, and enjoy the ride. I know it is so hard. But you are doing all the great work of healing and no matter what happens, this man is part of your healing, however long or short he is in your life.....I have no doubt about it. He is showing you love and warmth and kindness, the way you deserve to be treated. I can imagine it is not easy but you are doing it and I am so excited for you. I want to encourage you to just speak to your nervous heart with the love you show so many people, even us on the board here. It's OK to feel whatever you are feeling.....you are growing through it all! Perhaps that might help your herpes heal too?

I would have suggested iodine too. How sweet of you to give all yours away. How sacrificial! Have you heard of visualization? It's where you visualize your herpes pimple to be gone.....that and/or meditation will help you hopefully to relax too.....there is an MD here Andrew Weil who talks a lot about it and there is a story of a little boy who imagined little 'somethings' killing or removing his brain tumor and it really worked.....of course that's a miracle but the MD's don't know why else he got rid of it.

But even if it doesn't get much better - it doesn't matter. This man sees all of your beauty, and your pimple doesn't undo any of that. In fact, if I were him, I'd be thinking - oh I am so glad this amazingly beautiful woman is human too, so maybe she'll give me a chance when she finds out about my weaknesses and my pain. I don't know how good a job of hiding my nervousness I have been doing...." ))) And surely he has had some pain......we all have some....and you know, if he lives the usual modern life, he needs all you know about food and living and eating too...some day.

I love all the raw fish you are eating. What an inspiration. I haven't gotten there yet but am making some ceviche soon.

I am thinking of you and sending you lots of love and healing thoughts - for your heart and that little tiny shrinking almost gone pimple

### nonchalant — 2013-06-17T17:14:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88244>

With all the iodine in seaweed water, I would think it would help as well.

### Inger — 2013-06-18T07:13:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88326>

@ Audrey,
my burgers are not cooked at all..lol as I use so low temp. The meat just dries a bit out. So that way they almost seem like "cooked", maybe. The meat in the middle is a bit more reddish and on the outside it is darker because when meat dries it becomes darker. If I make them very thin it is almost the same color through.

@ Hope,
you write so very beautifully I got tears in my eyes. IDK how have you gotten such a beautiful heart? I need a heart like yours, Hope. I am working on it, but it goes so slow, it seems. My heart is rather full of scars and not so pretty. But slowly it will heal I know. The herpes is almost gone BTW! And I will think about what you said above when I meet this guy today.

\- - - -

Yesterdays dinner was 1,5 mackerels, raw as tartar, and the heads and roe and fishmilk and liver as a smoothie with some seaweed. I felt like a queen after.. oh man! I LOVE those fishead + roe + fishmilk + liver + seaweed smoothies!

I just had breakfast made of maybe 1/2 pound raw reindeer with marrow, left in the dehydrator overnight. 2 whole slices of organic sweet pineapple, and a handful of linden leaves.

I feel so good. So crazy good. What can it be from? I drink one glass of that golden fluid every morning.. and I also do oil pulling for a few minutes. Makes my teeth so white, I love it. I also drink a big green smoothie from nettles + some other wild greens + water every day, at least once, sometimes twice. Maybe it is that? I am out in the sun every single second that I have the possibility. Anyhow, what I do works... I am slimming down too.. without even trying. Life is good.

### caroline — 2013-06-18T09:20:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88333>

You are a recipe for success Inger .... good luck today or maybe forget about the luck just be in the moment and have fun

### Inger — 2013-06-18T21:04:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88364>

We did not meet today. He called me a few hours before our date and we talked a while. He explained how he had been over the weekend by he's ex with he's girl (I knew that BTW, he told it to me Friday) and that he just realized he has not left that relationship 100% yet, and that as long as that is he just have a hard time start anything new. So he needs some more time. I so understand. There are children at play too that makes it all way more complicated. I always hope families could stay together if it is a possibility. Sure my heart ached a bit, but I also am able to see the reality. I also reminded me that I was the one not feeling ready yet..lol and now I got some more time to heal. I know how long time I have needed to heal from the wounds the breakup with my ex lover has taken... That deep was it. Here is a pic from us after the break up, maybe 3 years ago or so. we met once every month as long as I lived in Germany, he took me out for dinner.. mostly sushi. He is the most amazing man and I still cannot believe how I could choose to stay with hubs instead.

So I really understand this guy I met.. he is as amazing as my ex lover really. Very beautiful heart. He is only separated from he's ex a bit less than one year. That is not much time.

Now.., sure it hurt. Huh. But then I went to work inside the pasture.. I stood there in the sun in my bikini.. and felt the sun and the wind on my skin.. and then it was so beautiful around me, so very beautiful and I just could not be sad anymore. It was just too much beauty! I felt so happy to have all that around, my heart ache just could not exist anymore.. at least it was so tiny it really did not hurt. I did so many fun things with my brother the whole day long. We built a new little house for the sheep, and we grilled a fish my bro got in the river, and shared it.. and we repaired the fence.. it was so much fun. I am so fortunate to have such amazing brothers, they are amazing. I got some more time for healing too. I have told myself when I am 40 yo then I am blooming if not earlier. I still can take 3 years easily.. I am very happy on my own too for sure.

I do told the guy I think he is something special.. and that he can always contact me if he needs a friend or anything. He was very happy in the end. I also have learned from him... to be more organized! Ha, I really needed this. he made me want to organize everything here..lol

So now I feel.. a bit weird but good. Vulnerability is the birthplace of innovation, creativity and change.... I have that in my kitchen on the wall. It is not dangerous to feel vulnerable.. it can emerge into something very beautiful.

My other brother that lives ion Norway has purchased a cottage, in the wild, there is not even cellphone signal, and there is a own spring!!! Huh I cannot wait to visit him there, he lives there for a while now and works a bit away, 45 minutes drive. He said to me today in the phone he loves it. He is such a crazy guy too.. I have told him everything what Jack has learned me.. and he feels it is right just intuitively. He is a very smart guy. He will come visit in 2 weeks only.. that is going to be so much fun

### Hope — 2013-06-19T02:34:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88381>

I am sorry to hear this Inger....and you are handling it all beautifully. I was wondering about his story, how long he was divorced. and with a kid it's so hard too. but wow, what a gift you gave him and he you, even for such a short period of time.....you are an incredible woman, Inger, and I have no doubt the right man will show up. Love your picture too. This man looks very German (oh boy, here we go with the stereotypes). He must have had a hard time too with you going back to hubs. No doubt. Any man losing you would have a hard time. Very, I am sure.

I love too what you say about beauty in nature. And, how amazing about your brother. This is where I have to do a lot of healing...and in fact I just started to see a special counselor to help me deal with my grief over my lack of a family, outside my mom (and dad). And it was so weird, because even this woman said, it's in our DNA to wish to know where we came from, spiritually, emotionally, physically.....what the family stories were etc. And because of my circumstances, it's almost like I was adopted since I never knew or met anyone else, really...which is so hard, and my mom doesn't want to share much of what she knows, and often complains when I ask questions. I think that is the hardest part about the potential of not being a mom myself. No one before, maybe no one after me. Although of course it also has always been the reason I have always been open to adoption. I could love any kid if I had the chance. So one kid without parents out there at least has one or two people loving him or her dearly. But we shall see what happens. I am not giving up hope. There are amazing stories out there.

Anyway, HOW WONDERFUL you have siblings, some of which you can connect with and share some pretty deep things with.......it must be an amazing thing to know someone forever.....

I love too your saying about vulnerability. I think I need to print that too and hang it up. I don't have much trouble being vulnerable, but my DH does. I think from HIS very legalistic, perhaps even controlling upbringing and trying to stay under the radar, he is incredibly shy and so afraid of opening up...even to me. Though of course I can be very dramatic and intense. I am a leo after all. LOL. Always so much to learn. Today I got a letter from a friend who at 33 had her first baby this past month and she described the pregnancy as being physically 'easy' (and she has been a vegetarian for a while)....and I thought, wow, I'd like something in my life to be easy. LOL. But I know if I had it all easy, I would not be getting better and stronger and more compassionate.....my mom always says, people that have it easy, don't know how to handle pain. And my acupuncturist, who knows a ton about Taoism, says, when things are too easy for too long or life goes your way all the time, it's like a drug and the people have no depth. I don't know why I shared this just now.....but somehow I felt I should share this.

Know I am thinking of you Inger...and sending you lots of love. You are my sister in heart  And I know great things are in store for you. Well, they are already here, too..but you know what I mean.

### Inger — 2013-06-19T07:06:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88400>

Hope, from now on you are my sister in Heart  Thank you so much for the above. You know.. I still keep thinking I should not have told him about my past. It is just so sad.. maybe it was too much for him too, who knows. But he kept asking so many questions. Otherwise I would not have told. But it sure is possible he really is not ready yet too.. and I very appreciate he's honesty for sure.

I think what you shared about pain was very beautiful Hope. I am going to contemplate on this as I go to work.. I have to work today and tomorrow all day long. It is going to be fun, my coworkers are very fun always.

I am so sorry you have no sister and brothers. I have 14 and I could share them with you! But not only is it that biological siblings are real sisters and brothers.. often one can feel like strangers too. And the other way around, sisters in heart can feel tied in a way deeper way to each other even if they are not biologically sisters..

### prAna303 — 2013-06-19T10:24:38+0300

Hi again...

I must say that i see your happening with some great peace. Quite a few times i have the same meetings like yours that lead to nothing, according to the rules set by our surroundings. It is a sign that you are becoming more YOU. Even if it is for just a day, week, year or a lifetime these small parts counts into your whole. When this happends to me i canÂ´t feel sad because it has added so much to me and i have added to the other part. I have loved and lost but never forgotten.

### caroline — 2013-06-19T11:19:03+0300

My dear Inger......all of lifes experiences are incredibly worthwhile no matter the outcome. You met an amazing man and he enriched your life as I am sure you did his ..... there are endless possibilities to come......as long as our heart is open...

I love what Hope said above .......all the things that happen to us give us depth and colour and contours and vulnerability and passion and vision...... the whole package that is ....us!

### Hope — 2013-06-20T02:35:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88458>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=88400)
>
> Hope, from now on you are my sister in Heart  Thank you so much for the above. You know.. I still keep thinking I should not have told him about my past. It is just so sad.. maybe it was too much for him too, who knows. But he kept asking so many questions. Otherwise I would not have told. But it sure is possible he really is not ready yet too.. and I very appreciate he's honesty for sure.
>
> I think what you shared about pain was very beautiful Hope. I am going to contemplate on this as I go to work.. I have to work today and tomorrow all day long. It is going to be fun, my coworkers are very fun always.
>
> I am so sorry you have no sister and brothers. I have 14 and I could share them with you! But not only is it that biological siblings are real sisters and brothers.. often one can feel like strangers too. And the other way around, sisters in heart can feel tied in a way deeper way to each other even if they are not biologically sisters..

My dear sister Inger

please, please don't feel badly about what you shared with this man from your past....i don't know what is in his mind, but a truly beautiful and mature person - the kind you want to be with - will love you for and through the pain you have experienced....all of it. Maybe not understand it, but still love and accept you for it all, and love you more because of it. Only someone weak and fearful would not want to be with you for your past.....I don't want to guess this man's reasoning, and it doesn't matter....what matters is you....He asked you questions and you responded, and that is very courageous. it's good he said this now because if he's not ready - and if he has been away from his wife such a short time i doubt he is - he wouldn't be right for you. He might have been Mr Not Right Now. You deserve someone as amazing as you, Inger....and some day, you will meet him, I am sure of it. I just don't know where and how. Maybe I shouldn't say this as I am not a prophet to tell the future...but the light in your heart can't be kept secret to all the world's men... This man is part of the beautiful picture that you are painting of you......and I just in my heart believe you will have a great partnership some day.

Having no siblings has been hard sometimes. I wish I could reminisce together with a sibling, somehow you know for all your life. But I know, sometimes siblings aren't close. I see it in some friends. I think sometimes I feel in this country like i don't have roots, somehow. I guess when you have not had a big family, or only a really small one, you long for it deeply. Well, I do. One of my now dearest friends, from Russia, he is an only child too, never knew his dad.....and he won't have kids because he is gay and says he never had an interest anyway. And he is totally fine with it, growing old and not having family to be there, or care, or spend holidays with, he has his friends. I admire him greatly. We are still great friends now, and i think will always be. In fact, he is someone I was deeply in love with 20 years ago...but at the time, he was not 'out' yet...I had no idea he was gay. He was and is amazing. It was very hard to hear he could never like me. LOL. But I eventually accepted it and the pain was replaced by a deeper, brotherly love.....and he ended up choosing me as the first person to 'come out' to, which was very difficult for him - he didn't even tell his best guy friend until 10 years later, out of fear. I had a lot to learn about myself, and still do....I picked unavailable men for a long time. I always felt not good enough....and created situations that would perpetuate my belief I am not worth that great love, or something I really want. I have only recently realized that part, with the whole baby thing......underneath it all, I have always thought, i wouldn't be good enough to be a mom, that i am somehow deeply flawed and different from all the other billions of women who have become mothers. It's a lie from a long time ago. And it's so healing to see it at all and then get to work on cracking the lie. I hope you do believe you deserve that great, honest, deep, amazing love......that will weather all the storms and tsunamis that the reality of our daily lives can bring and make you stronger in the end. The kind that Audrey talks so beautifully about....

Sending you much love, my dear sister in heart

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-20T03:22:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88462>

he did you a favor.......when people think the past matters more then the future they are building a fence around their possible outcomes.......all you create with a fence is a caged animal or a sheep.......without a fence you become an eagle who can soar without limits........Never settle for less than you are capable of........but make sure you continue to jump......potential energy is wasted if it is not used.......at 40 it is time regardless of what is in the heart. Progress requires action and action requires movement. Food for thought.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-20T03:56:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88466>

I say the following from the honesty of my soul........I hope you take the words in the fashion I mean them. I don't mean to offend.......but this needs to be said to you in my humble opinion:

A Clay Pot endured the fire of the furnace before becoming Porcelain.

A Black coal endured the intense pressure and heat before becoming a precious diamond.

A Body builder developed muscles by editing the resistance of the weights.

Your endured heart ache to get to the place to experience great love...........but you can't score unless you shoot.

There may be pain in the process but we need to constant push ourself towards excellence or else the gravity of lethargy will cause you to take the path of least resistance.

I am worried that is where you are now.......paralysis in action due to analysis of your past that no longer is relevant to now.

Stamina is convincing your mind that you can go beyond your comfort zone and glass ceiling.

Stamina can be developed over time , but make a realistic goal to leave your comfort zone in your parents house and your ex husband space and break your glass ceiling your are artificially creating for yourself.

Do not let the microeconomic damage a bad relationship has caused you to led you to a life of macroeconomic impotence in love.

Make yourself stronger than your excuses…….just think differently about love

Our thoughts are the architects of our futures. One cannot think crooked and begin to walk straight.

Become fully aware that the human mind is a wonderful servant but usually is a horrible master.

Inger, if not now, When?

It is time.........to act. Time is not promised to any one for any reason.

### Inger — 2013-06-20T20:38:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-88509>

Thank you all for your support. You give me so much to think about! Hope, my dear sister! I always had this issue too, I was not good enough! But it is starting to change now... I was thinking how it all went.. and now I am sure it is not because of my past he turned to he's ex..lol After I told him we had to go and I asked him to wait as I needed to use the ladies room. When I come out again he stood there and looked at me with a look that said more than thousand words, you know?  And right after in the car he asked me if Tuesday would be ok for me to meet again with him. He told me at the telephone as he cancelled our date after visiting he's ex that he had gone back to he's ex before, too. He said they cannot live with each other and not without. I bet it is kind of a hate love relationship that many suffers from. And do not have the courage to jump off it and try something new.. What can one do..lol I am so not sad anymore, IDK how I am just thinking.. oh well.. he does not know what treatments he would have got from this girl.. if he knew, he would regret what he did for sure.. HA! . (and I am NOT talking about fishhead smoothies..LOL) He said he will contact me when/if he has cleared this stuff up. But I already deleted everything from him. Messages, number, all. I am not going to sit here and rot and wait huh. But okay, not too many possibilities for dating right now but I am very ok with that. They will come for sure!

Jack, you pushed me on my weak spot. But I am working on it. You do not even imagine how much! I can tell you I have no issue to leave everything in a heartbeat if needed! I live here only because debt to my parents and my shitty economic situation. I have to wait certain stuff out and then I will leave. I have everything planned in my head already. I just need to be patient. I need to have money for food, great food. Not cheap pasta or potatoes. And in the meanwhile I am going to enjoy every single day I have here! I do not want to waste not one day. I am the only one of my sibling that have been able to live here without fighting with my parents or converting to their religion. Everyone else have run away. Or converted. So I have done lots of work to achieve that too, what has made me realize everything depends on me, really. Kind of amazing!

> Stamina can be developed over time , but make a realistic goal to leave your comfort zone in your parents house and your ex husband space and break your glass ceiling your are artificially creating for yourself.

I will!

I just came home from work and had this big green smoothie with wild edibles.. yum! My lips are green from the smoothie..lol not lipstick..

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I thought I was alone as my mom went for shopping.. and I started to sing.. so loud that the walls kind of vibrated. I LOVE it. I always sing when I am alone, from all my heart. it is my secret therapy. I often make the songs up, at least the words. Then I all of a sudden heard some noise from downstairs.. and I run to check what it was... OMG my other brother was coming for the weekend.. He said, was it you singing? hhmmm.. he must have been thinking I am crazy! Tomorrow comes my little 19 yo sister and I cannot wait.. Oh.. and one little chick has hurt her feet.. so she is jumping around on one feet. Poor little girl. But there is nothing I can do really. I hope it will heal fast.. they become pray so easily when they are not 100% well, as they run around in the yard. At least she has already survived 2 days.

So beautiful, Inger...thanks for sharing. I don't have much time for writing now, must go but wanted to say, I'm glad Jack finally said something on here - every day I was thinking, I am sure he is reading this, I wonder WHEN he will SAY something. And here he goes. Perhaps he read my mind. LOL.

Inger, I know you will do it.....you will do it all. You are doing it already. Many hugs~

### Inger — 2013-06-28T07:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89118>

Oh man I got so busy no time to write at all. Sister was here one week, and now brother comes next week.. lots to do with the animals and in the garden. I got my drivers license too!!! YAY! I never thought I will get it I am so bad in driving a car, really. I bet my teacher was very surprised too. lol He seemed very happy though, that I got it by the first try. I was singing a song I made up when I was riding my bike to the test that went like this; I can drive.... lol Must have helped! I was stressing quite a bit over this drivers license I have to say, so I am so happy it is finally over. Now I have been driving quite a bit this week already for real, it is fun.

Our hens are crazy exited to make babies..huh! I just took care of 11, 9 is left and doing well.. and now there are already new ones coming! Yesterday 2 got born.. and there are so many eggs! 5 hens are laying on their eggs huh. You can see a little bit of one chick in the picture, it is yellow but run to hide under her mom when I tried to picture her.

Those two hens are laying in a tiny "cage" that only fits one but they are fighting about the eggs and now they lay there both.. even if there is so little space. They are so fun..

I am feeling so great. Life is so beautiful. It is hot and warm here now, and I enjoy it all! Today we will drive to the sea again.. my favorite place. I have to take some pictures. It is magic there.

### Inger — 2013-06-28T08:20:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89119>

I am planning to visit my brothers cottage in Norway this fall.. with my other bro and sister. The cottage has no electricity, neither has the neighbors, no cellphone signal either. No water inside. But there is a 100% natural spring very close. And a clean river with plenty of fish. So you are totally on your own there. Wow. I love that! We are goung to do at least one week and eating only what we get from the river and the Ocean and maybe hunting. The Antlantic is only a 45 minute drive from he's cottage, with lots and lots of fish! I really cannot wait. It is going to be the best vacation ever, I already know.  Maybe... I cannot leave.. and will stay there who knows... I am in love with those lonely wild places, it is like my soul craves them. Maybe I need to try out and live there for a while. My bro use to live there too and work from there when he is not in he's other place. He has then 45 minutes drive to he's work. Not too bad!

### Inger — 2013-06-28T09:37:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89122>

These worlds keep singing in my head.. facing the ambivalence and denial coming from religious dogmas around me.

Keep me away

from your world

I can't relate to it

it's all too sick

leave me alone

in my heaven

do not come close to me it's all too weird

\- - - -

I have to take good care of me and not get close to it. Or it will take my energy and happiness away pretty fast.

I have eaten the First wild strawberries of the year. Oh my they were amazing! And I love all the wild greens around here. Today I am going to collect lots of seaweed from the sea, for our sheep. They love it. I bet it is good for them. I am going to collect and dry for winter too. I tried them too and they were nice but the sea here is not so clean so I rather order mine dried from France. When on my brothers cottage in Norway I plan to collect lots and lots of seaweed and dry it. No need to buy anymore! And I am going to eat Oysters as much as my heart desires.. for free.. I just need to take my oyster knife. Maybe you understand now why I play with the thought moving there?  Only thing I need for that move, that I have not yet is a car. We'll see..

### caroline — 2013-06-28T10:56:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89123>

I love how happy you are right now ..... that is what we have ...... right now

See ..... you love the vitality of the sun too - It makes us feel so amazing and everything seems so beautiful and it seems like magic ..... to me at least....

The sun on our skin is like a life force to me .... it takes my breath away....

### caroline — 2013-06-28T11:00:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89124>

Congratulations on your driver's license .... yeah! You are going to have a wonderful summer and fall ..... and beyond....
no fences.....and no boundaries ..... just a life to be lived to the absolute fullest - to the nth degree..... so many experiences just waiting for us......

### Inger — 2013-06-29T10:41:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89197>

Thank you Audrey! I really like to drive I have to say!  Yes I love the sun. I make sure to embrace every season I live in to the fullest these days because of a special man that owns this forum.  Fall used to be my favorite season. I loved the crispy fresh air and the beautiful colors. I always got so much energy then. I am born in fall so maybe it has something to do with that, who knows!

I am feeling like a robber. I realized one more hen was laying on a bunch of eggs in a corner.. oh no. That is way too much. I had no other choice than take her eggs away. She got so very upset and tried to attack me. But I managed to get all the 5 eggs away. I felt so bad! Thankfully later when I checked how she was doing, she was sitting on a stick close to the cock looking quite happy. She had already forgot lol.

My mentally sick brother arrived for the weekend and I really am thinking about giving him my organic pineapple that I have ripened with much love by the window for many days now. Oh man it smells divine. He has quit eating sugar and sweets. Okay he still is a vegetarian.. but I feel I should encourage every little step he takes in the right direction. I rather go without the pineapple (and I can tell you it is delish!) I do not do too well with more fruit anyways, even if it is summer. I do have papaya and grapefruit. Soon the wild raspberries will be ripe and then I will eat as much as my heart desires.

Amazing how tired I get in the evening even is the sun is still up! Like yesterday.. at 9 PM I went to bed with this sweet tiredness, and the sun was shining at the tree tops lol.
Thanks to Jack.

I went for a swim in the river, naked as always, and I always am amazed of how sexy it feels. Now who needs a boyfriend when you have all this sensual moments with the nature? Just the sun and wind on my skin.. and the grass touching my legs when I walk outside.. wow. I told my little sister to embrace those things instead of longing for a boyfriend (and because of that jumping in and out of destructive relationships). It will come when it is time.. and thankfully there are plenty of ways to be sensual and feel love even without.

### marcus — 2013-06-29T21:56:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89205>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=89197)
>
> Amazing how tired I get in the evening even is the sun is still up! Like yesterday.. at 9 PM I went to bed with this sweet tiredness, and the sun was shining at the tree tops lol.

I just had the same experience. As I started living according circadian rhythms, I expected to get tired around 11p.m. in summer... but currently I get tired before sunset and without wearing any blue blockers.

### caroline — 2013-06-30T04:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89222>

I had the same experience of being tired before sunset in the summer - needing to go to bed very early and sleeping well.
very different now.....

### Inger — 2013-07-01T08:33:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89282>

Audrey,
I got dead tired by 9 PM last winter.. that is weird you do not. You do not use any lights in the evening, do you? And has it cool too? and eats not late? I am quite sure you do that all right. How weird. there must be a answer though. We just have to keep on digging! Could be the place you live.. maybe you need to look for a cottage close to the earth somewhere out of the city? You need to find a place near a waterfall. I LOVE my bedroom as I can hear the waterfall all night long. I am addicted to that sound and cannot sleep well without anymore...

Dinner yesterday. Had wildcaught local Salmon chevice with fresh dill and onion from the garden, avocado and red pepper. Then Oysters. Oh man it was yummy! For dessert a few strawberries from the garden.
I was sitting outside on my balcony opening the oysters in the sunshine. I always eat outside when I can. I did not have time to make it pretty but I had to take a pic anyways..lol

I built two doors fro the chick's outdoorspace (they need to be in "cage" outdoors until they get a bit bigger or they will be eaten by predators). It was so much fun! I have never built a door before but it was not all that hard.

I am going out now for a swim in the river and spending the whole day outside again. How can life be this good!?  And my skin.. I cannot believe how smooth it is.. even it I get so much sun! I use EVOO on it every day. My grand grand mom did that too. Her husband was a sailor and they lived winters in Spain and summers in Norway. She lived to over 90 yo and she always used olive oil on her skin says my mom.

### caroline — 2013-07-01T10:23:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89283>

Inger - I think my sleeping problems are because of more computer time and more coffee ..... I need to put an end to that for sure!

So - I want a picture of you in a tool belt! LOL you seem very proficient at a lot of things.....

### Hope — 2013-07-02T01:51:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89332>

Inger, wow, so much news! Congrats, yes, on your license, woohoo! I got mine at 28, very late as I didn't need it before. I am sure the car will come if you need it for your move

I love all you share.......and your meals continue to look amazing!!!!! I am hoping to make your mackerel dish very soon...the fermented one. We shall see. Or was it herring.

Well I must go, have one more place to check before I hopefully finally unpack from our trip to the beach, whoa.

So happy for you~ and keep sharing pictures...love all the animals. One of these years, when you have your farm, I want to come visit, and work with and for you. That would be a dream.

### Inger — 2013-07-03T22:28:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89466>

@ Audrey.. you see? I know you knew the answer...LOL

@ Hope, I would love to work with you on a farm  when I have my own one day I'll let you know!

\- - - -

Now much magic happening here these day. Maybe because I have had mackerel head smoothies last 3 days in a row?
(I can hardly write my cat is on my lap and kisses my fingers all the time..lol)
Look what I found.. hidden behind some stuff in the chickens house!

And just as I had gotten no fish for the goose couple my daddy found these in the river and run to show them to me.. exited as a kid

Thousand and thousand of mini fishes!!!! CRAZY! I can tell I got exited too.. so much food for the goose couple! Oh my I have 5 small bags in the freezer now. There was so much fish.

And then. This nice man called me today.. and wants to see me tomorrow. I had forgot about him really. I have so much to do here, my dear brother is here now too and I just enjoy it all so immensely.. and I do not have he's number either as I deleted it. He had called me 3 times from 2 different numbers. I did not hear it as I never carry my cellphone on me. I thought it was maybe from work so I called back. Too weird! Now I am feeling... weird? But I am going. I need to check this out..lol Hope I do not fall in coma when I see he's eyes again? I really have forgot.

Life certainly never get boring here.....

### caroline — 2013-07-04T03:52:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89477>

Take care Inger ..... and no boring life for us!

### Inger — 2013-07-11T22:13:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89905>

Family pic tonight. Me and my Princess cat.  (we both did not made our hair..lol sorry!)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I was at my favorit lake today. I did not take anything with me, not even water. I just ate lots and lots of blueberries by the lake... and drank from the lake. The water is totally clean there. And I can tan and swim nude, no one is there or they are too far to see. It is an hour for me to get there with my bike, and the last 20 minutes I need to walk because it is rocky and just a narrow path through the woods up there. I love that place. I always "meet" Martin there, my dear friend that got cerebral malaria in Africa. I have not heard from him since, but I always talk to him by the magic lake. I can feel he's presence so strong, it is weird! He loved the nature, he just was such a special one. Too sad we could not meet. We do planned to. But now I can meet him at this lake.

I love to escape from all stupid smart meters and stuff that I do am surrounded by here. By the lake I get so regenerated, just two hours and I am new! Cannot wait to try out brothers cottage this fall with no electricity and no cellphone signal available there either.

I am planning to live by my bro next winter and work in Norway. maybe I'll move there in the end. At least I am very much able to support myself there.. and I could get free oysters as much as I want... oh my what a dream!
Now I have a ticket to Norway and back for August, I am going to visit my sisters in Stavanger for one week. That is going to be so much fun! They live close to the ocean..

I finally got my Magnesium L-Threonate and I can tell it made me so relaxed yesterday evening.. I slept so well and dreamed crazy much! A little stressy here right now with people in the house so it was great stuff! I do sleep well, but looks like that stuff made my sleep even better...

### Lahelada — 2013-07-11T22:51:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89907>

I love the idea of that lake . It sounds idyllic . I looked but until now I have not found anywhere cool to swim. There is a lake at about 60 kms, but it is manmade and not for swimming so they say.

Lovely Inger. Do you take the mag at nite?

### Inger — 2013-07-12T12:02:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89941>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=89936)
>
> Lovely Inger. Do you take the mag at nite?

Yes I did Audrey  but today I took one at BAB and one at lunch and will take one at dinner. Need to try this out.

That guy I was dating turned out to what I actually could intuitively feel... a man that does not know what he wants. Pretty unsexy. I should have listened to that tiny uneasy feeling in my guts for sure. I do not need such stuff at all now, I only need a man that know what he wants. No plays. So I left that romance behind me.

Now I really need to listen to that tiny voice in my heart. Not easy always in this world so full of distractions. But it is possible! I will make this to my signature for now.. thanks to Jack;

> Those who embrace discomfort and curiosity are the most happy and have the best ASI: They shun short term gains for long term rewards and they do not compare themselves of their results to others.........This is why I say all the time embrace your discomfort and enhance your curiosity and ask questions and dont settle

### caroline — 2013-07-12T12:13:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89942>

very, very unsexy Inger...... so true. A man in your life has to be captain of his own ship .....master of his own destiny. He has to see the woman that he wants and then drag her to his cave!

The big news is - you are listening to that inner voice. That is huge progress. And we definitely aren't going to settle....no how..no way. We have come to far and we deserve so much more!

### caroline — 2013-07-12T12:16:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89943>

I think it is still lovely to have rose coloured glasses and see all the beauty that is around us......but we are smarter now.....

### Inger — 2013-07-12T21:57:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-89988>

Lahelada.. did you know that Finland (where I live now) has many many thousands of lakes? That really makes this country so beautiful! Always somewhere to swim. And the Finns enjoy nude swimming. It is part of their culture. They do it alone or with family, but they do. It belongs to this culture.. nude swimming in the lonely nature. Absolutely beautiful IMHO.

Audrey, I got angry at myself today. Oh man, I went to the shop and got me one bottle of organic Rose' Wine. Thought I needed a date with the buzz. because of this stupid dating thing and me not listening to my intuition again!!! I had two glasses. Oh my. I got crazy fat cravings. I always do when I drink alcohol.. I had non for many weeks. I then ate 100 rams of French great Gorgonzola, raw sheep cheese. Yum!!! Then I had maybe 5 spoonful of coconut flakes, raw and dried. Then I hade a bowl of green salad from the garden with lots of Olive oil and some Balsamic vinegar and salt. Then I had 2 egg yolks and 1 whole egg whisked into foam with 1 teaspoon raw honey, and 2 spoonful of wild lingonberries. Now I am sooo stuffed but man, feels great! This is all way too late, and I already had 3 meals today, dinner was almost one pound of raw Pollock.. I had zero hunger. But wine always make me crave fat like crazy! I guess I am drunk now? Anyways, I thought I need a date with the buzz because of this stupid dating but then i decided no, not worth it I will drink a glass for my dear friend Martin that got so sick with malaria instead. Only for him. For the memories. They were worth it.

Anyhow.. now I am stuffed..lol One new problem have emerged. It is so dry here now, only sun and no rain. The pastures are not growing any new grass. The sheep have no food anymore. Now I need to go and cut all their food from places around where I live, and carry it to their pasture to eat. 3-4 times / day minimum. A huge pile. I do it all barefoot, every time. I cannot say I hate it...lol I usually take my Emme girl with me. She is so tied to me still, she walks right behind me everywhere! Even if she has grown so big! Looks like she never forget she slept in my bed lol. She gives me so much love. I do not regret one second that I rescued her and all the work it was, was so worth it.

Oh Audrey, I forgot to tell you something about that man I dated. One thing does not felt right from the beginning...I felt there was missing a certain peace.. he was kind of "busy" in he's spirit, if it makes any sense? I tried to ignore it, or think, ah, maybe he is just nervous... but now when I think about it it really was present. Even if it was "under the surface". I really need to take serious what i feel deep inside if I am going to date a man again. He also reminded me of my ex lover. Why do my brain always play this trick with me? I think I learned a important lesson.

> He has to see the woman that he wants and then drag her to his cave!

I loved what you said here. Yeah... I need a man that drags me to he's cave... LOL Audrey you are just too sweet xoxoxo
(I am pretty wild and full of energy so it ain't going to be too easy for him..lol but that is what I need, yes!)

### Hope — 2013-07-12T23:12:09+0300

Hi Inger, so great to see you here. That is so funny about the wine. How is your friend Martin? Any news about him? Do you hear from any of his friends? I hope he is OK.

Emme sounds so cute. Can you post a picture of her sometime too? I dream of having all those animals around. Sorry to hear you are lacking rain. We have had a ton over here. Maybe nature is dumping it all on the Eastern US. LOL.

And way to go that you noticed the new guy is or was like your ex.....great to see those patterns....

### caroline — 2013-07-13T11:40:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-90024>

When he can appreciate all you have to offer - you will know it is right and he will be very special too......

### Inger — 2013-07-14T22:24:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-90120>

Hope, hier is Emme now. Very hard to take a pic as she wants to be so close to me all the time! When I go and cut greens for the sheep to eat I take her with me, she always follow.

I emailed Martin and he answered! That really made me happy! Very short but he is alive. No idea how he is doing, I then wrote him back and asked but IDK if he can write much. He told he had 2 other bouts of serious malaria after the last time we talked. Crazy guy. He has some wounds to heal I know. Just hope he'll make it. But we all have to choose our way.

I have choose my way too. This fall I will leave here. To Norway, first. Then I'll see. I am already so exited. I am going to just jump.. and I know it will be all good. I am going to make it. I feel so ready now. I have nothing anymore that ties me here really. I need to explore life, not live under my parents rules forever. Sure my heart will break to leave the lambs. But I am sure I can get the lamb skin of Emme. As a memory. And there can always come new Emmes. I just need to take my cat with me but that will be ok I hope. I just cannot kill her and she is too afraid of other people and her heart will break if I leave her I know. She loves me so much. But my ex has promised to drive me there (he's offer) and my cat, otherwise I can take her in the plane too. Or I rent a car and drive myself..lol How great to have a license!

I cannot wait for the oysters and the ocean in Norway.... it just is so amazing! Sure I am freaked out to jump out in nowhere but I have gotten this need to do it now. I have already stuff organized..

### Hope — 2013-07-15T03:11:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-90123>

Oh, Inger, that is so cute. What do you mean about Emme's skin??? Will you be killing her soon? I hope not. That's the part I could not do. Unless there were no other butchers I guess and I had no choice. She is just so cute.

I am so excited for you. You will do amazing out there, I have no doubt. It is really not that hard. I moved across continents when I was 22, with not much but a dream and a huge passion and desire to start a very different life (I would actually do the same now, except in the other direction, LOL, but I am stuck with my DH who is not nearly as adventurous as me. But oh well. I can travel at least.....).

Little Emme is so so cute, indeed. Thank you for sharing that picture! I was outside a lot today and came across a lot of dogs. I talked to all of them, in my heart. Some of them out loud...though the owners probably thought I was a bit nuts. Some of them appreciated the attention, I guess.

Thanks for all you share here~ many hugs across the big pond!

### Inger — 2013-07-18T09:37:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-90313>

@ Hope, had to smile thinking about you talking to the dogs..lol I do that too all the time, I talk to the chicks.. and the lambs.. and the goose too. And my cat..lol We are both crazy it seems haha. We (or my parents) will have to kill 5 lambs this falls, yes. I so would like to take Emme with me to Norway. But then I would have to take 2 lambs as a lambs do not do well alone.. and that is just impossible. So if I get her skin I will have a memory of her for sure, beautiful memories. She is so loving I cannot believe it, really. But I could never kill her myself. I am not a good killer, I actually hate that part. I think that is something men have to do? Hmmm.. I can do the job after, easily, cutting the meat and stuff. But not the killing part.

@ Sally, yes I am so ripe now to leave. Freedom.. with any challenges that come.. is all so much better than to live under others rules that are certainly not my own. I am not scared one bit.

Now.. I do have planned some.. or it is a dream of mine. That is, first I will earn money a few months, and live in my brothers garage (it has 2 windows..lol and it has a oven to make fire in and I can cook my tea or coffee there, but no electricity) for free, I'll just help him with stuff in the house/garden. Then, I will buy a car. Then I will move to he's cottage. He said I could live there for free if I do some work on it, whatever he wants me to do. I hope to get there for Christmas. There I will stay for the winter.. in the cold and the dark without electricity and any comfort. It is a very old cottage. But I LOVE it. I will take my cat there too. We will be there totally alone. There I will work on a thing I have dreamed about for a year or more already. And I will heal too. It is going to be crazy cold.. haha and no water inside either, but there is a well and I can melt snow. There is a river too and I will make a hole in it and dip in it every morning. I need to climb on a "mountain" 100 meters above the cottage to get any cellphone signals, so tat I need to do every day to get messages or do calls. And to get electricity to my computer I have to travel 10 km to the nearest small town and recharge my battery. And also if I want to use internet. It is going to be challenging and so much fun.. my dream...!

### Inger — 2013-07-18T09:43:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-90314>

I had some sleepless nights because I itched so badly from the stinging nettle burns I had gotten collecting grass for the sheep. Oh man it was terrible. Very hot nights, and it itched and itched! The second night I was about to turn crazy, really.. then I got an idea to go down and take a dip in the river, so I did, 0:30 AM. It was magic, and not dark at all. I dipped in the river, just a minute or so, and went back into bed. As soon as I lied down there was no itching anymore, 100% gone! WTH? I felt so relaxed, and so well! Wow, totally magic! How could such a short dip take away all the discomfort? I waited it to return soon.. but it did not. Not nthe whole night long. Wow, that was kind of magic!

I also met a deer with her 3 babies, and a huge elk last time I was by my favorite lake. I have never seen a elk there before, just lots of elk poop. I always wondered where they hide? But now I got to see one. Wow it was so beautiful and powerful!

### Inger — 2013-07-19T20:44:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-90388>

I am realizing I possibly will get hard time in Norway..lol I think they are different there that here.. more "spoiled" or unnatural. My cousin that lives in Norway (he is special in a very good way) is here with he's beautiful wife and 5 months old kid.. and they are totally sweet and so interested in everything I tell them! Told me when they get home they will sure go to swim in the ocean... take cold baths.. eat seaweeds.. quit sugar and bad stuff.. lol they are just totally sweet! But in Norway people seem to very much follow mainstream especially when about mothers, breastfeeding etc. She was shocked when I told her a baby should get mothers milk at least until 3 yo. She said maybe she could pump milk and give in the bottle? That much tabu it is to breastfed in that age.. crazy! Their baby already get feed porrige and stuff.. she is only 5 moths old.

My brother baby is 1 year today, and he lives close by. I have told the mother there is no need to feed anything else than her milk until the kid is one year.. so that she has done, almost. Just a tiny bit food last weeks. And that has been quite healthy foods like butter, shrimps roe (he loved it!), etc.Their kid is amazing! So smooth, so full of harmony, so very clever. Not fat at all, just perfect body. So very perfect. Almost never cries. Cousins baby is a bit on the fats side.. and cries quite a lot. Heavy energy.. Looks a bit unhappy somehow, very very sweet girl but you just cannot believe what a difference between almost only breastfed and getting grains and stuff.

The girlfriend of my brother sees how different her boy is.. and she is so happy she listened to me now and not to her doctor, that said to her many times she MUST give him solid foods and porridge at 6 moths old at least. Weird world, really.

### fitness@home — 2013-07-20T00:05:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-90407>

Inger, you are having a positive ripple effect on your family.

I had three children and breast fed the first two. When I compare them as infants and toddlers, my youngest (who was bottle fed) was definitely more chubby.

Totally regret to this day that I did not breast feed her because of the benefits she could have reaped from it.

### jib — 2013-07-20T03:23:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-90415>

> I am realizing I possibly will get hard time in Norway..lol I think they are different there that here.. more "spoiled" or unnatural.

It seems you will do well where ever you go~ Kudos to you...

I've always been very curious about Norway, I don't know if the travel shows do it justice or not. When you have some of this ancestery in your veins, I think it is natural to "wonder" what it might be like to actually go back and trace some of your "people" down.

### Inger — 2013-07-20T08:35:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-90420>

@ f@h, interesting. We need to tell this truth to every mother we meet. And every women! I get so angry when i think about what brainwash is going on from the medical world. It is just not real. Even my mom said yesterday she thinks it must be a conspiracy, otherwise she cannot understand.. why does doctors do not want women to breast feed??? The most natural thing? It is like they want us to be stupid. I hate conspiracy theories and never want to believe them but slowly I see no other option that it to be true.

@ Jib,

> It seems you will do well where ever you go~ Kudos to you...

nope.. this is not true. I struggle a lot. I have so much work to do on myself still. I am not too good in helping others yet but I do what I can, I do. I just have too little power still. But I so see forward to the months in my brothers cottage in the wild, where I can be totally alone in the dark and cold. I so need it.

Norway is just as beautiful as in the pictures you have seen.  The Nature there is magic.. and so many resources. I really do not get why they are so superficial? The Finns and Germans are not like that.. they are not looking down on you if you are a health nut. Especially the Germans are very health conscious and diciplined, I really liked to live there, the best place I have lived so far. Hamburg in northern Germany. So many great memories!
Maybe the Norwegians have had it too easy with getting rich so fast with the oil and then a certain humbleness is missing. They eat very very unhealthy there. But they move a lot around in the mountains or spend time on their cottages and eat codliver oil lol But hey, I'll see myself when I go there. I just have what my siblings have told me that lives there. I need to do my own experience! Maybe it is totally different! Weird.. I have not ever lived there, but I am all Norwegian.

\- - - - -

The magnesium L-threonate makes me dream so much! Or rather, it makes me remember my dream way clearer! Cool. I have slept very well too. I take one pill in the morning and two in the afternoon/evening. I have the Life extension brand. How cool to feel such an effect in the brain. Super clear dreams.

### Inger — 2013-07-24T09:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-90715>

I suffer a bit from very dry and warm weather. When comes rain.. oh man how amazing!  We had some rain last days. And now comes a dry and hot period again. I would prefer rain.. IDK why. I have more energy then, the water in the river feels more energizing too! I LOVE to swim there after rain! Wow.. I think I love rain. How weird.

My aunt was here. She told about her trip to Africa. she was visiting a very poor area. She said she was amazed how aware people was there. They always "connected" with you! No one was left outside, no one needed to feel like they was not seen. She said it was a huge difference from how people are here. I thought it was amazing too. This greater awareness. Kindness, love. Ignorance is not so beautiful.. To recognize another, to really pay attention, I think that is very beautiful. I really need to expand my awareness.

My aunt is special..lol She is so very organized! To the point she even suffers from it. but she is great in economy! Her husband left her alone with many kids.. and she managed to take drivers license, buy a car, buy a house, feed the family.. all alone with working as a nurse! Not even full time! Wow. I told her she needs to teach me economy, and how to become more organized. I really need that! She said she will..lol I can imagine how I soon will have a car.. a house.. everything..ha. She said you need to plan, always. So that is what I will start doing.  I really really need to start exersicing that part of my personality! To be structured. That is what I really need. I am very confident I will be able to change. I just need to.. do it!

### Inger — 2013-07-24T13:02:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-90718>

So I need a car. And that's about it. That is why I need to work a few months to earn money to buy one. And then I'll hide in my brothers cottage and write my book I have in my head for a year now. It will tell in a easy way what I have learned from Jack. For my family and friends.. in a easy and fun way with some recipes and stuff and pictures to make it a easy read. I am going to give them each one as a gift from my heart. All my 14 siblings. Some are already waiting...lol My cousin said when he was here, he wait so much to read it.. because he thought everything was so logical and sounded so great what I told him. They started to walk bare feet all the time with he's beautiful wife in the yard..lol But man there is no way I can write that book here. Too many suppressive powers.. my creativity cannot flow.. and it feels awkward.. all the extra energy goes to keep the balance huh! So counterproductive.
Just a few weeks left.  My ex said he will drive me there! The whole way to Norway! So I can take my cat too. I think that is very very kind! That way I'll get some more stuff with me too. My ex and I have worked out such a beautiful relationship. Crazy. I cannot believe it myself but that is how it is. We are divorcing, staying best friends as ever, and we can even live together if must be (we do not now), but we are just best friends and if we found a new partner we still stay friends as ever. No one can tell the other what to do. And we totally agree on this. And I have promised to be he's private health couch..lol He eats fish every day now! And looks pretty good! Great stuff.

And I have made me a spray bottle filled with rainwater. When I spray it on my hair I feel it gets even more shiny and curly! Must be something magic with the rainwater... Thank you rain. Thank you God/heaven.

Just had raw salmon for luch, sliced into thin slices with some raw organic apple cider vinegar, super fresh raw EVOO and salt. Yum! To that I had a salad made of squash from own garden, and red pepper from my sisters garden.. and more olive oil. I eat lots of olive oil now. I have found this yummy brand, where you can track it back to the day and place it was produced! Cool! It is just about 6 months old only. Pretty fresh! 9 € / bottle. Not too bad. I can tell it is addictive, I use one bottle / week or more... I put it on my body too if I feel I need it.

### fitness@home — 2013-07-24T21:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-90747>

Inger, I am excited for you. Your positive energy really comes through in your post ^.

Wishing you all the best in your book writing endeavor

### caroline — 2013-07-25T04:33:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-90772>

Inger - I have been thinking so much about you lately. I don't think you really understand how strong you are. You are living in a situation where there is so much mind bending going on and you are managing to stay strong and be true to all your core beliefs and your personal truth.

You are going to soar like a bird when you leave your parent's place...it is going to be very hard ...and yet very easy. You are going to spread your wings and fly to amazing places. The possibilities are endless .... we have learned so much - things we didn't know and stuff about ourselves that we have forgotten or were suppressed for too long. We are now free to be the people we want to be and the people we need to be to live a full,amazing and rewarding and honest life.

### Inger — 2013-07-29T15:27:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-91085>

Thank you F@H. That really is kind of a art for me. I am so exited to make that small book. Only for family and friends, you know. It is not a commercial thing at all. That is what makes it all so much fun!

Audrey, IDK about being strong. I am not. I cry alone on my bed when it all grows over my head.. and I do things I really am not strong enough to do at all only because I know I have to but it hurts. I crave loneliness and last weeks there were none! That is when stuff grow over my head and I start to suffer big. If I can I take my bike and escape to my favorit lake..lol. I really really wait for living my own life. It does is amazing to see how things change in a good way when I manage to be loving despite of facing cruelness. It needs more courage than I have right now to face it at time. so it does tear me down. I am not strong enough to carry that pain really.. I only do because I have not had any other place to be / or that would have been in a even worse environment..lol

\- - - - -

I was at the lake yesterday, for 5 hours. On the way I stuffed myself on wild raspberries.. so yummy! Blueberries too. I tanned on the hot rocks warmed by the sun and cooled off in the quite warm water. Made me feel 100% great again! I had nothing with me, no water or food. I just drink the water from the lake. Lots of it. It tastes great! I bet it is very structured.  I took a pic.. the while lake is full of those lonely places with rocks by the water, so even if there is other folks around they are far enough to not care that you are nude (me thinks). Everyone has their own privacy. And it is totally silent there. Many Finnish people love to regenerate by lakes like this.. in the wild nature.. with silence.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I have been stuffing myself with foods for three weeks now. Eaten when not hungry, way too much.. uh!
I do think I might maybe have got a tiny layer of fat on my body.. somehow I am, bigger but I still am amazed I have not got fatter, I have been eating I bet 3000 calories / day at least. Pretty cool.
Maybe because I run barefoot all the time.

Now my parents left for vacation, I am alone with my brother. Finally peace arrived.  Now I am going to take good care of me for sure! And stop gorging on food when not hungry. I have this bad habit to comfort myself with foods when I feel stressed and bad so I eat way more than I need. I forget what hunger feels like because I never feel it. That is weird too. Now it feels like a dark heavy cloud have lifted from this place. Finally I have time alone, I can sing again! I never want to sing when they listen.. it feels awkward. weird!
I can use shorts too now.. YAY! I need to go a few times / day and fill a cart with fresh grass and flowers I find in the neighborhood to feed the lambs.. I use to take Emme with me. She and me have so much fun. She walks right behind me, wherever I go. I gorge on berries.. and she eats flowers and grass by my side. I think there is nothing funnier to do for work in this world than that for sure!

Ok this was a quick update, I feel a little bad I am so behind here with the posts, not time for being online much at all. But great to spend less time on the computer for sure.
The magnesium threonate makes me still dream crazy vivid dreams. I am going to order more of it, feels like it does good stuff for my brain!

And.. I just wanted to say I like Jack's blog more and more. I liked it from the very beginning but I am totally amazed on where he is taking us. Just wow. It just resonates with my deepest instincts.. that kind of always was there I just did not realized them much enough to understand or make the right choises. Kind of easy to get distracted from the truth you feel inside when it seems like the whole world goes another way. But not anymore. No chance. Now we have it all scientifically explained too. It is rock solid. Feels amazing for sure. And feels even more weird to see how people can not see it, but continue to make the wrong choises even if you explain why they still suffer. So many lies around. So many false assumptions (like.. isn't it better to be a vegetarian? Every health book says that..). My family comes to me often and ask me for help but they have big issues to do. They fall into false dogmas again and again. It is almost like a comedy..? Like how can this be for real? Hmmm...
