### Inger — 2013-11-22T12:05:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-101331>

Audrey my cat did not wanted to sleep with me at all when I did it "wrong" with the magnetico. After having enough space between me and the mattress, like they tell to do on the magnetico site, my cat sleeps between my feet every single night!
My cat is a special clever princess. With thick black shiny fur. eating mostly hand peeled shrimp... what else can you expect..lol
But the weird thing is I got the worse after I actually changed it and put it under my mattress! That is when I started to feel weird and that is right after I did the paint. But the effect might have come delayed, for sure
I still sweat weirdly in the night, I did not before. But I feel super otherwise. I woke up totally wet in the morning today... from a bad dream. I could not run away from a man that came after me.... My parents had a religious friend (man) for visit here yesterday and 2 nights and he came up in my apartment to talk and I feel so bad vibes from him. He is not healthy IMHO. I am so crazy sensitive about people. I did not wanted to have anything to do with him. He looks good, slim and nice face and hands etc. no doubt, divorced.. looking for a women I think. But I do not want to have anything to do with him at all. He has a nice new Volvo, is self employed he said I can drive it if I want but I did not. I just wanted him to leave! he had a bad gait/poise..... weird something was so off. Even if he use no computer and has no TV either because of his religious believes. He gave me a gift, a card with something from the bible and about love. It was beautiful so I am grateful for that but I still just wanted to stay far away from him

Sooperb next summer if I am still here come and visit me! I live 30 km west from Helsinki, pretty close to the coast (maybe 10 km), between Espoo and Karjaa. Here are few moscitos, they do not bother me at all in fact. This summer I did not even needed to use my mosquito net at all that hangs over my bed even if I always have my window wide open. We can drive to the beautiful coast every day and spend all day there, fishing if you like! The coast and Baltic Ocean are just magic! My favorite place to be in summer... so very healing! My favorite lake too for sure, we can go there too  Now I have drivers license and I can take you wherever you want

### sooperb — 2013-11-22T13:36:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-101337>

Inger it sounds beautiful  I'm not sure about the fishing, I'm a lot protective over how we treat earth's creatures. I fully understand the need to catch animals for food, but for pleasure it's a big no no for me. There has been a story in our papers about a woman who shoots for fun, she pictured with the most beautiful big male lion that she had just shot, she had a big smile on her face, I wanted to shoot her for destroying such a majestic creature. There is another on Facebook of a whole American family posing with a dead elephant that apparently they shot while it was eating, its mouth still holding the grass, they were all smiling too. It makes me cry even thinking about it. I don't mind squashing mosquitoes though lol but even then, only if they are biting me. I don't kill wasps or bees or spiders.

I understand what you say about the visitor. My daughter is very sensitive to people too, she has what she calls her Gaydar, she can always tell if someone prefers the same gender  Plus she instinctively knows who to trust. I'm not sure about religion either lol, it seems to me that so many religions are created to benefit their creator rather than for altruistic reasons. Those at the top always seem to have so much more than their followers and there is lots of secrecy involved. I would tread a Buddhist path but I can't be vegetarian so I can only try to follow the principles. Gosh, I do go on, sorry

Hand peeled shrimp? Hmmm, my cats have hand opened pouches lol.

I read an old book about magnetism in nature, curry and hartmann lines to be more specific. In Sweden we have always said that cats find their resting spot on the crossing points and dogs in the free areas. This was used many times to find the resting spot for us in homes. The thing is that we humans should not sleep at crosses. This have been a norm that been erased from our way of thinking for long but i feel the same as them. When a dog finds a good place i also like it there, cats for me do not do it... I love cats but not next to me at sleep, dogs no problem.

Just something to try perhaps?

Read today on Jacks facebook site.. I just had to re post

> Do you own 100 percent of your focus? Talent does not separate people, endurance does. Too often people just quit when the situation becomes tough. Ironically, they forget that endurance leads us to success in our endeavors. Patience is the antidote of ego in times of trial. Every violent storm gives way to sunlight, and every dark evening fades to dawn. Do you wait for it, or seek to circumvent things? Bravado may stir the crowd, but courage needs no audience. **Some days you will feel no song in your heart, but what stops you from singing? People who have been to hell and back seem to know that a hunger for a taste of the future returns for all those who overcome difficulty.** It's not about what you're capable of, it's about what you are willing to endure. Today, realize the sky is not your limit.......you are.

This is exactly what I have been pondering lately. That there is days in between when one feel... not so amazing. But it will pass... it always does. I just continue to do the good stuff and sunshine always
returns.. or the song in my heart. I cannot believe Jack posted this, exactly what has been in my mind too lately

like eating yummy cake but way better

### Inger — 2013-12-04T11:20:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-102271>

I started exercising. After almost 2 years on Jack's protocols I thought it is time! I need to be careful not to do too much tho, at least in the beginning. I got a gym membership from hubs and I plan on doing 3 times / week 1/2 to 1 hour each. I was Sunday and yesterday.. and now I have so sore inner thigh muscles! outch! I do 9 minutes fast walking and 1 minute running to warm up and 30 minutes weights, that is. Doing the exercise only when sun is up. I think this is going to do me good

I am zero carb by now and everything feels very good

I got real blue blockers yesterday and I think they are great! Now if I am with family on the holidays they can see my eyes too. Better than my dark sunglasses for sure. I do not like to talk to people not seeing their eyes either.

Coffee is practically out for me, but I have a tiny espresso cup once in a while if I feel like it. But not every day and very small amount in middle of the day. I feel coffee in the morning is not good for me. No clue why but my body says no. So if I have it, it is around lunch time, a tiny amount. I drink green or white tea in the morning instead, my favorite right now is white tea with peppermint.... I love it!

My food is very simple these day. Raw beef heart, liver, raw fish/seafood... raw beef fat, seaweeds. I got hungry yesterday evening because I exercised, me thinks. Had lots of raw beef heart and fat after the exercise but it must have been too little. I ate nothing though, just went to bed. Today morning I was not hungry anymore! But I made me a huge breakfast of beef liver, very gently warmed in some french butter in the iron pan, raw in the middle.. that is what I craved yesterday evening! Had some raw beef fat and high heart too, and some 100% chocolate. Got to stop buying that 100% chocolate... I cannot resist it..lol

Life is good

### nonchalant — 2013-12-04T17:17:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-102285>

Hi Inger! I haven't started exercising either, but last weekend we replenished our firewood supply. The sun was shining bright, so I went out in shorts and a tank top and worked all day barefoot. The neighbors again expressed their surprise that I wasn't cold in that outfit, but really, I would have been cold if my bare skin wasn't soaking up those rays. I surprised DH by lifting a fairly large oak log about 2 meters long. Guess my scoliosis is improving! I moved our saw horse out of the encroaching shade of the barn in the afternoon and got a lot more work done, but I was really tired in the evening. I layed flat on my tummy on the ground in a spot of the yard that still had a little sun for about 15 minutes, and recovered somewhat. But after dinner I had to ice my wrist that was sore from sawing. I went to bed early and slept a long time.

To my surprise the next day I only had some soreness on that same arm, the bicep and lower arm. Wrist was fine. Back was fine, despite splitting wood with an axe, which always used to do me in, though I love splitting wood. Something about the beauty and coordination of the entire movement...

I waited for soreness on the third day as well, but all was good. My chiropractor saw nothing unusual yesterday, just a few minor adjustments. This is amazing for me. I really should start some sort of regular exercise, like strolling around the block in the evenings with DH again.

### Inger — 2013-12-14T08:39:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-103395>

I did not got my period... very weird! I never miss it. Maybe i was because I started exercise, and made it too heavy on the top of it... and I even once dropped the weight on my abdomen as I wanted to do bench press, oh man! It hurt a bit and I had difficult to breath and i felt nauseous too for a few minutes. But then I was all fine again, no pain nothing. But I got pretty scared I hurt me badly, not so fun to get something heavy thrown into your belly! Ha. Maybe my body got a shock so it did not wanted to bless me with a period. Anyways, I feel great so I think I will not worry. I think I will train only once or twice a week instead of 3. IDK I like to dance and clean the chicken house and make firewood way better.. such kind of exercise! And.. hubs asked if I can help with his business and I agreed, so quite a bit to do there too! At least I get some more money now and I can work from home too which is just great in thes EMF crazy times - so that is awesome! Pretty exciting as I have no clue where this will go, my expertise is in waitress works not construction lol but we need to make some risks too at time, as long as they are well thought through. But impossible to know all the stuff for sure, so sometimes I just jump.

I come up with an amazing hair mask! The same I use to make in summer with special wild herbs but now here are no herbs anymore so I make it like this instead;

2 organic egg yolks
maybe 1 or 2 tablespoons of lemon juice (I do fresh, organic)
some drops to one teaspoon EVOO depending on how fatty you want it to be (dry hair - more oil)
1-2 tablespoon Silica gel

Mix it all in a cup and put it on your dry hair... and face! Awesome face mask too! Let it sit for quite a bit, with a shower hat or other plastic stuff wrapped around your hair - maybe half an hour or more or less, rinse, and wash your hair as usual. I only use Ghassul mud/clay.
I rinse my hair after the wash in river or rain water.

I use to have a spray bottle filled with rainwater, if I want some more "negative entropy" in my hair I just use it like a hair spray, and let my hair dry. Makes nice curls! Who needs any poison hairspray?

Oh.. and I forgot to share something very cool! The goose has started laying eggs!!! I thought she would only in spring/summer, as she stopped doing it in late summer but now there have been 3 days in a row a nice huge goose eggs waiting for me, yay! I make me a nice bulletproof coffee from it, with lukewarm (to not destroy any heat sensible nutrients in the yolk/coconut oil) organic coffee with organic whole cardamom seeds, lots of raw coconut oil, a duck egg yolk and a little salt. Mixing it into a nice frothy yummy coffee. I allow me one of these / day if I feel like it. Now when I am not concerned about my bloodsugar, and I clearly do not need coffee to get energy or wake me up in the morning either, I think it is ok with a little bit of the goodness! I have gotten me lots of nice teas too, herbal, green and white. I love Pukka's spicy teas, I just drink one with cardamom, cinnamon, ginger and licorice..mmm

The duck egg is super healthy as they 2 ducks get almost a pound of fresh wild caught fatty fish from me every day! And then oats too and shrimp heads and peels. The chicken get raw meat and organ pet food mix and oats and also sprouted oats and raw grassfed beef fat pieces and sunflower seeds and flax seeds... they look very healthy and happy. the gooses are so in love too, it is funny to see.. at least my goose egg is fertilized! haha.. I have taking them out for a swim every day when it is not freezing temps, I just take water from the basement into their frozen pond and they swim and wash themselves in it. Funny to look at. I love taking care of the animals, maybe because they love the healthy food I give them! hahaha... maybe because they are the only ones health I can control...! So it feels so damn good to be able to do that in this crazy world where few are willing to take the steps to get real change in their health. they might will be willing to take the help when they have really bad issues? That is what I hope just I hope it will not be too late by then.. - you can only lead the horse to water, not make it drink -, I just do it myself and for the animals and it makes me happy

### SeaHorse — 2013-12-14T14:44:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-103398>

Inger....yesterday I found six eggs in the chicken's nest boxes....they haven't been laying since September and usually don't start up again until the light comes back, but I guess their clocks are set a bit early! The eggs were frozen solid too, so I'm going to have to be more vigilant about checking...

I feel the same way about feeding my animals....and it's fun to watch them select what they need for minerals etc. if you give them lots of choices. One of my horses got into the area where I store their vitamin and mineral mixes, he broke open a container of salt/mineral mix and ended up dumping a lot of it into their water trough. Although the trough is fed by a stream and has a drain so it is constantly flowing...for some reason the salt mixture must have settled on the bottom below the drain and so far no ice has formed on the top! Usually I have to keep removing the ice when it's this cold....so he lowered the freezing temperature on his own! Clever horse.....and he got to make a huge mess which he loves to do.

My ewe who had the twins last May is pregnant again, so there will be winter lambs at some point...It's is 10 degrees below zero this morning, so everyone will get something special for breakfast.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-14T17:17:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-103404>

I put some epsom salts in the rabbits water bowls, but they still froze solid during our week-long cold spell, even if I changed them twice/day. Perhaps I didn't put enough salts in.
Also, their hutches are a lot colder this winter, since I now realize that some cold is good. When we move to the country, I want to have them on the ground, so that they get good electrons all the time, their water won't freeze easily, and so they stay cooler in summer. One wire connecting their cages to ground isn't enough.

I was never a country girl - altho I lived on a tobacco farm when I was a teen .... I do remember loving it but hated the isolation - my mother was a wack job.

so much has changed in my life .... maybe, one day, I will have baby lambs and chickens etc.

I think there is a quantum life force surrounding all of us......

### Inger — 2013-12-15T10:12:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-103486>

LOL...! I WISH! But no chance... if I am not virgin Maria... lol if this ever happens Audrey, you will be a godmother, grandmother, whatever you wish, I promise...! Nothing would make me more exited and happier than a tiny oyster baby  don't make me think about it or I might start to run out to look for someone special and that is not the right timing for right now..lol

I always looked for isolation. Loved to spend time in the woods alone. But that might be because I have 14 siblings and then you have so much social life going on anyways you need to escape it at times huh

@ Seahorse & Sally, do you guys put Epsom salt in the water for your animals...? Is that good for them? I never thought about that. I just change the water for our sheep once or twice a day. When there is snow I have seen they eat the snow, so then it would not be too bad if their water is frozen. I have no heat for the sheep... they are outside all winter long and have only a small house with open doors to stay dry and away from wind and to get shade. For the hens I have installed a heater over their water bowls, because in their house is no heat either and gets freezing cold in winter. I do change their water every day as the goose couple make it so freaking dirty! The goose couple is together with the hens, they are all friends.

Seahorse that sounds so nice to have horses! They are so beautiful.... Hope your horse did not got a vitamin overload.....

Sally yes you need to make your rabbits a cage on the ground =) do you eat them too? We have way too many hens now for the winter, 18. I buy them so much goodie food now in winter and much of my money goes to animal feed huh! So I asked my bro to help killing some. He might come today and I might leave only 8-10 because they are crazy for making babies all summer long and them we will have new chickens again. In summer I feed them just some oats as they are out all day long without any restrictions walking free around the yard, they even go to the neighbors..lol eating insects and grass
I cannot kill... or I could if I had no other choice but I hate it. Oh man, to kill those sweet babies I am feeding and talking to every day :-( My bro said he does not care he can do it. So kind! I will do all the other work after the killing part. Lots of work huh! To get all the feathers off and stomach content nicely out and all.. but our hens are tasting so great! They have a dark meat and not too much meat on them either. Almost like game meat!

### HoneyChild — 2013-12-15T18:04:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-103512>

The other week I saw a pigeon with a twig in its beak, obviously it must think it's that time of year again.

### SeaHorse — 2013-12-15T22:22:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-103541>

I think the clock was a bit early because of the heat lamp I put in a few weeks ago to keep their water from freezing...it is a red light, but it is still enough of a trigger for them to start laying early. Most years if I don't have any heat lamps...they will begin to lay in late January...because they naturally molted and had an egg laying break already, I'm going to keep the heat on for now. These chickens seem pretty hardy, but I've had some get frozen combs and toes in these temperatures. (I do appreciate your suggestions to observe however!)

Inger...I don't use Epsom salts (mag.sulfate)...what ended up in the trough was a mixture of seasalt and minerals. I have made solutions of magnesium chloride as an oral supplement for the horses, but it is very strong and bitter, so I would image the epsom salts might be too.

This year we were pleasantly surprised by the long season of egg laying by my friend's guinea hens. They normally just lay from spring to mid summer, but this year they layed all the way to Autumn.

I know as soon as we get a salt block for our horse and goats they go nuts for it and the goats like to like the salt off of our vehicles. I know its not the right salt for they won't stay in the fence.

### sooperb — 2013-12-16T14:06:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-103573>

We have our own chickens and we used to just put a light in their hutch to increase the laying period. We only have 3 at the moment, one has only ever laid about six times and the other two have stopped because of day length and one is moulting, she looks so shabby lol. OH said about putting a light in there again but tbh I don't think that is very natural so I said no. If I wanted rubbish eggs I'd buy them cheap from the supermarket, instead when I have to I buy organic free range but of course I do wonder if they get extra light because they are obviously still laying.

### Inger — 2013-12-28T09:41:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-104343>

House is full of people.. and I again have to realize children are magic!

Have started to take 10 mg PQQ every morning.. and I have the feeling it is very potent! Has made me lose my desire for coffee 100%. Very weird. And I have always loved coffee! Now I do not want it.. I just drink tea instead, green tea and herbal teas.
I got an infection in my finger before Christmas, I was doing some work on the house, repairing the wooden stairs as I wanted to oil them with linseed oil, and then painting too.. and I think that is why. Next day my finger was so swollen and hurt so bad! Many nights I could barely sleep. I had to keep my hand out of the duvet and it got cold air and that helped but in the end it hurt so bad it did not help either. I slept so little 4 nights in a row. Then I finally found a place where to poke a hole in my poor inflamed finger and that is the end of my suffering.
But here comes the weird thing! Even if I slept so little, I was never tired? I did not had any coffee either... and now my finger is almost 100% healed. So glad it is, I was a little worried, does not look pretty at all when it was at its thickest! And the last thing I would want is antibiotics.

So I am thinking the PQQ is a good thing to take  must be a super energy booster!

### Inger — 2014-01-08T13:53:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-105058>

I fell into a hole after the kids were gone......... a dark place to be. I had such a hard time to come out of it! I kind of knew it was all my fault and my thinking but I was stuck

Monday I felt such a strong urge to take the car and drive to my favorite lake, have not been there since I went to Norway end of the summer. It was starting to get dark, and I had quite a boit to walk through the forest until I arrived at the lake. It was so silent! Not even a bird was singing.... magic. I took off my clothes and stepped into the lake. As I stood there for a few minutes into my neck, an eagle suddenly appeared and flied in circle above me. I could only hear the sound of its wings moving... wow

As I come home it was as something has clicked in my head. I so clearly could see it was my lately pretty flawed thinking, and how I let the surroundings effect my inner joy and peace. How wrong... no wonder i suffered huh! Next morning I woke up so happy and I totally got, how we think is what we get! **No none else ever is the fault of my unhappiness - only me.** I for sure knew this, but somehow still slipped into the dark, how weird!

Through all this, I learned a ton. That is good. Weird how the wrong thoughts come easier... lol at least when times get a little rough. I am a little disappointed at myself so to say - but hey, lets look forward

And pretty magic how the loneliness in the wild brings back the sanity  I kind of felt I needed to go days before but I kept postponing the trip because of all the "noise" around. stupid me! Next time I feel even the slightest "entropy" I will go out there and dip in the magic lake. It is really a place of total silence - and not one soul is there at this time of the year. You are as alone as it gets... with the wild animals

### Inger — 2014-01-08T14:05:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-105059>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=103573)
>
> We have our own chickens and we used to just put a light in their hutch to increase the laying period. We only have 3 at the moment, one has only ever laid about six times and the other two have stopped because of day length and one is moulting, she looks so shabby lol. OH said about putting a light in there again but tbh I don't think that is very natural so I said no. If I wanted rubbish eggs I'd buy them cheap from the supermarket, instead when I have to I buy organic free range but of course I do wonder if they get extra light because they are obviously still laying.

I cleaned up the chicken house and it is now very nice! Put clean hay everywhere... We have about 16 chicks and 3 roosters. But get only about 2-3 eggs / day now. Almost every day one duck egg though! I put the lights on at daytime and off between 4-6 PM. Your chickens are pretty good making that many eggs in winter! You are right that they make quite a mess, and there is much work doing cleaning! But I like it.. I like this kind of work, it is so "close to earth" ;-)

The one that is moulting... that is funny! Our do not do that when it is this cold and dark.... and if they did I might take the eggs away because the baby chicks will die in this cold, I have no heating on in their house except over their water place if it gets very cold outside so it does not freeze. They drink a ton! So you get baby chicks soon... oh man. I am envious. They are so sweet!

I think that they get enough protein is important for the laying too. But that is the expensive feed so the eggs get really expensive if I buy them so much meat... The 2 ducks get almost a pound of fresh fish every day so no wonder she makes eggs! ;-)

Do you know what I do for the chicks too once in a while? Sprouted oats! They love them! You can make oats grass too for them in winter, as they need grass and when snow and cold outside they do not get any. I let the chicks out to walk freely in the yard everyday still as it is so warm here now but comes weekend it will be cold and I might start some oat sprouting for them again.

Animals are so much fun =)

### Inger — 2014-01-08T14:08:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-105060>

and... one more thing! My experiment with the flowers I told Sean I will do.. it is almost finished! I put a couple of roses in two separate glasses of water, close to another, one grounded, one not. I already see a difference!!! The grounded is looking better, seriously! I was amazed as I realized it a few days ago. This means, my grounding rod will be back on my bod every night pretty soon

This is beautiful Inger. You are so right. It is our reaction to adversity that determines good or bad times. DH & I were talking about that. We had spent a day cutting and chopping wood for our antique stoves. And I know that my ancestors bought them a gas stove & heaters the first chance they had. To get away from the labor of the firewood and mess of the ashes. Sure, we are just playing with it all right now, and I imagine it could get old after a while. But our attitude is turning a drugery into an adventure. I suppose having a choice in the matter makes a big difference.

Your magic lake sounds like paradise. So glad you listened to your intuition.

we really do need to tune out the "noise" ..... and let the magic in! xo

### sooperb — 2014-01-09T10:47:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-105138>

Inger we don't have chicks as we don't have a cockerel  we had our first egg in two months as the other hen also started moulting, the hen house looked like a fox had got in, feathers everywhere.

My daughter got a cockerel and two hens once, he spent all day jumping on the hens, they never had a moments peace. Then he started attacking the children, they were only small and if they got too close to "his" girls he would fly at them with his feet and the youngest had a big rip in her head and the other child had a big gash down her leg. He was very fierce and only being protective but he had to go.

She had other hens before that and several cockerels, they used to fight each other and there were lots of baby chicks which got eaten by weasels and things, even so she was getting more and more hens and being overwhelmed. She gave a lot away and eventually they went through natural wastage one way or another, it was a big relief to me.

Your lake sounds such an amazing place, one of my meditations is walking to such a place in warm sunshine, standing under the waterfall, diving into the natural pool and floating for a while and then drying in the sun on a big slab of water worn flat rock. One day I might find a real place to do it

### Inger — 2014-01-09T14:37:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-105150>

Sally to have choices really make it way easier. I use to think, I can do whatever I want! But I choose to do this... (whatever it is) and it helps huge and feels like freedom. Because it is true. I do have so many opportunities, but I know what I want right now. Even if it is maybe not so comfortable in every aspect.. Makes it all way easier.

Sooperb that sounds like your daughters chicken club got a bit out of hand...lol Yeah.. they make babies like crazy here too in summer. But if I am not over protective nature does take care of it in a pretty good way so they do not get too many as they run free around all day long. I just lock them in for the night, they go inside themselves as it gets dark. They are very good in natural circadian rhythm management hehe It always amazes me! Even if I forget the lights on... they as soon as it starts to get a little dark, fly up on their sleeping places and sit there very silent and waiting for the dark.

The two roosters were fighting for real today. First time I see this. There are one king. And then the two young rooster siblings. That was pretty fun to watch! Like a boxing match... I am excited to go down and see if they are both alive still. Should be only one rooster for sure but I have kept those two as reserve because they can always end as prey as long as I let them out. The fox killed our last rooster king. I need to have one rooster always to get fertilized eggs. They are healthier eggs for sure.

Too sad your rooster was aggressive. You should try again. I remember as I was a kid we had a very aggressive rooster he jumped and bit me in my ass, ouch it hurt very bad! He wanted to bite everyone! But our roosters lately have been absolutely gentlemen. They never hurt anyone! Non of our animals do. Not the ram either. he is just the sweetest boy! The kids played with him at Christmas, he was like an angel. So nice to have kind animals when children are around.

I do believe the hens are happier with a rooster ;-) you sure will find a gentleman with some patience!

### caroline — 2014-01-09T22:24:18+0200

Inger - I love it when you speak of your animals ....... I know nothing about this.

really - my only memories are watching my father chop a chickens head off and it running around headless for ages. It terrified me!

They also used to have target practice with a sack full of kittens ...... I am still outraged .....

### Inger — 2014-01-10T20:55:23+0200

>

Oh my! You have pretty scary memories Audrey, poor kid! Uh.. I have a few of those too.. with the kittens  not fun.

I get terrified at times too now because I have no clue how to do things and especially if some get sick. I am so clueless! And I hate when animals are sick, even worse as when human get sick because they cannot tell me where it hurts. So I really do all I can to keep them healthy. I knew nothing about it as I came here but I just read and search and ask and now I know a bit more. Learning by doing so to say... not always fun if you do wrong! But I love animals. I let Emme, Mimosa and Kingi out today to run free around as I jumped on the trampoline outside in the garden and did some other stuff / work. They always come after me and stay where I am, no need to tie them.

Today was the last "summer day" I think? So warm, + 3 degree C, with tiny tiny raindrops flying around. Very nice, I enjoyed it so much and it was so refreshing! I got so warm I was too warm... even if I use the same clothes inside and outside, this is weird! Because it is way warmer inside? (16 degree C)
Tomorrow should be cold! And I have to drive pretty far to buy some grassfed meat for me and hay for the animals. I am hoping it will not be too icy on the road... not so used to drive on icy roads yet

### caroline — 2014-01-11T00:11:06+0200

Inger - I thought you had grown up around animals your whole life?

### Inger — 2014-01-12T11:18:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-105432>

Not really Audrey.. As I lived at home we had a few chickens and I had a goose and two lambs but I was not the one responsible in the way I am now.. and do not needed to worry if someone got sick or about buying the food and stuff. I actually do not remember too much of it, I do not even remember what I fed my goose? I just remember he was pretty aggressive lol Weird how there are times from when my memories are very unclear. I wonder why. Maybe I was not feeling too well and my brain was not really there.
I do remember one of my two lambs died because she had eaten too much oats. It was horrible. The other one, a ram, survived, even if he ballooned out quite a bit. I do remember how I slept outside on the grass in his pasture in summer and he lied down next to me. Those are beautiful memories..!
Back then my one bro was the chicken expert. I think. He still loves them very much and when he comes visit he helps me with them if I ask him, he is also the one that helps me slaughter. He does not mind at all! I cannot kill.... only if I have no other choice. He is a web designer lol but he loves all things with the animals.. goes fishing with me.. picking mushrooms... he is special for sure! He can repair just.. anything! He was like this since he was a kid - and he also never says no if you ask for help. Maybe he is an angel
On another note - I do think the animals suffer from the increased EMF that was not here as I was a kid. It hurts my heart to realize.. and makes me worry
I bet that is what made our ram Isak sick and made him die way too early as I was in Norway. He just got so fat and he had hard time breath and one day he just lied there dead in the pasture. He got only grass and hay and some veggies and fruits the neighbor gave them and apples from the garden.
Also.. as I come home from Norway and took care of the chickens again I realized our rooster king and some of the hens have this white stuff on their feet.. that is like a thickening cover? I have looked it up on google and I found it is not dangerous but some kind of mites that make this. But if it get very thick the cover, they can have issues walking. It does not look like they have that yet so I just wait it out. There are some spray you can put on them but IDK where to get that - no such stores around here. And maybe it will disappear comes summer. I hope! I will try to keep their home clean because that might help I guess.

The roads were dry and nice, so I had a fun drive. The old man I buy the hay from lives far on the countryside and he is so nice! We always talk quite a bit. Imagine, he has lived on this same place since he was a child! He said there are way less people around now. It is so silent out there. He looks very healthy.. he is so kind I almost fall in love with him.. lol He sells the stuff so cheap to me too, I got the car full with hay (maybe 12 pieces) and then 50 kg oats for just 30 €.

I got so much nice grassfed Angus meat too! The lady that owns the place with her husband is so lovely too. How are there so many lovely people around??! Her meat is the best, it tastes just awesome! That is a very silent place too... with large pastures all around the beautiful houses. Very nice place. I got 2 ox tails for just around 13 €, they had such lovely yellowish fat on them, I had to munch on it it was so yummy! I got many other things too. So fun to pick your food directly from the producer, IDK but it has something very special to it.

Feels so good to be back to simple eating. Yesterday I had raw grassfed beef heart for breakfast, for lunch some grassfed ground meat just warmed in some butter, still totally raw inside, for dinner a big cup of fish head smoothie (baltic herring heads) raw salmon and 4 oysters.. a bit later a raw duck egg + raw cacao + raw coconut oil + peppermint oil + water drink (this was a treat...lol)

I washed the car outside by hand.. and it was snowing! That was pretty funny! I had to hurry because it got colder and colder.. the water was freezing.. so I had to kept getting warm water to rinse with. Never had so much fun washing the car =)

It was cold tonight, the face dunk rainwater under my bed was frozen this morning..huh! My nose does got a bit cold in the night. I should make me a nose protector because this is the only place that gets cold even if it is - 20 degree. I do have a little hard time to go up from my warm bed.. it is just warm enough to feel so cosy under my duvet.. and the room is sooo cold and I just kept laying there as it feels so nice

looks so nice now as a thin cover of light snow is covering everything! The lambs are laying in their pasture right now (I can see them from my window)... in the snow, and they have snow on the top of them too..lol Looks like they love it! Imagine they do not seem to get bothered by the cold at all...

### sooperb — 2014-01-12T16:43:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-105450>

You have to be careful your sheep don't get buried Inger. If it snows too much and they don't move, their coats become saturated and then they are too heavy to move.

I found this which suggests quite a cheap way to treat the scaly leg mite:

"They get those mites and their legs, flesh around the ears, cones, and gizzards get effected by them. To help their legs, coat with Canola Oil and Petroleum-based or mineral oils on the legs and feet. I wouldn't try the areas on the head just yet though, wait until the legs are clearing up. But do this everyday or else the chicken will begin to limp, become crippled, eat and not gain weight, and die. We had that happen to a hen earlier this year.
If you have any way of isolating your chicken, do so, because these mites can spread. Our chicken coop has a wood floor and shavings for bedding, then the chickens are able to have a huge mini pasture who they share with 2 goats. Most chickens who are aloud to be free-range and outside, in dirt, etc, have the best risk of getting these mites. It's just mites that get under the scales of the legs and cause a lot of irritation. I said before how if this gets real bad, the chicken will begin to limp and the legs may even appear to have swelled up a bit."

### Inger — 2014-01-13T21:05:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-105568>

Sooperb no worry, it came only a little snow, maybe 10 cm... wish there were more! But it is cold. Again my water in my bedroom was frozen. I like cold but I hate when the water for the animals freeze in just a few hours huh
I hope the mites does not get that out of hand.. so far no one looks to have difficulties to walk. I think if it gets so far I just ask my bro to slaughter the ones that are affected. It is just a too big mess to start treating the "sick" ones, I cannot do it alone as I need someone to hold them for me, they are not all easy to catch.. the rooster is very easy but some hens let you just close but as soon as you try to catch them they fly away lol

I do hope the mites get frozen off and killed as it is freezing temps in the chickens house right now!

Jack that sound like summer.. that is nice too for sure! I really try to dive deep into this winter as long as it lasts, and enjoy it fully. with its darkness and all. It will soon be gone and spring will be here

### sooperb — 2014-01-14T15:03:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-105628>

The best way to catch them is in the morning. I don't know what kind of hutch they have but if it is one you can open without them getting out that makes it easy or you catch them if you can just reach in. Or just grab them as they come out of the door hole. I agree they are almost impossible to catch even when they are poorly. The leg mites burrow under their leg scales so I don't think the cold will kill them unfortunately. Lucky Jack in the warm.

### Inger — 2014-01-14T21:29:23+0200

I might try it.. they are inside now as they do not want to go out when there is snow, even if I keep doors open.

So cold here now that I wake up to a \*BAM\*... a glass water bottle under my bed exploded because it had frozen..HA Lesson learned - never store water bottles of glass under your bed in winter. I could not imagine it got so cold in my bedroom - I did not freeze at all.. my cat slept next to me all night long and she did not complained either - slept like a rock lol
I had to put the heater on in the kitchen, it was only 6 degrees there. Felt pretty warm tho, in comparison to the bedroom

Had to work quite a bit with the ax to make a hole in the ice today. I was in only 2 minutes but it felt like enough!

### Inger — 2014-01-15T11:14:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-105752>

lol

I am NOT worth any award for sure! I am a CT wimp lol I do only 2 minutes it is nothing, and I do sleep with covers! I have two very thin summer duvets and I sleep nude and that is just enough to keep me warm. If I get a little cooled down on the skin surface I just turn around and the downside of my skin that touch the mattress will always be warm. So I am really not suffering lol I could not sleep if I were freezing!

What is weird is, I think I always get warmer when I sleep. Like, my nose can be pretty cold and I even have to warm it a bit under the blanket at times, but then I fall asleep and when I wake up my nose is warm? And the I lay there for a while and then it gets cold again. Very weird. Maybe in sleep we relax so much that the body warms up way better? I have to figure out what I do wrong! I want my nose and my hands and my feet to be hot all the time huh! But they are not. I need gloves when outside and wool socks inside. When I am in "normal" room temp as others are used to I never have cold hands and feet for sure. But if I sit in front of the computer and work I need to cover my feet or they get pretty cold, my hands get a bit cold at the computer too I have realized. In front of the computer is no way healthy place to be. That is why I am going out now to clean the chicken house lol

always when I am out and in the nature I feel sooo good!

Oh, forgot to tell what I eat Audrey! I eat like normal, but pretty much - lots of fish and fat and meat. I feel when I eat more it keeps me warm.. I must be so unoptimal! I would rather that my body should use my fat to keep warm..lol but hey, I am not complaining because it is my own fault I am sitting in front of the computer it always makes me eat more! I always eat when I feel I need it. So I eat. As long as my clothes are fitting and I look about the same it is okay. Maybe I have a bit more fat than in summer but I know clearly why, no surprise at all....

So my conclusion = computer is an energy robber
Nature is an energy giver.

That is all I need to know

works for any health issue IMHO

### nonchalant — 2014-01-15T17:32:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-105766>

When I was a child, I experimented with wearing just one sock when I went to bed in the winter. I found that in the morning, the foot with the sock was ice cold, and the other foot was warm and happy. I wondered about it, but figured it was a circulation issue. I guess I was playing with CT at an early age, lol. When your skin contacts the cold sheets, it goes into CT mode. The socked foot, however, like any part of you that you do not submerge while in a CT bath, did not get the benefit at all. Another reason to sleep naked.

We should stop turning down the bed in the winter. We usually do that, when in our country house, in order for the sheets to warm up a bit with the evening fire.

### sooperb — 2014-01-15T18:51:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-105773>

I'm fed up of waking up sweating. I go to bed and often have the window open but am still hot so lay outside the covers until I feel cool enough to pull the duvet over me. I fall asleep but keep waking up hot and sweaty with palpitations, so it's off with the cover until I cool down again, sometimes this happens several times a night, others just once. I can't fathom it. I'm not sure if I like the idea of the room being so cold that water freezes lol but as a child our home only had a fire in the living room, often in winter the bedroom windows would be frosty on the inside so I guess it's not that bad for you as none of us suffered as far as I can remember.

And if Inger gets a reward for sleeping with one cat, what's on offer for sleeping with two and a dog? Only the cats settle on the bed sometimes

Once I started sleeping nude - I can't wear even a t-shirt. We have turned into an odd lot!

### Inger — 2014-01-21T20:14:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-106488>

Oh my I got soo sick. I ate sausages. Hubs was here and I fried up some sausages for him at 7 PM, they were the best sausages ever, from the lady I drive and buy my grassfed Angus meat from. They make these sausages, only from Angus meat, lard and spices and ascorbic acid. ThatÂ´s it. Should be very healthy sausages, no? But my body rebelled... it kind of did not was able to digest them at all? Weird as I do make me some rare steaks at times and eat some cooked seafood to once in a while so my body can tolerate cooked foods great even if I feel better on raw. But I guess because these sausages were cooked 2 times maybe (they were precooked sausages, not raw). That might have been it. Or the lard...dunno it must have been from regular pork, no idea. Hubby did not get sick at all - he ate way more of them than I, I just ate 1 and a half and they are those thin and longs sausages. so it could not be anything wrong with them either. Just my body hated them!
Next day I felt soo sick too and I could not eat... whohoho that is crazy, I always have appetite. My belly hurt too. I thought oh my now I have issues like Sean! OMG what if the issues stay... I got a bit scared, not used to suffer like this, I can eat rotten meat and raw fish heads, even older ones, and have no issues. But now... I had not have had sausages in maybe 7 years.. I then made me a shake from one raw duck egg, 2 tbsp coconut oil, and cacao powder and mint oil. I got it down but right after I felt even sicker. After a few hours I vomited it all.. and I got the runs too... pretty bad. Oh my it was a horrible day as I had to sign an important business paper in the city and I just felt so yuck. Hubby was nice to me and drove me there and home, thanks God.

So yesterday I went to bed totally empty and a bit dehydrated, even water hurt my tummy. I had only maybe 1 bottle the whole day. I went to bed very early. And I slept like a princess! Wow.. just amazing sleep! In the morning I tried to drink some water and it did not make tummy ache so I figured IÂ´ll try a raw oyster. That went nice too, so an hour later I took two grassfed Angus Osso BuccoÂ´s and warmed them gently in the pan in French butter. I ate both big OssoÂ´s. Oh it was so yummy! Totally blue, bluer then blue lol but I like it that way a lot! No issues.
So a bit later I though I might try a bulletproof duck egg coffee. With a raw duck egg and raw coconut oil. But that did not feel too good at all. I regretted it. But thankfully no tummy ache or any other nastiness. So then for dinner I had 6 oysters and a seaweed smoothie, thatÂ´s it. Felt awesome. Now I feel awesome, tummy is 100% healthy again!

And I will not ever touch sausages again.............

@ Sally that is cool with the one sock on! You was a funny kid doing such experiments already then lol

@ Sooperb,
could you use thinner covers? I tried to use hubbyÂ´s duvet as he is not here now... because I felt a bit sick from the sausage.. and it is a thick duvet. I use normally two very very thin duvets, like double. It is just enough that I will not freeze. So, I thought I will be cosy warm with the hubbyÂ´s one.

First it felt nice... but in the night I was too warm! So it was definitely too warm a cover for me. Tonight I will do my thin duvets again, if I feel a little too cool I rather put a wool blanket over me on the top. I need just enough, not too much cover it seems! And weird is, I had eaten nothing the whole day (or I tried but vomited it all) so I would think I would not make so much heat in the night but I did!

One night hubby was here and I felt so much like cuddling and I asked if I could sleep by him and he said yes of course! So I crawled under his blanket with full intentions to sleep in his bed the whole night... but after a hour or so laying there and enjoying it I started to feel just too warm so I had to escape to my freezer huh! poor hubby was a bit disappointed I guess...
I sleep so well in my freezer on my magnetico! The best sleeps I have when I have had nothing after 3 or 4 PM, the earlier the better. And no 100% chocolate - a little more of it makes my heart race

@ Audrey yes me too... I love the shock you get when you jump nude into the ice cold bed...lol and the you warm up so fast! I just make sure my feet are warm when I get into bed, and the rest of my body warms up in no time. When feet are cold and I go to bed that can be hard to get them warm. I then put my toes on my legs for warming up, it helps!
hehe.. I used to sleep with my big teddy when hubs was away many years ago...lol made me feel safe. Now I am so old I feel safe alone too ;-)

### Inger — 2014-01-27T16:14:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-107035>

Audrey I was scared for so many things... too many!!! I wish that on no one.

I have realized it depends a lot what food I eat cooked how it feels/digests. From cooked mussels I do feel great! But meat that is heated a bit longer.. is not so good - raw is way easier to me. I digest it so well it feels like I have not eaten at all lol
I should relisten to the webinar too... but might be too late. I do loved it!

I have realized, it is VERY important to choose high quality nuts when / if eating them. What a crazy difference. Normal nuts from the store makes me feel not good at all, I can literally feel/see how they give me inflammation.. when with raw, organic, fresh nuts I have no issues at all! Not to talk about the taste.. if you get used to the high quality you cannot eat any other nuts anymore they just taste terrible. I ate so many nuts this Christmas, pistachios, walnuts, macadamias, almonds... but I ordered them from Germany from the only company I trust when it comes to nuts. I tried the "regular" walnuts from the store and I had to spit them out!

I finally figured out in my head what a 90% water diet means. hehe that is how slow I am. Now I am 100% sure it means the volume of food eaten. pretty cool! I wonder how I became sure of this only today?

I am feeling so amazing lately! I am pretty sure my body is getting leaner too?! No clue why that is.. I do so many things! I have started to try to eat only when it is light outside. Pretty short time here in winter so I will get at least a 16 hour fast every night, even longer. I sometimes break my rule tho, and eat dinner around 5 PM but I try not to.

I also try to get day/sunlight in my eyes, 30 min. / day minim. is what I aim for but I do not always do that. But at least 10 minutes and when sun is out I look at it directly for the last minutes before it drops down on the horizon. I sit on my balcony... or I jump on the trampoline in the backyard. I love to jump on it and sungaze at the same time!

And the exercise feels so good! I do so little but I already am tightening up. Imagine just around 40 min. once / week it is nothing! I do have started to jump a few minutes on the trampoline outside looking at the sky every day. That is some of my favorite way of moving!

I am so lucky to mostly be able to sleep as long as I want. So I go to sleep between 9 and 10 PM and then I wake up... no idea? I have no clock to look at in my bedroom... so I just lay there in the dark and feel good.. and think.. and then I often fall a sleep again and when I wake up it is starting to get light, around 8-8:30 AM Oh man it feels so good! I love to spend so much time in my dark and freezing bedroom. Feels like winter sleep, really! And it feels so right to do that. So healing... I really have cut back on activity now in winter. I have it dark all evening, I use just salt lamps and candles + blueblockers.

haha what a boring life I have! But I have to say I enjoy it so much! I take care to get all the work done as long as it is light, so all the dark time I use for reading and studying or others stuff.

### Hope — 2014-01-27T16:29:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-107037>

Hi Inger, I haven't said Hi in a while but I keep thinking of you wishing I could eat raw like you, but not yet  I still need to cook everything and need a lot of vegetables, but they help me so much.

I LOVE that you have the life you do...it sounds magical. And how wonderful you are sleeping so much! I wake up early still.....it's kind of weird though it doesn't get light here till about 715am. But I love being in the dark too. I even love the super cold and snow, just not the ice on which I almost feel. I truly believe all the seasons now are magical...they all have their place. I only hate high humidity here in summer bc we are in a moldy home but hopefully I can actually live in a different place come summer

perhaps all that sleep you get helps you also with the feeling slimmer? But you looked amazing even last year.

Have a great day and week!! Hugs to you ~

### Inger — 2014-01-27T16:29:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-107038>

Yesterday my bro killed the rooster king for me. He took another rooster too and one hen that was not looking good and had that white stuff on her feet too. I did all the other work alone, feathers off... innards out etc. I felt bad. The worst part for me is to dig out the innards as the rooster still was warm.. it felt so awkward, like he was still alive as his heat was still present. I took the hearts from the roosters and saved for me. I will eat them raw tomorrow. The liver too.

Now it is so silent in the chickenhouse. I stood there for quite a while today and watched them as the sun set. They know when it is, that is exactly when they go and sit on their "sticks". They never sleep on the floor or in the small "houses" where they lay eggs. They are like birds sleeping in a tree! Chicken are fascinating animals. The rooster king that now is dead used to sit always on the same place. Yesterday evening the new king, the young rooster, did not take his place yet, it was empty. But today, he did. I watched it happen it was so cool! He has some work to do to have the hens accept him in the place of their beloved king, he do was amazing. But I think the new one will do it the hens have no other choice than to take him lol

No eggs today and yesterday tho... weird. Must be because they are grieving.... Otherwise there has always been at least one egg, in average 2-3 / day now in winter. We now have 14 hens and 1 rooster, counted them today.
I am very curious how the next days will look like in the chicken house! They are like a clan for sure! And the ducks... haha.. they are just walking around in the chickenhouse with their clumsy feet talking and talking lol

### Inger — 2014-01-27T16:52:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-107039>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=107037)
>
> Hi Inger, I haven't said Hi in a while but I keep thinking of you wishing I could eat raw like you, but not yet  I still need to cook everything and need a lot of vegetables, but they help me so much.
>
> I LOVE that you have the life you do...it sounds magical. And how wonderful you are sleeping so much! I wake up early still.....it's kind of weird though it doesn't get light here till about 715am. But I love being in the dark too. I even love the super cold and snow, just not the ice on which I almost feel. I truly believe all the seasons now are magical...they all have their place. I only hate high humidity here in summer bc we are in a moldy home but hopefully I can actually live in a different place come summer
>
> perhaps all that sleep you get helps you also with the feeling slimmer? But you looked amazing even last year.
>
> Have a great day and week!! Hugs to you ~

Hi Hope!  I wrote a long reply to your journal the other day but just as I wanted to post it the forum crashed...! I wanted to say you something.... if it could make you feel any better. I found a huge mold spot on my magnetico mattress some days ago..
I lifted my mattress and between it and the magnetico, where my back and butt use to be... was this black mold. I got pretty shocked! Went to the shop and got some mold killer stuff I think it was based on vetyperoxide? Not chlorine, as it was meant for textiles. I sprayed it on the mold and let it sit and dried it off with a drier as good as I could. Of course it does not help much,. no way can I get it away I cannot wash the mattress either and it is grown into the magnetico. I throw away the mattress too and use the other only now. I had two as this is a queen size bed and only the one was moldy that i normally sleep on. So now I have a pretty narrow space to lay on lol but works perfect! I am thinking I should pull the mattress away from the magnetico every day so it can air, during the day. And spray some more of the mold killer on it!

I think I know why this happened. First.. I get very warm in the night. I am not sweating at all, but still the room is ice cold and my body is warm. And in the daytime I have stored my water bottles precisely on that spot where the mold was! So I bet whatever damp was still in the mattress could not come out because of the glass bottles. Even if I always remove my duvet to air the bed in the daytime. But stupid me forgot to think the bottles will prevent that to a way higher degree
yeah. That is how clever I am.

I should have put the bottles somewhere where my warm body had not touched the mattress the whole night! but you know... there was a bump right there.. and I though that bump is great to put the bottles in! Oh my that was a mold bump LOL
actually IDK if I should cry or laugh. Certainly not fun when your super expensive dear magnetico gets moldy....! Not going to throw it away for sure. I am going to trust my bod it can get rid of the mold.

Hope your body can too! Just do all you can in any other aspect so your redox potential becomes crazy strong! And try to be as much outside you can... and keep window always wide open in the night, the mold will fly out the window
and... reducing stress have been something I have worked on a lot lately. I just refuse to let anything make me stressed! I listened to Nora Gedgaudas latest talk and she told nothing else you do will help if you are stressing / have a high stress level. I bet this is something we women should really think about. We REALLY really need to NOT stress. It will destroy us, literally! So.. I am starting to be better at that all the time, which I think also contributes to this amazing well being-feeling I have lately...

It is not so much what circumstances we are in, it is how we choose to react to them that matters. I am pretty sure stress reduces our redox potential dramatically.

### Hope — 2014-01-27T17:40:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-107045>

Oh Inger, what a bummer about the lost blog post! I would have so loved to read it  But so happy to hear from you here. I am so sorry about the magnetico. Wow. I have to ask them about that, and check under mine too! I do put water bottles on here, too, but usually at the bottom of the bed. But how cool about the warmth you create, that is just so cool!  I am so happy for you.

I admire you so much with the chickens. I couldn't do what you did. I think it's a genetic thing. My grandfather, they had chickens too, but he could not kill or do anything with them at all. Had to give it to his friend who was a butcher. It is quite weird because he was brave in other ways. He hid my mom from being picked up by Hitler's youth group for girls as he didn't want her to go there. He knew that guy was bad news. He also ran away from the Russians once (all in WWII) and broke a leg trying to get away. but, other people killed whole families in their town, before the Russians could come and hurt them. SO sad. And yet he also abused my mom, hit her really hard she had to see a doctor, UGH. a very strange man. But then, he was a radio mechanic with his own little business, made his own TV remote in what became East Germany. Also ended up with cancer....and I am certain EMF had a part in that. So sad.

Anyway. YOU are amazing....I just couldn't do it. Yesterday I almost slid in the car into another because I didn't want to hurt a squirrel that was thinking of running into the street. Such a wimp, me.

Yes I agree about the mold and stress. I got pretty sick from it. It totally explains SO MUCH about me......my symptoms and issues...it has all come together for me. I do try to do what I can to increase my redox potential. Be outside as much as I can. Just took a walk earlier and thought of you and wished I could be in Europe  I do try to do so much......and yes I am needing to learn to stress less. It is the worst. Stress and worry too. I am definitely stressed about our living situation. I do have the windows open as much as possible. ALso have two HEPA filters running which should help to clean the air of the spores a little. But the key is my mitochondria. They have been bad for years, I am certain. Of course my very low MSH levels have not helped. I am sure they have been dropping for 8 years. I just started a new drug to help with the mold toxins. I hate taking it but I feel somehow it might be helping me. I won't take it long and hope to stop once we move....it is not easy finding a place around here. But I trust the right place will come along.

Thanks again for sharing your adventures, indeed.....always love it!!!!! Hugs and love from across the big pond ~

### Inger — 2014-01-30T18:36:55+0200

It is not so strange at all Audrey. Look, I kept lots of glassbottles directly on that place where my body had warmed up the mattress the most, it should have needed to air in the daytime. Very stupid of me! I can clearly see my mistake.

But not too sad as I feel great sleeping on that moldy magnetico too lol

People in a hot and humid climate will have no issues if they just let the bed air normally during daytime I am sure! not keeping a ton of bottles on the critical places huh

I just was charging my batteries in the icehole, totally dark.. -11 dergee C and my feet tend to freeze to the ground when I come up from the water hehe
now I lay in front of the fireplace on a lambs skin warming up... I so love fireplaces! Life is good... And now I am going to go read the new blog

### Hope — 2014-02-01T18:26:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-107729>

Inger, here is what Magnetico emailed me back when I asked.....I am a bit confused about where you had the water bottle...I thought it was on the bed, not in between your regular mattress and magnetico.

it sounded like her actual bed mattress got wet and as the mold began to grow on the bed mattress, the mold then started on the Magnetico pad as the Magnetico pad is in contact with the sleeping mattress. With a water bottle under the sleeping mattress, it seems to me maybe the water bottles leaked and this is what caused the mold to develop ( not necessarily her body temp)
She talks about not being able to wash the mattress. I interpret this to mean she can not wash her bed mattress and that she tried to spray her bed mattress with the mold killer stuff... When she mentions throwing out the mattress- I am assuming she refers to her bed mattress and not the Magnetico pad?
Dr Bonlie has said that the Magnetico pad can be sprayed with the mold killer product. She would need to remove the zipper cover ( please note that she would have to mark the actual sleep pad with the direction that faces up as once the outer zipper cover is removed, there are no markings on the actual pad as to which side faces up and which side faces down ) and spray the actual Magnetico pad with the mold killer product and then she needs to ensure that the pad thoroughly dries. Dr Bonlie recommends getting it out in the sunshine once it's been sprayed so that the sun can also aid in the drying of the Magnetico pad.
She can purchase a replacement zipper cover as well. We don't recommend washing the cover as it is made of cotton so there is the risk that it will shrink.

### Hope — 2014-02-01T18:29:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-107730>

Inger, I also wanted to ask you...how did you start your CT? Did you write about it? Would you mind sharing what you did? I really have to get into it asap..I have not done it enough...how long did it take you to do the CT in a creek? last spring I did quite a bit of CT in a nearby lake where you aren't allowed to swim but I was wading....lol. But I wonder if I can try it in a creek that is in the mountains, and pretty clean....but not sure how to determine how much to do before I do something deeper....I guess just try.

I did start putting ice around my neck and I must say it actually feels good.

### sooperb — 2014-02-01T19:35:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-107746>

I wonder if the cause of the mould is nothing more than Inger sweating at night. Apparently we lose about a litre of liquid which in most cases is absorbed by the bedding. Inger says she is always warm and sometimes hot so if the bed mattress is absorbing her sweat and that is then coming into contact with the metal, would that be enough, over time, to cause mould to form, especially as she keeps her bedroom cold? I know my daughter had this problem in her last home, she had a latex mattress over her standard mattress and then one day when she moved the top one, she discovered that the other had lots of mould on it. Not only that but it was so cold in her bedroom that the carpet under the bed was mouldy too, not so good because she is asthmatic, it didn't help at all.

Hope, you are an Angel...! Thank you so much for asking for me! Then I have done it all right. But I need to buy a better heater to dry it lets see if I can do that next week. Then I will spray it some more and dry it again. No change to dry it in the sun right now... it has been snowy and overcast. But comes spring I will do that too!
So glad he said the magnetico will not be destroyed from the mold killer that was my biggest concern.

I did store the bottles on top of the mattress, not directly on the magnetico(or in between the two mattresses). He might be right a bottle might have leaked without me realizing it. That is possible for sure.

### Inger — 2014-02-02T17:23:12+0200

>
> I did start putting ice around my neck and I must say it actually feels good.

I did not start Hope. I kind of got born into this. Many up here are. Already as kids we rolled naked in the snow when we were in the sauna.. and we run bare feet in the snow each spring.. and I had been dipping in an ice hole many times before reading about CT.
I would say.. if you have no serious heart condition or illness just jump in! That is what they say here at least. Everyone can do ice dips, just not if you have some serious troubles with your heart, then it might be dangerous.
So just start with rolling in the snow or jump into that creek! You will not regret it I promise! No need to be so long, I mostly do only 2 minutes. My mom just do a few seconds! You can start with just 5 seconds or so. Yesterday I did not dip in the river at all.. I was lazy! I warmed at the fireplace.. and then I rolled nude in the snow at my balcony.. lol After eating lots of my apple cider marinated raw Baltic herring with raw onions and 3 excellent raw oysters, so yummy, fat and sweet! After the rolling I warmed up by the fireplace again. so nice!

Today was warm! -4 degree C. I was 2 minutes in the river, the water is right at 0 C I think. Sometimes it goes even a bit under but does not freeze, cool! Maybe because the water is moving under the ice.

### Inger — 2014-02-17T09:12:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-110742>

Hope... you cannot imagine what stupid thing I did again..! I thought I will detox some of the fluoride again I had to ingest as a kid (we had to eat those fluoride pills, yuck!) and I am sure I have some bromide too, and as I am always so impatient I though, ah, I'll help nature to do it faster with some Lugol's. I did last spring and got bad detox back then but I figured I have felt so crazy good lately, I can take it. So I took pretty much.. eh.. and I even painted it everywhere on my body, it is like I got obsessed with it (I love to paint, always have... must be why!) and after a few days of doing this... I got weird pimples (thanks God smaller and way less bad than last spring) and I all of a sudden felt so FAT! Must have been swollen... oh man the amazing feeling went a bit away. So I realized I do not want this either and stopped the Lugol's. Now I am going to take a tiny drop here and there and sometimes paint me too but holy moly I totally overdid it!

I read that too strong detox can destroy your hormones too... yikes! Hope we need to be careful with that stuff, better to do it the natural way or then veeeery slow with some help. I know wild greens should be very safe, and seaweeds, and just real food. And water! Those things are probably the safe way... IDK what it is in human nature that like to mess and experiment with all kind of weird stuff lol and I am always exaggerating! So might be better for me to keep my fingers off stuff like that. I think my redox potential is ok, not optimal but not bad either, really. And still I get such bad reaction to big amounts of Iodine. My body clearly cannot handle so much toxins at once. So if your redox sucks you need to be very careful.

I now feel so great again I could fly :-D
I have been drinking up to 4 liter of water from my magnetico mattress daily, lately, I realized when I have water by me at my desk too and drink as I work I am able to drink more because otherwise I easily forget! I do recognice way better now that I have to drink, if I drink a little less I get dry mouth and I feel a bit thirsty, cool! It really feels good to drink so much. I often make a vortex in my 0,5 l glass, with a wooden spoon. The water tastes so soft and sweet to me, amazing.

The egg yolk foam I do with 4 egg yolks and maybe 1/2 teaspoon wild and raw honey (from bees that get no sugar in the winter at all, just their own honey). Just put it in a bowl and whisk until thick and foamy, this is what it looks like

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

You can eat it with wild berries (not cultivated, they have way too much fructose/sugar) or make it into chocolate mousse! For that you melt 100% chocolate and mix some raw coconut oil in it and I do peppermint oil too quite many drops and then you carefully blend it into the egg yolk foam and let it cool a bit so it becomes thick. Oh my it is crazy yummy. You can use 90% chocolate too but then there will be sugar so I use 100% and tiny bit raw honey. You can use raw cocoa powder too, and blend it with melted coconut oil only or also melted butter added, blend and add to the egg yolk foam.
This is a real threat  and a healthy one

### Hope — 2014-02-22T17:54:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-111698>

Oh Inger, I just now caught this as I have been thinking of you, imagining you delighting your sister and her family all week. You are such a treasure!  You sure sound like your RP is much better than mine!  I am thankful that by now, I can easily handle 50mg iodoral (I can't do the drops, for some reason they are too strong for my stomach, but whatever)...and I love it. It's one supplement I don't want to be without. But I also know that by now, my F and Bromide levels are way lower, based on testing....

How awesome you paint your body with it. I have no doubt you are a great painter..body and otherwise!! You are so good at anything you set your mind to.....

And oh, Inger I cannot wait to try that recipe..I can't use honey or any sugar - I respond really badly to even the tiniest bit. Well to the next person might not be much but I can just feel in my brain, it is not good to use any at all. But I love raw cocao, plain, and adding CO is awesome too. I have a hard time eating it "plain" but this is an awesome addition. Just wow. It's perfect timing too because I think I have been eating too much almond butter - organic but roasted. And though I don't feel any different with our without it, I don't want to add oxidized PUFAs to my body either if I don't have to. So this is a great dessert alternative, bc I don't eat any dessert except the almond butter with cocoa...that is now super, super sweet to me.  so this would be a great option.

So I hope you had a wonderful time..and will share with us your adventures with your sister and company!

### Inger — 2014-02-23T15:58:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-111801>

Hope I had an AWESOME week!!! My sister had a lot of healthy goodies around... and yeah I maybe ate a bit much, and way too much HWC (her home made sugar free parfait was just too awesome).. and dark chocolate.. but I had so much fun I do not think it affected me at all.. at least I feel great!
I got some fresh cod roe too... and whale meat... yum! And fresh shrimps and champagne... and so much love. Those kids are just unbelievable, the oldest just turned 4 years and he can read???!! He read to me and he is so young.. amazing. And the other guy that turns 2 yo - the strongest manliest little gentleman I have ever seen!- just wanted to drink from my water bottle ALL THE TIME...hehe.. he never got tired of it so funny! That was about all he needed to stay happy..lol I had carbonated spring water and he loved that too, cool!
The baby was just the sweetest little thing, only 2 weeks old...;

and the feet.... huh! I kind of got addicted to them!

When I arrived at Oslo Airport with my bro his car was waiting for us at the parking lot and had the most beautiful ice roses inside on the window

I now need to catch up because I have been avoiding computers and internet 100% all the week. It felt so good. So good I really felt sad having to read my emails and be on the internet again :-(
It is so different to cuddle with kids and talk to my dear siblings all day long. So very different. Like in paradise

I feel so blessed to be allowed to be an aunt - really blessed

### Hope — 2014-02-23T19:09:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-111832>

How awesome, Inger....and reading emails, you do not 'have' to, do you? That's what Jack told me in a consult....and outside of work, he is right. THough I love seeing this update. You are blessed indeed....brought tears to my eyes...I love kids and babies so much too...but no siblings or babies around me. Maybe some day.

Anyway thanks for all the pictures, including the ice. I am kind of tired of people complaining about snow here. I miss it already. LOL.

Big hug to you ~

### Inger — 2014-02-26T09:01:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-112249>

Yeah, snow is magic! And the ice roses on the windows in winter always take my breath away...

Hope maybe you could work as a nanny!!! I would do that! I sometimes look for nanny jobs lol Because I love kids so much, they makes me feel so good!
My sisters baby is a very lucky one, gets only mothers milk as much as she wants... and my sister eats fairly healthy too! I am so thankful for such mothers it just warms my heart...

Yeah I love to switch OFF my computer. I get this.. ahhhhh.. feeling. I get ugly face when I sit much in front of it, go figure. It must be very unhealthy!!!

So, I measured the PH of our well water and it was about 7,5. Yay! I thought so because it is pretty hard water full of minerals I guess. Very good tasting to me. 30 m deep from the ground. I feel so fortunate to have this water and for free. I feel more and more fortunate every day BTW. It is almost unreal? I sleep so great on my magnetico it is almost unreal too! Oh man feels so good to lay on it I have hard time to leave it in the mornings - not because I am sleepy but because I feel so freaking good laying there in the cool and dark and magnetized ;-)

It is so warm here and spring is coming already, huh? Yesterday I was outside many hours watching the sheep grass in the yard and the hens was outside too and the goose I put outside in their pool, they loved it and finally they look clean and white again! The hens and the rooster were crazy about the grass, eating and eating... temps was maybe + 5 degree C. And sunny. It was just magic! Tonight was maybe -6 degree C but day will be sunny and on the + side. Last year I remember when our lambs got born on Easter Sunday and it was - 17 degree C that night and the poor babies almost freeze to death so I had to warm them inside next morning.. What a huge difference. Our animals love an early spring for sure
