The baby is just so lovely and lucky to have a mother who will feed her on demand, so much it wrong with the way people raise children these days. I think you almost have a life of bliss, maybe just the love element missing huh? That will happen, someone out there is missing his yin and it's got to be you. Fate, as always, holds the key.

### Inger — 2014-03-08T13:48:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-114136>

Oh Sooperb that is sweet of you...  unfortunately I do not really feel the need for any man right now.. I am just so happy with my life! It can be just so very sensual even without any partner, weird. If there will be a special someone out there he will have to drag me kicking and screaming into his cave, no other option...lol I just enjoy my life so much Now and he will have to be someone very special. Maybe when I am 40 yo he will pop up that is a great time span I think

Was out eating for a birthday and felt not so good after. I had snails in garlic butter and mussles on a salad. IDK if it was the food or the person who had birthday, she talked so much negative stuff it could have been that too? We do had some fun too but what a different way looking at life.. It felt awkward. In the night I woke up and had such bad anxiety, I lied there in bed and though oh man, that is how it must feel to be in hell, darn! I have not felt anything like this such a long time. Thankfully it was way better in the morning and a few days later I am feeling as good as before. But weird. I also got some weird bowel issues, but they are gone now too. Could have been something in the food even if I chose the best I could. I have not been eating out like this in so long time. Maybe it was fake butter.

I dream so much every night, and have so nice dreams it is really amazing. I sleep on my 10 gauss, bed a tiny bit inclined, and grounding strap on into the river. I have felt it more than once when I wake up my cells are laughing from well being. Very very nice.

I found the first wild greens, nettles and dandelion and something else IDK in english. They tasted divine! I just LOVE when the first greens pop up in spring, something magic about eating them, with some earth on for sure lol maybe the earth made me high?

I have started to drink French clay in the morning. I have so much of it and I have forgot to drink it! It tastes like creamy coffee... yummy!

Nude tanning season have started  a few days ago

### Hope — 2014-03-08T21:44:14+0200

Oh Inger how wonderful. I remember Emma. I cannot believe it is nearly a year since then!

SO happy for your sleep....and wow you sound like me when going out to eat which is why I don't like to. And you know I have become so sensitive to people's negativity too, even if I have my own as well. BTW I love your nanny idea. My mom did that after I was born, and I've loved babies and little kids ever since. It made my dolls...boring and I never got into them. lol. They just sat there looking pretty. I love fish prepared and preserved in all the traditional ways. I somehow feel that when I am aligning with the ways in which my Scandinavian ancestors lived I am much healthier. ### Inger — 2014-03-09T09:16:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-114271>

@ Hope,
nannying babies/children I bet would be even nicer than dogs .. I always get so blessed from children it really is special. Sure dogs are nice too. Weird but i think we women have this strong instinct to nurture helpless small beings and it is a huge source of joy for us? Sometimes we get it wrong with our instincts and try to nurture our partners hehe.. very bad idea!

@ Grmoline,
oh that is nice  Scandinavia is magic for sure ! Something about the wilderness here that I always have loved and makes me feel amazing! A warm climate does not fit me well at all. I always get a need to cool when too warm. Yesterdays tanning on the (maybe it was 12 degree C in the room and outside +6) kitchen floor at the door opening was making me way too hot so I had to go and cool down with a dip in the +1 degree C river. Later in the evening I made fire in the fireplace and read in front of it and it got way too hot so I had to take a dip in the river again! It was dark as in the night outside, but the sky was so clear that even if the moon was only half it shined pretty strong. I swam around in the river in the moonlight and watched the stars too. That was magic! So nice to warm up by the fireplace after that.
There is something I really like with dipping in the river in pitch dark. IDK what it is and why, but I just love it! Also... another great thing - the neighbors cannot see me as I love to dip nude..
Grmoline you can do all this stuff now too, and go out and pick some wild greens and taste them, they are amazing! I am in love with wild foods. I am sure you can find some too where you live  (just do not choose the poisonous ones!)

@ Audrey you are so right - if eating out again I will do as I have done normally, just order something plain and raw. Like raw fish unprepaired or a steak. Olive and balsamic vinegar, butter on the side etc.So I can see it is real! I never has issues with that. Very expensive restaurants usually are great for eating out but the middle class ones are not.... ugh. Except you order outside of menu

\- - - - - -

I dreamed such an amazing dream again tonight. It is hours since I woke up but I can still feel it...??!

I dreamed I was on a conference of a sort, IDK what kind really, Jack was there too. Me and Jack went for a walk together. It was winter, and we had winter clothes on. I walked so close to Jack all the way, so close that I touched him. He let me.. and I felt this magic energy from him. It came and came and came.. and the walk lasted and lasted. We talked about many things... it was just so amazing. At one point he wanted to show me a picture I was not quite ready to see as he told me what it was..lol (not going to tell ya) so I just closed my eyes and rested my head on his arm as we continued to walk. I trusted he will lead me and I will not stumble. It felt very special. Then Jack said we will get something to eat, and there was a restaurant in front of us and we went in. He took place on the bar on a bench and I saw how he had taken care there was empty space beside him so I could sit close to him there too.... and then I woke up.. because my shoulder hurt pretty bad as my cat was laying on me in a way that pushed my arm high and my cat is heavy! It also started to get light outside.

Oh man that was one amazing dream.

This all seems so incredibly special..... I always wonder how I got so lucky.....I have had so any spectacular people come and go in my life .......I think my life is like a dream maybe.......

### Inger — 2014-03-11T18:25:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-114813>

I know nettles are high in silica.. and I usually just eat a few leaves. I love any wild edibles though, chick weeds, dandelion leaves... there are many! I am sure there must be something to taste a bit outside the city? In Hamburg there was, at least... Linden trees grew everywhere and their leaves and flowers too are one of my favorite wild green. Just so yummy! Maybe do not pick right beside a road... but I have done that too and am still alive lol

So.. today I got pretty shocked!

Yesterday I got me a body voltage meter. And today.. measured so many things. The shock was as I realized I have been electrocuting myself every night with my grounding rod  instead of grounding me it did the opposite...
I had to find the reason! I searched and tested quite a while and finally I guess I know why. The end of the rod was still in the river. But the thick copper grounding rod was lost. Somehow the rod attracted so much electricity on its way to my bed it was not funny. I think the reason might have been a power line that is a very small one just to our house and the neighbors but still I cannot find another reason for it than that. My grounding rod was running on the earth under that powerline. When I stay right under it it just measures 0,3 gauss or so, but it could still be the reason especially with the thick grounding rod lost. IDK when that happened, I hope not long ago. As I did the flower test some moths ago it seemed everything was ok...?

So I tried many different things and in the end I dug the end of the rod in the earth right under my window. Up in my bedroom the grounding rod worked great! I did not even have a real copper ground just the rod with naked ends. Cool. I am curious how sleep will be tonight. Have had some nights lately when I have been waking up early, I wondered why. I was not so concerned tho as I do feel great, but my period too has been a bit too late and unregular last 2 months or so.. that was weird as i am always so regular. I thought it might be some detox or my body sorting something out but maybe it was the electrocuting messing with my poor hormones

SO glad I measured! I measured other things too.. very funny. I measured if my sheep are grounding me if I cuddle with them having thick soled shoes on. And they grounded me very good! But not so if I only touched their fur/wool. I had to touch their face, nose was very good and the ears too! Those grounded me just amazingly well

After all the work I took my shoes off, sat down on a damp tree trunk in the pasture, placed my feet on the damp earth and cuddled with Kingi. The ram. He always want so much love.
That was so nice... the sun shined on my face and I felt like in heaven. What a nice way to heal

### sooperb — 2014-03-11T19:14:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-114816>

Aren't you still using the magnetico Inger, surely that would mitigate some of the effects of not being attached to the grounding rod? I love the idea of cuddling a ram's or sheep's face. Most sheep run away when you approach but I guess yours feel your love and are more than happy to return it

### Inger — 2014-03-11T19:38:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-114819>

I do sleep on my magnetico for sure Soo  but I want it all - grounding too!

Our sheep like people very much... they run to everyone walking past the house, or guests that are staying here, to cuddle..lol When I take them out from their pasture and let them run free they run after me all the time, I do not need to tie them at all..lol They think I am their shepherd I guess! I do think to be a shepherd is my favorite job ever

Do you fit any work in too? I can't remember if you said you do or don't. Plus you have your husband for cuddles when necessary. It sounds idyllic

### Inger — 2014-03-13T09:02:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-115136>

Audrey no babies yet! But I am sooo excited!!! I need to clean out the tiny sheep house today as the babies might come and I do put new hay on the floor often but now the floor has gotten pretty high and before the babies come I would like to get the old stuff away and make it clean and nice

Sooperb yeah I have quite a paradise here . The weird thing is - as Jack use to say, the same thing can open the door to heaven or to hell. That thing is ME
This fact has been in my mind often lately. It is pretty crazy! I want to dive deeper.. and deeper but man I have too much fat on me so I just cannot reach too deep so far but it makes so much fun to know the possibilities and I am on my way! So much work to do on this girl... almost embarrassing!

I do work.. I work for the construction business I own with a friend of hubs (hubs is the boss after all) and yeah might be pretty risky but I choose to do that because then I can work from home and I love that. Right now. I do all the payments for our workers etc and taxes and stuff and work together with our bookkeeper. Not too much work for me but I am terrible at it as I am very unorganized.. and never done this kind of work. As a waitress I was super organized (weird! Must be because I learned that work in Germany!) and very good at my work... it is kinda painful to all of a sudden be bad at your work lol but hey, that is how we learn! So it gets better all the time

And yeah.. I do have 21 children so to say. lol (chickens, goose-or I think they are ducks!-sheep, cat) Need to take care of them too every day.
Yeah I can cuddle with hubby  but sometimes he makes me go crazy with all his weird issues with his girlfriend and all eh! Thankfully I am very much on my own and he do is a great friend too even if I sometimes do not understand lol
I feel better the less I mix into others issues for sure - this is a lesson I have learned well this past year (still I can stumble and fall right on my nose lol)

I do not plan to stay here forever. But right now I am super happy to be here

I think I will quit the use of any grounding rod. I just got to think, what if I do act as an antenna...? I am not sure this is a good thing at all. My bedroom shows pretty much zero levels with the AM radio and also the gauss meter, but still. I am not getting peace in my mind about this anymore so I will just ground outside now! And cuddling with the sheep
this is safe, at least

When I told hubs about how I have been electrocuting myself through the rod he laughed so much and said soon I will find out the magnetico mattress does something similar too HA... hehehe he sure is used to my weird stuff he just thinks it is so funny (he does not like my high meat in the fridge at all but I am very careful to air it on the balcony for sure, have learned my lessons lol)

### caroline — 2014-03-13T09:11:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-115138>

I can't wait for the babies to come!

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-13T09:25:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-115139>

A real live shepherdess on the forum … I'm not being funny; I really am impressed.

Here's a delightful little song where a shepherd mourns his absent love (who's probably a shepherdess):

> Go, gentle gales, and bear my sighs along,
> The birds shall cease to tune their ev’ning song,
> The winds to blow, the waving woods to move,
> And streams to murmur e’er I cease to love.
> Not bubbling fountains to the thirsty swain,
> Nor balmy sleep to lab’rors spent with pain,
> Nor flow’rs to larks, nor sunshine to the bee,
> Are half so pleasing as thy sight to me ..

### Inger — 2014-03-15T19:33:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-115640>

Audrey... hubs moved to his girlfriend officially now.. and the divorce is also getting on the final stages. I have knew this for long and hubs has had his girlfriend 3 years already.. (of course tried to tell me otherwise  ) and me and hubs have been just friends for years already... but still it gets to me. It hurts-. All the hard times we got through together the last 15 years.. when he was close to death and survived.. these things you just never forget

I do not even know why this hurts so much

But I know it will pass. Also pain belongs to life.

### Inger — 2014-03-15T19:36:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-115641>

The thing is I am afraid it wont last with his girlfriend and soon he will be knocking on the door again  but it just cannot go on like that and to be able to heal I need some consistency and truth and normality in my life.
I really need that.

Even if this hurts so bad it feels good too. It feels... like relief

### nicld — 2014-03-15T19:46:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-115643>

Oh Inger, that's to bad but everything happens for a reason. You have invested so much of yourself to someone one else and losing that will hurt.

Take care and go cuddle your sheep.

### Inger — 2014-03-15T19:56:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-115648>

Yeah Nicole that is exactly why I think.. cannot stop thinking about his shirts I have been ironing... his clothes I have been washing... all those little things... they caused me joy. His coffee cup.. in the kitchen... how I used to worry about his health and well being..
But we do stay great friends that is great for sure.

I am so blessed to have all those sweet animals to cuddle with! And the kids of my siblings too... and my neighbor that is in love with me...  the 80 yo artist and author. I cannot tell I am divorcing because he have told me if his wife dies and I am alone he will ask me to marry him!

### nicld — 2014-03-15T20:01:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-115649>

Well, is the neighbor a spry 80 year old.....hehehehe?

### Inger — 2014-03-15T20:17:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-115652>

nah... he has quite a belly LOL... (I told him to swim in the river, as it goes right behind their house too!) but he is sweet, very deep thinking and philosophic  if he stops by our house when he drives by and I am outside he talks to me about how much he loves me and how beautiful I am and how he has hot dreams about me  and how his hormones are working well LOL I used to be very annoyed, but lately I just find it amusing... hehe...
He has a very beautiful and totally wonderful and kind wife BTW! Quite a bit younger than him too. Ahhh... neighbors.. lol they are one funny thing! I am sooo glad we have such amazing neighbors! They truly are!!! The other close neighbor is a very health conscious dear family too, totally sweet, feeding our sheep only organic veggies and stuff! Their kids are amazingly beautiful.. only because of them I find it worth to have the animals, as I think they are a great source of joy! Also other parents are walking by with their kids to cuddle with the sheep and watch the ducks swim in their pool...

Nope... in the end I am one lucky chick

### Inger — 2014-03-15T21:36:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-115669>

Nicole this dirty old man is very kind tho! He let me take/make as much hay in summer as I want for free - from his property! And he says I gives him so much inspiration for his art work too.. so much love.. and that is good for sure!
I now tell him, I am so happy I can be an inspiration, and I am glad his hormones are working so well  what is better than giving an old man some joy the last years of his life

### caroline — 2014-03-16T01:37:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-115703>

I think that it is so odd. ..... An older man who is still in love with life and who can appreciate a young, lovely woman on many levels.......and we call him a dirty old man. He is the one appreciating all the joy and really living life.

This morning I went to a big market with neighbours. They always go early every Sunday morning and ask if I want to come along. I speak to Robin often from my balcony or see her walking to work or I stop at her work and say hi. I have only said hi to her DH a few times.
It. Was so disconcerting to me to have a man around to carry things. He insisted on getting my cases of water for me and even insisted on carrying them up three flights and putting them in my kitchen.
I am still shaken by this ....and he has such beautiful manners. Just a very quiet man but so lovely.
My DH was very ill for so long and I was the one that did all the carrying. Don't get me wrong - it was an absolute honor and privilege ... But it seems I have gotten quite self sufficient - except I still can't open a bottle of wine properly!

I actually have a funny little problem with this neighbor ....she has been calling me by the wrong name for a year now!
She calls me Andrea .... And I love that name! It feels more like me. I think that is why my DH always called me Caroline. Now you know my life story - how embarrassing!

### caroline — 2014-03-16T01:39:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-115704>

I guess I am a dirty old woman ..... Yikes!

And how can anyone not be in love with you? Your whole being exudes loveliness and love.....I am sure many of us even feel/sense it in your writing...

I am so glad you can appreciate all the love and beauty in your life.

Audrey...I am so sorry for your loss. now I know why you call yourself Caroline here. Both are beautiful names, as are you.

Sending a great big hug to both of you, across the continents......

### sooperb — 2014-03-16T15:40:00+0200

Screw tops Audrey, screw tops

I love the name Caroline, so much it's what I called my second daughter.

See, we are surrounded by gorgeous people and we just don't know sometimes.

Inger it was what it was and it is now what it is. There is no need for regret, sometimes we don't benefit from our investments, sometimes we do, but riches are all around us. You have unlocked the secret to living at one with yourself, joy must shine out of you like a beacon. The Earth loves you and you can hug that to your heart and be peaceful. ### Inger — 2014-03-21T09:51:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-116903>

Sooperb, I just am not one bit sad anymore..lol I figured it all out it seems, and now I feel just so blessed  It is crazy how our thoughts and commitment to reality and to the beauty of life/love can change everything - from one moment on. I am struck by this fact again and again. It is all about daring! To see the light - it seems so scary but it is not in the end, the opposite is true..lol
Widen your horizon.. yeah that is as easy as it is

So I also have been thinking a lot about this; A warrior never complains! Complaining is so wrong - what does it help? Nothing! I am so angry at myself every single time I have complained, darn! Instead - do!!! Ha.. it is so easy.  Face the truth - face your self! Maybe something needs to be changed... with me!
It just requires a bit effort to change bad habits.... but it is only in the beginning - and then all of a sudden it becomes all natural
A dear friend of mine used to say; Don't wish, do!
I loved that...

I have a new routine now... I make a fire every evening in the fire place - and lay in front of it clotheless on lambs skin reading until I go to bed. It is amazing! If I get too hot I go and take a dip in the river - like yesterday. It snowed so it was not dark at all... it was beautiful! The warmth from the fireplace feels so good on my skin.. and I like when my skin can breath. It feels good. And then I jump into the cold bed around 9 PM all warm and sleepy
Life is good

Yesterday the kids of our neighbor was here watching the animals and playing with the sheep, the kids are so sweet  they are waiting so much for the babies too! Looks like weather will be warmer from now on - makes it safer if the babies are born when I am not there (in the night) as they have no heating on at all.

AFAIK, the notion was that they should never _scold_. I guess the implication was that he who lashes with the tongue is afraid to strike. Keep silence or deal in blows. I'm not suggesting you take a deadly weapon to anyone, of course ...

Mind you, in the Old North they had this custom of flyting -- swapping insults.

> "Shame didst thou win, | that women thou slewest ..."

[http://www.sacred-texts.com/neu/poe/poe08.htm](http://www.sacred-texts.com/neu/poe/poe08.htm)

I'm deeply envious of your open fire and sheepskins and river to swim in. Had a good walk today, though.

### Inger — 2014-03-22T08:13:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-117099>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=116955)

Krista if you could see my fireplace it is not nice at all LOL It is kind of a old stove my dad has taken from the old hose we lived in, it is not like a open fireplace either! I never thought about using it before my ex husband started to... he made lots of fires in it all winter long because he wanted it warm lol So that made me realize I liked the cozy warmth from it too! And then I discovered it felt even better to "tan" in front of it LOL I sometimes dry brush my bod at the same time, listening to podcasts or webinar... drinking cold magnetized well water.. just chilling out. Perfect way to spend the evening IMO

Krista you can do it too! Just let it burn for a short time before you set down by the stove - it will then be so warm and nice... and after a hour you will get too hot! I use candle lights too.

A stock tank for outside, or even a small pool, would be awesome!

### Inger — 2014-03-22T08:24:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-117101>

Gangrad and Spinergie... I think the "warrior" word can have so many meanings... I meant the kind of warrior, not fighting! But watching my mind...

That is so weird but I did not fight at all... it was just like all of a sudden it opened up something, so I could understand/see. I did nothing! Except of singing so loud the roof was about to lift lol and I cut fresh herring for our ducks in the kitchen.. and then I went to the bathroom.. looked in the mirror as I put some lipstick on (gotta be pretty for the ducks ..) and BOOM - something had changed!
I have thought about this so much... and it seems to me like having a open mind is what is the culprit. Maybe the singing opened up something..?

It is like, laying back.. relax.. and let the sunshine in    "fighting" in the wrongs sense will just make you drown deeper in the shit  Like, I need to get quiet.. and listen. Might be all weird talk! I just try to make some sense of the happenings.. but pretty hard to put it into words for sure.

So now... I see what all the ex husband has given me.. I feels so blessed and thankful - and how we continue to give each other - it is quite a story  Just lets hope I have not turned insane

### caroline — 2014-03-22T08:56:27+0200

I think you are so right Inger ..... we are letting the sunshine in...... what could be more important

### Inger — 2014-03-29T08:35:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-118628>

^^ thanks guys

I was outside yesterday, it was so nice and sunny, maybe + 5 degree C in shorts and bikini top cutting the sheep. I used a regular scissor and I though I would do one / day as it is pretty tough do - but in the end I did all 3 and I got so much sun my back almost burned  (it was a tiny bit pink yesterday but is brown today) I was barefoot all the time too. Our sheep and ram are so sweet I do not even need to tie them when cutting their wool, they just love it. I do have to move a bit around "following them" and the ram wants so much love all the time! lol But otherwise it is just a easy game! Now they look all so pretty  I did cut Emme a little.. just once but only the skin - and I cut my finger too - all of a sudden I realized I bleed.. I did not feel the cut at all and I do not know how it happened either, a weird L formed cut...lol I had no time for cleaning the wound or get a plaster so I just wiped it off with Emmes wool from the ground. The wound is looking great today

No lambs so far..... I am so exited that all will go well.. I do not want any lambs to die this year

After that I washed the car manually outside barefoot too. And then I drove to get some grassfed meat from the ranch 30 minute drive from here. The people there are just too lovely! I got 1,2 kg grassfed Angus mince, 2 kg liver, one ox tail, 1 heart, 2 kg Osso Bucco and maybe 2 kg pure grassfed beef fat for just 80 € I totally forgot I wanted tongue too! Ahhhh! Next Friday
My ex husband is so very kind to let me have the car mostly every weekend now, so I can drive around and forage/hunt stuff  I think I am going to my favorite lake today....

I had weird food yesterday - all I ate was tongue... 3/4 of 1 grass fed Angus beef tongue shared between 3 meals!  had some pieces of high heart with it and some spoonful raw organic sauerkraut and some raw cucumber and 4 pieces of 100% chocolate and one raw duck egg too fresh from the ground..
I was just too busy being outside in the sun and wanted to use as little time as possible on eating and the tongue smiled to me from the fridge lol

### caroline — 2014-03-29T10:09:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-118636>

sounds like Heaven Inger...... all that time outside and touching the sheep - quantum entanglement!

It sounds so lovely .... we are pretty easy to please these days aren't we! Mother nature is giving you a feast for your senses ....every last one of them

It is quite amazing to be high on life ... how did we get so lucky?

### caroline — 2014-03-30T04:45:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-118746>

X ray quantum entanglement??? I like it a lot!

### Inger — 2014-03-30T10:21:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-118780>

I really think to eat a day of cooked is no good for my mood
I hate to think I just "imagine" stuff but slowly I have tried it out so many times I start to be pretty 100% sure about it. Raw makes me feel different - it lifts me up. Keeps dark moods and anxiety away. I had that day of slowly and gently cooked beef tongue 3 meals - and my mood got darker. WTH..?
it is just weird  I am going raw - I want to feel good..  I just do not understand... how can others eat all that cooked meat and fish and feel good. maybe because I am so broken, I need so much of the good stuff  A tiny bit cooked does not matter but if I eat more... not so fun

I had raw Norwegian cod and fresh oysters for dinner yesterday and today for breakfast I had raw cod again... mmm  Going to have some mackerel head smoothie today and high heart too. And raw liver. a raw fresh duck egg too, yum. Those foods just makes me happy  I cannot meat the challenges in my life if my mood is dark  I need to feel the "quantum magic"

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-30T10:54:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-118794>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=118780)
>
> I had raw Norwegian cod and fresh oysters for dinner yesterday and today for breakfast I had raw cod again... mmm

Can you get the cod livers, Inger?

They seem to have been much prized in Northern Europe in the past. Weston Price says the fisherfolk in the Hebrides used to eat cod heads stuffed with oatmeal and chopped cod liver -- particularly giving them to children, IIRC.

But maybe they go to be made into cod liver oil these days.

I got hold of some cans of cod liver "in its own oil and juice" once. It was OK. But most canned things seem a bit soft and a bit insipid to me. I know you'll not disagree with that!

### Inger — 2014-03-30T21:00:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-118844>

I was barefoot many hours today in the sunshine    - I dug the duck pool deeper (gosh that was quite a muddy, heavy work out!) barefoot and scantily clad - after I drove to my favorite lake and walked through the forest by the lake for an hour maybe, barefoot too... it was so nice. I did not swim, there was still ice on the lake but soon it will melt, I guess!
I tanned on the balcony too. the ex husband was here shortly to get the car and I got hugs and kisses that was good.. oh man

### Andrea — 2014-04-04T19:40:40+0300

That sounds lovely, Inger! Especially the mud between your toes.

And you're right, it's quite warm outside. Even here. Since I don't eat lunch, but I have a paid lunch break, I go outside and sit in the sun as often as I can. Wonderful! So far I've only been tanning my face and arms, but I think I may have found a spot where I could take my shirt off. However, I will have to check for security cameras first... The security staff don't need this form of entertainment!

### sooperb — 2014-04-05T12:02:19+0300

### Inger — 2014-04-05T23:02:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-119950>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=119896)
>
> How do you dig with bare feet???
>
> Hardly any sunshine here in the UK, lots of fog and mist though.

Sue I just use my feet on the shovel as I would do with shoes. I cannot push as hard tho, or it will hurt, but it works pretty fine as long as the earth is not too compact/hard. You should try
My hair looks better when it is rainy and gray... when it is sunny and dry it is not as pretty - weird huh. If I am outside a foggy gray day I feel great too! Here was sunny today but sooo stormy, it was crazy! I drove to the lake and walked in the woods around it for a bit.. barefoot. The ice had melted! But I did not swim... IDK it was so super stormy... I did not feel like dipping at all. Instead I lied down in the moss and rested where the wind was silent between the trees.. it was nice

I was sad today. It helped to go to the lake and think. Sometimes I do not understand my mind at all. I get angry at it! I read this on Jack facebook site and it is just for me!

> Not every leader has compassion yet every leader needs to show it. Leadership is about people and heart. When you have compassion you show people you have a heart. When you show them yours, they will show theirs and follow. Acts of kindness, compassion, empathy, generosity — combine to move my focus on to other people a_nd away from the kind of brooding on my own condition_.

gosh.. I hate the brooding on my own condition thing. But I tend to do it at times I think. I just hate divorces.  Then - my bro called and he is sick.. has had high fever for a week... so I decided to drive to his place and it was scary, I have never drove there before, it is in the city - the roads were so confusing t0 me! and it started to get late... ugh. I was freaked out but I did it. I brought him some stuff to help. I am a bit worried.. he gets so much EMF... in his home and at work. I told him to come here for a few days if he does not get better.. He does try to live healthy.

So... then I had some Bordeaux (I have not had any wine for so long...) after the drive I was still pretty freaked out  I am not a good driver! I drive fast but I still have issues concentrating on 1000 things at once  and that is not good when driving. And the roads into he city are sooo confusing! But. I am not going to hide my head in the sand so I do what is scary, one day I will be a great driver.
So... now I should be in bed long ago and my cat is asleep on my lap

oh.. and I got me some organic Chlorella. I thought I would try them out, as Dr Bonlie said they are great when detoxing on magnetico. IDK if I have any detox, I feel great but I still wanted to try so I started with chewing a few and OMG. I crave them? I can eat and eat and eat. The ex husband was here and I told him to try them and he thought they tasted awful (and he is not too picky with taste..) but I think they taste like candy and I cannot stop eating them.  it is kind of weird. I wonder if the taste reminds me of something nice... I really have no idea.

I am waiting so much for the next blog to show up...!

oh my now I am super tired.. have not stayed up this late for so long.. gotta take the cat and go to bed

### caroline — 2014-04-06T01:06:54+0300

so much for us to keep learning and doing Inger ..... how lucky we are .... .never ending magic it seems to me

### Inger — 2014-04-06T20:20:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-120077>

True, Audrey

I got to spend time with an angel today... it was a young woman, who come to do some paperwork at my "office" here at home for a worker that we have that is trying to get lasting work permission, he is a Russian and it is not easy. Anyways this lady and her husband are his friends, they belong to the same church. They are very strong believers. She is such an amazing lady... I just drove her to the train station, we talked there for a bit - and I still feel like drugged... Her presence calms me... and makes my whole body feel so sweetly relaxed and... almost drugged! I want her as my friend! She is my friend..
I really wonder what weird magic is that!  I wish i had this drug 24/7

How on earth can just a persons presence have such a strong impact... wow

I now wonder about her secret. Or maybe we just resonated... maybe she was just what I needed right now

### caroline — 2014-04-07T07:23:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-120181>

I think we are so lucky when someone like that comes into our life and touches us ..... Like Jack and Jeremy. That relationship is so powerful ... Can you imagine what they will accomplish together?

A woman once told me, many years ago ..... When someone so special comes into your life - you must care for them....whatever it takes....

Jack and Jeremy together are a powerful force to be reckoned with .....in this case 1+1 = 100

### Inger — 2014-04-07T11:15:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-120203>

We are indeed very, very lucky Audrey

you always speak right from my heart - how can you know..?

I just got a package delivered on my door.. from one amazing guy - how he smiled and looked at me, just sunshine and love (it is rainy here today) - I could have fallen for him in a heart beat  I had to rush inside and not stay there too long talking because he could see me blush
maybe he was my angel for the day

### caroline — 2014-04-07T12:17:24+0300

### Inger — 2014-04-15T21:14:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-121708>

hahaha... I had lovey underwear on.. sure LOL and no I am not keeping anything from you - I promise!

I met another in the shop today (a worker there)... he offered me advice looking for a product.. and he was too kind.. he was a testosterone bomb I promise. I could feel it very strongly! And he spent so much time talking and helping me, like he had nothing else to do in this world and there were other customers waiting for him.. I got very lucky

so.. things are happening here! We got lambs...!!!    Today morning there were 3 sweet ones born and everything is perfect! I need to take some pics! Mimosa is such a great and loving mom... i am so proud of her.. the lambs are just amazing.. so sweet! 1 dark brown tiny guy, one black guy and one black girl with a few white hairs on her head lol
Oh, I have worried so much, that everything will go okay. I am so happy
I got lots of goodies for the mom, organic carrots, celery, cabbage, seaweed... dried nettles too

Other things happens too.. with my thoughts... all of a sudden my mind figured it out

I have been eating only raw lately, and many fish heads and raw liver too and I have not chewed much
have been walking barefoot too every day - love it

I need to catch up on the forums, too many interesting discussions going on !

### SeaHorse — 2014-04-17T04:38:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-122051>

There are a bunch of sheep breeds that have course hair instead of wool…from the islands: St. Croix and Barbados Black Belly are two kinds….they don't need shearing and are raised for meat. Navajo sheep do well in dry hot climate…and have useful wool too.
My lambing this year was very sad…my one ewe got stuck on her back during the night she went into labor and both lambs were stillborn. I was lucky to save her as she was in shock. The other ewe had twins, but I was away and one of them didn't make it through his first very cold, below zero night. He probably didn't get his collostrum in time..so we have one ram lamb named Miles who is thriving. He was named after a musician friend, but our neighbour thought he should be called Kilometers because my husband is Canadian….(insert drum roll here)

### nonchalant — 2014-04-17T05:23:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-122055>

There are some Painted Desert sheep for sale nearby, which are a Barbados cross... Nice self-shedding feature.
We have coyotes & bobcats near our land, though. Hmm. Maybe sheep are too small. Perhaps I should look into longhorn cattle?

### Inger — 2014-04-17T07:13:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-122065>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=122051)
>
> My lambing this year was very sad…my one ewe got stuck on her back during the night she went into labor and both lambs were stillborn. I was lucky to save her as she was in shock. The other ewe had twins, but I was away and one of them didn't make it through his first very cold, below zero night. He probably didn't get his collostrum in time..so we have one ram lamb named Miles who is thriving. He was named after a musician friend, but our neighbour thought he should be called Kilometers because my husband is Canadian….(insert drum roll here)

I feel for you  oh man that must have been real hard
I have worried so much too that all goes well, last spring I tried to rescue 2 lambs too but they died in the end and it almost broke my heart
I also worry for the health of my sheep, I bet the EMF is not good for them either. I do what I can, feed them my exclusive French dried seaweeds and dried herbs, and give them no grains not even oat, just hay, and do anything I can think of that would do them good, I so hate if they get sick! I am way too sensitive like that.  The bigger was my joy when everything went so well! The 3 lambs are so strong and healthy, they was on the pasture all day yesterday with the mom, Emme and our ram Kingi. I could not stop watching them, it was idyllic  the small baby lambs walked and jumped after their mom playing around.. lol - and all of a sudden they got tired and lied down in the sun to rest

Now lets see if Emme gets babies too! She is not that big at all, maybe she has only one. Lets hope

I have 2 liters of colostrum in the freezer, maybe I will have to drink it all myself  but I have to wait for Emme....

### Inger — 2014-04-17T08:48:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-122083>

Here are the lambs with their mom on the pasture yesterday, they are only 1 day old. The brown little guy looks like the toughest, he just walked and walked by his mom after the other two was sleeping in the sun long ago... would never have thought that... the smallest one, the toughest

### Inger — 2014-04-17T14:16:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-122120>

not sure she is preggo but I hope - she do has a tiny belly!

I told them they can climb into the pasture and play if the like to. Our ram is so kind i guess it will be no issue at all. I watched him today "babysit" lol He is so cool with the small ones! He pushes them a bit here and there sure, he is the dad

Their mom calls theme every so often; come and drink milk, cuties! I bet every 10 minutes lol
I wish we would do the same

### nicld — 2014-04-18T17:48:54+0300

Oh Inger, they are adorable. A new couple just moved in down the road from us and have a whole bunch of sheep and a llama and they are so cute. Next place I would like to get some. Right now all I have it two old cranky goats and a horse.

I thought of you and Caroline yesterday when a radio host was talking about the passing of her father. The other host on the show said that her father was "a collector of people" and any where he went he always had people gather around him and had such energy. I see you, Jack, Jeremy and Caroline as a collector of people and that is just awesome.

### Inger — 2014-04-19T11:09:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-122507>

Nicole... you are just too sweet...  I cannot see myself as a collector really.. I think I have way too much struggles and I also eat stuff most would not ever want to try  but if I can be to any help it makes me so happy!!!! That is why I share what works for me... it is very basic and simple.. You know, after I got to feel so good I want everyone to feel it too! I just love this place and the people here

Ok I took some more pictures yesterday as I was watching the lambs eating in the backyard. I have the mommas tied to a rope just to be able to quickly remove them if the get too close to the neighbors tulips...!
The ram needs no rope he is running after the moms wherever they go lol. I would not need ropes if the neighbors would not have flowers growing but I cannot risk them being sheep food

Here is the daddy.. and the babies doing French kissing behind his back....

Brother and sister got tired and lied down in the sun

.... but this little guy, the smallest of them all - he never tires!

### Inger — 2014-04-19T11:11:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-122508>

### Inger — 2014-04-19T11:20:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-122509>

I was at the lake yesterday, I took a dip too. And walked around barefoot - a lot!

Here you can see what I mean when I say I am fatter in winter... I eat the same.. I am pretty sedentary, in summer I am just way more outside and grounding and in sunshine.
You can see in the summer pic on the right side how there is less fat and more muscle.
I eat ketotic year round. Only in late summer I eat lots of wild berries but the berries we have here do not have much carbs at all.

...........................winter belly................................................................. summer belly.................

### Inger — 2014-04-20T09:49:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-122654>

Ahh..... guys!!!  you make me laugh... but there is a difference. Josh sounds like he has felt my belly.... lol but I am sure he is right! i do not mind Neil studying my belly at all, he must be the most gentle doc, I am sure of that.
Now, my butt is the real game changer. It looks way better in summer. But I am not going to post pics of that

Happy Easter to you too Audrey  it is such a nice weather here... it was freezing temps in the night, now it is +5 but it will be +15 degree C today, and sunny.. almost no wind at all.. I am going to build fences today for the sheep. I already started this morning  what a beautiful morning
Holidays are not too easy for me always.. here are many people and everyone is home. It is dragging me.. at times (I know it is only me that has my focus wrong at times, darn  so far from optimal still, but working on it ) To feel great I so far still kind of need that "breath" I have when my parents and bro are away for work in the week. but I am so fortunate to be able to work outside - it helps everything!

### Inger — 2014-04-23T18:18:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-123205>

Emme gave birth to twins yesterday    me, my uncle, my bro and mom and dad watched it all live from the terrace right above the pasture. It was so exciting.. i was so worried all will go well. But I decided to let her instincts rule and I did nothing - let her be out in the middle of the pasture and give birth alone. And she did it all perfect

One white guy and one brown girl.. too cute - and a bit like premature born.. they are so small! I have given them a little bit raw cows colostrum to help them get stronger fast as the guy was almost too weak to suckle... but Emme is soooo loving and caring for them, she wants them to suckle all the time and she does all to make it better for them, so touching! She almost takes her momma role too serious, and can get a bit nervous! She kisses and talks to them all the way....lol
I have spent so much time down on the pasture barefoot, just watching that all is well. I have gotten so many hours grounding in last days, pretty amazing  and lots and lots of sun too
just have not had time to read on the forum / blog much...

### sooperb — 2014-04-23T20:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-123220>

### Inger — 2014-04-25T10:45:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-123405>

yes looks like that Audrey - she is as worried about her babies as I was for her lol
yesterday I lied on the lawn watching them grass and Emme's twins come and lied next to me, one touching my feet and the other my arm. The ram come too and lied down next to me and put his head on my breast it was pretty magic

I washed the car again manually in he sunshine yesterday listening to one awesome song.. and I thought about the ex husband and how beautiful a love we have, and how painful it has been but now it is no more... like when I let go of the dogmas how i thought it should be and instead moved on to reality everything changed into something very beautiful.... wow!
It i just crazy how our reality can change almost over night? This is how it was meant to be, now I see it clearly - i was just too scared of letting go!
the more we hold onto old ways the more we will suffer... easy

### sooperb — 2014-04-25T11:05:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-123410>

Inger the Shepherdess, how idyllic it sounds  Letting a love go is so painful isn't it, I'm sure we've all been there and suffered yet come out the other side a better person. I still remember my first love and how painful it was realising that it was over for good. Yet looking back, I see now that it would not have been a good or happy union. He's been married 3 times already lol. He was a lovely person but a lost soul, no direction in life.

### Inger — 2014-04-25T11:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-123414>

yeah Sue, shepherdess is my favorite work ever

The love thing is so interesting - you know... I always intuitively knew me and hubs were not meant to me partners in that sense - i could feel it by his touch and kisses... (he married me as a mafioso that was on the escape from the Russian mafia... so he took/needed my name... I rescued him from that.. we escaped to Germany with literally nothing at all and built a new life - and he rescued me from the dogmas in my family, helped me learn to think for myself and get rid of the brainwash... a win win    ) but still i was so full of dogma (I have been grown up in a environment and family that sees divorce as very sinful. once married it must be forever and you can never remarry) I consciously knew it was not right but learned behaviors when it comes to religion are very tough to beat. The toughest ever I bet. It is the darn uncoscious that are so hard to convince

I feel so free now. I feel just amazing in my heart about me and the ex. Our love is the same as it was, and will always be. It was just a different one - we help each others with everything, and we can even live together, we are like two children loving each other in a pure, amazing way

I am SO lucky.....   it has not been easy for me to come to this point, but the easy road never made me exited

imagine, we was married for 15 years.......

### sooperb — 2014-04-25T12:10:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-123422>

Wow Inger, you never fail to surprise lol. Having been through such a lot together it's a shame that it wasn't for keeps. I find it quite sad that you so obviously have feelings for him but perhaps not enough to stay together.

Ah religion, so much grief it causes! Is this what God envisioned when he handed out his message? I doubt it somehow, it's all gone very wrong. Religious fanatics seem to be the most dogmatic in the world, they don't see the irony do they that in killing for their religion they completely disobey God's will? That's if you believe in God, I don't. I respect the right of people to have and follow a religion if they wish, but not to actively try to convert everyone to their beliefs and take a life if they refuse. I don't want to be persuaded or convinced that one path is better than another. Living in a family where the religion is imposed on you rather than you having a choice must be difficult, more so if the said religion is outside mainstream or has principles that curtail personal freedom as well as religious freedom. I know that for those living it it just seems normal, but for others, looking in from the outside, they see practices that are restrictive, cruel and dangerous and which go against personal liberty and freedom of choice.

Oops, sorry, soapbox got in the way again. There is a plan for everyone, it's the journey that's so exciting sometimes

### sooperb — 2014-04-25T15:05:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-123438>

Acceptance is a gift, to be able to allow someone their beliefs, even if you don't agree or accept them yourself. There aren't many people who practice it though and it can take years to learn the value of it. Not only does it save you a lot of personal stress, but it can enhance and improve relationships with others, being judged by your peers is difficult enough without the lecture that usually accompanies the judgement. If people only realised how hurtful they are being when they point the finger at those who don't walk the same path as themselves, life could be so much easier all round.

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-26T12:26:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-123495>

That's a big family. It must make for some busy Christmases if you all get together!

I've got nine nephews and nieces. Fortunately, only one is a vegetarian. He was a little withdrawn at one time and not sociable or communicative. He's a lot better now -- much more outgoing -- so maybe it wasn't an effect of the diet. He had a girlfriend who was even worse them him, actually vegan and very withdrawn, and he came out of his shell and normalised a bit after he broke up with her. I do have a slight concern that he'll get ill from eating like this, or feminize himself, or both. I don't think it's good for males (or females) to eat all that soy, because of the phyto-oestrogens if nothing else. I think veggies get misled into thinking they need to make sure they get enough protein, since they know that is a problem with their diet, and they think these artificial hamburgers and sausages are the answer. Really free-range eggs and raw cheese would be far better. His personality seems to me to be more "feminine" -- hard to define that, I know -- than that of his brothers. I have to think some beef and oysters would do him good.

But he has become interested in nature and the outdoors and is working at a nature reserve and doing gardening, so he's going in a good direction. And you can't worry too much about family members, and it's useless and possibly counterproductive to try to persuade. People have to go their own way here as well, don't they?

### sooperb — 2014-04-26T12:50:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-123496>

Interesting point about your nephew, mine is vegan too, he's a big softy, I think leans more to the feminine side. I don't see much of him as he lives in Spain so I couldn't say if he is healthy or not.

My niece tried being veggie for quite a few years. She was always ill, never felt well; one day she just started to eat meat again. In not too long her health improved and she felt much better. I suspect her diet isn't brilliant as she is in her early 40s and is always full of coughs and colds along with her two small children. She didn't learn to cook at her mother’s knee that's for sure  how can people still not understand what a huge difference the right food can make?

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-27T18:16:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-123585>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=123496)
>
> Interesting point about your nephew, mine is vegan too, he's a big softy, I think leans more to the feminine side. I don't see much of him as he lives in Spain so I couldn't say if he is healthy or not.

I mustn't post too much in Inger's space, but … It seems just possible to me that drinking soy milk as a small boy may have influenced the way his personality developed. He had problems with eczema even before weaning, but cows' milk was a no-go. The doctor suggested he moved to goats' then sheeps' and finally my sister was told to give him soy-formula. He "grew out of it" as they say and now drinks milk with no apparent problem. Knowing what I know now, I suspect her gut microbiome had got impacted in one way or another, and, of course, he'd inherit it. But the long-term effect of that soy -- plus what he's eating now? I think that no-one who's listened carefully to Kaayla Daniel could feel totally at ease on that score. Those xeno-oestrogens? And possible mineral imbalances?

When I said to my mother, "His personality seems less masculine than ------'s" she immediately seemed to know what I meant and agreed. But just what that means … I think by it we sometimes have an ethical (using the term in a very broad sense) distinction in mind rather like the strong distinction between Pagan and Christian ethics that Nietzsche drew when championing the former (or his understanding of them). That's to say, pride-based ethics as opposed to compassion-based ethics. But we probably mean a lot of other things. And we certainly seem to mean that women are, or can be, more emotional than men. There's an interesting interview online with John Nicholson, who was a vegan for years. When he began to eat meat again, his wife, and others, remarked that his personality became "more masculine". He says that on the soy-filled diet he would weep very easily -- when watching films and so on. That just went away when he stopped eating all the garbage and began getting the meat he needed.

[http://www.blogtalkradio.com/drloradio/2013/08/12/the-meat-fix-with-john-nicholson](http://www.blogtalkradio.com/drloradio/2013/08/12/the-meat-fix-with-john-nicholson)

I am so behind..!  Need to catch up on the forum.... My sister have been here the last days with family, she has 8 children... so I have been surrounded with them all day long they follow me everywhere I can barely go to the toilet... LOL
They are too cute.. they want to try all my food, raw fish and stuff and they always say, yummmm... I love aunt Ingers food, it is so healthy! 5 yo kids (twins) haha... i can hardly believe it  They are leaving today... I have been out barefoot all day long scantily clad in the sunshine all day too last weeks. So nice weather here, 15-17 degree C, even the river starts to get warm, + 8 degree C in the daytime! Now it will get colder and rainy but I welcome it. We need some rain for the grass to grow and I love rainy days too

Children... give me so much electrons! They want to cuddle and be close all the time and it is just too nice    LUCKY ME

My shoulders... no pain no more
I knew it will heal them, barefoot out in the sun drinking plenty of magnetized well water  munching on wild herbs and eating mainly raw seafood.

I sleep so well! The magnetico makes me feel so good, in the morning when I wake up as sun rises I just want to stay in bed forever - even if I am not tired - it is just so sweet a feeling

### Inger — 2014-04-30T13:54:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-124032>

You say something...
Now, I think I lost some electrons too because of other things... you cannot even imagine how much it hurt me to see when parents harm their children with mental and physical abuse but sure they do not see it

You can see it on the children how they are not totally harmonic and have many fears. It is sad. When I took them to the shop they were so proud and told me they play I am their mom lol... but the girl said, in the shop are only dangerous and evil people. I said, what? In my shop people are very nice  Their parents have teach them everyone that has not the same clothing and rules as they are dangerous and bad people... they even had made me a letter how I have to pray and read the bible every day and never wear cute "princess dresses" as they say.. I have to throw the cute ones away and the dress need to long. And I will not come to heaven when i die because I am not in their church... etc. But I know it is not the childrens fault, and as they were leaving one girl cried so much she wanted to stay by me, to live by me. Because she loved to be out all day long barefoot on the pasture with the lambs.. and she loved my home too... Their dad do not let them take the clothes and shoes off... but they did when they was with me....
It is tough to try to focus on only the beautiful things when the ugly and mad stuff are present too but darn I have to learn it!

So... I kind of crashed a bit after they left... mentally. Also one lamb has gotten a bit injuried somehow from all the carrying the children wanted so badly to touch them and I thought if anything happens it will heal... so I have had to take him in and warm him in front of the fireplace and give him extra milk
I do tried to teach the children how to behave nicely with animals

So..... finally today I felt I needed some wine! Emme has broken out from the fence many times and darn I have to watch them all the time the fences are not in too great shape even if I work on them but still much to do... I drove to the shop in my working clothes and dirty hands.. and got me a nice organic Bordeaux and raw sheep cheese (Roquefort).. and in the shop was the guy again.. that was there last time and was so kind to me. He come and asked if I need help, but I knew what I wanted this time  and then I told him I need to cool my nerves with some great wine my sheep are escaping from the fence and everything is chaos.. but I had to hurry back so I could not talk long...
There is something in the way he cannot take his eyes off me.......... and he is the absolute gentleman - I think I will have to go and buy wine more often!

If you figure out how to keep you sheep in the pasture let me know, I have two goat that won't stay in either.

Abuse aside you see children sat on in an unpleasant way around you all the time. People continually on children's backs: don't do this; don't do that. Half the time it's totally trivial stuff. I've seen a woman shout at her child in a coffee shop because he'd spilt the beaker of drink she'd given him from her bag. As if he'd intended to! It was too large, heavy for him to hold. They don't understand situations that are going on around them, and the child gets the blame.

> they even had made me a letter how I have to pray and read the bible every day and never wear cute "princess dresses" as they say.. I have to throw the cute ones away and the dress need to long. And I will not come to heaven when i die because I am not in their church... etc.

You know what Bernard of Clairvaux said?

> God comes unarmed; he wants to save you, not to punish you. And lest you should say, “I heard your voice and I hid myself,” look—he is here, an infant with no voice. The cry of a baby is something to be pitied not to be frightened of. He is made a little child; the Virgin Mother has wrapped his tender limbs in swaddling bands; so why are you still quaking with fear? This tells you that God has come to save you, not to lose you; to rescue you, not to imprison you.

Why people want to make Christianity of all things something fearful when, yes, the New Testament has God "coming unarmed". It would be better not to bother with it.

### Gagnrad — 2014-05-01T10:41:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-124219>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=124212)
>
> Sue they are not Quakers, they are some kind of a community with strict rules, Christians.

I knew Sue hadn't seen the post you deleted when she said that. Quakers, of course, have not been violent since their very early days (when they were exceedingly so). I find it difficult to imagine a Quaker even thinking what you'd mentioned there. Not that such an action would be remotely compatible with anything in the New Testament anyway.

> What is weird to me is, they follow those rules with clothes and stuff... and still they have so big issues disciplining themselves when it comes to food...

The food is not linked to _membership_.

Sometimes you will still hear people talk as if religious beliefs were an attempt to understand the physical phenomena around them -- a kind of proto-science, something theoretical, as it were. That kind of explanation of religion is 18th century nonsense. One thing is certain: whether or not there is a real reference to anything transcendent in religious belief (which is a wholly inadequate way of stating it) it is, at any rate, rooted in our social nature. There should be no doubt about this after Durkheim. (Among other things he showed that the rate of suicide was higher in Protestant countries than in Roman Catholic countries, a clear reminder that we a dealing with a social phenomenon.)

### Inger — 2014-05-01T10:43:41+0300

### Gagnrad — 2014-05-01T14:09:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-124254>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=124249)
>
> I agree. It wouldn't be the kind of Quakers I was raised by….

Yeah, the obvious connection made by Sue was with the plain clothing. This was long a feature of the Quakers.

Interesting history. They originated as a kind of violent revolutionary movement. One has to bear in mind that this is in the 17th century in the aftermath of Civil War, when there were all kinds of strange sects around. The response they naturally encountered rather traumatised them and made them rethink, so that they flipped 180 degrees and became pacifists.

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b01f67y4](http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b01f67y4)

### nonchalant — 2014-05-01T16:39:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-124302>

As Mike said, "The food is not linked to _membership_."
The dogma of the group you strongly identify with, whether a religious group, social or political group, a group of people linked by a certain disease or affliction, by a profession or whatever, can be very compelling. One would fight hard to maintain that dogma, in order not to lose membership they need. Especially if the group was interested in controlling the actions of their members.

I feel strongly linked with the people here at this site, but I don't feel much pressure to conform. Sometimes I quickly jump on ideas Jack has, other times I wait for a bit to see how others do with it. I never went too far with one of the protocols because it just didn't seem to work for me. I did pick up, and keep, some elements of it, however.

We all should watch out for the encroachment of dogma, since it can be compelling if you are a human being. Jack does an excellent, detailed job of explaining why his protocols work.

### sooperb — 2014-05-01T17:13:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-124319>

I did indeed associate the clothing with the Quakers, I discounted the Amish because I wasn't sure if they lived outside of the States. Every religion has it's rules, that's how they exert control, they don't have much else. The Mormons are interesting in that respect, they ban the use of coffee and alcohol because they consider them stimulants and stimulants can have unwanted effects. Curiously they permit chocolate, higher in caffeine than coffee, it doesn't make too much sense. On the face of it their community thrives on respect and shared care, in reality there is a darker side that they keep much quieter. I am not religious, I can't join any religion that seeks to control me by the use of punishments and which does not allow personal freedom.

Neither, they were stupid pets that my mom convince me to get for the DD when she was 12!!! Oh, they don't live very long she said, that was 11 years ago!!!!!! I am not opposed to eating them now but they are so old and cranky we don't think they would taste very good.

### Inger — 2014-05-01T18:22:15+0300

Great stuff guys... i want to talk to you more..! I have been planting cucumber seeds..and doing work in the yard barefoot in the rain, yay!- first real spring rai, it is +7 degree C,
the lambs are so cute I can watch them forever.... now little Alex guy do great and jumps around like crazy lol
i need to post some pics when I have time - still behind with my works. Had to clean after the kids too

Now i have to watch the webinar, finally...! made a fire in the stove and do not watch my dirty feet
at least I have gotten lots of electrons

### sooperb — 2014-05-01T19:03:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-124363>

Dirty feet are a bit of a problem, since I started going barefoot I can't get them clean no matter how much I soak and scrub them. Just as well it's all underneath

After 11 years you couldn't possibly eat them, it would be like killing and eating your children  They say that goats are the only other animals after dogs that really recognise their owners.

### Inger — 2014-05-02T07:56:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-124519>

Nicole, you could eat them? I bet they would taste awesome! If you give them only hay and they are on the pasture?
We ate our older ewe last fall and she tasted absolutely awesome too  Goat meat is very good! Just not so fatty, but very delicious
You could eat the brain, and organs... and tendons.. and skin YAY when I think about it I get all exited

Sue I eat my "children" all the time  it does hurt a bit, not the eating part... but that they are no more.... but I keep telling me, that's life. And they kind of got to live through me instead  or, they gave me life
I do love the seafood eating, I am not feeling bad at all killing an oyster! A fish.. a little worse, but I manage it. Lambs... I can no way kill.. my brothers do that work, thanks God!

### Inger — 2014-05-02T08:06:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-124520>

Yeah... that is weird about the dogma. My sister that have been in USA for many months told about Amish people she got to know, or former Amish. Pretty horrendous.. and they do so much right! But as soon as the rules start to be rules you push on others everything goes south... IMHO
It needs to be no pushing, but it has to work through attraction... there needs to be a "draw" to the beauty instead of pushing anything on anyone

I have to confess... I sometimes even doubt Jack!  ok they are very rare moments, but still they exist  I need to think critical about everything.... i have seen very close where blind obedience have lead

My little sister is getting married to her American boyfriend this fall... she wants me to be her bridesmaid
they do not live too far from Jack!!! A little more North from the Gulf...
now I need to really save money and do like Audrey... eating he cheapest stuff

### Gagnrad — 2014-05-02T12:26:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-124535>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=124520)
>
> Yeah... that is weird about the dogma. My sister that have been in USA for many months told about Amish people she got to know, or former Amish. Pretty horrendous.. and they do so much right! But as soon as the rules start to be rules you push on others everything goes south... IMHO

The Amish seem to be a form of Mennonites, who have roots in the Anabaptist movement:

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mennonite](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mennonite)

Notice:

> This meeting marks the beginning of the Anabaptist movement. In the spirit of the times, many groups followed, preaching about reducing hierarchy, relations with the state, eschatology, and sexual license, running from utter abandon to extreme chastity. These movements are together referred to as the Radical Reformation.

This is perhaps a less attractive side of the Reformation. People thinking for themselves is great, but sometimes they come up with pretty strange ideas. The (brilliant) English translator William Tyndale said to another cleric, "I will make a boy that driveth the plough know more of the Scripture than thou dost" -- and that has an immediate resonance with us in the modern world. But there's this other side to it. Interpreting something as potentially explosive as the Bible for oneself outside of any tradition of interpretation ... well, sometimes people come up with something pretty silly. There you've got people coming up with anything from one extreme to the other. The Old Testament has to be handled particularly warily, of course.

But I guess this isn't just a post-Reformation phenomenon. The Middle Ages was rife with strange movements:

[http://www.amazon.com/The-Pursuit-Millennium-Revolutionary-Millenarians/dp/0712656642/](http://www.amazon.com/The-Pursuit-Millennium-Revolutionary-Millenarians/dp/0712656642/)

### Inger — 2014-05-08T15:07:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-125843>

One morning I woke up and saw big snowflakes coming down outside my window... so beautiful - everything was white! It melted during the day tho.. pretty fast!
But it has been cold... today only + 4 degree C and raining. I was out with the lambs on the neighbors pasture to let them eat, they have no more grass on their own pasture anymore and I paid the neighbor some money and asked if I can let the grass on theirs and she was just happy to let them do that
so today I see how little Barte was trembling so much... he is the smallest of them, so skinny! I took him inside and gave him warm raw milk and made a fire to warm him up by the fireplace. Poor little guy. Now he is fine tho.
We got the first chick yesterday too... I think there are two, I have to check today - they are too cute!

Here is Emme with her boy and her girl, Buna and Barte - they are just the cutest tiny lambs

and here are the others playing... I watched them from my balcony

So I started to work in my parents company too, cleaning a Golf club 3 mornings / week, to get some extra cash and I can then use their car too as much as I like. Nice

I have been planting so many cucumber seeds... oh man! I am thinking to get well hydrated with structured cucumbers all summer long
my ex husbands girlfriend promised to make us fermented cucumbers then, she know how to do that

### fitness@home — 2014-05-08T15:14:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-125846>

The lambs are adorable! Inger, you have the best photos & videos.

Really envy your living out in the country. We looked at a piece property last week with a spring fed pond on it. Now to figure out how to afford it. It is a bit over 10 acres...we could have chickens and other farm animals.

### Tanya — 2014-05-08T17:56:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-125888>

Hi Inger. I really enjoyed watching those cute lambs! Those cucumbers sound really good - they also contain lots of silica, and we know that we could always use some of it....

### Lahelada — 2014-05-08T18:41:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-125895>

No development about thw work and the car,more nature to explore .
Btw I also pictured you as the Norwegian answer to Heidi,just like Gagnrad

### Inger — 2014-05-08T20:31:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-125905>

Lahelada, more nature is always good IMHO
I remember as a kid reading the Heidi books... that is always how I wanted to live... since a kid
Now this is not very close but sure not too far either
I sometimes get thoughts I am stuck here and I must move to my own place where I can make my own rules... but. I have promised myself to never move anymore closer to a city than this. Only further away. So it can take some time to find such a place, and meanwhile I am going to enjoy my life here and make the most out of it - and I can learn some great lessons as here are challenges for sure

Hi Tanya
yeah... I know cucumbers have high silica... that is what I am after too, great for skin and hair, I am planting the short ones that grows outside, they are sure better than the long ones - you cant grow them here without a greenhouse. I am no fan of greenhouses at all. I think veggies need real sun and earth to be healthy
I have planted onions already. I also am going to plant lots of carrots to eat with dirt on, and dill! And peas. they are so good to munch on. Some salad too... but that is more for my mom, I rather eat wild herbs

f@h here are big properties on the countryside always cheaper than a small one close to a city, actually the more remote the cheaper. How good for us
just.. I have no money at all to buy anything - but glad I can enjoy my parents place like I do  and they are very happy to have me here too - makes it a win win  even if their rules sometimes makes me crazy
but I need to remind myself, what is most important to me - what is my goal, and then it gets pretty easy
My parents are not fans of technology - yay !

>
> Thanks Caroline! That makes sense, mixing them to a smoothie. I guess I was a bit too much into swallowing large chunks without much chewing...  I haven't found a video, but there's a blog where Inger has described the Mackerel Head Smoothie: [http://jackkruse.com/winter-solstice-special-blog-the-inger-effect/](http://jackkruse.com/winter-solstice-special-blog-the-inger-effect/) There's also a thread about MHS on this forum: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/mackerel-head-smoothie.2403/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/mackerel-head-smoothie.2403/) (I haven't read all if it yet, though.) Breakfast, anyone?  Actually, I don't think I would have problems with it, although I'm not sure my co-workers would appreciate my breath...

Yeah, I put them, the whole head (mackerel heads I cut a bit smaller with scissors beforehand), in a mixer and run with water until drinkable... I sometimes add soaked seaweed too

I do try to blend it less so there are chunks, which I swallow whole.
Chewing the head and swallow bigger pieces would be superior, sure! But I am not there yet  i even have to hold my nose drinking the MHS i think it fr sure is NOT delicious! After I rinse my mouth and put something better tasting in my mouth

Your breath will not smell from fish head smoothie...lol at least not if the fish head was fresh
but.... from high meat if the meat get stuck in between your teeth!!!!!
So floss and rinse - maybe even brush.. after eating high meat. If you do that you will smell just great, and no one will have a clue you just ate the stinkiest thing...
