### Inger — 2014-09-21T12:19:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-141513>

Well I just got shocked.....

My ex called and they were measuring their apartment - that we thought was good EMF wise, as there is poor cellphone coverage and surrounded by pine forest, right by the sea shore, maybe 500 meters away only... but...
the gauss meter showed up to 100 milligauss in the whole apartment right now... in the evening another day it showed less than 3 milligauss..... This is serious!!!! I got so freaking angry!!!!!
WTH are they thinking??? To rent such a place to anyone? Criminal huh? I told them to call the landlords and show it to them.... Where does this crazy high reading come from? Nothing you can see with your eyes around the house either... My ex said he slept so soundly by me.... no wonder. Here it shows close to zero everywhere.

My ex has trashed hormones. I could totally feel it when he was here... i know him too well. Now I know why

I told him to buy a big tent and sleep in the forest with his girlfriend and her daughter, until they get another place. I doubt he will... he never liked to sleep in tents....

### prAna303 — 2014-09-21T14:36:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-141520>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=141519)
>
> Told my ex about it. Thanks guys. He needs to contact the house owner and they will have to fix it. It is an apartment in a house with maybe 20 apartments or so....

Many small things...

\- Is the house made of concrete? Watch out for the reinforcing steel...
\- Do the house have any companies? During daytime, big load of electronics but also at night when they crunch backups.
\- Washing during daytime, big one.
\- Many at home at day? Everyone is online when they have spare time.
\- If you got bad reception, all others in the house will probably have it also. Every little phone will scream for connection.
\- Modern homes use WiFi or Lan to everything, think the connected home. Wires are a big no no today, to bad i think, all gadgets work to steal bandwith all the time.

Dear Inger,
in a house with 20 appartements such a reading is no surprise at all in my opinion. Nearly every one has internet over Wifi and you can get such readings from neighborhood. In my last home in cologne i measured readings above 2000 mg permanently from the neighbours appartement and many other sources around 100 mg fro the neighborhood. An EMF engineer measured an average of 75 mg in my appartement. So the simple solution for me was to move and find a house with only three apartments to rent in it near cologne. And i measured as i looked for the appartment, which was a good move!

### Inger — 2014-09-23T14:56:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-141719>

I got me a huge 100% grassfed Galloway beef heart, very fresh! Now half of it is fermenting in a jar.... finally i will soon have my beloved high heart again!
I have it in room temp (13-16 degree C in my kitchen) so it will ferment faster.
The rest I eat raw, as carpaccio... with raw onion slices.

Had the vessels and fat and some heart meat for breakfast today, gently warmed til half raw on the stove with curcumin and black pepper... yum.
First course was 4 oysters...

Lunch was a big and thick 0,5 liter smoothie made of herring roe and herring fish milk... oh lala that was a bomb  that stuff had sit for almost a week in my fridge
but I figured i will digest it just fine, so far my belly is happy!
After the a bit yucky smoothie I had raw celery and 1/2 cup og hazelnuts.. that was yummy
and a spoonful of raw organic pollen....mmm.

\- - - -

I am sure now my hair is curlier when it is colder.. it is not only a humidity thing.... interesting

### louise — 2014-09-25T13:37:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-141906>

I love reading your journal inger especially the descriptions about the nature where you live...... I love the countryside also
but am feeling a little scared when I read what you eat ....omg I didn't realise this was the epi paleo diet!!!!

### Inger — 2014-09-25T13:51:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-141910>

My diet is freaky Louise.. and the worst thing is... seems it is hard to scare men away with telling about it.....  unbelievable but true
this diet I eat in combination with the Kruse protocols gives me a feeling like being on drugs, so I continue.... no mater what
Interesting things are happening here lately... but I cannot tell yet... but I can say it freaks me out! In a good way  but it is kind of too much.. i am way too sensitive it seems....
i might have overdone it on the oysters......  i cannot cool my boiling hormones down

### louise — 2014-09-25T14:00:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-141912>

I think your diet is incredible inger......I know how amazing I felt just by kicking the wheat and junk out so I cant imagine how you must feel.
im going to read your journal carefully and see what I can learn. loads im sure!!!

### caroline — 2014-09-25T15:53:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-141924>

Oh Inger .... we truly are sisters! hormones in the stratosphere ....yikes!

### Inger — 2014-09-25T16:01:07+0300

yeah.. quantum magic

it is not entirely pleasant either... a cold shower might help? Can we accuse Jack for our sufferings Audrey? What thinks you?

### caroline — 2014-09-25T16:11:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-141927>

This is all definitely Jack's fault! how many cold showers can one person have??????

everything is just oozing out of me.... someone is driving this Ferrari ...and I don't think it is me!!!

I can't even park it .... I am in idle!

### caroline — 2014-09-25T16:16:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-141928>

I feel like I am falling in love with the idea of love ... all the beautiful words. It seems to be two separate entities ... the words ... and the person. Somehow there is a disconnect that I don't understand.

### caroline — 2014-09-25T16:21:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-141929>

I just feel so high on life all the time ...and that really attracts people. But I have to be careful around my friends husbands now ....I never used to think about that....

### Inger — 2014-09-25T16:24:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-141930>

well... can you try to connect the two in your mind? Or just.. tell them.. no more words.. but spended time together instead so there will be a balance?

Might be you need time Audrey.... it is not always that we fall in love the first time either... I know I have needed a few hours.... and then BOM or it emerges slowly.... might be your resistance is tougher because of your husband.... If the man has lots of hormones.. and he falls in love with you.. and he is nice... I would say.... it is only time needed... and... to relax ?

### Inger — 2014-09-29T19:20:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-142164>

Yesterday I went to do my favorite thing.....

It was no sun but pretty warm, maybe 16 degree C... and I found so many beauties...

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I picked so many I could fit in my huge bag...... oyster supply for a week again
heaven on earth... IDK why I love it so much but when I pick those oysters at the ocean floor it just feels... divine....

I printed a quote from Peter and hang it in the kitchen - and I printed a picture from me as a child too... to remind me when I forget how to think.....
when I look into the eyes of that child it reminds me of what life is all about......  that is the place i need to be in.... it is easy.. I just need to DO IT

### caroline — 2014-09-30T00:12:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-142194>

beautiful Inger......xo

I feel now that all the unimportant stuff just falls away .... doesn't even take much effort, if any, to see the important thing/moments/people in our life.

There seems to be a natural rhythm to the simplicity of life....the things that really matter to me.

and then there are the things I can't control ..... so those are the wtf moments - says it all!

### Inger — 2014-10-02T17:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-142546>

So true Audrey ^^^^

Today is a special day  - I will celebrate it with champagne, oysters... and I got me a huge Galloway grassfed Ribeye steak

I woke up at 5 AM... seems to do that a lot lately (I go to bed shortly after 9 PM or earlier...i cannot manage to stay up longer I get sooo tired!)... it is when the roosters start to "sing"  - there are many of them around here! It is so dark in the horse barn, I can see nothing... and I have no watch there either... but when I hear the roosters I know it is 5 AM.. cool

My terrace at sunrise.... I eat my breakfast here, watching it getting lighter and lighter. It is so amazingly silent! I love the silence.... just sounds of the nature all around

Breakfast today was raw lambs hearts from the neighborhood. 2 of them, with balsamic vinegar and raw onions.. and chickweed and raw pistachios.. yum! First course was 2 big oysters...... and I had 1/2 cup of a cardamom coffee too

I can tell the wild oysters are way harder to open up as the cultivated ones. I bought me a oyster book... mostly recipes.. that I do not care for at all.. but I got some great info in there too - like, lots of sunshine makes the oyster shells thicker! I bet the oysters I pick have gotten lots of sun, as the shell can be crazy thick. I have a great supply of oyster knifes to rescue but I really have to be careful to not break them.

The 2 knives at left I have broken already .. and the next one with wood is my favorite one. But I need to be very gentle or it will break. The next is my second favorite, it wont break! And the one on the right is my last favorite...

### louise — 2014-10-02T17:11:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-142548>

fabulous photos inger.....gorgeous baby pic of you.....those dark round innocent eyes of babies just makes your heart melt sometimes.
didn't know you can eat chickweed.....there must be loads by me but never realised I could eat it.......I have a large supply of wild watercress in the forest stream...lovely
I cant wait til I need to take a few cold showers lol...... caroline has just introduced me to the oxytocin thread and im captivated......such hardships in the quest for health !!!!!!!

### Inger — 2014-10-02T17:17:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-142551>

well... Louise... hormones can get dangerously wild on a diet consisting of so many oysters and sunlight....  IDK if I can recommend...!  lets say.. at your own risk

Yeah, chickweed is very very healthy! And it is so delicious too, definitely you must try! It has a very mild and crispy and juicy taste, maybe a bit red beet taste in there
fits well in every salad if you like. I like to eat it plain
You need to start eating wild greens Louise.. they sure are magic

### Inger — 2014-10-02T17:19:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-142554>

innocence... that is what babies are....... so beautiful
it is just so healing to be around them.

Inger, what on earth are you doing with your knives? I think you need to be more gentle and loving. The oysters aren't your enemy!
I've been using my favorite oyster knife for ages, and it works like a charm - even though it's not even a "real" oyster knife. It's absolutely fine with both farmed and wild oysters. It cost me less than 5 EUR:

### Inger — 2014-10-02T19:14:16+0300

### Inger — 2014-10-02T19:33:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-142583>

I dream so much every single night. In color. I am sure. Because the woman in my dream tonight had red hair. It was my boss... the lady Doc.. and she was in love with me........ she could not understand how I did not love her (like... partner love not friendship love....) and she said so many hot things to me  it was such a weird dream.... seriously! I did not tell her today at work

i just shut up and hold tight.. and climbed in huge spider webs and spiders around washing windows on their big house - at the outside... it was nice
but I swear... I have never seen such huge spiders... in Finland they were NOT!
i am not afraid of spiders anymore, seriously... but those almost - but only almost!- freaked me out

### Andrea — 2014-10-02T19:44:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-142584>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=142573)
>
> Oh... I would never have thought of that Rerun... I am going to try...!  You are so smart
>
> Do you have the same bastards I do? Watch this... incredibly thick a shell..... the big ones sometimes are a PITA.. so funny to find.. but open.. not
>

Smart? Haha! Sometimes I simply have luck when thinking...
But, you know, when what you're doing doesn't work, it is a good idea to try something different.

Yep, Crassostrea Gigas. The same ones that I have been stealing here. And yeah, the shell is pretty thick. But it doesn't really matter. I just find a good spot for my knife and "feel" my way into the shell. I can't fight the oyster, because the blade of my knife is so long and I don't want to end up injured. So I open it gently.

### Inger — 2014-10-02T20:05:33+0300

> fabulous photos inger.....gorgeous baby pic of you.....those dark round innocent eyes of babies just makes your heart melt sometimes.

watch this Louise.... I want this innocence back... so open to life........ this was once me. I love to watch her in her eyes.... it has some magic
Is it possible to regain the innocence?
When life has hit hard? Can wounds heal? I mean.... really heal.. so there are no scars
i sometimes feel like it is possible......... but it is not every day as of yet

Rerun... I always thought you were smarter than me.. in fact I know it...
I am more the artist.. and not smart a cat...
but I learn from you.
Thank you

### Inger — 2014-10-02T20:30:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-142594>

Hi.. I am Inger. I have had too much Ribeye and too much Champagne and I have dirty hair today.
I am sitting on the terrace -my cat is with me as always.. - watching the sunset. Celebrating my birth. 38 years and counting..
I am forever thankful to my parents.....
I am an A.A.O.J.K. (anonymous addict of Jack Kruse) I am forever thankful to Jack. INVALUABLE what I have learned from him....
Forever thankful to my siblings that I all love so dearly... and forever thankful to my ex husband that I love dearly too.
I love my life.

### Inger — 2014-10-03T20:23:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-142715>

Thank you so much all, for the kind wishes - I forgot to say how thankful I am to have you all.....!!! This is such and awesome community...........

\- - - - - - -

I went to sleep on my terrace yesterday evening.... with my cat of course.. it should not rain, and it was such an amazingly warm evening! But at 3 AM I waked up because it was raining on me....lol I lied in the rain for a bit but as it got heavier I had to move the mattress inside or it would have gone wet. I then walked down to the horse barn and slept really well until 7 AM

Today I have cleaned my bedroom out there... many year old horse shit.... oh man I can tell it was hard work! I had to use a steel brush... and lots of water.... But I loved it! I cleaned almost the whole floor... and the walls too.. just one corner is back. It got sunny so I had to stop cleaning and then went to the lake and took a swim  awesome.... I ate and picked chickweed with me for tomorrow breakfast

I am SO proud of the horse barn bedroom..... it is going to be beautiful!!! I carried my wooden bed out there too today.... and when the floor is dry I will bring a nice, white colored thick wool carpet I have no place for in my home. In the barn-bedroom on my cellphone I have only 0 to 2 out of 5 for connection.. so it really is a great place to sleep EMF wise... YAY
I just crave to sleep there.... When I have it all ready I will take a pic

The landlord helped me fill air into my bikes tire today, and as he did it he asked... _What is it... is it a love nest out there...???_
hahaha...... i said I am doing a biohack  That is the truth for sure. I am testing what I have learned from Jack

### Inger — 2014-10-03T20:46:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-142717>

Took this pic today as I was at the lake in the sunset... it is so magically beautiful out there... maybe you can feel it through the picture... the lake is almost not noticeable, behind the beautiful tree

### Inger — 2014-10-03T20:55:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-142718>

I took this pic too, because this also is a magic place to me. Under this tree, is zero cellphone connection. zero. It is like a small crater somehow... immediately when I saw it I thought that must be a great place...I am thinking to sleep under it a night.... i often rest there for a few minutes when I am walking to the lake.. it is on the way. It feels so very peaceful to rest under it.... The other day as I did, a deer jumped close to me... into the woods.. and stopped there to look at me for a few minutes... so nice

### Inger — 2014-10-10T18:34:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-143418>

I found a pearl in an oyster.. a tiny one... I put it on the kitchen counter to save it.. but later I realized I had cleaned the counter and my pearl was gone

I bet I have eaten/swallowed many pearls!

The other day it was some amazing weather... in the night. It was a clear full moon moonlight evening - and at the same time thunder and lightning! How crazy... I have never seen anything like that... the thunderstorm was right behind the house on the other side of the moon.... but I could see and hear the lightning sitting outside in the moonlight.... very cool!

### Inger — 2014-10-12T15:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-143590>

Today morning.... it was bright moonlight when I woke up... maybe at 5:30 or 6 AM not sure because, my clock had stopped in the kitchen, I realized later!
I just had to take pictures... I was at the terrace watching the moon disappear and the sun rise... it was so beautiful... and silent
At left the sun was rising.. and at the same moment on the right, the moon was shining.. and the stars.. wow

### 2Timothy1:7 — 2014-11-10T17:30:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-146765>

Inger! Your video of the MHS was so good; one day I will be as brave as you.
I adapted your pickle recipe because I was wondering whether it would work, so I used ACV diluted down with water, onions, garlic and cumin seeds, and left that overnight. In the morning I added some lamb and left that for ~6hrs. It was sooooo good! I wasn't sure what'd happen leaving the meat in longer but the acid just turned the outside brown as it would with cooking. Give it a try!
Have you ever had hear meat raw? Or pickled it? I can get some really good grass fed ox heart and liver but i've never had any organ meats raw so I look to thee the great woman of wisdom on all things raw-related

### Inger — 2014-11-11T12:01:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-146904>

Well Timothy... you already are brave... just.. do it
I eat raw heart all the time! I have lambs hearts in my fridge that are pretty aged now... and I love the taste... I have them there for a month or more. I do beef heart too. I prefer heart way over muscle meat and I eat it way more often too. It is about the only "meat" that is not seafood, I eat, actually. I do have a steak here and there but it is rare. Liver is very good raw too. Tastes sweet and mild, very good with raw onion. I mostly just cut pieces off the liver with a scissor and eat plain..lol Very easy and fast food
Your pickled herring recipe sounds good!  it is fun to experiment

### Andrea — 2014-11-21T18:49:09+0200

### Andrea — 2014-11-21T18:51:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148083>

By the way, you too eat weird stuff like fish heads... What do you think about lamb brains? I've found a store where I can get lamb heads. Wanna come over for dinner?

### Inger — 2014-11-21T18:55:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148084>

I LOVE lambs brains!!! I will take the next flight....!
I have eaten our own lambs brains... I cannot get it anywhere else. They taste great.. and makes my brain sharper! I sure could feel that! Just take care too eat them raw so you get all the benefits
I have openend the skulls of our lambs myself, is not too hard. It was a bit yucky as they were kind of my pets but I just imagined I was Jack Kruse on the operating table....  and I just bit my teeth together and did it. Watch on google how to open a skull.
You need a knife, a hammer and an ax, that is.

### Andrea — 2014-11-21T19:06:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148086>

Raw, you say... Hmmm... I was thinking of cooking it gently, to make it a bit more firm. But sure, I'm fine with raw.  Du you suggest any spices or something else to go with it, or just eat a piece just as it is?

The downside is that these heads come frozen, from New Zealand. So, conventionally farmed.
You see, here in Sweden animal heads are "high risk waste", so we throw them away. And then we import frozen ones from somewhere else.  Not even the butcher's where I bought a whole sheep would give me the brain. At least this last time I got the ears...

### Inger — 2014-11-21T19:25:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148087>

hmmm... IDK with conventionally farmed lamb brains. I am picky that it should be grassfed. There do are the proton diseases. But I think with a lamb the risk is close to 100% as they are pretty sure grassfed. Cows brain would be riskier. I cannot say for sure...... what to do. I just know if I eat raw lambs or other animals brain i will be sure to know how they were raised and where they come from.
IDK if cooking helps eliminate any of the bad stuff, if it happens to be contaminated?
I know the issues is when they feed on their own species..... that is really dangerous. That is what happened in Britain with their cows and the mad cows disease. I guess today no one does that anymore? Certainly not with sheep/lambs?
I am not sure cookin g wilkl make it more firm... lambs brain is pretty much fat only. It is pretty firm. Have you seen my video where I eat lambs brain?

### Andrea — 2014-11-21T19:47:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148088>

Thanks for posting the video! It does look quite firm. Good!
I know there are risks with prion diseases. But from what I have read, New Zealand is considered scrapie free.
As far as I know (but I may be too naive?) lambs are raised outdoors, even in New Zealand. They mostly eat grass. Here, however, farmers usually feed them grains too so that they will grow faster.

### Inger — 2014-11-21T19:51:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148090>

you will find someone that does not feed grains I am sure. Just start searching? I think New Zealand is safe too.
And that said... some additional grain feed might still not be so bad that you could not eat the brains. If the lambs were healthy. But Sweden have pretty strong control over diseases I think, like Finland too? They control every lamb/sheep that is laughtered

### Andrea — 2014-11-21T19:58:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148091>

Oh, I have a farmer about an hour from here who doesn't feed their lambs anything, they find their food on the pasture. And they slaughter them at a small slaughterhouse, with strict control and hopefully without too much stress. I bought one such ewe a couple weeks ago. I got the heart, lungs, kidneys, feet and ears. But not the brains.
For brains my only choice is from New Zealand, frozen.
I think I'll give it a go next time I'm near that store...

### Andrea — 2014-11-23T08:05:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148215>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=148150)
>
> WTH did you do with the EARS, Rerun...?  and the feet......
> tell me..

The ears are candy...  I cook them sous vide for a long time, cut in pieces and deep fry them. A little salt, maybe black pepper and some herbs. Crunchy on the outside, soft inside. Yum!
As for the feet, I cook them for a long time too, and then put them on a grill or make a stew. Delicious! I would gladly add the tail to the stew, but the type of sheep that I'm buying have just very short tails.
I don't know why, but I love eating softened collagen. Haven't figured out how to eat this stuff raw though.

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=148150)
>
> You might get the heads from the farmer near to you with the grass fed lambs if you do not tell you eat the brains... you could say you need to exercise open up skulls?

Nope, I can't! They have to throw it away, as high risk waste. The butcher could lose his license otherwise. They are so happy that I want all parts of the animal, but they refuse to give me the head.

> I don't know why, but I love eating softened collagen. Haven't figured out how to eat this stuff raw though.

Can they tell you where they throw them \[properly wrapped\] and then you an retrieve them when they are busy elsewhere???

### Andrea — 2014-11-23T09:18:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148218>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=148217)

> Can they tell you where they throw them \[properly wrapped\] and then you an retrieve them when they are busy elsewhere???

They throw the risk waste in containers that are transported away for destruction.
I have tried my best to get a head or two, but to no avail. I'm not going to fight with them any more, because all in all, I am happy that they do follow the regulations and that they do their job carefully and well. If I could make them break laws or regulations, how could I (or anyone else for that matter) trust them to follow all other regulations?

### Inger — 2014-11-25T17:44:37+0200

well... my period should start this weekend..... i never have sore nipples. hmmm... I need to watch out closely if they start growing or turning darker
Maybe my hormones are just increasing from all the oxy...lol
i did felt weirdly tired for a few days and i had very vivid dreams last days too...  I dreamed I ate sooo much delicious stuff.... my moms cookies.. a huge seafood buffet, it was amazing.. I just ate and ate.. so cool! I was thinking in my dream... well. i should not eat in the night time.. but man this tastes so good.. and it seems to be a dream so I can eat all I want...haha.. weird!

Today as I came home from the walk in the woods a package waited in front of my house... it was a pair of very sexy high heels.... from the boyfriend! hahaha.. he has a shoe fetish for sure
I do not care... I can walk in sexy shoes every evening for him if I get to eat all the weird foods I love so much - great deal!
But he has told me I am **not** allowed to eat raw herring and raw heart in bed... he loves to eat breakfast in bed on lazy mornings....(soft boiled eggs or oats..lol)
i can live with that.. I get so nice morning oxy every morning I can eat my herring in the kitchen

### Inger — 2014-11-25T17:53:34+0200

The other day we played for 1,5 hours in the woods.. climbed tress and stuff. I was just so amazing I would love to do that every single day!!!
The 9 yo Lena looked at me as I climbed a tree and I was so scared but I did it anyways.. and she said.. Inger, adults never climb trees. They do not do that kind of stuff. But you do.

lol so cute

### Inger — 2014-11-26T17:08:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148555>

Well..... there is nothing I would like more than an oyster baby

but... I am sure I am not pregnant. I never was so far in my life.. not even close...
I must just make stuff up in my head! uh! Because it is so exciting after all....

My boyfriend has to laugh about me...lol
He calls me... and I say... darling... I have sore nipples... what do you think...?
He says..;
oh... do you think.... a baby King? But sweetheart... last month... there was a baby King on his way too... and where did he disappear...?

because last month my period was a little late.. yeah.. and I was thinking.. maybe....
oh man.. I need to stop thinking too much.......lol
But sure.. today at work I felt a bit light headed and fatigued and I got so crazy hungry........ but it might be because I was washing the car of my boss (from the inside) and that was really hard work.. she is a vet and the car was dirty... oh man I washed and vacuumed it for 3 hours... + also did other work in the house with no rest at all. It also is a bit stressing that I live here and he lives there and all the traveling and my cat does not like driving at all, she complains all the way during the drive, the poor thing. I do sing for her and cuddle her as good as I can. She does like to be by the boyfriend tho, he has this cute cat Rocky.....

### Inger — 2014-11-27T18:13:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148649>

No...lol I always make up stuff in my head...lol I swear! My imagination is pretty vivid
I will wait for the next week to see if I get my period before buying any tests.. and my boyfriend has promised to examine my breasts if there are any changes... (he really likes to do that pregnant or not...haha... good for me!)
But today as I dipped in the rain barrel (water was maybe 2-3 degree C) for a minute or two, my nipples felt quite sore until an hour after... poor things. But they usually do well in cold water. Hm. It might be too that my hormones are just healing and getting boosted from all the oxy my boyfriend so generously gives me?
Well... If I get preggo one day I will sure celebrate _wildly_
But I am just as happy if not, and so is the boyfriend, we both have the exact same relaxed way of looking at this... which I very much like

@ Nicole... that is one crazy cat....lol

I was at the shore picking oysters today... + 3 degree air temp and windy.. I had to walk 4 km to the shore, pick them, and walk 4 km back. It was amazing! I picked only 19 oysters as I was barefoot and my toes got sooo cold... and I was scared to cut me.. I actually did... but not too deep. I all of a sudden saw some blood in the mud.... When the feet get that cold you want feel anything much and that is a bit dangerous.... after all I had to be able to walk back on my feet and there were not one person around on the beach...i was totally alone. So I kept it short. I need to buy me rubber shoes!

At the beginning of the walk I felt so powerless in my legs? So weird! I normally have strong legs. I almost was scared to do the long walk, man. Really weird. I had a small breakfast and all before... I cannot understand why my legs felt like that. But I went anyways, in the windy, icy air.. and it get a tiny bit better, so when I finished the 8 km walk I felt less tired in my legs...lol
I got such a huge appetite coming home so I ate my half kitchen empty..uh! Man was it good  I really needed that

### kovita — 2014-11-27T18:56:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148653>

darling, you still watch out! It would be great to have some pregnant company on this forum! I envy the beach, we go next weekend to the Atlantic for almost three weeks, I cannot wait to walk the beaches and warming up by gorging on oysters. I hope we can move there completely during the spring. That is my big Christmas wish for this year.
I will have a question. I got lucky and have now in my freezer a huge piece of wild boar hunted in the deep woods including all organs. unfortunately the way to me was complicated so it is all frozen. I am thinking what to do with the organs, maybe you can throw some ideas.

### Inger — 2014-11-27T20:41:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148656>

I promise to watch out
would be so funny to be pregnant together for sure...... you could be my counsellor

Well, about the boar organs.. you know my habits are weird but I would probably just swallow raw pieces of most of the organs.. like nutrition bombs. From the heart I would make carpaccio.. it is my favorite heart dish
I do not like lungs, kidneys etc. too much so I would just cut off small pieces with my scissors, swallow and do not care to make anything out of them at all. That is what I did with my elk organs I used to freeze in Finland.
I just thawed a piece here and there and ate it as medicine. Great stuff.
I did a smoothie once from lamb balls and elk kidney, all raw... and that was the bomb..... oh man it really was a strong boost..
Delicious it was not! But medicine mostly is not anyways so I do not worry about the taste.

Rerun is the girl who knows how to make great stuff out of ears and legs and stuff!

You are really lucky to have gotten a wild boar, wow.  I love boar meat, I sometimes could get it in Finland but seldom. I loved it raw, slightly dried on my dehydrator over night at 40 degree F. yummy!
well if you have it all in one piece so you cant take and thaw organs piece by piece.... then it is a bit more complicated, they do not last too long I guess after they have thawed.. I would try to eat it in a few days or a week or so. You might find some nice recipes with organs on internet? I am sure there are some.. I just have never tried any as I just do them raw.

### nicld — 2014-11-27T21:03:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148657>

Chop them up small and put them in a bone broth based stew with cabbage, onions and rutabaga? I do this with liver and it makes for a very earthy and deli-sh soup.

### kovita — 2014-11-28T08:26:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148681>

thanks, girls. I think I will eat the organs raw and make somethi yummy presentable to the rest of the family form the meat. I love wild boar as well. while in Cevennes this spring we were meating families of wild boars on every walk we took. They looked so yummy I convinced the owner of the place to go and shoot one and I think the best was the broth, very dRk and incredibly tasty. Well, there were so many of them it was allowed for the local guys to shoot even out of the season providing they were not shooting mums and babies.

### Andrea — 2014-11-28T09:37:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148682>

> [kovita said:](/goto/post?id=148653)
>
> I will have a question. I got lucky and have now in my freezer a huge piece of wild boar hunted in the deep woods including all organs. unfortunately the way to me was complicated so it is all frozen. I am thinking what to do with the organs, maybe you can throw some ideas.

I'm a bit worried... Has the boar been tested for trichinosis? I don't know how it is in your area, but here in south Sweden boars with huge amounts of those nasty little things have been found. It's definitely not a parasite I would like to eat.

As for organs, personally, I like the heart and liver raw, but the rest I prefer gently cooked. Kidneys I cut in smaller pieces, wash and clean thoroughly, sear some onion and caraway in a pan and add the kidneys for a few seconds. Lungs I like to cook for a while, cut in pieces and fry.
The liver, if it is in one big frozen piece... Maybe some liver paté (cut in pieces, gently fry them with some onion and herbs/spices, maybe some mushrooms too, then run it all in a blender and add a large piece of butter - pour into a container and let set), which can be frozen?

### kovita — 2014-11-28T12:30:39+0200

> I'm a bit worried... Has the boar been tested for trichinosis? I don't know how it is in your area, but here in south Sweden boars with huge amounts of those nasty little things have been found. It's definitely not a parasite I would like to eat.
>
> As for organs, personally, I like the heart and liver raw, but the rest I prefer gently cooked. Kidneys I cut in smaller pieces, wash and clean thoroughly, sear some onion and caraway in a pan and add the kidneys for a few seconds. Lungs I like to cook for a while, cut in pieces and fry.
> The liver, if it is in one big frozen piece... Maybe some liver paté (cut in pieces, gently fry them with some onion and herbs/spices, maybe some mushrooms too, then run it all in a blender and add a large piece of butter - pour into a container and let set), which can be frozen?

good point. it looks like it is still not uncommon infection between the wild boars from the area where it was hunted. I will let it frozen for few more weeks and cook the meat well.

### Inger — 2014-11-28T20:47:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-148716>

I felt so amazing today waking up in the ice cold horse barn... I just have not felt THIS good for quite a while!
I think I finally got rid of the cigarette poison... uh. It is more than a week since the last one but the stuff has to be cleared out I guess. That was a stupid biohack. It just take off the edge of my well being feeling. So weird. Even if I smoked just a few natural cigarettes / day or some times none. But today I finally felt that drugged high feeling again really strong as I was in the woods... without any drugs at all yay  This very strong feeling of joy and oneness with my surroundings and deep peace and and and... uh I love it. I bet that is how people feel when they take mushrooms... but the forest and the sky.. then wilderness.. they are my magic mushroom.. yay  It feels sacred too.. and so strong it brings tears to my eyes when I am out there. Like the feelings are too big to fit in me somehow.

Maybe the walk to the shore and the barefoot picking oyster tour yesterday did something good to me too  I love to be outside in the icy and cold wind. It is addicting.
Maybe.. the colder it is the more we need to be outside! The better we will feel  so cool. Yeah.. that is how it feels to me.

I have been dipping a minute or two every morning in the rain barrel and the water is now close to freezing temps. And my nipples hurt/are sore up to one hour after? I do not understand that... I cannot remember it was like this last winter. I always go nude in the barrel. If any women on the forum that do ice baths too read this... are you nipples sore after icy temps?
I am trying to figure out my nipple issue
They are not red or inflamed or anything, looking good! I just cant remember icy water ever made them sore like this. Maybe I have become a wimp

My high lamb hearts in the fridge taste divine... first time I have made high meat of lambs hearts. And they are turning out to be the best tasting high meat ever. Best to date. Just crazy delicious!!! like really really aged, yummy raw cheese......

But strangely, it doesn't taste as bad as it smells. A bit sweet.

### Inger — 2014-12-07T19:13:14+0200

I am home.. and I was by the ocean yesterday.. it was a gorgeous day, not much wind, and sunshine. I skinnydipped in the ocean.. and it was pretty cold, uh! I just stayed in shortly as I was completely alone and the water was deep. But it was amazing. I need to do this more often. ### caroline — 2014-12-08T03:06:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-149467>

Just saying "Hi" Inger. As you know we are pretty much in the same situation ...it is so easy to get off track a little - and I actually think it is okay for a bit ...... and then we realise what is important for our own n=1. We already know it is different for everyone - so someone in our life has to find their own path and definitely respect ours. To me - that is what enduring love is all about.

When someone truly loves you - they want what is best for you..... but we have to captain our own ship. We need to be strong .... and certain. We have been on this path for 3+ years now. We have learned so much about ourselves and we know what we need and what we want out of our life and we are on this wonderful journey off self discovery. The significant People in our life have to do the same for themselves.

It is so great sharing stuff and indulging a little ...... but when it comes right down to it - we now know what is best for us ...what keeps us healthy and strong and vibrant. That is the person they fell in love with!

We need to be strong and lead by example ......but a late nite here and there and a little wine now and then and eating a little later than normal..... that is real life.

Now - down the track a little ...... I am becoming a lite/nnEMF/food Nazi etc. etc. etc.

But ..... look what we have gained ......lots of hugging, kissing, oxy, laughter ....... and a huge amount of respect for the person we are and what we have accomplished.

There are so many wonderful positives .....I am being constantly reminded to drink more water! I was dragged out of bed the other day to walk to the beach and watch the sun come up ...and then in the evening to see the sun set.
to watch the nite sky and the stars and the moon. To roll around in the grass like a kid ........

I wouldn't trade this for anything .....I am much more alive and vital and glowing ....and I am sure that you are too!

If we give up what is important to us ......that says everything about us and nothing about the other person!
xo

### Inger — 2014-12-08T12:27:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-149501>

So true Audrey........ I LOVE what you write above.
It is actually exactly what I concluded myself yesterday after feeling somehow depressed...... I realized it was all my fault, darn! Funny how the brain can toy with me at times. I kind of get angry at it...lol
I do see the reason why my brain and feelings go off the rails and I have to start there. It is so much easier when you know why... and pretty amazing cause then it is just to change it

I do am waiting for the summer a bit now.... wintertime is the hardest time to try to "merge with the rest of the world"  that is when the difference really get obvious! I understand very well the boyfriend think I am extreme..... blue blockers.. early sleep... cold.... it is just too much in once...haha In summer none of this will be present, very different somehow.
I need my hibernation...lol
I do have to say I am proud of my boyfriend. He has put the heating in his house down to 18 degree C. Because of me. And he let me open the windows in the bedroom in the night.... even if he thinks it is way too cold! I have found some thick duvets for us and that has helped.. it is funny how he freezes and I am so way too warm....lol I tell him to warm his hands on my hot body and he thinks I am so crazy because I like that! I love it it is so nicely cooling
Well... and I can eat exactly what I want. That is really nice.... he does not try to make me eat any stuff I do not want to so diet is pretty good by him. He does think it is weird that I like to drink my eggs raw etc. I do eat some cooked stuff too, like burgers, with him. Yeah, he liked my burgers  I made him a huge plate full and he eats them just like that with some mustard or hot sauce. Cool  He did not eat bread once as I was there last time. He even said, Inger, do you realize I have not eaten bread once this week? Because of you? Well... I thought that was sweet.. as I have told him he should eat anything he wants he just needs to stay healthy  But he knows I am no fan of bread... but he does eat cookies...lol not too much tho. Yeah... it is funny and interesting and challenging and I love it all - there is so much for me to learn! I think I have had it way too easy here alone? Easy is not always the best..........

### Hope — 2014-12-10T15:25:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-149691>

Hmmmmmm I am not sure I understand what you mean about something being your fault? Something you are depressed about? Hope all is well!! And way to go for your BF eating less bread.

I seem to appreciate winter more and more........in fact I find I crave the darkness  Pretty soon we'll start getting more sunshine again..hard to believe how fast time flies!

### Inger — 2014-12-10T20:01:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-149731>

Well... Hope.. I am so not perfect. And the weird thing is... when I am out in the nature... away from any EMF... I can see so clearly! I can see the issues, I find the solutions..... it is just magic.
It makes me cry. It is very humbling and it is so beautiful too and gives me hope and power and joy
but when I stay inside i kind of sometimes get lost
what is it? Is it the wifi.... or the absence of trees, grass.... the heaven.... IDK. i just know it is such a difference. And that is why I must go out to the horse barn to sleep every night.
Is it fun? Sure not so.... in a way... it would be way more comfortable to stay inside the warm house and go into a cozy bed instead of the ice cold a bit moist bed out there..... uh it is cold now.
but something inside me needs it. so the evenings I so do not feel like going out there... I go anyways. I just go.
call me crazy
I know Peter would understand. I sometimes want to scream... Peteeerrr... where are you when I need you
It is hard to be alone with this sort of stuff as people do not understand. Peter does. I know. I need to get strong. I was in the woods today at sunset and it was so beautiful... I ate lots of wild herbs from the ground.. looked into the sun as it set and tears just ran down my cheeks... i could not help it. 2 beautiful deer jumped over the path in front of me, playfully......
I got all the solutions to my confusion, the answers just popped up into my brain. It was amazing. I need to go out there every single day. That is what keeps me sane. I have to direct and structure my sensibility, i am so sensible. I sometimes hate it... it can do great pain. But it is only when it get into chaos. To be in the wild structures it all. So beautifully
There is a LOT of work to do with this girls brain yet. ahhh

but man is it nice to see the solutions to issues that arise... that is pretty much amazing stuff
now just to put it into work!

### Inger — 2014-12-12T18:56:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-149916>

I should probably start taking the bedding inside and let dry during daytime.... I cannot cover the mattress tho.... as the body gives off water in the night too from heat it needs to dry up too and if I cover it with plastic it will sure get moldy......
this is new to me that it is so humid....... maybe it does not hurt me but I am not sure... I have a minor issue on and off last weeks I need to sort out but i am not sure from what.... to many things happening...lol

### Inger — 2014-12-14T09:55:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-150047>

I had amazing dreams tonight............
I was dreaming about Jack..... and I and he danced together.. and there was so much magic energy around....huh... crazy!
I do not understand... every time I dream about Jack I get a huge energy boost... it is crazy??! Like... really really strong stuff i can still feel it after waking up

I was almost 2 hours yesterday in the woods... walking... eating wild greens... skinny dipping in the magic lake... and I did sprints too... so cool! I wanted to.... i had not planned it at all. cool.
It was warm... I think 5 degree C..... and the sun was shining, i watched it as it disappeared down at the horizon

### Inger — 2015-01-01T18:15:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-151315>

Well..... i had an awesome NYE... I just was alone..... contemplated... and made plans for 2015... with some good port wine..... and lots of good food
Well.... i just was able to see where my issues came from... so crystal clear! All of a sudden. My brain is so weird... it really needs peace and loneliness to figure stuff out it seems. I was invited to a NYE party but I did not go... i just knew i had stuff to figure out. I have been suffering mentally quite a bit the last weeks on and off. I guess late nights and... yeah... cigarettes  took a toll on me. I started to smoke again by the boyfriend, no more than 5 cigarettes a day, I just cant do more, but still they really affect me i guess. It is really easy for me to quit tho... which is cool. I just love the feeling I get when not smoking...haha... So why do I smoked again at all! I guess i got so confused in my head. It is really big a challenge for me to take someone into my life it seems... because of trust issues from past marriage etc... oh man i am so damaged  I just realize it now. But this all is really bringing it up to the surface and that is good! So lots of stuff to work on for this girl. I got a breakthrough at NYE tho and for that I am SO thankful. No idea how it happened, it just all of a sudden did... i decided to think differently and everything changed. Crazy what power thoughts have on us. Crazy. I went for a 2,5 hour walk in the woods today and at one point I thought I was lost.... err! I got a little scared.. because I was thirsty! I had done some sprints on the way and after them I really wanted to drink but had no water.... i panicked thinking if i get lost and i have this thirst and no where any water to drink..... well sooner or later i would have found my way out but i did not like to go thirsty at all. Funny how good i feel even after drinking the portwine, sleeping very little because of the noise outside this special night and all....

So for this year I have decided to
trust more (this is a real biggie for me)
love and care more, for myself and others
and remind myself often, that everything depends on me - I have all the power in the world to change
I am 100% responsible for my life, no one else is... I have to make me happy... that is my job. To have any expectations on others is not going to bring me happiness..... quite the opposite! I have to be the change/love I want to see in the world. It is so easy actually, pretty amazing ! I sometimes get mad at my brain....lol i seriously is not proud of what thoughts it produces at times  but hey, good thing is, I can control and steer my thoughts, thanks God

cheers

### Inger — 2015-01-03T12:48:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-151447>

I have clearly lost a little bit of fat fat....... i do not weigh so i have no idea but i can see and feel it. Mostly people gain fat over the Christmas holidays.... seems the opposite happened to me..lol Now i have no clue what is the reason! I have lately had more fat and less protein.... have been taking sulfur (MSM)... have been sprinting a little and done some exercise too..... my calories tho have sure been high! I am happy about it... as i have had not too good a sleep last weeks... because of late nights etc. Because of convenience I have skipped breakfast most days and had a coffee instead with about 50 grams of butter and a teaspoon of coconut oil in it..... and i have been drinking lots of water.. i really want the water and i do not have to remind myself of drinking. I dream so much! Very clear dreams.... very cool..... One night a few days ago i flied in my dream.. i have not done that for quite a while and it was sooo funny. I just jump up and move my arms and get higher and higher in the air.. I can totally move up there like a fish in water and it is crazy cool feeling! Very real feeling dreams too.

I really am glad i started to exercise some... it feels great.. it feels great in my body  and the body shapes up nicely too!

Curious to see how the MSM is going to affect me. I think it does something good with my mood. Somehow i think it impacts it. Not 100% sure cause it is so hard to know what comes from what tho but i am pretty sure it does something good. I put a MSM and water mix on my face too morning and evening....... i did a hair mask with MSM, Ghassul mud (my usual shampoo) and some olive oil.. and that felt really good. I read on a Swedish site about a women that got rid of her cellulite after taking sulfur! And many other good things too happened... cool (she was high fat low carb too btw) I do not have much cellulite at all right now, but i think there is a tiny tiny bit lets see if it disappears

### Inger — 2015-01-04T21:33:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-151617>

Hope... it is kinda hard for me to go to sleep without my partner... got to learn that. My ex husband always went to sleep with me....... he was not a night owl....lol But sure there must be a way to deal with it... many couples even have separate bedrooms...  I am just addicted to skin to skin contact....... almost like a drug to me  How did you learned to fall asleep without your husband? Give me some tips  When I am alone I have no issues... i fall to sleep like a kid..... i do not understand this mismatch at all..lol

I do dream like crazy lately. Tonight in my dream I cleaned and cleaned a very dirty house.... and lots of weird stuff happened...lol and then I feasted on potato gratin - I was thinking, oh man this is carb stuff... but hey... it does not matter so much... i will eat the whole thing man is it good! And it is for breakfast.... and this probably is a dream anyways.... hehe And then I dreamed about Emme... my sweet lamb... she had gotten two baby lambs... they were both white... and she did it all so well. All in one night.

I dreamed much before too.. but after the MSM it has gotten even more! It is like living a separate life in the night.. and i lay in my bed mornings for a while and think about all the fun dreams... and feel just amazing

Emme is by my one sister and her kids now BTW. The kids take so good care of her. She is very happy there and she does some good to the kids I am sure. She gives so much love. She is special for sure. My mom made me gloves from Emmes wool for Christmas present.... it really touched me... what a beautiful present to get. Nice, white gloves.

The little girl from my neighborhood visited me today.. and we went out in the woods and played together.. climbed and did all kind of fun stuff. I love when she comes by... so much fun with that little kid   Tomorrow we will meet again
I found lots of wild greens/herbs that i munched on too... it is warm here... weird winter! I would love some snow.. but hey, at least i can eat lots of wild greens

### Optimalbound — 2015-01-05T20:48:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-151716>

I'm not really a big believer in dream interpretation, but dreams about [housework](http://www.dreambible.com/search.php?q=Housework) or [cleaning something](http://www.dreambible.com/search.php?q=Cleaning) are apparently quite common, and supposedly means this:

> **Housework**
>
> To dream of doing housework represents a perspective or situation that is being restored to returned to normal. A situation in your life that you take for granted or that you've put off is being put back to normal. You may have taken a break or been distracted and are now focused on making something go back to what it's supposed to do.

> **Cleaning**
>
> To dream of cleaning anything represents negativity you are removing from some area of your life. Self-improvement, overcoming obstacles, or improving a relationship. Making progress or moving forward. Cleaning may also reflect old problems or faulty relationships you have found solutions for. Giving up bad habits or finally telling the truth.

Nearly everyone reading that would probably go "Oh, that fits me exactly!"

Inger, sprinting always does fantastic things for my body, too. I used to do "sprinterval training" (ha ha) early last year and it made a huge difference in my strength and body composition. I'm starting it up again now. I also know that brief, maximal intensity exercise increases mitochondrial density. I always find it so energizing. Makes it hard to sit for long during the day at work, though. Not that that's a bad thing!

### Inger — 2015-01-06T13:41:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-151804>

Do you do totally fasted, or BPC/fat fast, HK?

I am no sprint queen yet... I just do a few, 3 or 4 or 5 just how I feel like.. and I guess my sprints are more like 50 meter than 100...lol But I give all, and it is a lot of fun! I really really love to try to run as fast as I can... it just makes me laugh...lol I realize it takes pretty heavy on the belly muscles too.... cool.

I got sick in my belly yesterday.....  (Rerun... how is it going? ..)
I went to play in the woods with the neighbor girl... and at one point she offered me a candy bar (coconut-chocolate) as we made a rest... I though oh well i will take one small bit from her gift... it will not kill me (I never ever eat stuff like that) so i took a half mouthful and hide the rest in my pocket. Then we went to drink from a old water pump... my friend said she always drinks from it.. and we have tasted it together once before, the water tastes pretty iron-like but if you pump a bit longer it gets a little better. People used to have horses there and it was the drinking water for them. So we had a few sips of that water... yeah. Then a while after i started to feel a bit weird... tired kind of, and a little nausea. Later at home my belly really hurt... and I had to vomit  the food i had eaten before going out to play had not digested at all  it was raw herring and eggs. But that i have often and never any issues. Must have been the bit of candy or the water. Maybe my belly was like... WTF... what piece of poison is this.... candy??? No way am I going to digest this shit....  I later in the evening got feverish... and in the night I had fever too. Felt pretty sick but weirdly enough it felt kinda good...  In the morning I was way better and now i feel pretty much normal, have had the same raw herring again, avocado... some salami too.... BPC.... no belly ache at all.
Weird.

Tonight i dreamed weirdly... I woke up from the pain... it was pretty bad... i dreamed that my sister hit me in the leg and man it hurt like crazy.. in a weird way.. so I woke up from it... and was happy it was just a dream!

### Andrea — 2015-01-06T14:35:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-151808>

Ouch! I'm so sorry about your stomach. I hope it weren't my germs that got to you...
Could it have been that the water was very cold? When I was hiking in Nepal many years ago I had a large glass of fresh glacier water - it was really cold, directly from the river - and soon after my stomach curled together and made me nauseous. The water was clean from germs - I used chlorine dioxide - and I drank the same water both before and after this incident without problems, just not that cold.

By the way, there were some geomagnetic disturbances yesterday and the day before...

Anyway, what a sweet girl to give you something that's precious to her. Even if it's candy.

I do it completely fasted. The important thing is not how long you do it but rather the intensity. 100% effort for as long as you can manage it. Recover your breath. Repeat. You won't believe how quickly you'll adapt. Every day you'll be able to run faster and longer.

### Hansen Kenimer — 2015-01-06T17:59:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-151832>

> [Hansen Kenimer said:](/goto/post?id=151826)
>
> I do it completely fasted. The important thing is not how long you do it but rather the intensity. 100% effort for as long as you can manage it. Recover your breath. Repeat. You won't believe how quickly you'll adapt. Every day you'll be able to run faster and longer.

As an aside, even though I haven't done sprints in about 10 months, I'm finding I'm coming back to them ahead of where I left off last year. Running at the same speed but for 15 seconds longer and needing 30 seconds less time to recover my breath. I think that's entirely due to ketosis fueled by DHA, because last year I was in ketosis, but without the DHA. Huge difference. HUGE.

> Oh man i feel so good.... it is like layer and layer of the onion get peeled off...... so COOL    and it gets better and better
> I tanned nude today on my terrace..... it was such a sunny beautiful morning and after my cold shower and face dunks i just stood there and warmed up in the sun...... so warm here now... i guess +6 degree C I am sitting at my terrace right now too with bare feet grounding... and sucking in the winter sun right now... soon i will go for a few sprints in the woods.......
> i will eat some wild greens too...life is good

Oh I would love to see sunshine with temps like that. Today schools closed due to the windchill getting down to -35F (-37C). I don't mind the cold but really cannot sit outside and get some sun when the skin can freeze in less then 10 minutes. But, I still do not wear a jacket but I did wear a hat today since the hair was still wet.

### Hansen Kenimer — 2015-01-07T17:06:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-151964>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=151945)
>
> Thanks... you do quite a lot! How often do you do them? Once a week or more?
>
> Oh man i feel so good.... it is like layer and layer of the onion get peeled off...... so COOL    and it gets better and better
> I tanned nude today on my terrace..... it was such a sunny beautiful morning and after my cold shower and face dunks i just stood there and warmed up in the sun...... so warm here now... i guess +6 degree C I am sitting at my terrace right now too with bare feet grounding... and sucking in the winter sun right now... soon i will go for a few sprints in the woods.......
> i will eat some wild greens too...life is good

Every day, more or less. They make me feel so good. It's really not the same doing them on the treadmill because you can't really accelerate like you can off the ground (adjusting your body position and foot speed) so I'm really missing the indoor track I had at my old gym, although the surface was hard and I couldn't sprint more than every other day or my shins would hurt. When it warms up just a smidge (it was -11 degrees F with the wind chill this morning) I will start doing them at the high school nearby that has a great track with synthetic surface.

I've noticed that since starting sprints again I seem to be tolerating my EMF exposure downtown during the day much better. I'm not getting that sugar craving at 2 pm every day. Seem to be able to tap into my fat stores better to keep up with the metabolic demands. Not ideal, but I just have to cope as best I can until I'm able to find something out where I live so I can minimize my EMF exposure.

### Hope — 2015-01-07T18:13:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-151976>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=151617)
>
> Hope... it is kinda hard for me to go to sleep without my partner... got to learn that. My ex husband always went to sleep with me....... he was not a night owl....lol But sure there must be a way to deal with it... many couples even have separate bedrooms...  I am just addicted to skin to skin contact....... almost like a drug to me  How did you learned to fall asleep without your husband? Give me some tips  When I am alone I have no issues... i fall to sleep like a kid..... i do not understand this mismatch at all..lol
>
> I do dream like crazy lately. Tonight in my dream I cleaned and cleaned a very dirty house.... and lots of weird stuff happened...lol and then I feasted on potato gratin - I was thinking, oh man this is carb stuff... but hey... it does not matter so much... i will eat the whole thing man is it good! And it is for breakfast.... and this probably is a dream anyways.... hehe And then I dreamed about Emme... my sweet lamb... she had gotten two baby lambs... they were both white... and she did it all so well. All in one night.
>
> I dreamed much before too.. but after the MSM it has gotten even more! It is like living a separate life in the night.. and i lay in my bed mornings for a while and think about all the fun dreams... and feel just amazing
>
> Emme is by my one sister and her kids now BTW. The kids take so good care of her. She is very happy there and she does some good to the kids I am sure. She gives so much love. She is special for sure. My mom made me gloves from Emmes wool for Christmas present.... it really touched me... what a beautiful present to get. Nice, white gloves.
>
> The little girl from my neighborhood visited me today.. and we went out in the woods and played together.. climbed and did all kind of fun stuff. I love when she comes by... so much fun with that little kid   Tomorrow we will meet again
> I found lots of wild greens/herbs that i munched on too... it is warm here... weird winter! I would love some snow.. but hey, at least i can eat lots of wild greens

Dear Inger, I'm not sure my experience is helpful at all. I think it has a lot to do with the fact that for many years - 17 years - I lived alone mostly, and got used to it. Of course it's nice to go to bed at the same time, and I used to stay up with him until maybe a year or two ago, when I tested how I felt going to bed earlier or later....and clearly I do much better going to bed early, and especially in the pitch dark. So I think I just deeply decided that no matter what, I need to go to sleep early, and then it became a habit. It was definitely extremely difficult at first.....because I love spending time at night when it's all quiet outside. But then after a while it became easier. It sure took a lot of willpower.

That is so great about Emme, being OK. I wondered about her, but didn't dare ask for fear she might have become meat. So happy she is OK!! And wow you have her wool as gloves. That is a truly precious thing indeed.

I dream a lot too. Last night I dreamt about finding my cellphone, which I lost on Monday, at or outside of a grocery store, I think. Kind of hard to believe I'd dream about that. I guess I just feel so bad about losing it, it went into my subconscious. LOL.

Big hug to you!!

### Josh — 2015-01-07T20:20:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-151996>

> [Rerun said:](/goto/post?id=151982)
>
> Brace yourself...
>
> [View attachment 1263](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/1263/)

> The K-index, and by extension the Planetary K-index, are used to characterize the magnitude of geomagnetic storms. Kp is an excellent indicator of disturbances in the Earth's magnetic field and is used by SWPC to decide whether geomagnetic alerts and warnings need to be issued for users who are affected by these disturbances.
>
> The principal users affected by geomagnetic storms are the electrical power grid, spacecraft operations, users of radio signals that reflect off of or pass through the ionosphere, and observers of the aurora.
>
> This chart updates every minute. The Estimated 3-hour Planetary Kp-index is derived at the NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center using data from the following ground-based magnetometers: Sitka, Alaska; Meanook, Canada; Ottawa, Canada; Fredericksburg, Virginia; Hartland, UK; Wingst, Germany; Niemegk, Germany; and Canberra, Australia. These data are made available thanks to the cooperative efforts between SWPC and data providers around the world, which currently includes the U.S. Geological Survey, Natural Resources Canada (NRCAN), the British Geological Survey, the German Research Centre for Geosciences (GFZ), and Geoscience Australia. Important magnetometer observations are also contributed by the Institut de Physique du Globe de Paris and the Korean Space Weather Center K-index Watches are issued when the highest predicted NOAA estimated Kp-indices for a day are K = 5, 6, 7, or >= 8 and is reported in terms of the NOAA G scale. K-index Warnings are issued when NOAA estimated Kp-indices of 4, 5, 6, and 7 or greater are expected. K-index Alerts are issued when the NOAA estimated Kp-indices reach 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, or 9.

[http://www.swpc.noaa.gov/products/planetary-k-index](http://www.swpc.noaa.gov/products/planetary-k-index)

Funny, Sunday I was "underwater" shall we say and today I am a few feet off of the Earth....

Hope , did you find your cell phone? That is a bummer to lose it for sure  even if it sure has its up-sides

With the sleeping habits... I feel you. It seems to be one will have to do some compromises in the beginning...... maybe.. Not easy at all to know what to do always! The boyfriend seems to not want to give me up yet tho..lol fish head smoothies and all  If one could hold together despite differences... and grow together... i guess that would be really beautiful...... I just cannot compromise my health too much tho. Time will tell. But sure lots of great oxy helps a lot

Yep, I am so very happy Emme is alive and enjoying a beautiful life  I know she is just an animal but I get so attached to animals when i care for them like that... my cat too..... i really love her! The boyfriend teases me a lot about how i feed her... he says i need to take her to a star restaurant and let her choose from the menu.... because i often make her fresh raw salmon, fresh chicken fillets, and organic minced meat on a plate so she can choose what she likes... sometimes she does not want anything and then i have to look if there is maybe tuna at home to offer her... or raw eggs... if she likes that...  she is my princess. She is very healthy tho... and my theory is she needs to be able to choose from different foods as she knows what she needs for that meal  But yeah... he is right about that i might be a bit too caring for her....lol well... when you have no kids you get strange like that

\- - - - -

At work today the 11 yo son of the vet asked me again how old i am.. he have asked that many times before... i said.. 38. He said... but you look like.... 23..???
cool... maybe it was the egg yolk mask i used yesterday ........ I cannot quite understand it tho as i sure have some wrinkles? Maybe he did not see them. But i do think having it dark after sunset really helps not to age too fast....... fish heads might help too

I am sure you'll figure something out with the BF eventually on the sleep front!  I have so much to learn about relationships, negotiating them etc. It's hard when people don't get it.

I think it's great to love animals. I sure do too. When I watch my friend's dogs, I often cook something for them, or give them raw meat, or even fish, not just the kibble. And they LOVE the real food. They know what's good for them. Your cat is a super lucky cat, indeed.

My phone - I lost it outside.....either walking to a store, or in the store, and I won't get it back. I have called the store etc, even went back to where I parked, all around there, nothing. Whoever found it, and I'm sure someone did, could easily have called my favorite number, which is listed - I'd do that if I found a phone, for sure. I've turned a phone in when I found one. It's sad, but as you said, it's good in some ways....less or no temptation to check email so much, etc. I will have to replace it eventually for sure, of course, for when I travel etc. We'll see.

Big hug to you!!

Hope... Hi  well.... I just use organic, raw egg yolk straight on my face. I figured it must be great because it has lots of sulfur in it
I have an old book where it says to wash your face with egg yolk every evening,m great for removing make up etc. Well, I do love egg yolk masks

I am going to try a new mask tomorrow on my hair. Avocado banana olive oil mask. I have bought me a banana just for that today...lol It was weird... so long since i bought a banana.... I love to try out natural beauty stuff, it is funny! Now... it MUST go well as i have 2 work appointments tomorrow and if my hair looks like a mess i will sure not get the job  but i do not want to wait.. i am not patinet with trying stuff out, if i get an idea or read about it i must try it ASAP
I just love to make my own beauty treatment, all natural

Well... it is kind of cool to be without a cellphone... I bet it feels great in a way...  in many ways    I love to leave my cellphone home when i go out in the woods.... makes me feel free

### Inger — 2015-01-10T23:17:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-152355>

I got the job....!!!        Yay!!! The restaurant is a really nice one....... directly by the sea.... in a tiny tiny village.. almost no houses...
and the name of the restaurant is "happy at sea"  (Glücklich am Meer)
I have to drive half and hour... but half way on the way down there is my oyster supply beach... how practical! Very nice boss, a German lady in the age of my mom... and the guests are really cultivated and nice..... doctors.... real nice people coming there to eat because they have a great menu.... dimmed lights at night.... not too late work, 10-11 PM max and day time work too where you get to leave at 5 PM I can work as much or as little I want... it is like a dream?! The first job I saw... and called... I got.. and it was this amazing. Someone is taking care of me above the sky i feel... cause this is too good to be true...

### Inger — 2015-01-11T09:37:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-152388>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=152360)
>
> What makes the oysters there better?
>
> SO excited for you. That place sounds lovely. Makes me want to go there now.....whew.
>
> You look like a model indeed!
>
> Congratulations!

Thanks, Hope
now you need to visit me and come eat in the restaurant
it is wild and windy here

Well... the oysters here are wild.... i have to search them myself... and that is so cool  But... I actually did not mean to say the oysters were so different, just i can have more of them here... and i get fresh Atlantic herring and mackerel.. and other fishes too, wildcaught... that i could not (or barely) get in Finland...... and salmon is cheaper, I get wildcaught frozen salmon here very cheap so i can eat a lot of that  the best is the herring for sure... dirt cheap and sooo full of DHA! I had a huge smoothie with herring heads, soaked seaweed-water and herring fish milk and roe yesterday before going to work. And for dessert 150 grams of gravlax (raw cured salmon). That was at 4 PM and after that i had nothing.....

Well.. there is a lot to improve on this body still...lol but i feel great i have to say.... like a 10 yo
I do quite a bit of IF lately i realize.... mostly my last meal is at 4-5 PM.. and first meal (except the fat coffee in the morning) anywhere between 11 and 2 PM. I am not pushing anything tho.. and a few days i have been eating at 7:30 PM  but i really try to avoid that. It is easy to stop eating in the evenings when you make it to a habit! As soon as i do one later meal i want it the next day too... but if I say, no, today no more food... it goes away. Weird! I eat as much as i want when i do eat.... the other day I had 700 grams of raw fish in a 4 hour window...... + butter.....lol

### Inger — 2015-01-11T22:07:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-152428>

I forgot to tell about something else I have been doing too BTW... for maybe 2 months already..... maybe it has did some good too..... Hope you might know about this?

It is a German product, a salt with minerals, that is high PH, 8,5, you can use it in baths, wash with it, use as deo... use it as "wet socks" over night for pretty feet etc. I really like it!I use it after my evening shower usually, every night. I shower hot, then rinse off with as cold as it gets, dry off and wash my whole body with a wash cloth that i dip into water mixed with this salt. I always feel amazingly relaxed after this.

The water is so gray every time after finishing, I cannot believe where all this dirt comes from??? I shower every morning and evening... and i am clean before i wash with the salt water, but somehow it must detox and remove toxins i guess. I know it works because it makes the skin so nicely soft too and the feeling after is really good.

This is how the package looks, and at the backside you can read what is in it. IDK if there is anything like this in the USA... here you can buy it in health stores. It is quite expensive, 1 kg cost a bit over 20 €

As for deodorant, I made a discovery last summer, when I was tanning and bathing on my lunchbreak one of the first times. I had forgotten to bring a deodorant, but it turned out that I didn't need it. The dried in ocean water keeps my armpits dry and odorless. Since then I've been using a pinch of epsom salt dissolved in water instead of deodorant. Works just great!
