### Inger — 2015-10-27T09:51:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-180544>

We did  but it was stressful for me and that is why I got sick........ you know I had forgot that all the conflicts they carry affects my energy like.. it really sucks me dry..... it is funny.......
I love them, because I am forever thankful that I got born and it is their fault.. and they are very sweet and nice... but the ambivalence is so strong! Very interesting observations I made.... I react so strong to peoples energy these days it is pretty crazy!
Made me realize how important it is to choose what people you are surrounded with daily.

I am way better today, still a little sick so I got one more day off from work (sooo nice  ) and I am going to tan nude today too on a lambs skin in the sun.... earthing....
it really feel so divine........that is what I have missed so much, and now I get it

\- - - - -

I got the new job I asked for    the season at my oyster beach ended and they will close for the winter..... so I needed something new. I quit by the Vets. It is not so ideal because I have to vacuum clean a lot and it is pretty much radiation
It is a very nice restaurant and it is a very old house a little below the earth surface... like a cellar...... the boss is awesome and he does not have a cellphone and do not use internet either.... and is quite a wine expert as he has a big wine shop too..... very very nice. I am sooo happy   I cant believe I got that job!
It is going to be much work but I rather work a lot and have money for living un dependent  and waitressing is super cool... I love it  so many interesting people you meet

### Inger — 2015-11-16T20:58:03+0200

### thomas — 2015-11-17T13:46:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-182161>

Nice is one of the best places to be in europe right now, with most of meditteranean france, very beautiful place, in one of the departements of france with most forest, and sea, and sun and seafood (I got family living there)!! I feel so good when I am there, very much a fan of meditteranean climate!
Good(or should i say passable) places to be in europe as well now are south of portugal, most of spain, west/south of italy, coastal croatia, coastal montenegro, coastal albania, and mostly the west/south of greece, as well as any mediterranean islands. Still, that's very little areas with decent fall-winter climate for a whole continent!!

### prAna303 — 2015-11-17T13:58:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-182163>

> Still, that's very little areas with decent fall-winter climate for a whole continent!!

I think this can be a major factor for most people.

We tend to search for heaven and at the same time forget to let go and go all in. If we leave the modern city, that is a good start you will soon be tested. Will you try to build your own perfect world or do you simply go out and expose yourself to that place?

As soon as we start to mess with the place you are in and now i mean nature we create a mismatch, time comes into our lives when our cells have to work out of nature. Since we are great at adapting we also are shitty bad. What once was good is now bad when we live in a box...

My personal take has always been, go out and let your systems do its work. If you let the seasons decide how you should react we do function. This means for me that i need winters here beacuse i have endless summers also. Go south i take what i need, here i think you would create a mismatch by doing to much good stuff, take note... There is a great difference between ill, sick and good.

### prAna303 — 2015-11-17T21:41:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-182215>

>
> As soon as we start to mess with the place you are in and now i mean nature we create a mismatch, time comes into our lives when our cells have to work out of nature. Since we are great at adapting we also are shitty bad. What once was good is now bad when we live in a box...
>
> My personal take has always been, go out and let your systems do its work. If you let the seasons decide how you should react we do function. This means for me that i need winters here beacuse i have endless summers also. Go south i take what i need, here i think you would create a mismatch by doing to much good stuff, take note... There is a great difference between ill, sick and good.

Also one thing we should think about...

Lets take that we want to have a nice and decent winter, how many of us is willing?

Living up north, here in the northern sweden you will get some nice and cold days and nights now. Slowly the days closes their doors, not far from now i only get sun for an hour a day... Here we love tech and social media, we love hot homes with newest tv set. I promise you all that dream of a winter, it is not that easy to go the whole range. I have not done it yet but... The only ones i meet out on these days are the crosscountry skiers. It means that most days end at seven, in bed.

As soon as people get here from lets say France, they are in deep shock. But do they play along? No, they do what all the others do. Live modern life.

But in the end, i know that when the sun is here 24/7 i am gonna get lucky, big time.

### Inger — 2015-12-01T15:02:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-182963>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=182215)
>
> Also one thing we should think about...
>
> Lets take that we want to have a nice and decent winter, how many of us is willing?
>
> Living up north, here in the northern sweden you will get some nice and cold days and nights now. Slowly the days closes their doors, not far from now i only get sun for an hour a day... Here we love tech and social media, we love hot homes with newest tv set. I promise you all that dream of a winter, it is not that easy to go the whole range. I have not done it yet but... The only ones i meet out on these days are the crosscountry skiers. It means that most days end at seven, in bed.
>
> As soon as people get here from lets say France, they are in deep shock. But do they play along? No, they do what all the others do. Live modern life.
>
> But in the end, i know that when the sun is here 24/7 i am gonna get lucky, big time.

That is why I love to live here too.. as long as I am connected to nature I feel amazing here too...... even when it is less sunny..... because nature always have huge powers as long as it is wild
and not invaded with people and buildings etc.
and here is lots of wild nature still
I am addicted to the wild.. and I think it has made me a bit wild too... because.. I have this strange drive..... nothing can stop me.... it is crazy

I realize I might have to stay single forever.. because I just cant tolerate to share my life with someone that are not willing to walk the path with me. It just is not going to work.. long term. When men are in love they might tell you they like all the stuff you do and they might even change stuff... for you. But it is not enough.
I need a man who changes things to the better because of himself... not because of me. Because if it is for me he changes, because he wants to posses me... It will not last... because it is not really out of free will and passion....... I need passion... passion to change things to the better.. to explore... to be open to new.... to always learn......
and when I realize a man is not like that.... I lose interest. It is a bit scary. Am I hard? IDK. Maybe I am just protecting myself..... because to live with someone who have bad habits... will sooner or later destroy me too.. I know my weakness. I am capable to drive myself.. but I am not willing to having to be the driving force in a partnership. I do not want that as a woman. it feels wrong.. I am just too sensible.... I need the man to drive himself. For he´s sake.. because he loves _himself_..... and so he loves me too and we will grow together. Not sure where to meet such a man.. it is hard and I am not going to look either. I am happy alone, very happy.. I have the sun and the moon and the trees and the birds and everything beautiful

Working a lot still...and the new job is very demanding.. many days are long... until midnight.. and I go to bed at 1 or 2 AM. Very bad. I can tolerate it because I know it is only for a short time. So I do everything healing in my time off. I can tan in the 10 AM sun.... I have my UV lamps... I have my ice cold rain barrel to dip in.. I have my horse barn to sleep in where it is as cold as outside... and dark! I have my oysters... raw wild caught seafood.. great foods..
It all keeps me going. But ideal it is not.
The ex husband still lives here.... and he seems to get some business going which will mean I can cut off at work.. and I cant wait for that to happen
He is very generous with me when he earns money... he always want to share and help me fulfill my dreams  and I help him when he is in need....
if his business does not work out I still have my plan ready how to survive and well....  so I am not dependent either. It is a little tougher but I can do it
Life is exciting... uh! It is like a drug.. I cant get enough!

and when I have less time in the wild... I suck it in even more when I am there.. like a dry sponge sucks in water..............
it is like meeting your lover after a long time apart..........
for a long time already, I have felt the sun is m y lover... how weird is that... it is so erotic.. so sensual.....
because I have that, I am not dependent of having a relationship for my happiness.. and it feels like.... freedom

### Inger — 2015-12-10T18:15:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-183872>

I did a biohack with UV and H2O2 and herpes
a few days ago I had a real hard day... I was feeling mentally totally broke because of something unpleasant, and independent from me (so I had zero influence on it which was even harder), that happened... having to work late until 11 PM did not help... so next day I was sure I was getting a herpes on my upper lip.. I noticed it in the night coming home from work... I put H2O on it 2-3 times and sat a few minutes in front of my blacklight. Went to bed.
Next morning as I woke up... no herpes or anything that felt like it anymore    completely gone
Yay! I will have to try this next time I am about to get one again.. have not had one for quite a while.. when I get one it is mostly when I am mentally overloaded
If it works again I am very sure that was what healed it - nice cure to have and so easy

Have had some nice days of sun here. Oh man I enjoyed it  That is the good part about working late... I can tan all day long and I do every minute when sun is out  and I go to work really charged

### Inger — 2015-12-27T13:29:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-184995>

I did a hack.... well I knew it was wrong but I had to try how I react..lol

So I had a bunch of dried organic papaya and fresh coconut... one evening.. in the darkest days of winter. Sound pretty healthy what I ate, no?.. but OMG my body hated it.
I got a weird anxious feeling....! And my belly felt unhappy. The anxious feeling lasted into the next day.. horrible. Sleep was crap. My joyful wellbeing feeling was gone. How on earth did it have such a huge impact. It must have made my body so confused! Like WTH... this does not belong to where we are.. what is going on...lol

Never anymore...now I know for sure, my body needs to be tuned into the environment in any possible way to feel the best! Well.. I did know it before too... I just had to test

I was reading a book about suns light and it said, the sun when it hit our bodies, helps to transport the copper that is stored in our liver, to our heart and brains etc where it is needed.....
I thought it was impressive.. and started to wonder about what the fake light and nnEMF does to copper? Does it destroy it, or it just accumulates in the wrong places maybe?

Copper rich foods and sunlight must be an awesome combo
eating oysters in the sunlight is one of my favorite things to do

### notsoperfick — 2016-01-02T17:43:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-185281>

"I realize I might have to stay single forever.. because I just cant tolerate to share my life with someone that are not willing to walk the path with me. It just is not going to work.. long term. When men are in love they might tell you they like all the stuff you do and they might even change stuff... for you. But it is not enough.
I need a man who changes things to the better because of himself... not because of me. Because if it is for me he changes, because he wants to posses me... It will not last... because it is not really out of free will and passion....... I need passion... passion to change things to the better.. to explore... to be open to new.... to always learn......
and when I realize a man is not like that.... I lose interest. It is a bit scary. Am I hard? IDK. Maybe I am just protecting myself..... because to live with someone who have bad habits... will sooner or later destroy me too.. I know my weakness. I am capable to drive myself.. but I am not willing to having to be the driving force in a partnership. I do not want that as a woman. it feels wrong.. I am just too sensible.... I need the man to drive himself. For he´s sake.. because he loves _himself_..... and so he loves me too and we will grow together. Not sure where to meet such a man.. it is hard and I am not going to look either. I am happy alone, very happy.. I have the sun and the moon and the trees and the birds and everything beautiful "

I can honestly empathise with that. My husband is a good man in many ways but lots of areas are difficult because of his refusal to engage in better practices. Leaving is not an option but I feel oppressed sometimes by his attitudes. My daughter would also empathise with your dilemma, she, too, would love to meet a man who is in harmony with her beliefs but she has pretty much given up, where would she meet such a man, she lives in a city. I'm sure city dwellers have a much different focus, it's certainly not on walking in step with nature! There should be a dating site for such people lol, maybe you could start one Inger

### Inger — 2016-01-14T16:48:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-186274>

Notsoperfick,
yes, that is why I am careful. I do not want the problems I see coming! Then I rather stay alone because I am so happy alone too!
I date men and I am able to sense now pretty soon if it is right or not. If not they have no chance.....I am not going to leave my paradise for anything in this world! They have to come into mine
If I already were married, it is a little different maybe. But I am free, so I can choose what I want and need.

I read this today at Jacks facebook site, I loved it so much I had to post it here for me so it will not get lost.....

> To be intuitive is to possess the characteristic of anticipation. You become able to see trends before most. Your decisions are better with less information. Paucity of information becomes a benefit because it limits choice eliminating decisions while elevating satisfaction. Foresight of phenomenon and power over them depend on knowledge of their sequences. How things are connected, related and correlated. It has little to do with any notion we may have formed respecting their origin or inmost nature. Life is about correlated novelties and seeing that trend without our eyes.

> Your decisions are better with less information. Paucity of information becomes a benefit because it limits choice eliminating decisions while elevating satisfaction.

wow........ just wow! This, is what I want to achieve....and I realize, I am on the right path thanks to Jack
I think it is an endless evolution that will never stop and it just gets better and better

### Inger — 2016-01-16T10:18:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-186464>

I and the ex husband have gotten into a habit where we - about an hour before going to sleep - just sit on the couch with blacklights shining through the room.. and talk. No other lights not even a candle. Doing nothing. We just sit there side by side and talk and laugh a lot because it is so beautiful and the blacklights relaxes us so much! I keep the further away on the other side of the wall... I have 3 of them.... and the whole living room is like bathed in moonlight!

My ex says it makes him so sleepy! Me too....lol It feels like such a luxury to be able to do that. Just doing nothing... but bath in that moonlight. Often we have a fire in the stove too..... a nice mix... fire.. and then moonlight
Beautiful......

Tomorrow I will get a guest, a guy that I have dated a few times and it is pretty exciting... My ex have met him once...... I wonder if he will like my moonlight

Lots of interesting stuff happening... life sure never gets boring even if I try to make it as boring as possible. Now when I have quit work for a few months... I can be home and enjoy the evenings away from fake light and noise... it is such a gift! I am able to explore the dark.. and the silence.. for real. Feels so good... so very good. Taking long walks in the woods every day, watching the sky.. breathing the cold fresh air.. what a blessing!

### thomas — 2016-01-17T15:17:43+0200

Maybe you are in love with your ex-husband, and he is in love with you. By love I mean, real love, where you want to spend time with him, want him to be happy, tell him stuff you wouldn't tell anyone..
More like a pure innocent child love, which is real and genuine and don't need labeling as opposed to what most adults call "love", which is mostly nothing more than a contract, with it's rules and expectations and formalities, and is more about your ego than the other person.

### Inger — 2016-03-02T15:10:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-190075>

It is so funny. The boyfriend cannot understand how I am so hot in the night.
I have to uncover, with window wide open.. and he is cold.. and he feels how I radiate heat, and he do not understand

So one morning he woke up and felt how hot I was and he said this is scary! So he needs to measure my temp.. because this is not normal! so he went to get a thermometer and he measured my temp as I lied there in bed...lol I felt a little like in a hospital
He lit a candle... to see the numbers.. and then he measured himself too - and I had more than one whole degree warmer temp than him.... and he thought it was crazy
and then he likes to measure my blood pressure...lol he sure thinks I am an anomaly - he do not get that I eat so much and do not get fat either.

### Inger — 2016-03-06T15:32:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-190269>

Well.... I feel like I cant really return to civilisation and I feel....like I die... when I have to give up the wild girl in me.......
I feel like... people might get attracted to the energy of the wild... but when they learn about how to harvest the energy they do not understand.. or are not willing to take the step...........?
I need to figure it out, I need to be better! How can I combine the two worlds? Is it possible, or do I need to choose one? Freedom..... or to be held captive? Can I gain/harvest so much energy that I can break through... and adapt.... to many different things.....strong as a Lion
Why am I so attracted to the wild..... why do I panic when I seem to have to give some of it up...why do I hate norms... and rules..... rules often seems to pointless to me, I just do not get them.. at least some of them.
What are these weird rules we make.
I only want to be out in the woods. hear the birds sing and the trees whisper.. and feel the breath......feel the vibration and the rhythm surrounding me there.. it is just so amazing

I bought my first tan through bikini....
maybe not the prettiest.. but great to use when neighbors can watch into the garden!

I splashed myself with some epsom salt water to cool off a bit.. like Sally does  Today I will get in another 4 hours I am sure.... uh..... it feels so good, so very good.

I really have missed the sun for sure. Almost only cloudy days lately. I am outside anyways and it does me so good but when sun is out it is a different thing

He asked what I do with the heads and he thought it was so funny as I told about my smoothie

### Inger — 2016-04-03T21:17:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-191971>

I and my loved one were in Norway for a family meeting last weekend and it was fun  and it was sooo nice to see my siblings and parents again..... My sister made us reindeer meat for dinner...it was so tasty... and we talked and talked.
In the hotel I got to eat loads of cold smoked salmon for breakfast and other nice things. After we arrived back home spring have finally arrived   Sun is here and finally I can charge my batteries for real

My fiancee took lots of pictures..lol what I always forget to do huh. Here I am eating raw smoked halibut on the airport in Amsterdam... he thought that was fun  The grass is fake  but at least we could sit outside a little as we waited for the next flight...weather was pretty nice with quite some clouds and about 10 degree C

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

and here are me and my mom and sisters, 2 sisters are not there as they could not come
I love them all so much  I really do. I always get a little sad when leaving them. Something in me wants us to stay together forever and not live in different countries far apart....but that is life. Good thing is they are still in my heart always.

So me and my loved one have started a fun testing thing/biohack with blood glucose levels, we measure our blood glucose, blood pressure and temps every so often and write it down to see the differences as he eats pretty different than I, with grains and sugar included, even if I have to say he does not have the worst diet  he loves steaks and an egg with his breakfast
He is shocked at how even my sugar levels are... in comparison to his.
Mine are hovering between 75/90, 5 points up 5 points down including meals, and his are showing differences around 40 points after and before meals.
We also have tested many times, that oxy lowers bloodsugar for both of us! Isn´t that cool    Now my fiancee likes to use oxy for lowering his blood sugar...ha... great for me   and he has started eating more fish too, he even got a little upset one day that I had eaten his smoked salmon that he had bought for him...

I also have noticed, doing the blood glucose test so many times over the day, that I am pretty sure my blood gets brighter red when I am outside tanning for hours....I wonder if it means sunshine oxygenates my blood? So sunshine lowers hypoxia for real.....very cool

I am a little addicted to measuring now....  and testing all kind of stuff.... I one day had half a glass full of raw honey only, for a meal, to test what happens, and my blood sugar went sky high! I got a not so funny anxious feeling...uh! But it dropped very fast... thankfully. I am not going to repeat that hack again...  I just had to test that I am not diabetic

It has been rough to move away from my home I was used to.... but sun helps a lot. Now I need to get oysters again! Have not had them for maybe 2 months now as I left my car for my ex to use until he gets one. So I cant go and pick oysters until then but I think next week I will travel there and pick me a bag because i sure need them!!! Not doing everything so optimal lately as too many thing s are happening  but now as the sun comes out for real and I get more used to live here it gets better and better

### Inger — 2016-04-03T21:52:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-191972>

It is so moving how my partner lit candles after sunset for me..... shut off fake lights.... got me a landline and corded internet... shuts off the wifi.... reads books loud with me in candle light.... got me a rainbarrel in the garden to dip in.... buys me all the seafood I want.....plants things in the garden to give me more privacy.. etc.... it is just amazing... because he did not know anything of this at all and i am so crazy to him....lol we are the complete opposites.... I think as longs as you really love each other it does not matter tho... because we are both learning together... I have a lot to learn too.. a lot! Life is such an interesting journey huh

### endless — 2016-04-04T18:58:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-192023>

Fiancee?? Wow, I have been away too long, I need to get up to speed on your journal I think!

### Inger — 2016-04-06T15:40:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-192170>

well.... my life never get boring even if I so wish it would

Yesterday at work I talked with a chef in the kitchen... she is an older lady, overweight and not healthy looking... She started to tell me about her depressions.. she complained about all her black moods and pains and how much she suffer.... how her husband do not get her at all.. etc..and that she is heavily medicated...... and we talked about it and I told her what she could do and that I believe for sure she can heal/improve
She then was interested even if she said her doctor said she cant heal and have to take medicine all her life
but I said, no she can heal! She just need to change a lot of things and she will start to heal for sure, and I told what.
So then... she said... oh... yes... maybe... but I am so old.. I cant and do not want to change anything... I am happy to live like this
I said, of course, if you are fine with how you live now it is fine

I later thought about it...
and that is how many seem to think... like they are happy about being miserable! How weird is that. They rather suffer than change and start to thrive...
very weird for sure! But then, she has the right to suffer too.
It is kinda weird to think about it tho. It amazes me a bit
weird is, they forget to think how others feel around them, them complaining and suffering like that.... pretty egoistic somehow

### Inger — 2016-04-07T19:19:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-192239>

My fiancee bought some poison fluid to get rid of the green stuff that grows on the terrace stones in our garden.... I said no no I do not want it I can do it naturally, but he said we need that. So he bought it, and then went for a work trip this week and I am alone.. and today I scrubbed the half of the terrace stones with a steel brush and water... all the green stuff disappeared  and I got some nice earthing in too as I was barefoot  wow it felt nice! My hands are a bit swollen from the hard work but I loved it   tomorrow I will take the rest.. he comes home Sunday and it is a surprise  hopefully he will not get angry   but then it is too late

I want no poison on the terrace where I walk barefoot and tan with bare skin all the time......not to think about the nature who suffers....and the fish I eat

and... I bought me a second tan through bikini, this is real hot  leo print and string tanga

### Inger — 2016-04-12T21:25:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-192596>

Getting more and more color now  Sun has been out and I have been many hours tanning, in my tan through....
My fiancee LOVES the Tan Through leo print tanga version!!! He thinks it is totally hot  he has no issues with me walking around with it either...lol he is just crazy about me it seems.... He offered me to buy me another similar one even! How cool is that   feels sooo amazing to have someone around that is so crazy about you... i could not dream of more oxy... I am so lucky    (I have no clue where this guy get all his hormones from but sure he has a lot..)

I have been planting seeds.. and we are digging a pond in the garden  it will be beautiful I think! Love to have some water around  I will plant crinoids in the pond  and we will make a small bridge over it. It is just a very small pond at the tanning terrace but it will look so nice  We have fun in the garden together

I have been ironing, washing windows and doing stuff outside earthed scantily clad all day. Feels like summer is finally here

### Veg — 2016-04-14T06:51:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-192660>

**Whey Pickled Herring**
\=================
Dear Inger, lately I am making Pickled Herring (with whey and very little salt ... maybe 2%)
I would say it tastes way way better than German Matjes.
I even liked it better than Holland's Matjes.
Previously I also tried Herring in Apple cider, but I never personally like apple cider in my life.

Pickling in whey also makes herring preserved for 2 weeks. It is very good while I get a Carton of 6 kg, and I need at least 2 weeks to eat it.

I love herring, but it didn't tast me so good up till now. It is like a revolution for me.

For others, here is the recipe for pickling in whey:

[http://awesomepickle.com/pickled-herring-recipe-how-to-fillet-a-fish/](http://awesomepickle.com/pickled-herring-recipe-how-to-fillet-a-fish/)

It is very easy, very tasty. It takes only 1 day to pickle it and next day you could start eating it. But it starts tasting much much better after keeping it for 3 days in the refrigerator. You can also pickle mackerel or salmon too.

Special thanks to Inger, who inspired me towards herring and mackerel and other things and changed and revolutionized my miserable life too.

We are going to buy a Cabriolet with as little nnEMF as possible for me so I can drive a tan.... cool

My fiancee says he is open to move somewhere into more sunshine and more optimal living with me.... YAY
not right yet but slowly... This is a great place too but it can always be better, and it feels nice to think about that the other is open for such explorations

### Veg — 2016-04-15T13:23:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-192732>

Regarding Whey: Please don't use the finished Whey from superstore. That is heated up and has no living bacteria. Best option is to hang the Joghurt and let the whey drop in the jar.

### lohd2015 — 2016-04-18T22:14:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-192987>

Inger, thank you for sharing so much. You are truly an inspiration (you should have lost count by now how many people have told you that).

I find your culinary creativity most contagious. I wish I can be as brave to enter the world of raw meats, as there aren't many small butchers where I can safely buy raw meats.

However, I did try something new this morning. Instead of eating them separately one after another as I always do, I put together in a bowl:

commercial pickled herring with onions (Vita from Canada, wild caught)
homemade goat milk yogurt
organic heavy cream
some fresh cut organic cilantro and rosemary leaves
dash of organic black pepper.
Wow, I could never imagine the combo to taste SOOOOOO yummy! Thank you, Inger.

Oh.. thank you Lohd...  you are so sweet

I have been eating all kinds of meat raw but my belly can digest stones I guess
I do take care to get good quality meat always when I can tho. But I have been eating raw prepackaged minced meat from supermarket (organic) too and other not so ideal stuff. ### Inger — 2016-04-20T16:52:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193071>

I cut myself real bad today as I opened an oyster.. surprisingly it was filled with mud inside and as I used my bare hands as always the knife slide through fast and cut a deep wound into my palm... like getting crucified

The knife was full of mud that all got into the wound.. I rinsed it with cold water and let it bleed but the bleeding stopped fast...
After I while I decided to put iodine on it, which hurt, uh! I was sitting in the morning sun eating my breakfast as it happened so I just expose the wound to the sun and opened the rest of my oysters more careful this time to not cut me twice!

I have had the wound almost all day outside in sunlight, and I am so happy it does not hurt - does not bleed and I have it just uncovered. I was in the woods and picked me a bag of nettles and greens and ate some on the way to help healing...need extra biophotons... need to up my game with wild greens! They are growing like crazy here now

I am glad my fiancee is on work trip....... or he might not allow me to open oysters anymore.. without protection

It is so exciting to watch how well or not a wound heals... need to get it to heal fast as I have a lot of digging to do in the garden still!
I am going to put some wound healing herbs on it in the night I think.. found some in the woods too

### Inger — 2016-04-20T17:39:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193078>

If you use ground beef and have a dehydrator you can make crazy delicious burgers  raw

Just make patties, rather thin, and dehydrate over night / 12 hours at 35-40 degree C
enjoy for breakfast with coconut oil, butter.. or whatever else you like... they are like sandwiches, really
I like to put coconut oil, some slices of raw onion and cucumber on them  veeery nice

### Inger — 2016-04-20T17:39:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193079>

I do them without any spices or salt but you can for sure spice them up before dehydrating with whatever you wish for

### Inger — 2016-04-21T11:22:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193142>

Well, it depends,
if the meat is very fatty they will get crispier, they are crispy outside and softer inside, they get a bit chewy too, if you dry them for more trhan 12 hours they get chewier and chewier
I like them just over night, slightly warm out of the dehydrator, chewy/crispy outside and softer inside... totally yummy
Have not had them in quite a while, now I want to make them again as we talk about it
They are SUPER for breakfast as they need no time for preparation, you just eat them right away, whit whatever you want on the top, or just plain like that  Something for lazy folks like me

\- - - -

The wound have healed so fast I cant believe it! It do not hurt at all, and yesterday I cleaned the whoile house, washed my hair... took a bath... ironed... did all kind of stuff... without any protection at all.... I just was a bit more careful to not get anythging hard hit on the wound. I did not even put on any herbs because I did not feel like it needed it.... no pain in the night... everything just awesome!
Is it the sun? The oysters? The grounding? Magic
And I was scared it would get infected...lol
I do not have taken any tetanus shot since adulthood either.. so I am always a little tiny bit worried when I get deep dirty wounds like that... but seems I was worrying without reason

My fiancee came home yesterday and he brought me a nice pink summer dress from his trip in Spain  so cute
He told me he would consider living in Spain... he likes it there  We will go there one day together so I get a feeling about it too, I never was there yet!

### lohd2015 — 2016-04-21T17:51:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193169>

The dehydrated burger preparations really sounds delicious! I love crispy outside, soft inside--anything.
Thank you Inger, for the detailed explanations and tips on prep time and recipes. It will save me plenty of time in having to do 'trial by error'. XOXOXO.

I don't think you can call yourself 'lazy'. Your meals are always presented with such style and artistry. That takes time and effort.
It probably is a time management issue. These days, in order to spend more time on my studies, I have to cut back on spending time on things that are less 'important', such as meal preparation. Surprisingly, the dopamine increase is helping me do more in same amount of time and with less stress. It is really miraculous.
(I hope my hubby will like the dehyrdated burgers. I know I will love them)

It is amazing and such a relieve to hear that your wound healed so fast and well. Congratulations! I believe it is a sign that your body is happy and functioning optimally.

Do show us the new pink dress. I am sure it looks lovely on you. I have never been to Spain either. You can tell us how you like it when you go there with your Fiancee.

### Veg — 2016-04-23T18:32:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193312>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=193064)
>
> Veg, do you use regular pasteurized yoghurt? Or do you get raw one or make yourself from raw milk?

When I get hands to Herrings, I don't find any raw milk.
And when I get hands to raw milk, I don't find Herrings.

Therefore, I make whey out of commercial Joghurts (1.5% Fat). And it did work for me.

My consumption of Fish has gone more than double since I started fermenting them. Really they become so much delicious. Especially after they are lying in refrigerator for 3-4 days.

Moreover, time for Holland's Matjes is coming soon. I seriously recommend you not to miss it. You will forget German Matjes. Hollanders make it with only 2% salt and 0.5 mm [pancreas](http://www.dict.cc/englisch-deutsch/pancreas.html) of herring.
I have already asked this question to me more than 1000 times, why Germans don't make Herings the way Hollanders make it.

Nothing can beat it.

### Veg — 2016-04-24T08:10:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193344>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=193336)
>
> That looks extremely tasty!

How tasty it is?
You can imagine it by this that Hollanders have a special Celebration every year for this Matjes Herring. Every one waits for it eagerly every year. It starts in May and ends in the first week of July (at that time FAT content of Herring is about 16% to 22%, with a lot of Omega 3).

Taste aside, it is a Super Food when it comes to the health benefits.
Perhaps Inger's few other specialities like raw lamb testicles or raw organs (heart/liver) could beat it in health benefits, otherwise none could match this Matjes Herring.

### Inger — 2016-04-24T11:50:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193348>

Oh.... I LOVE those matjes/fermented herrings from Holland   I buy them occasionally, and you are so right about that they is totally different than German matjes!!!
I have wanted to make them myself but I am not sure how to find the pancreas organ......and they need to stay whole I have read, during the fermentation process..so you have to take the guts out of the head somehow

I get fresh herrings here all the time. Now this week I got mackerel too, I ordered 10 mackerels for next week .

### Alper — 2016-04-24T11:56:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193351>

>

Inger, where do you get your fish? You live close to Lüneburg, don´t you? In Hannover its quite hard to get fresh/good quality fish.

### Inger — 2016-04-24T12:11:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193353>

I go to the fishmarket, they are those wagons that drive around in different places different days of the week. We have one in this small town every thursday. Then we have another wagon about 10 km away every tuesday and saturday... and other towns around have them other days so practically I can get to buy fresh fish every day of the week if I dare to drive about 30 minutes
But I usually buy my fish 1 to 2 days / week as it lasts for days when I prepare it with raw ACV. The heads I freeze in small bags and use as a smoothie once in a while, when I run out of fresh fish

You need to ask around when the fish wagon arrives to your town

### Alper — 2016-04-24T12:14:53+0300

apple cider vinegar
I get a raw unfiltered version here at the "Biomarkt"

### Inger — 2016-04-25T17:56:14+0300

We have been eating that with my fiancee lately, as he loves it, and now i am addicted! He eats it with German cold smoked ham like they use to do here, and I love raw salmon or raw meat with it
It is aspargus time here now and you get fresh local aspargus everywhere (mostly the white version, today I had green), very epi paelo

### Veg — 2016-04-25T22:27:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193470>

> [Alper said:](/goto/post?id=193345)
>
> That Herring looks really really good. Are you from Holland, Veg?

No Alper, I am not from Holland. But I am in love of Holland since I have started eating Holland's Matjes. LOL.
Actually, I went for my last Holidays to Holland in order to eat Matjes.
Holland is super duper green (I think there are also "Salzwiesen" (salt fields) in Holland, which Inger mentioned earlier). Every where cows and sheep. I was able to get hands to RAW milk in the camping place where I stayed.

### Veg — 2016-04-25T22:42:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193473>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=193348)
>
> Oh.... I LOVE those matjes/fermented herrings from Holland   I buy them occasionally, and you are so right about that they is totally different than German matjes!!!
> I have wanted to make them myself but I am not sure how to find the pancreas organ......and they need to stay whole I have read, during the fermentation process..so you have to take the guts out of the head somehow
>
> I get fresh herrings here all the time. Now this week I got mackerel too, I ordered 10 mackerels for next week .

Inger, you would not believe it, but I had also eaten Holland's Matjes in Germany earlier. But Holland's Matjes in Holland tastes still different than Holland's Matjes in Germany. And I don't know the reason. One fish monger was of opinion that the difference lies in the guidelines that the two governments set. German government makes it obligatory to use a lot more salt for even Hollandische Matjes.

In simple words, you should be in Holland to taste the real Matjes.

Secondly, Holland's Matjes are not fermented (to describe it in strict sense), but they are split and made tender through pancreas juice.

I was stunned as I was watching how Hollanders fillet the herring. I wished I also got such filleting skills. I need 20 times more time in order to fillet one fish.

### Inger — 2016-04-26T13:20:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193525>

Cool
I have been to Holland once, and tasted their fresh matjes  awesome!
But you can get original matjes from Holland here too. Not the "Holländische Matjes" the Germans make.. they are crap..lol
The important thing is that they are made with pancreatis juice
but you are right about that most of the matjes you get here are the fake ones......
I have gotten real ones at the fish market but only certain times of the year. Then there are different quality, the "young Matjes" are the best ones, the most tender ones. They are very fresh, and I think you get them in fall? Not sure.. at least they are only a few months of the year
But you might be right about that they use more salt for the ones exported to Germany, because they need to last longer?

I think they can fillet them so fast because they are already tendered through the pancreatis juice
But anyways awesome work
Imagine working as a matjes filleter....lol You could munch on herring every so often! That would be a fun work!

I just try to avoid dairy but once in a while I buy me some raw stuff, it is sooo delicious

### Inger — 2016-04-28T12:19:32+0300

I know.... dreams can be so weird! Maybe you have a secret dream of being a woman....

Only raining here last days...uh. I had to hit the tanning bed today to get some UV! And on the way I got me 10 nice mackerels from the fish market
I wonder if one can ferment mackerels with whey too?
I am thinking about making raw cheese from the raw milk I can buy here.. and then let the whey stay out on the counter and get a bit sour.. and use that.... wonder if it would work?

### Veg — 2016-04-28T13:17:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193750>

Yes Inger, whey also ferments beautifully Mackerel and even Wild Salmon and all other fat fishes too.
And I am sure that whey out of raw milk is better option for fermenting as compared to pasteurized milk.

I have found out that fish should be very fresh for the good results. Also somehow larger pieces of herring also provide better results for me.
And I bought 3 times herring from Selgros-FEGRO, but they always gave very bad results. I don't know where they catch their fish. Best results I got from herrings from Norway VIKOMAR company.

### Inger — 2016-04-28T13:20:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193751>

Veg,
is your herring always fresh, or prefrozen? Do you buy it at the weekly fish market?
I know prefrozen animal foods do not ferment well.. it gets weird.
Or..
Could they use something to make it keep longer, that affects the fermentation process?

### Veg — 2016-04-28T17:48:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-193762>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=193751)
>
> Veg,
> is your herring always fresh, or prefrozen? Do you buy it at the weekly fish market?
> I know prefrozen animal foods do not ferment well.. it gets weird.
> Or..
> Could they use something to make it keep longer, that affects the fermentation process?

(1) I used fresh herring from FEGRO. Results were very bad.
(2) I used fresh herring from "Deutsche SEE". Results were good.
(3) I used prefrozen herring from Norway. Results were excellent. I got real tasty fish. The size of fish was large.
(4) Fishmongers in my city have fresh Mackerel. I fermented mackerel too with very good results.

I don't think prefrozen fish makes any difference, while main fermenting agent is whey.
If we do salt fermentation, then prefrozen fish or prefrozen cabbage etc. are not acceptable while they don't have any living bacterias. But I don't think that it makes a difference in case of whey fermentation, while whey has itself living bacterias.

However, I am not an expert in fermentation. So please do experiments yourself and inform us too from the results. Thanks.

### Inger — 2016-05-18T12:18:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-195006>

We had an amazing vacation in Nice... it was just 5 days.... but wow what an experience

We stayed at my good friends place, she lives with her daughter right by the most beautiful, stony beach in Nice
We got tons of sun.... and we had the most amazing food in a restaurant... oysters and sea snails and crevettes...... my fiancee tried oysters for the first time in his life and he loved them!!!
Now comes the funny thing...lol He did not even had that many, maybe 10 all together during the trip..... but that.. and the strong beautiful sun and the sea worked like crazy hormone booster for him... he got shocked....
He has lots of testosterone anyways... and now he got so pumped up he said no more oysters for me... otherwise he would make me break...    it was crazy!
Nice has some magic energy there...... even if I slept in 5 wifi signals.... and had way too much cheese... and wine! Way too late... My nose did not got clogged at all as it does from dairy... and I slept so well.... what I do not do here if I eat and drink wine too late.....
It must have been the strong energy from the sun and the mediterranean sea

My fiancee wants to move there now...... he got addicted!!! So we are starting to make plans already..... I am so happy
If we could find a cheap place somewhere outside the towns on the mediterranean coast in France,.... it would be heaven.... we are thinking about starting some kind of business there.........
Oysters are cheap there too... and they have the most amazing fish markets!!! Uh... it is a nirvana...... I love it here too... but there is so much healing energy I would move in a heart beat.......

I need to post some pictures from our vacation when I get some time....

I did another experiment with making my own crystal sauce.. as I love it, and it is very expensive here. I fermented some pepperoni peppers with whey and salt and water.. and then mixed them as they where soft, into a smooth sauce... it got amazing! Just like crystal sauce
I need to make more of it, because my fiancee loved it too

### thomas — 2016-05-18T15:05:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-195017>

Funny, even if I am attached to brittany where I spent my whole life, I would be a fool to not admit provence is better for me, I am certainly happier there, everything is more alive. I certainly agree it's one of the best place in europe for us Krusians and outdoorsers, like if you try it you get hooked. Funny I got potential plans to get a room in a villa in antibes as well so maybe we'll meet(plus i got family there). I always want to do so many things at the same time raghh!
potential downside is vacation season in july/august, it can get crowded. If you can find a place with less density(and the coast has a lot of density, but a little away from it and there's much less), i bet it'd be even better.

btw, Do you know that Jeane calment (122 yo) is from arles? not that surprising!

Oh.. I did not know Calmet lived in Arles! wow... one more reason to move to the French mediterranean coast

some pics from Nice...

and in the city where they had awesome oysters and sea snails and shrimps...yum... my fiancee tested his first oysters here, and he loved them   .)

I did stone therapy  to my friends daughter (and she did to me) that spent lots of time with us.. she is such a sweetie...

at Nice airport... going home... need to be back soon....

### Inger — 2016-05-21T11:23:38+0300

Tightening up my diet a bit... have been a bit too loose the last months! I am going way higher raw... always makes me feel so great and vibrant  And wine has to be way limited..lol 1 glass or two at daytime a few times / week is fine... but in the evening is a no go... I am not it Nice now....
I did the whey fermented herrings yesterday.. and going to try them for dinner, I am so exited! I made the whey myself from raw organic milk I get from the farm right here at the neighbors.

### Inger — 2016-05-24T10:45:34+0300

### Inger — 2016-05-30T18:18:31+0300

### Inger — 2016-06-02T08:12:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-195853>

I did a nice "hack" yesterday
As I could not go to the beach, have no car yet... I decided to "bring the beach" here. So I spent hours outside in the sun (sans clothes) and made me a bowl with epsom salt and water. I splashed my body with it all the time...and it mixed a bit with the fine sand that I had put between the terrace stones sometime ago.. so it was kinda like a peeling... I used ice gelpacks too, iced my carotids.. my face/eyes.. belly.. neck... After doing this for hours the rain come and I went inside. I noticed my skin had become so smooth... like silk! And I got that deep relaxed feeling I get when I am at the sea..! Wow.. I have never had this silky skin
I need to repeat this for sure!

This is how I grew up... I am the one sitting left downstairs from my mum
we spent all summer long outside barefoot with little clothes... we were so lucky
we were pretty poor but had lots of nature!
Thank you mom

### Queen of Elves — 2016-06-03T17:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-195936>

Your food always looks amazing! How do you dehydrate beef, with a dehydrator or in the oven?

### Inger — 2016-06-03T17:55:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-195942>

Thanks  I do love my food   so tasty and juicy

I use a dehydrator for my meat/heart and make sure to not rise the temp above body temp. That way the enzymes and bacteria will still remain, at least part of them

I now dehydrate because we just have a pretty small fridge and my big beef hearts and meats are not fitting too well in there...lol but I eat most of it fresh tho, I think that is the most healthy. But dehydrating is very practical tho  and tasty too

### Inger — 2016-06-08T08:12:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-196133>

I could not stay by my fiance anymore because..... it was too much Jekyll/Hyde
I would have wanted to continue the relationship both in our own homes so we could both heal because I still love him so dearly.. I would have wanted to work out the issues as we would have more space now but.... he seem to not accept that... so what can I do. I just cant live in a place where I cant relax. It hurts like hell but I know the pain will go away one day.
I feel hurt but simultaneously I feel very happy and relieved too. I did feel a little unsure all the time.... in my belly.... like something was not 100% right... but I wanted to give it a chance.... It hurt me so much to see his broken family in the end....it came to the surface more and more. my heart just aches when I think about it... But there is not much I can do, except offer my help if they want it. But I cant let myself imprison because of it. I get destroyed.
It does feel like a failure tho. Like I failed badly... that everything would have been different if I would have done better... But I need to look forward and not back, and live here and now and not dwell in the past...

I just have this instinct that warns me I cant sacrifice myself for someone else in a way that destroys me... who can I be good for/help if I am losing myself, my joy, my peace.. my passion?

At least his diabetic daughter have now gone low carb and she has already noticed her bloodsugar is way better and stable, she is getting real benefits, her HbA1c values never was this good, big improvements!   I told her about the sun too how much she needs it, I would have loved to help more as she was very open but maybe I sparked a light in her and gave her hope there is things she can do to improve.

Gosh... so many impressions.... so much to ponder... so much to learn...... I do am thankful for all I have learned through this, every experience is worthy and helps me grow. Also the painful ones.
I do think I need to listen to my belly more. My belly is very sensible somehow.. weird!

### caroline — 2016-06-08T09:02:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-196137>

relationships take a huge amount of love and a huge amount of kindness and gentleness and forgiveness every single day.....

both have to come to the party tho ....or it is no bueno....

We can not sacrifice our life for someone else ..... we now know way better than that.

We need to be whole to be happy and healthy and love unconditionally .... and, I Suppose, selfish.

xoxo

### Inger — 2016-06-08T09:52:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-196139>

Caroline.... guess what..... it seemed to be the right move after all (of course I know it was the only right thing to do...)
because mister just called.. after days of silence..and now he wants me to help them... and if someone needs my help I will always give... what I can! But only in a way where I do not break. My freedom no one can take away... ever.
So we will meet probably on sunday and talk  This feels so good! If I could just help one person to a better, more fulfilled and peaceful joyful life it was so worth it    But the will and need for it has to come from themselves. Pushing never works.

I am not going to move anywhere, I will stay here in my little home with its horse barn...  with the peace, freedom.. and all possibilities to evolve.. travel.. visit friends.. create.. to follow my passion   I have so much passion and I no way will let anyone suppress it

Thank you Caroline for being here.... you have such a beautiful heart

### Inger — 2016-06-20T12:25:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-196941>

Something funny just happened...lol

I sat on my terrace with a mirror and picked some tiny blackheads I could find on my cheek.... just something I do once in a while... and it is great to do outside in strong light  sorry this is a little weird...lol
then I heard, all of a sudden, a voice right by me. and there was my landlord! 2 meters away... looking at me and he said, beautiful Lady? I said OMG why are you looking at me picking on my face! It was sooo embarrassing...lol I thought I was completely alone.....
and he says... well, I thought you lost something on the corridor in the horse barn.. you might want to check it out.....
So there I went.... and in the middle of the corridor was my.... my pink flowered undies
I had lost them on my way today morning........ gosh... a little funny tho but uh oh pretty embarrassing....lol

I got a HUGE herpes on my lip after moving back home.. and on my back all of a sudden was lots of tiny bumps..... and I felt a little sick for a day or two....
Maybe it was all from stress... or some detox... I have been eating so many raw oysters last days, and many raw lambs balls too! Power food...lol But now the bumps are pretty gone... my herpes are healing just fine.. and I feel just great   real great
The body sometimes does funny stuff!

I wonder if thin skin is a good thing or if it should be thick. I have pretty thin skin I have realized... when I compare to people around me I have tested on..I can pick my skin easily up with my fingers and drag and it is very elastic! It is pretty funny in fact! I chose to believe it is good with a thin skin as maybe the sun penetrates it better?
Or maybe I am just getting old and that is why my skin is so thin...lol

### Inger — 2016-06-28T20:31:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-197450>

So I have been measuring a lot last days, with my new cornet. Very interesting. The worst thing I experienced was a building in a small sweet town, the name of the building was "Gesundheitscentrum" and it means "the center of Health".. a hospital. And just a few feet away from it was a huge cellphone tower.. and as we drove past the building my meter showed like way more than 400 milliwatt RF. It was so unreal.... how can this be allowed?????? My meter went crazy and beeped and it was all red.....

I realized there are a lot of towers I could not see but my meter went real high, like way more than 400 milliwatt RF (=400 000 microwatt) and even more... a few times it showed 0 (zero) because it could not go higher. And it was on places like small towns with lots of nature around... scary!!! Who would have known!!!

**We all need a meter these days, I would say it is a MUST**. It cost less than 200 bucks and it has huge benefits so we can know _what we cant see and feel_.
I have around 45 microwatt in my home, that is 0,045 milliwatt (RF). Some places even lower. But I cant go under 0,030 microwatt. I put many layers of tinfoil around the meter but it still measured around 0,030 microwatt so I guess it must be somehow calibrated that way?

The railwaystation/train was real bad... magnetic radiation was up to 800 milligauss!!! But it varied a lot. I also got low readings like 2-5 milligauss. But RF reading was too mostly very bad. It seemed like the train acted like an antenna?? Because I drove the car about the same area and it is pretty remote, just cellphone towers once in a while... lots of green acres and woods...pastures.. and in the train the readings were way worse! Of course if someone was close with their iphones calling etc. levels skyrocketed too.

I realized, calling on the cellphone in the car with bluetooth gives crazy high values. Also 400 milliwatt RF and more... to ranges even the meter cant measure anymore. Imagine you drive and call a lot, what many business people do.... scary!

As I come home from my trip, being electrocuted for hours.... I immediately got my tan through on and went out in the sun on my terrace, eating plenty of raw oysters and drinking spring water.. earthed.. boah.. it felt so good.. got to repair all the damage

I am so glad I have no smartphone and no bluetooth and stuff like that..huh!
And I will prefer to drive with my old Hyundai instead of taking the train next time! Was pretty shocking to see the levels to be honest.

### Inger — 2016-11-07T10:06:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-203682>

Time for an update!

Last months have been crazy stressful... including a lot of work and other crazy things happening! But now finally is time for recharging and healing    When times are hard it is amazing how much one can survive tho. I did get sick once with flu and had to take one day off...but that is what happens when you overwork...  no surprise..

We had a party at work as the season ended and my boss turned 40.. it was a lot of fun and dancing included.. and wine

I have been eating so many oysters as I have been so out of money and they are free food for me, what sure have helped me to survive the stress. Had to take a picture of the beauty and the beast...

In between all the stress I had a short vacation to Nice, just a few days but it was so wonderful to get some sun and beach time!

Seeing forward to a winter in the dark, cold, and getting some rest. Finally having time to catch up here and reading books and writing  I am working just 2 days/week in the winter months as that is all I need to survive. I have become an expert in living cheap it seems, oysters and lamb hearts are what makes me spend almost no money on food...  There are so much I just do not need, I have realized! Less money spent means more time to connect with nature and friends.  Things that really count.
I am cleaning up my diet too. I have had way too much HWC at work and coffee...
I now weirdly do not want coffee in the mornings at all, since a month or so. I just drink green tea.. and have coffee later. I wonder why this is. It just feels so nice and relaxing to start the mornings with tea instead. It is funny, as I always liked my morning coffee so much! All of a sudden, no more....

We have had some nice and sunny days here last weeks, where I could tan in my tan through for hours when I had a day off....the sun was so warm and nice! Now it is turning colder and has been rainy. Winter is arriving. But I like it too. Somehow it feels good to turn into winter/hibernation-modus

### Inger — 2016-11-08T18:13:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-203723>

I came up with a new way to use the oyster shells I get from shucking my own oysters...
works great

Went for a walk in the woods and took a short swim in the little lake there... a short one.. because it was so cold, uh! In the night we had freezing temps so my sun-water on the terrace was icy on the top.
But it did so good.......   Cold sure is magic   now I finally have time to walk more in the woods and take more of those lake-swims

### Inger — 2016-11-21T17:03:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-204152>

So I am desperately househunting now. We want to rent only, before we buy anything, because we dont know where we go soon....maybe to the sun ... but I have issues to find a good place to live, health wise. We just have too many wind power parks here and they are not good for health at all.

So I found, finally. a very silent place away from civilisation... have an appointment to watch the house tomorrow... and now.. just a very short way away I saw THIS.. on google maps!  This is a military base and they are antennas for listening to funk signals... Now I wonder is this bad for health? Are they only receiving or are they sending too? I do not like to live near a military base at all. UH. You cant see it from the house but it close.. maybe a km or two or even closer.
It is very close to Danish border. But otherwise ideal, very few people and close to the sea.
But this military base makes me nervous. I wonder if my nnEMF meters will catch anything. I always measure any place I check out.

I found out there are pretty many military bases up here close to Danish border and the both seas. Might be a bad thing to stay here for long. I practically lost my horse barn too to sleep in now as new owners moved in here with horses... and I have less privacy...to tan nude.. I think they have more nnEMF equipment too as I wake up often too early and something feels off. Maybe it is only stress from too many things happening lately, but I think it might have to do with the new owners. They are very kind tho, and I am sure they will be very sad when I move but I just feel I cant stay here for much longer. I feel stressed and I feel I need to move away somewhere.... Life can change all of a sudden......

### Danny — 2016-11-24T14:49:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-204283>

Hi Inger, I'm not exactly sure what these are. Are you friends w/ George Parker on the EHS facebook group? He would probably know. Our measuring equipment doesn't pick up everything. For instance, most RF meters will not pick up Hamm radio signals. I think the extreme low frequency waves need more sensitive equipment. As we know, ELF is very disrupting too. Good luck.

### Inger — 2016-11-29T18:18:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-204492>

Well I do not have too many rules with mixing foods and my belly can digest almost.. anything
I do not eat much coconut oil at all. Once in a while tho, I mix myself a coconut oil-raw cacao-honey-eggyolk-threat  and I eat it as dessert, no matter what I had before. Oysters, steak, raw meat, lambhearts....raw salmon....

When I eat raw fish I mostly make a chevice with raw onion, white balsamic vinegar or ACV... and as fat I use olive oil or maybe avocado, if the fish is not fatty. Because I like it and need some O6 too I guess!

I often eat oysters as a starter and then a steak or something else like raw salmon or carpaccio. Because I need too many oysters for long time satiety.

I do often eat a breakfast with oysters only before I go to work. Maybe 10-20 oysters. They are very energizing and makes me feel great and light   after about 4-5 hours I have dinner at work and I do fine until then, no hunger pangs.

When I eat mackerel I usually do not eat any other fat...lol... it is so fatty i get very nicely satiated from the mackerel fat only. Same with salmon.
But if I do mix with other fats, I feel fine too.

### Inger — 2016-11-29T18:26:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-204494>

We did not rent that house by the military base. I just feel not good about it

So... today I did something very cool!
I made my own tanning salon
It was +2 degree C and sunny at 10:30 AM and I went outside after dipping in the rain barrel, had to take away the ice on the top before dipping in it.
I rewarmed nude in the sun and it was so nice! I then sat down to tan more on my lambs skin... and then come to think about making the sun stronger with mirrors! So I brought 2 big mirrors from inside outside and arranged them on my right and left side so that I had **3 suns**
it got so hot and nice from the strong sunlight! Just awesome

Why have I not tried out this before? The sun got much much stronger and warmer  I tanned for 2 hours and then clouds covered the sky.. and I went for walk in the woods..in the soft rain. But I felt so amazing! Like very light and full of nice energy  . I have not felt this good in weeks/months. Was it the stronger sun? I have no clue.. but good it felt

Next sunny day I will repeat my experiment and see how I feel then

### Rubicon — 2016-11-29T20:27:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-204500>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=204494)
>
> ... and then come to think about making the sun stronger with mirrors! So I brought 2 big mirrors from inside outside and arranged them on my right and left side so that I had **3 suns**
> it got so hot and nice from the strong sunlight! Just awesome

[I tried this last spring](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/page-2#post-191007). Although using mirrors is great for warmth, my skin didn't feel great after a few rounds. Something seemed off.

After learning that mirrors don't reflect UV, I thought maybe the problem was that the reflected spectrum from a mirror is not ideal. Aluminum is a better reflector, so for now I've opted to cover a big board with aluminum foil and use that.

Multiple suns is nice

I will try the aluminum for sure, thank you for sharing

I am now doing IR whole body treatment in front of the fireplace....on a lamb skin.. earthing my bare legs and arms on the stone floor
Winter have some magic in it

### Rubicon — 2016-11-29T22:09:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-204507>

I felt a difference with the aluminum instead of the mirror. It feels more like regular sun exposure. I ended up using the aluminum reflector all through the Canadian summer without any issues.

Aluminum foil is not quite as warm as the mirror. My guess is that it's because the relatively crinkled surface scatters the sunlight a bit. \`

>

### Inger — 2016-12-02T18:54:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-204645>

> [Veg said:](/goto/post?id=204550)
>
> Perhaps the last question, what about the Animal Fat (suet etc.)?
>
> And is this Steak and Carpaccio from 100% grass fed lamb?

well, I try to get grassfed beef, I order 3-5 kg chunks that I have on a wooden plate in my fridge and often eat it raw like carpaccio and also I do steaks "rare" when I feel like it. I eat lots of lamb hearts I buy from the local butcher, the lambs are on the pasture but no idea if they get fed additional food. They taste great to me .

For fat I buy grassfed beef fat in chunks too that I eat raw and also fry gently with my steak if I make me one. I also use butter when I have no beef fat. Sometimes raw butter when I get it, otherwise Kerrygold or organic butter.

I always try to get the best quality... if I cant, I am not nervous either... as I know the most important thing after all is.... light   so outside I go.. even more then  and I feel great

Right now I pretty much live off alaskan pollack, prefrozen... fresh mussels that I cook, and raw oysters picked by me.. I am so out of money...lol and these are my cheapest foods

### Inger — 2016-12-02T19:05:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-204646>

Today was an AWESOME day
blue sky, no clouds... +1 degree C

I went oustide for tanning in my tan through bikini (landlord was in the backyard so could not tan nude..lol), tanned for close to 4 hours... with the aluminium foil thing!
THAT was awsome! I had plates covered with the foil to my right, to my left, and the wall behind me too! I got tanned from all over except my butt...lol as I sat on it
and it got so hot   it was nice... so nice

another benefit on top of it was, I measured the RF radiation on my meter as I sat there in my "tanning salon".. and the numbers had dropped 40%!!!! It was acting as a faraday cage! Very cool  in the direction of the sun I have no towers, wifis or anything like that.
I got so happy seeing the readings

I tanned... and had lots of sunwater as I got so thirsty in the hot sun...

Then the landlords 4 yo daughter come to play with me and wated to watch me dip in my rainbarrel..lol she wanted to go in there too   but her mom said no  she wants to come into my home and terrace all the time to play with me...lol I have a new friend for sure
Children are just so cute  like a piece of heaven

Now I have filled up on enough energy to go through two hard long days of work this weekend

### Veg — 2016-12-03T13:52:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-204703>

Thanks Inger. You are living really cheap with pre frozen Pollock and lamb hearts (but not sure how cheap is grass fed Beef and fat and raw butter).

**An Idea**:
We are living in big City and difficult to move from here. But planning to have a little Garden (kleingarten) which is close to the city and has very good connection to Train Station (with electricity and water). Only negative thing is this that there is a Train Line some 400 meters away. Garden is super green and super cheap (around 250 Euros per year inclusive a beautiful wooden hut there). No problem in going to work either due to good train connection.

I would suggest if it is difficult for people to move out of city (due to work), then they should consider having such small garden. We can stay there even in Winters due to the presence of electricity and water.

But I am sure if you rent such garden, then you will be willing to have it without electricity. Mostly these gardens are away from different kind of Radiations too and provide healthy atmosphere (at least there is no Hot Spot in the garden that we intend to have). Perhaps in emergency, you could also consider renting such garden. You could keep your registration at your friend's house.

### Inger — 2017-01-10T17:39:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-206515>

Thanks Veg! That is a nice idea  Just be careful to test the kleingarden with a meter... if it is in a city as it often is in Germany... it might not be too good.. 400 m away from the rails might not be too bad. But you need to test! Do you have a meter? Might be antennas close that you cant see... I love my meter, seriously it feels like an additional eye to me

I am careful to not obsess too much about food as I have seen people doing that and they get sicker and sicker... . I am the crazy one eating raw lambs brains and high meat and lamb testicle smoothie..(says my coworkers and boss..lol) But then I am also free for thinking that food is the only savior.. and I can eat less optimal and still feel fine for a while. Nature is what is the most important thing. Light! I have a love-relationship with light! Uh it is crazy but true....

I am so lucky to be able to earth all the time at home as my living room floor is stones that earth me. I measured it. So lucky . Everything is not perfect here but I am using what I have . We are now looking for a house to rent where is no wifi and towers and other stuff around. It is a bit hard because I do not care if it is an old house..just need to be healthy.. and he needs a nice new house! So to get it all to fit can take some time..lol Until then I will enjoy the beauty I have here  my magic wood etc.

I was walking in the woods the other day and it was gray and windy and cold.. and all I could think of was how magic it all is.. how perfect the nature is... When it is dark and no sun, we get the cold.. the cold icy wind makes me feel so good! Uh! I would never survive the dark winter without the cold I think! So it is the perfect match  Cold and dark . In summer it is warm... but we have the sun! So it is perfect match too... and the leaves are there to gives us shade when we need.. and in winter there are no leaves because we need all the light we can get, and no leaves means more sun . Well... it is such a perfect design it just amazes me!

### Inger — 2017-01-13T12:46:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-206758>

Well, Veg, not thinking that is gonna happen, but it would be fun for sure!   Love the thought... feeding baby with oysters and breastfed as long as possible.. keeping it close to my body all the time.....
But even if I do not have own kids, I have many more kids   the kids in my surroundings...gives me so much love  makes me feel that I do not miss out of anything

I am doing some nnEMF biohacking these days... pretty interesting!

I have been covering 2 of the walls in my bedroom with double auminium foil. The walls that face neighbors wifi signals. Got them reduced more than half strong, awesome!
I am aware of the issue making a not 100% faraday cage and the radiation accumulating inside the covered walls, so I measure all the time with my RF meter. It seems this was successful, as the signal is pretty much only coming from north and west. The walls I covered. I did not cover the roof because it is complictaed because of how the roof is build... and I suppose the neighbor on the north side have a extra wifi spot on second floor. The main router is downstairs tho, on the other side of my bedroom wall, I have seen it as I visited them  (that is why neighbor visits are good..lol)
I cant see much of an difference on my meter tho, which is mysterious. A little tiny bit reduced tho. But what is very mysterious to me is this;

I measure all day several times from early in bed to before I go to sleep, in between 7 AM and 10 PM. And there is a very clear pattern.
Mornings I get readings between 3 and 9 mycrogauss/m2
then it gradually increases, increase start around 9-10 AM.. and culminates in the evening where readings are the highest, between 19 and 42 mycrogauss/m2

I cant understand this! Neighbors do NOT switch off wifi in the night, I doubt they switch off anything, except lights  I have controlled for the wifi signals on my laptop many times. Always on.
Where on earth does this increase comes from???

No wonder I enjoy mornings so much....

Anyone have an idea?
