### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-14T16:09:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-206815>

It should give different readings at different times because electric and magnetic fields change as night and daylight changes. In fact as the January webinar tells you Vitamin A is a bio-molecule that is how the brain measures electric and magnetic fields. This is why all opsin are bound to Vitamin. It tells the opsin something powerful about light.

**The sun’s light is a cathode ray. When it hits the Earth in daytime, the Earth acts as an anode in this cosmologic circuit and this allows for more evaporation of water on the planet's surface to create more protons to dissipate in the atmosphere. Simultaneously the evaporaation effect will liberate more electrons from Earth that our tensegrity system connected to the Earth via our limbs is designed to collect. We collect and harvest this energy to store or use to do physiologic work to lower the resistance of our inner mitochondrial membrane. This reduces the electrical resistance of mitochondria and this helps stimulate autophagy and not apoptosis. During daytime the electric field of Earth is higher than it is at night when light is absent. Magnetic fields, however, are higher at night, and this is likely why sleep is linked to a loss of the DC electric current in diurnal animals.**

### Inger — 2017-04-03T13:49:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-211644>

I just spent 6 days in Cannes with the boyfriend. It was amazing place to be! Even nicer as Nice!
It is a smaller city, and it has 2 wonderful islands outside of it, 15 minutes with a boat to go there.
My favorite was the monk island, Saint Honorat.... There is a monastery, and wine yards.. they make their own, organic wines. We bought a bottle to share. It was awesome! It is a tiny tiny island. No cars. Only 1 restaurant.

I will post some pics when I get them. I found a small laguna at the monk island... it was my favorite memory from the trip. I want to go there again and just sleep on the beach.. no hotels. I do not need any comfort like that at all. I just need the nature and the sea and the sun!
It is the pre season there and not many tourists yet, which made it all even nicer

I realized I could eat ice cream and get no stuffed nose at all!
I even tried home made tarte de pomme in a small local restaurant - no issues at all... They had the best fish soup I have ever had!
Here I need to be very strict with my foods too, to feel my best. What a difference the sun and the environment makes! One day I will move to the mediterranean. I feel so good there. The people are so pretty there. There was a couple of old men and women who met at the beach every day, totally tanned, deep brown color.. and they went for a 40 minute swim in the ocean, water was 14 degree C. They seemed to enjoy life to 100%

Our hotel had a nice pool and garden.....but it had horrible readings with my RF meter! Up to 15 milli(!)watt/m2  every corner of the garden was totally radiated! On the hotel roof top was an antenna... hidden into a tree on the roof....
Our room had around 200-600 mikrowatt/m2... way much more than I have here (I have around 25 mikrowatt/m2 in my home and outside) our balcony even more. Thankfully we did not spend much time in the hotel room. Still, I slept pretty well! Must have been the sun   I really got a nice recharge

Using some on my skin too... thanks to Doris   Lets see where it takes me  I know it can be risky but I always have to test some stuff

### Inger — 2017-07-18T13:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-219245>

Time for an update!

I have had such nice results with the Iodine protocol!
Taking it for about 4 months now.... orally and also painting on my breasts and skin otherwhere...
After a few weeks painting my breasts, they swelled up and got sore.. and it lasted for a few weeks and then they got small again.. and then I realized the cyst I have had on my right breasts had totally disappeared! I was there since my teens, and it then often hurt.. and made me so scared.. It got better as I got healthier and it did not bothered me anymore at all... but it was still a tiny remnant there. Now.. it is gone! I cant believe it! I really needed high doses of Iodine to it completely disappear

Also, I have noticed some gray/white hair on the top of my head since a few years... you cant see it well as I am pretty blonde but now... the white hairs are getting less!

On the German Iodine facebook Gropu more than one has noticed their gray hair totally reverse since Iodine, I would love if that happens to me too  But they are getting less!!! yay

Also my skin is real nice now, I use Ghee mixed with 2 drops of Iodine on my face, I love it! I used to have this little bit of dry skin on my nose often, that is gone now

Of course I do loads of oysters, seafood, sun and everything else I have done all the time too  . Life is so good

I have noticed, Iodine also soothe my nerves in a very cool way... When the boyfriend fights with me I am just totally calm and I just have to laugh! At work when issues come up and coworkers get angry, I am just so calm and I do not get upset at all, not one bit... I just laugh all the time ....LOL It is very good, because it helps everybody . There cant be a fight from one person alone for sure!

I have had at least 2 times an awesome euphoria coming over my whole body... like every cell was laughing.... I often (i would say always!) have such a calm calm and nice feeling now.
I was feeling good before too, but now I feel even better

I think everyone is deficient in Iodine today, really. I believe we are meant to live by the sea, off the sea. No one does that today. Then, imagine all the Fluoride, Chloride and Bromide that have accumulated in our bodies through our lives, that block the receptors.... no wonder people are having issues with awareness.. because I truly believe Iodine is so needed for that! And of course, together with seafood and sun from dawn to dusk

The boyfriend says he has issues with concentrating and awareness.. he is starting the Iodine too now, just a small amount like 2 drops of 5 %.. I am so happy he does it because i notice he needs it!
When he is at my place we just sit outside on my terrace almost naked in the sun/light and read Osho and talk and cuddle, that is. He loves it.. I too  If he wants to see me that is what he has to do, I am stone hard on that. But now he has become to love it too   It is going slow, but it is going forward

My sister visited me on her drive with family from south Spain to Finland.. we had 2 days together and it was awesome   She is totally better now, and can tolerate nnEMF again!! She does not notice anything anymore when she uses their induction stove! And it used to make her so sick....
The sun really is the most powerful healer ever... She also have started Iodine.. and she is so happy being on the road to health  They use no lights at home, just candles.. her kids are so sweet . She now can tolerate foods she could not before.

I am now going to the oyster beach now picking a bag of oysters  Sun is out... life is good

Very interesting, especially the case with the white hair! I read, that some people use diluted iodine directly on the scalp, I consider trying that.
I also use 5% iodine solution for my thyroid, 1 drop at the moment. I also tried it on the skin, but it takes very long to disappear, what could be a sign, that one need not much more of it.
May I ask you which dose you are taking and how long it takes your body to soak it up?

### Inger — 2017-07-19T10:22:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-219362>

> [Queen of Elves said:](/goto/post?id=219357)
>
> Very interesting, especially the case with the white hair! I read, that some people use diluted iodine directly on the scalp, I consider trying that.
> I also use 5% iodine solution for my thyroid, 1 drop at the moment. I also tried it on the skin, but it takes very long to disappear, what could be a sign, that one need not much more of it.
> May I ask you which dose you are taking and how long it takes your body to soak it up?

I started with a few drops, like 4, and then increased in two weeks to 15, like boom . I did got detox like headache, some small pimples and rashes, tiredness, some swelling in my body like i thought I had gained but it was just water because it went away fast in a few days or weeks. I also got a real hurtful bromaderma under my arm, uh! But I knew it was detox because always after I felt even better as before . So it was worth the suffering .

I do believe when doing Iodine you need to really listen to your body, and sometimes take a break to let the body get rid of stuff, and do lots of things to help it do it. Like tanning nude, lots of water, oysters and other amazing foods with lots of Zink and other minerals, getting lots of fresh air on the skin, dry brush, I also wash my body once in a while with Meine Base, a mineral salt that has a PH about 8-9 - always feels so good after.. and the water get so grey at times even if I do it after a hot shower and dry brushing!

IDK what to think about the painting test on skin... For me it takes about 12 hours for it to disappear but it varies, where I paint it etc. I think it has also lot to do with skin thickness and dryness...

Also, if you cant assimilate the Iodine because your symporters does not work well, it makes no sense to take high doses either. Many get great results just with a drop or two / day... It sure is a biohack! And you need to know that you can get bad detox.. and be ready to rest then. If you have a stressing work and little freetime I would suggest to go very slow...
But then, some people say that taking high doses makes less detox than low.. It is all a testing out. But being careful can never hurt. I think if your mithocondria are not working well it might not be a good idea to take too much Iodine at all. But I am very healthy and I heal fast so I am not afraid to test more. I also have pretty much time to tan and heal because I work only 150 hours / month and always have time to be outside in the morning...and my work is also lot outside on the terrace in the restaurant - which is so good .

I also think it is a great thing to get lots of Zink, Selenium and magnesium and iron through food when you take Iodine. I also do seaweed. I am so lucky to be able to eat as many oysters I like for free

Also getting enough unrefined salt is so important.

I did the protocol a few years ago but I also got scared then by the strong detox (Real bad pimples on my face and depression) so I stopped. This time it is way easier and detox is not as bad  And I know more so I do it better . My environment is better (less nnEMF) and I have stronger sun and way more oysters

For very ill people it might be better to just get the Iodine through food - IDK.... at least until they heal more? Anyways, it is an interesting journey!

How is your thyroid doing with the Iodine?
You could join the German Iodine facebook group for more help - there are people who have totally healed their thyroid through Iodine . But I have the feeling, people who are successful go to the nature a lot and use other tools to help the detox and heal.

### Inger — 2017-07-19T10:24:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-219363>

> [Danny said:](/goto/post?id=219319)
>
> What a fanstastic update! So happy to hear the progress. I have been wanting to try iodine again, but I had such a negative experience w/ it a few years back. I think I over did it when I was trying it. I felt fantastic for a few months and just kept increasing the dose until I crashed down... foolish... but a good lesson in taking it slow.

Yes, that was a good lesson . It is so important to listen very well to the body, and also take breaks for a few days and not push too hard..... it is a long journey, and there are no quick fix - except being naked in the nature and sun...lol it heals right there in the moment

Thanks for so much input!
I will definitely look for the German Iodine facebook Group...
And I know MeineBase - I use it in my bathtub

I'm totally new to the iodine protocol and don't have much experience so far. I took 1 drop for several weeks but don't recognize anything. I think I will increase a bit. But surely not 15 drops, I'm not brave like you  No, I mean, I have no really good connection to my body, but I want to improve this, so that I can rely on myself. My mitochondria actually are not working well, so I will go slow

So far, there arent't any felt improvements with the thyroid but I have to do some labs. My MD told me to measure my temperature in the morning to see if it increases with the iodine (which is the goal) to 36,8 ° C, but mine is still lower.

### endless — 2017-08-01T09:23:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-220592>

Thanks for sharing your iodine experiences...I was doing it for awhile but stopped. I might just give it another try and do more topical than internal this time....

### Inger — 2017-08-03T20:55:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-220855>

Still taking Iodine, but just 5-8 drops now + topically. Feels good!
But it is only a good idea if one knows and listens well to ones body, I think.. and have decent working mithocondria.. It is important to read Lynne Farrows book the Iodine crisis, before starting the protocol, or the ones from Brownstein.

I bought the same nnEMF meter that Alex (Butters) have, and I like it! Comes a lot of info material with it, which makes it fun for beginners
@ Queen of Elves, this would be a great one for you! Any meter is better than non, tho, IMHO. It is SO important to own one today...

I was house hunting today... me and my fiance are looking for a house to rent, and it has not been easy to find one that suits boths needs! But this one could be it

It has a pretty big garden to tan in, and no neighbors can look into it so nude tanning is fine   That as a must for me! My partner likes it too . Neighbor is a organic farm, and the cows are grassing right behind the garden. Beautiful!
It has a big basement, with small windows even, so possibly one could sleep there  It is dry and nice . No mold. All rooms on ground floor.

Bad thing is there is an antenna about 500-600 meter away - you cant see it but it is there. As I measured in the garden with my meter it turned green-orange....so readings are pretty good still but not as good as where I live now tho.. I guess I will go with it, because it is a stone house and inside I guess will be fine. And on the other side there are no antennas but far away, so I can always take my bike and tan on the small lakes or somewhere in the nature if I want even less nnEMF - plenty of silent places there!

Good thing is, no wifi from neighbors, no smart meters either.
Only 200 people living in the town..and I can buy raw organic cheese and meat and eggs from the farm...and the farmers son was there too today as they showed me the house, he is around 20 yo I think and we immediately got a beautiful connection!!! We talked about the sun, and he told me how he never use sunglasses because the sun needs the sun to get signals for the skin to not burn! How cool! He was barefoot all the time.... and he said he loves to be like that.. he loves nature... he wants to come pick oysters with me   I told him I had a raw lambs lung smoothie for breakfast and he thought it was so cool..lol
He said he is soo happy if we move in there  They live just close to the house by the farmers shop

Uh this is so exciting

### Inger — 2017-08-04T12:31:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-220895>

well.. thinking about it... I do I am not so sure we will rent that house... I am feeling weird in my belly about the antenna...and the electric fence surrounding part of the garden to keep the cattle on the pasture. Antenna and fence are directly there where I am going to tan nude... Other side of the house has no tanning space and sun...
I am so happy my fiance is together with me on this, and says we will only take the best for us! If I feel uneasy about it, we will let it pass. He is so respecting all my health choices. Feels so good.. Huh! Not easy, this moving thing!

Feeling sad about the farmers / landlords boy tho.. I could see love in his eyes...we share the same passion.. he is about 20 yo and I am 40 yo but in the heart we were the same.. That I could feel so strong! Still.. I cant let that make me do wrong choices.....

### Inger — 2017-08-08T20:14:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-221199>

we did not take the house. We went there together once more yesterday to measure as the weather was great and sunny (when I was there alone it was gray and rainy and I suspected radiation would show less because of that..) and my meter showed RED!!! No way am I going to move in there as the garden where I would be tanning all the time was really not good, I would get blasted with nnEMF! RF really beeped red..uh! Where I live now it is all green......

Thankfully my fiance was totally on my side with the decision.. so the house hunt continues..lol
So happy I have this place because it has pretty darn good nnEMF levels, when i think about it
We decided to better go slow and really test well before moving together in a house. My fiance live in a not so ideal place he has higher levels that I, but we are hanging out way more on my place, which is good   He travels a lot too and is not too much at home anyways.

Funny how rain works as a faraday cage somehow. I feel great when it rains, and I sleep even better at rainy weather! I bet it is because radiation get blocked  I got it confirmed now at the house... at rainy weather RF was way lower. Good to know if house hunting, and always check levels when weather is sunny/clear.....

### Inger — 2017-08-09T10:39:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-221257>

well, Danny, this is just my guess, because Jack wrote somewhere that cold water was an excellent faraday cage... and then I figured rain is water too.. ok it is only drops but still it will block some of the waves, no? And as I measured on a rainy day and a sunny day the radiation was much higher on the sunny day. Could have been other reasons too but I do love rainy days . Tonight it rained and I slept like a baby 9 hours with no waking up! So nice, because I had way too little sleep lately because of working too late..(we had a wedding in the restaurant I work in) one day even until 2:30 AM, uh! But now I feel like new again

I have been doing vaginal tanning almost every morning now, between 1/2 hour and 2-3 hours and man it is soooo good! It really makes a difference.... My sweetheart loves it...lol I told me if I get sick he just have to carry me out naked on the ground and spread my legs wide in the sun.... He promised to do so... what can be better for healing!
Thanks to Rose  Every woman should do it!
Before the tanning I put some olive oil with Iodine down there too....  cool stuff

### Inger — 2017-09-13T12:17:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-223530>

Iodine experiment continues...
I have monitored my fasting blood glucose lately and also how food affects my blood glucose levels.. and I am pretty amazed how great levels I have!

My fasting blood glucose is constantly blow 88.. and mostly around 84, today it was even 78, but I had some red wine yesterday for dinner...

It does not vary much around the day, but stays almost always in between 10 points.. super stable!
I do eat low carb, seafood, organs, meat, salads... occasionally small amounts of honey or berries.. sometimes some cheat foods like HWC, which I have to stop..lol Dairy is not my friend, I know it
Leaving out the carbs gradually more and more as we go into fall here now and sun is sparse. Lots of rain!

I googled iodine and blood sugar and found this;

[http://drsircus.com/cancer/iodine/](http://drsircus.com/cancer/iodine/)

[http://www.healthy-eating-politics.com/diabetes-iodine.html](http://www.healthy-eating-politics.com/diabetes-iodine.html)

I used to check my blood glucose a year ago or so and struggled to go under 88 fasted.. thought it was because I eat mostly ketogenic.. But I have not changed my eating habits.. I wonder if iodine have made this change?

I am taking less now, the detox is not too fun if it builds up, and I guess I am pretty saturated now. I do a few drops in my salads, and use topically every day too. Adding in more seaweed as I take less iodine. But I am going to continue taking a few drops of 5 % Lugols each day, it just feels good  Using it on my face too mixed into jojoba oil or Ghee, my skin loves it

### Sheddie — 2017-09-13T19:11:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-223562>

Breast tissues love Lugol's, too!!

### Inger — 2017-09-13T20:36:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-223572>

So true Sheddie! I got rid of a bump in my breast through painting them with Lugols! Funny how eating a high seafood diet did not seem to be enough... As I started painting my breast they first swelled up and got sore for a few weeks, then shrunk again to normal.. and the bump was gone! It was a very small one though that has got a bit smaller through the years but still...  Iodine is pretty awesome  Detox can be awful tho....

\- - - -

So stormy here I cant even go outside! Uh! But now I got my bedroom finally all sorted out.. and painted two walls sunny yellow with some color leftovers I had.. With so much gray and rain I just needed some sunshine inside the room, love it

### Sheddie — 2017-09-14T02:06:50+0300

Inger, I think you are the sunshine in the room, so it's lovely the walls will reflect that back to you!

### Inger — 2017-11-03T14:49:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-227263>

> [Andrea said:](/goto/post?id=226041)
>
> So... Will you join me in Mexico in December?

I wish! Seems pretty impossible tho... too short time to save money because the tickets are not cheap anymore... BUT. I have decided to work just a little more the next season at the oyster beach restaurant, and save up easily enough to spend some weeks in Mexico next Christmas   I have the winters off., which is so cool  then I can travel to the sun

This winter I think I will take a trip to Spain to my sister instead, tickets very cheap so I can afford it.. and there is loads of sun.. and she is all in with Jacks stuff too   She has mutated into a new person! Totally healthy.. and in the first time of her life she can finally... THINK! I got a hand written letter from her last week (we write with each other old style, sans tech  ), and she told me all about it. She is overwhelmed...and so happy.. she looks younger.. and she feels so much alive   She has been taking Iodine too, but I do not think Iodine would have worked without the sun, seafood and other things she do... I am so happy for her... she is my sister! I love her.. and I have seen her suffering....since she was a child...

### Inger — 2017-11-03T14:55:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-227264>

My partner left. On my birthday, a month ago.
He said he could not come where I am going.... I am too extreme..
Sure, I am religious about my sunrises, spending time naked in the sun...quality foods, darkness after sunset...switching off the breakers in my house and my phone early every evening (yeah.. no one can reach me except coming on my door in person, and I love that) never am I going to give that up. They are my source of energy!

I get it must be hard... it is not easy to be in candle light only every evening if you are not used to the dark. But in my home I have those rules, and they are not going to change. I love the darkness, it makes me go inside, read, think... I think it is something special, something beautiful!
And the morning sunrises are as magic too....

It hurt so bad, because I love him so dearly! but at the same time, it is like... freedom...
Funny how something can hurt so bad and feel so good at the same time....

### Inger — 2017-11-03T14:57:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-227265>

and yesterday I spent 4 hours naked in the sun at my terrace   It is November, but it felt like summer! Those days are the most healing ones, when I get to spend all day outside sans clothes....
I love the fall/winter/spring sun.. It has something soft to it! And you never sweat in the sun, it just feel so comfortably warm, relaxing and energizing

### Lahelada — 2017-11-03T15:12:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-227266>

Hi Inger. Sorry to hear you are hurting. It is hard for new people. I am single ,too and once the novelty wears off , it becomes a drag for them. However, maybe it was a good thing for you. Someone who can say "happy birthday I am leaving" may be better lost than found. Enjoy the sun, don't forget about that trip to Spain in winter. Sounds an excellent idea that will make your mitos sing.

### Brent Patrick — 2017-11-05T05:12:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-227394>

This is the issue I often think about in meeting someone who is not on the same road as me ,,are well the way I see it I am a much better person I feel to be around due to all the things I have learned from Jack and on this Forum.

I suspect quantum entanglement/Higher Dopamine will guide me too better Options , hang in there Inger life has a funny way of unfolding, even though we may not always understand at first.

### Inger — 2017-11-05T11:42:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-227407>

Thank you for the nice words

I realize I have so much to learn! And that learning alone is pretty exciting

I realize there are many thing I can do better! So every struggle is there to help me see that, wow.. It is all a learning process and from the things that hurt the most I think there is a great learning potential.

We do still write and we met once, and it was very nice. I do realize his issue is dopamine.... and I so get why... He also is a very healthy man, even if he travels a lot and uses airplane and car all the time... but I have figured his mithocondria are great. He does had some issues with high blood sugar that scared him, and he cut on carbs and it immediately improved.. which made him forget about it again and go on as before..lol It is hard for people to see the benefits of this life style when they are not ill.. and I get that. But he still have changed stuff like he uses much more candles now and enjoys it, shut of wifi in the night at home and tries to avoid using cellphone to excess. But it is not in the way I do and live and he does not see the need to, which I get too....
I still cant compromise how I live, because I need it! I am way more sensible and I think... I feel on another level.. after all I am a woman...

He gave me money as a birthday present for a flight to the south to get sun.. which I think was very lovely.

He does say I am an angel.. which is so sweet. He does fear that I will kill myself with my biohacks!  But I am very careful doing them and I listen to my body always.. and I know how to heal again if something goes wrong. And I do a lot of reading and learning. I think he sees that too.
We agreed if he get any health issues I will be there for him, because he does trust I know what to do.. I will just drag him naked into the sun in the nature on the ground..and feed him lots of seafood and good water..lol He likes that! Way better than going to the doctor..

Life goes on and it is an interesting journey for sure! Full of surprises..
I guess one must have pretty high dopamine to live so different than anybody else and enjoy it...enjoy the wild ride

### caroline — 2017-11-06T12:05:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-227456>

wise words Inger.......sending you a big hug xo

Life does go on and there are many, many surprises and twists and turns and discomfort - I think we wouldn't want it any other way!

### Inger — 2017-11-20T20:04:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-228510>

Thak you Caroline...  that hug does so good! It still hurts a lot at times tho, and I miss him! I do love him still so much...  But I have to go forward....

So now I am really thinking about moving to Spain! My sister did a gene test some time ago and we are from the mediterranian, Jews... Uh, would not have thought that as I am blue eyed and light skinned! But my mother is very dark tho..dark skin dark eyes.. my father is paper white Irish/Scottisch..lol
No wonder I always need sun! I need to move.
Going to visit my sister in Spain beginning of next year, and see what I can figure out. She is doing awesome, as are the kids too. ADHD issues way less! They are just happier!

Now that I am alone, and the fiancee left, I am free to do whatever I want, even risky stuff..lol That is the upside
The downside is, I am freaking poor..lol
But I own a car! An old Opel, but it is a great car and will drive many km still  So that is an upside too   And I am healthy   I have two hands and two feet and a head that works somehow normally

I got a huge wound in my foot saturday. It was real deep and ugly.. Today, I cant even recognize it when I walk around... wow it healed so fast! It did not hurt at all either, weird! It just healed like that.. magic . Maybe because i grounded barefoot to my floor all day? I have this stone floor that grounds.. I bet it helped. And I did put iodine on the wound too.
I was scared it would get infected or something, because it barely bleed at all. But now it is already healed..wow

### Inger — 2017-11-27T10:54:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-228926>

Thank you Richelle

Yes, to have someone who supports one - the whole me.. that would be so beautiful! It was a lot of struggle with the him tho, but I am not afraid of struggles so I just kept doing what I felt in my heart was right, and kept loving him too, more and more.. but then he could not make it... I think if you cant understand where we are going here, it must be real hard. Like an alien!   It is so far away from mainstream.... So I really do get he could not continue. You need to have your heart in the game.

I have no time to look for a new partner right now as I want to finish a project i work on first... but when it is ready I will keep my eyes open...
I kind of enjoy being alone too... like yesterday.. I went to bed at 5:45 PM! And stayed there until 8 AM as it got light outside....
I just felt like I needed LOTS of darkness, to repair what I had destroyed last week, working 3 late shifts at work, was not home before midnight, and for me that is horror..lol All the fake lights.. and I usually am in bed at 9 PM!
So I just did not even want to use candle light yesterday.. and then I could not keep my eyes open anymore short after 5 PM, I got sooo sleepy!
What a luxury to just drown in the darkness and silence...

Somehow I really love the darkness in winter, it feels like it has a big function.
It also makes it super easy to fast for 16-18 hours. It is like everything slows down... Winter-modus   I am in love with my bed..lol The cold and dark bedroom.. and my cat.. we both just enjoy it, laying there, window wide open, watching the dark skies or stars and listening to the darkness

With a partner that do not get all this, it is hard to do. Most people are not used to so much darkness and silence.... To me, those two things are kind of... sacred

### Sue-UK — 2017-11-27T11:28:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-228930>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=228926)
>
> Somehow I really love the darkness in winter, it feels like it has a big function.
> It also makes it super easy to fast for 16-18 hours. It is like everything slows down... Winter-modus   I am in love with my bed..lol The cold and dark bedroom.. and my cat.. we both just enjoy it, laying there, window wide open, watching the dark skies or stars and listening to the darkness
>
> With a partner that do not get all this, it is hard to do. Most people are not used to so much darkness and silence.... To me, those two things are kind of... sacred

Its become a vital part of my winter hack - first couple of nights I did it felt weird, but now it feels "normal" and is definitely helping me. I found an article online about darkness and the importance of melatonin in ocular physiology. So I'm blue blocking from sunset until I go to bed at 8, and then it has to be complete darkness. DH is super cool about my hack because he understands why I am doing it - in my case with my eye problem, its too important not to do. I found an article online about darkness and the importance of melatonin in ocular physiology, apart from the pineal, melatonin is also produced in the retina, lens, iris, ciliary body and lacrimal gland, as well as the skin and gut (and I reckon the darkness influences the lung surface as well). So I'm trying a bit of full body darkness immersion for some quantum melatonin synchronicity.

### Richelle Jones — 2017-11-27T11:33:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-228931>

I would so go to bed at sunset if it wasn't socially unacceptable....as it is I wait one hour....then i go to bed....hahaa...alone time is great...i crave it all the time...there is so much pressure to have the best relationships and be everything to everyone....total myth...your winter must be something! No winter here where i am. just sun and beach all year round. I love it.

### Inger — 2017-11-27T12:19:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-228932>

@ Sue, that sounds like an awesome hack!  You are so lucky to have a hubby that understands  I too block all blue and use only candles after sunset but still going all dark is a different thing! It starts to get dark at 4:30 PM here already, so that is a really long darkness time if one uses no candles or red light either.. I am going to try get some days/evenings in where I black totally out at 6 PM... to see how it makes me feel. Not even a candle...

@ Richelle, yes. Too much pressure to keep light on in todays world, that is so true. Like it is not allowed to only.. be.. In darkness. With a partner it would be a really nice hack, as one can talk, and cuddle etc. Alone it is way harder, but somehow it still feels really nice, especially if I have had to work late nights and feeling how it drains me. Then such an extended darkness pariod really feels so healing

I do not have much sun at all here no, so I figure I have to use the other part of it.. the dark!  . But I am planning to move south too... probably to south Spain where my sister already has moved and thrives now . But it needs some time and planning... Latest for the next fall I want to be there, but maybe earlier  Working on it!

### Sue-UK — 2017-11-27T15:50:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-228944>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=228932)
>
> @ Sue, that sounds like an awesome hack!  You are so lucky to have a hubby that understands  I too block all blue and use only candles after sunset but still going all dark is a different thing! It starts to get dark at 4:30 PM here already, so that is a really long darkness time if one uses no candles or red light either.. I am going to try get some days/evenings in where I black totally out at 6 PM... to see how it makes me feel. Not even a candle...

I don't think red light at night is biologically neutral, and I've seen a diagram that put the kelvin of candle light at close to the sunrise and sunset kelvins but I don't think the frequencies match the sunset or minus 6 and 9 degree solar elevation frequencies. My eye hack n=1 concern is that red light and candles would still stimulate the cones, so my hack is based on the idea that if it was 12:12 day/night, allowing for 1 hour for light to drop naturally at sunset, I should aim for 11 hours natural darkness minimum. It should probably be more to make up for the shorter nights of summer at this latitude, but its been a starting point .... I'll be interested in how you get on doing the extra ...

Queen of Elves,
how are you doing with your moving plans? Anything new?

Now I am planning to maybe get it to work renting a small house in southern Spain and stay there for winters and keep this apartment for working here on the oyster beach in summer, if my ex husband helps me with it, cause he is all in! He and I are like sister and brother and he likes all the JK stuff, he tries out everything I talk about..lol he is very open minded . So if his business work is going well he wants to do this with me, so I was thinking, if anyone needs a place to stay in the sun anytime of year they could just come to our home there  That would be so cool! It is not expensive to rent a house on the countryside in Spain.

Uh, I so want to get this happen

\- - - - - -

Was taking a walk in the woods even if it rained all the time.. First I did not want to go out at all.. but then I just pushed my self out the door!
And I realized one thing.
It made me feel awesome!!!
It is so gray and rainy here all the time, so I have not wanted to go out. But it makes you go downwards for sure! I could not even imagine how much better a walk under the gray skies and rain could make me feel.. awesome! What does that tells me... Nature is what recharges me, sun or rain

> Unfortunately nothing new with moving. I also considered Spain or Canary Islands for the winters but I see no chance with my current job to spend every winter there.
> The other thing is, that my relationship is about to break because it is not possible to live like I need to with someone who isn't really interested about the WHY. He is able to agree to some things to do me a favor, but others won't be changing, like watching TV the whole day.
>
> Your plan sounds great and I wish I would be so flexible with working times.

That sounds sad  Sending you a big hug!
I know how much it hurts when the partner does not understand... it broke my heart
But I need to go forward, and deep in me I know, after rain always comes sun. I just could not live with a person watching TV all day long I guess... I so would wish they could wake up..... but if they do not, eventually they will leave I guess. Because that is what my fiancee did. I could not join him on the stuff that hurt me so he found another woman that did. I could not leave him tho because I did love him anyways, uh! Now I just need to go forward. Explore the beauty of life and live every second to the fullest, as Caroline uses to say

### caroline — 2017-12-07T02:46:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-229697>

yep ..... we can't drag someone along with us on our journey ....they make their own journey and their own decisions.

It is sad in some ways ....but you don't want them dragging you down ...that is definitely no bueno!

Imagine how life affirming it is when you are on this remarkable journey with a like minded partner. If that doesn't happen - you have so many options to explore and so many wonderful people to meet and influence.

Life is made up of challenges ...... do we buckle under the load? ....or do we survive and, in fact, thrive!

We have come so far on this optimal journey - we are strong, we are learning a ton of such important stuff that is so important for an optimal life, we are rolling with the punches and, hopefully, paying it all forward.

Life is so beautiful - live it!

### Inger — 2018-05-30T20:37:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-242382>

Wow, I almost could not find my journal!

I am enjoying the sunny and warm weather.... never experienced such a warm and sunny spring here, so awesome
I have got really tanned already, even if I am working full time, there is so much sun and I am out all the time I am not working.. feels like being on vacation...lol Also at work we work mostly outside on the terrace because that is where the guests want to eat when it is such a nice weather

I am feeling real good lately, have been working a lot with my thoughts and meditating.... and learning to live in the here and now.
I had a not so easy winter because of stress that I made myself because I was not living in the now. But I am learning to do it

I finally got some bloodwork done, has been years since I did my last ones. Doctor said all is wonderful, and i do thin k most of them are, just one, the BUN/Creatinine ratio is way high! Huh!

Vit D was 47,8 ng/ml (range 20-100)
I am not too impressed but taking it is spring, and we have had UVB just a month or so here in northern Germany, maybe it is not too bad after all . I have not supplemented anything, and on my vacation in february, to Spain, it rained all the time so I could not really build any vit D either. I am going to measure it again in fall to check if I could increase it!

HS CRP was 0,33 mg/l
Vit B 12 was 977 pg/l
HDL 190 mg/dl
Triglycerides 71 mg/dl
LDL 190 mg/dl

Creatinin (enzymatic) was 0,61 mg/dl
Urea/BUN was 39 mg/dl
and when I put them in a BUN/Creatinin calculator it gives a horrible 60 or something! And it should be 10!
This is a bit strange. I do have realized my skin tend to be on the dry side somehow lately, and it had been really hot and dry here, and I have been out in the sun a lot. I wonder if that can have an impact? Or is it the new windmills that have popped up in this town? Or am I doing too much nicotine gum?

I have been drinking a cup or two of coffee too lately.. and some wine a few times a week. I need to stop the coffee NOW.

My Hämatokrites were pretty low though, on the low end of range... which should tell the blood is fluid. So I am not drinking too little... or am I? Do I need more salt? I have been taking about 1/2 teaspoon in addition to food every morning, but I might increase it now.

Maybe I eat way too much protein and too little fat?
I do eat a lot of protein.

My hämoglobine was not impressive, at 13,1 g/dl, but I do not feel anemic at all?
Iron was in middle range.

Well, I am not the lab expert at all, but I get I have some dehydration going on. I am going to change some thing.
Less protein,
more fat,
more salt.
Quit coffee 100%.

I have measured some higher electric radiation around here lately. Just a minor increase but still. It is in the woods too. the same values that are in my home. I think it is weird! I wonder if it is the hot, sunny and dry weather? Or is it the windmills? I cant see or hear them tho from my home, as there are many houses between me and them but further away from the road I can see them.

Otherwise I am sleeping great, feeling good, happy, loving life loving work...

I am so little on my computer these days because I want to be outside all the time, so right now too I am laying outside writing this.

If anyone have an idea what else to do to get that BUN/Creatinine ratio down, please share

### Lahelada — 2018-05-30T22:58:19+0300

Good to hear from you. The iron in hemoglobin is used to make a larger EZ. Thinking backwards if you have less hemoglobin then you make a smaller EZ.
This quote from "Blood work =whats optimal" might shed some light for you.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=191315)

> You need to read........there are no short cuts. Just yesterday I posted this:Yesterday I heard a bulletin from the Irish Blood Transfusion board calling for donations of blood. The reason given for the shortage given was that over 40% of the blood collected in this last month had to be thrown out due to low hemoglobin content of donors. People in Ireland live in a very poor UV region globally and on top of it something happened this year to make it worse. This all occurred right at winter's end and is at the end of our worst winter on record over there; they had over 10 major storms and CONSTANT gloom from their weather data. Do you reckon this lack of sunlight could be responsible for the epidemic of low hemoglobin from donors? Erythropoetin is a solar hormone too people and we need it to avoid anemia to carry UV and IR light from our surfaces to our interiors. Hemoglobin has 4 key frequencies it absorbs in…one UV and 3 in the red……..what does that tell you? Light is my scalpel........always has been. Too bad for the critics that left the building and could not wait for the full harvest of the ideas. Sun light, not food, nature, not modern beliefs burying the sun. Nature's concepts not modern manufactured precepts. Reality trumps your perception of it. Attention follows energy, and the absence of light in proteins tells the sun where to refill us to avoid degeneration. Hemoglobin’s peak light absorption peaks at 4 specific frequencies "280 mm,420nm, 540nm, and 580nm" and has a very sharp cutoff at 600 nm. When you live in Ireland indoors under fake light you never see one of these frequencies of light.......NOT ONE. This points out why blue -green frequency spikes in fake light might be behind many blood disorders. Anemia is just one. These absorption peaks in hemoglobin are ideally match the spectral absorptions of the arteries in skin and the aorta deep inside of us where atherosclerosis predominates. These ideas go way past atherosclerosis and anemia. They reach all the way to neurologic diseases like tumors and to neurodegeneration too because of the effect on the SCN and the RPE and dopamine in the brain as I laid out in Ubiquitination 24 blog and the Time series. Light is the lever for chromosomes. Chromosomes are not the lever of light. Genes are quantum computers and the environment they exist in is their measuring devices. Measurement and observation effects are made by the respiratory protein to control the nuclear gene expression. The genome is an ‘organ of the cell’, not its dictator.

.\[/QUOTE\]

### Inger — 2018-05-31T09:56:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-242436>

Thank you Lahelada  That is very helpful!

Well I am every morning in my tan through bikini in the church yard on the other side of this house, I sit there meditating for an hour or two in the morning sun... it is so awesome! Then I come to my terrace and stay nude here until I go to work. And I watch the sunset too most days of the week, just not if I have to work after sun set. So I do get a lot of morning and evening and midday sun currently, and almost no fake light, because I am so little on my computer right now. t work of course I have to use the computer where we type in the orders but I am more outside than on that thing . In fact for weeks already, I am outside all day! because it has been sunny all May.
It is almost scary because it does not rain and it is so hot and sunny and dry, very very unusual. I wonder if it has dried me out a bit to be so much in the sun. I need more CT!
But the water in my rainbarrel is not cold anymore. Cold showers do help, and icepacks, I need to do them more, and swimming in the sea. I was at the sea yesterday for 5 hours and was a lot in the water, it is maybe 19 degree C so also very warm but still nicely cooling. It is so awesome there, so lonely, so huge a sea!

I really wonder if more salt can help too. I do eat about 70 to 100 oysters a week and they are very salty here. Can one get too much salt? I guess no?

Should nicotine help with dehydration, or is it rather the opposite?

well, I might be a dried out raisin but I feel so happy and peaceful    And I enjoy every minute of the day, I really feel like in Nirvana these days.
I can do whatever I want, being naked in the sun all day long! No partner that complains of me being sun addicted...lol what a relief

I am going to change some things as listed above and test again and check if anything get better. If not, I need to get out of this place and fast!
And find a new Nirvana

Hi Inger,
Nice journal. I was at your first entry and you said you had a lot of sunwater. What does that mean? When I say I drink sunwater, it is the unfloridated water I put in cobalt blue glass bottles and in the constant direct sun for a couple of hours and drink very cold. It is really delicious. Then I got an idea to put 2 flat kitchen magnets under it in the sun and the water almost feels effervescent to me when I drink it cold and even more delicious. Maybe its just my imagination.

I liked the idea of raw lamb and mackerel that you ate. Do you continue to drink coffee? I just never started drinking coffee, but continued with cow milk which turned out not to be better. Who knew?

I just read your signature about courage. When I mediate (not often enough) I scan my body for any slight discomforts. I am pain free now since following the laws of light, water and magnetism, so I might only feel a small pin prick on my skin. My conversation to the feeling starts with, "Thank you for your courage to surface for acceptance, forgiveness, and surrender. Within a minute, it disappears. I might need to do this several times with other tiny discomforts and then I sink into a deep soundless mediation.

I'm glad you popped up into my emails as an alert.

\- - - - -

I got to be naked in the sun for many hours today.... in the autumn sun.. It was so awesome! I love this time of the year! It reminds me of letting go... how the leaves fall to the earth and die and then in spring comes new ones.. it is like magic! And the trees willingly let the leaves fall.. they do not resist....

On my terrace the roses are blooming, huge roses with such a tender, awesome scent, and everywhere are small flowers popping up... between the stones... on the lawn, completely wild!
I was so in awe today sitting in the morning sun and seeing all the flowers! So much beauty, being present in front of my eyes. Just wow!

\- - - - -

The Marriage of Goodness and Wildness.
Truth is wild. It does not conform to expectation...

### Inger — 2018-11-12T15:14:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-255493>

Now I finally have more time to heal. I sooo much enjoy it    After a summer season with lots of work, restaurant is now closed for the winter, and I just work 2-3 days a week in my second job.

I recently have had such an awesome sleep, it is crazy!
I shut off all electronics around 6 -6:30 PM, and then I just have one candle on and I read before going to bed. The thing is, at 8 PM I cant keep my eyes open! So I go to bed.. and I wake up as it starts to get a little lighter, around 6 AM... wow. Slowly slowly I wake up, because my sleep is so deep it feels like an hibernation or something. I stay i bed for half and hour or sometimes even an hour after waking because that heavy relaxed feeling is so enjoyable..lol just laying there and watch the sky get brighter...
Crazy! But so awesome

Tonight I did not have that deep sleep tho, and I know why!
Yesterday I had a little unusual snack at 3:30 PM after my dinner. Just a handful of hazelnuts and almonds, mixed with some Christmas spices and a half teaspoon of honey and one single piece of dark chocolate. I wanted to make some pralines for my dad, so I did a test that I ate. But man, my body hated it! It was like WTF? Chocolate? Honey? We are going into winter!
And as came the evening, I did not got this heavy sleepiness as I normally do.. even if I did the same rutine I do before bed.. just reading and sitting in silence with a candle.

Of course, my body could feel something was off. Not going to do that error again! I so appreciate that deep deep sweet sleep.

My food is very boring and simple these days. I do a huge breakfast of local, minced lamb, extra fatty, that I order from the butcher. I make thin burgers of it and dry it at 35 degree C over night.. so it is still raw but a little warm and crispy. I add lots of raw pastured butter on the top and a few slices of raw onion. My favorite breakfast ever

Then I eat dinner/lunch around 2-3 PM and it is some kind of seafood, preferably raw, and some raw oysters with it.

When I keep dinner that early, I realize it also helps make this awesome deep sleep

Yesterday it was rainy but I still took a walk in the woods and I got completely wet..lol but it still felt so good.
Funny how the most important thing is to be outside in winter no matter what. It is still so much light, even if it is completely gray and overcast!

When I work too much, and especially in winter when it is dark, so I cannot enjoy the darkness fully because work means... fake light! that is when I start to feel off!
So I feel so lucky to be able to work way less in the winter. In summer I do not mind, the days are so long and light and there is plenty of sun to recharge in anyways.

### Inger — 2018-11-13T19:41:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-255568>

I am working on it Caroline   Always something to optimize! I had to update my avatar because the old one was more than 2 year old!

Last year was hard for me, lots of work, fiance left... and I started meditating/being in silence more and more I did not know what else to do I was in so much pain. and now I am so glad everything went as it did, because I learned, and continue to learn, a ton
Life is really the best school, but sometimes it does hurts like hell

Yes, I do wanted to move to the south but so far it did not happen because of changed circumstances... My eyes are wide open tho, if there comes a possibility in my way. That said, I feel really good here too, I love my both jobs, bosses, coworkers, where I live... my landlord is so good to me etc etc. and I have a seasonal work, which means winter is more silent time which I really really love.
And this is a pretty good place to live nnEMF wise so far. Not many people, not many antennas. Oysters for free,m plenty of nude beaches..lol

I just plastered my hallway with extra thick alufoil, and living room another layer on top of what I already had done, and wow, seems that it really did something! One pretty strong signal seems to be totally gone.. at least for now. I have new neighbors now, and they seem to have a pretty strong wifi, I want to get rid of!
I do feel a little sorry for them because the thick foil will probably reflect the signal back to them so they get even more fried... but what to do....

### Inger — 2018-11-23T12:50:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-256312>

Well, as I wrote above, I have been doing another layer on my wall, and also my hallway, with aluminium foil, but this time I did it better! Always learning something from my failures..lol

Thought I post it here what I did differently if someone else could have any use of not repeating my mistakes

So instead of using regular aluminium foil for the kitchen, I bought the one for construction, is not expensive either, and way thicker! So one layer is enough  Makes it way easier to cover the wall!
And... the wallpaper that I put on the top... I found this stone imitate, it was also cheap, and it helps covering up the uneven aluminium foil and tape underneath. Because I only used tape to keep it on place. That makes it really easy to remove when I move out, as this is a rental.

When you look at it briefly, without knowing what is underneath, you will not even notice it looks weird..lol
This can only be used if you are certain where the wifi signal comes from, and it is only from 1 or 2 directions. I would never make more that 2 walls in my room covered, I did not want to make a faraday cage, that can make it worse, and the windows would have to be covered too!

I still get a little bit of the wifi in here, but it is really reduced now. I guess the little signal that is left climbs over the wall and comes through the roof, but I am not going to cover the roof..lol

I feel really good in my apartment so I guess it is pretty good now   My ESI24 meter shows all green too.

This is as the wallpaper was still wet, just had put it on the wall, so it is a bit wrinkly, but that disappeared as it dried  ;

[](https://www.pic-upload.de/view-36266601/Foto0097.jpg.html)

[](https://www.pic-upload.de/view-36266603/Foto0098.jpg.html)

### Inger — 2018-11-23T13:07:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-256313>

Somehow I feel really good!
And it is getting darker and darker. And I love it!

I wonder if this darkness and the long nights and relaxing a ton, changes the hormones? Because my breasts have really gotten perkier.... and a little tender too at times. Like they are growing!
I am not sad about that at all because they were really tiny..lol

I do put iodine on them almost every other evening before going to bed. To make sure they get enough. And I make sure my breasts and nipples (in the ideal world I dream of shirts that has 2 holes for the nipples to get sun/light, because somehow I feel they are sort of an antenna - light information-antenna!) get the natural light information everyday, even if just a few minutes. If sunny, I keep them in the sun for hours.

Today I will have to endure some complete mismatch tho. I have to work(waitress) until 10 PM. Horrible I know. But if I do such a shift once or twice a month or so during the winter I guess I can heal it again with being really really strict with fake light after dark all the other days

Doing these shifts once in a while really makes me value my home environment even more. I so hate the alien lights.. even more after dark, it is pretty much a horror.. and when I am home, and use just one candle, I can just sit there on my couch and do nothing, just be soooo happy and thankful because it feels so freaking awesome, so silent, so peaceful, so healing

Who would have thought one can enjoy the plain darkness and silence so much!
Very interesting to observe.
Must be something we all crave in the deepest of our soul, if we just allow it, and endure and accept the dark feelings that might pop up at least in the beginning. When we stop running after something, or away from something... and just.. be.

[@recoen](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21522/), yes I am Norwegian, although my grand-grandma was Swedish and my grand-grandpa was from Ireland or Scotland.. I always forget which..lol
Yes weird how you feel like "home" when you visit the country of your ancestors! It sure is a nice feeling

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/), yes.. sounds like a real mismatch. One of my sisters husband is from Pakistan, and he recently got diagnosed with Diabetes 2. Doctor said he has to tale medicine because his blood sugar was so high, but he did not want to. He still has this natural intuition it could not be good. So he went very low carb instead, and his blood sugar improved dramatically very fast!
So he is now able to just monitor it with his diet
When he goes off rail though, he has to take the prescription pills from his doc to control his blood sugar.. which can happen at special occasions. I am so happy he was willing to quit the carbs. But sure, what he really need is sun, what will not happen much as they live in Sweden...

[@Eric](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/2730/), why do you think your mum will kill you? It will not destroy the walls or anything, very easy to remove if you move out . Try it! If it does not work you can always take it off. Cost is less than 100 euro  Just put a nice wallpaper on the top and no one will notice even

### Inger — 2019-01-16T16:52:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-260494>

..and tonight I flied in my dream... sooo high, over green mountains and valleys. Amazing!
Still dreaming so much and clearly every night, wow. And I never have to pee in the night anymore! I feel DDW helped with this. Because I sometimes had to pee once in the night. Not since my DDW batches I had during summer/fall. Qlarivia, 1/4. Nice!

I was in Finland over Christmas, visiting my parents. It was really nice. Went dipping in my moms ice-hole with my brother, did snow angels... talked and talked. So much love!
My mom had saved a raw deer heart for me! I ate that divided up on two breakfasts, raw, as carpaccio. It was so delicious! The best Christmas food I had all the time there   Made me feel really good! Raw, wild animal hearts always gives me somehow a magic feeling, very subtle, but it is there.

We measured our ages on my dads "True Age" machine, and laughed so much! I still had the best results, being 14 yo  (I am 42 yo) My mom (67) and dad (72) both were around 10-20 years younger than their age so that was great too, but they are really healthy I guess! At least no complaints and high functioning. But my uncle that live in my parents house too, did the test, it was horrible. He has Hepatitis C, and was really really sick, he is now doing a bit better as he got some new medicine from Norway that helps kill the virus. But he has high blodpressure too and many other issues, Reynauds etc. His real age is 60, and the "True Age" machine said he was 82 yo. Crazy! But he looks old too..

I have a new hack I am doing!
And this is to see what I can do for my skin - I just quit using nicotine gums, after using them for almost 5 years..uh! I realized I had got addicted.. not to the nicotine that much, but to the combination of sweetener I guess? and the chewing, and the nicotine! But I was not able to stop because my life had too much stress.. and now... this winter, I have made enough space for me to clear my head. And that is when I was able to stop using them. Yay!
But I guess all that nic.gum was not too great for my skin (in addition to tanning salong in winter) I guess it makes more wrinkles? So I wanted to see if I can do some good for my face

I am not doing tanning salon anymore either, instead I really try go outside more, and I am planning a vacation to the sun if I can afford it this winter.

So my hack is very easy and cheap, 10 euro

That is the cost of a cheap Philips infrared lamp 100 watt. I want a better one, but I have to save a little bit to get that. So I have started with this cheap lamp.

I do cold face dunks for a few minutes, then I use the red light about 3-4 minutes, and then I dunk in the cold water again, and do red light again. It all takes about 15 minutes max. I do this every morning and every evening, I try to do it near sunset if I can.

I want to know if this combination cold and red light does my skin any good . So far it feels great! I have just done it a week but I feel my skin already is improving a little?
I have not been doing real face dunks for long time! I do dunk my face a little when I sit in the rainbarrel every morning, but it is not so much.

Cant wait until I get the real red light of Alex from the facebook group!  I guess that will be a wise thing to invest in

Otherwise I am doing really well, enjoying the dark and cozy evenings. Not too many weeks of darkness left as it gets lighter every day now, so I really need to enjoy the dark silent time I have left before they days get long again!

### Danny — 2019-01-16T18:38:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-260513>

Inger...what a great update! I always enjoy reading about your progess and trials. IR / red and cold is a winner for sure. I love hearing your perspective on the winter...it makes me yearn for dark, cold, fire lit nights. I am really embracing the cold this winter w/ more CT in the ocean and face dunks. I try to add as much fire light as possible which makes winter come alive for me. Flying in your dreams is an exhilarating feeling. I love when that happens. Happy to hear your update!

### Inger — 2019-01-17T13:03:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-260598>

> [Danny said:](/goto/post?id=260513)
>
> Inger...what a great update! I always enjoy reading about your progess and trials. IR / red and cold is a winner for sure. I love hearing your perspective on the winter...it makes me yearn for dark, cold, fire lit nights. I am really embracing the cold this winter w/ more CT in the ocean and face dunks. I try to add as much fire light as possible which makes winter come alive for me. Flying in your dreams is an exhilarating feeling. I love when that happens. Happy to hear your update!

Danny, I flied in my dream tonight again..! But it was different, I wanted to take a jump, I walked on the side of a male friend, and I wanted to show him how I could jump and stay a bit longer in the air.. and to my wonder I was able to stay in the air like.. 100 meter or so as we walked along! He was totally shocked, and I too LOL That was a really magical feeling

Yes, the longer darkness indeed is magical when you explore it. As I stay about 11 hours in bed and full darkness, I mostly wake up around 4-5 AM I guess.. and just lay there in the dark, and then I fall asleep again.. this often repeats itself 2-3 times. And when I then fall asleep for a short while, I often dream really cool stuff!
I have learned from SueUK to do these minim. 10 hour full darkness nights, and the extra dreaming (REM sleep) that occurs in the morning hours deplete us further of deuterium
I love that. Especially as I could not afford Qlarivia the last months. I do it with sleeping and dreaming more instead

It is not easy to let oneself get so "lazy" (cold, dark and boring  ) in the winter.. because it is all aganst the culture I live in. But I am doing it for me, and I know it is good for me, so I do not need to feel guilty. Because it feels really really healing.

I observe people around me, how stressed they are... how they suffer, but they are like in a hamster wheel.. when we talk, and I try to give some insight how to stop the suffering, I realize they are just in too deep, they cant stop it. Buying stuff, spending money, working a tons..exhausted.. buying more stuff, keeping up a nice facade.. working a ton..having arguments, attacking each other, being exhausted..pushing on.. more tech and TV consuming the little free time one has.. and so it goes on and on.

Imagine if everyone would just... relax.

My parents somehow have escaped this crazyness. But they have no TV and tech either. My mom have a computer and cellphone she uses for her work and connecting with her many children and that is. My dad do not know how to use a computer and barely his old cellphone either.
They always have time to talk.

You certainly need not feel guilty about indulging in more rest and sleep in deep dark wintertime. All of nature in temperate climes slows down in activity or hibernates during this tamasic time (tamas is one of the Vedic 3 qualities, or gunas, of nature-- tamas quality is inertia, inactivity, dullness, or lethargy.) That does not necessarily mean we, as a part of nature, have to be terribly tamasic during this dark period but should just acknowledge we will have that tendency.

I enjoy some extra time in bed too, and I am so fond of more time for lucid dreaming.

### Inger — 2019-02-04T18:25:45+0200

Oh man Drezy! What if I do something wrong??   But that is sweet she said that though

After reading Ari Whittens book Red Light Therapy, I am a little unsure how to proceed with my simple 100 watt incandescent infrared lamp...
It seems quite precise how one have to proceed to get the benefits of skin rejuvenation.. like, you need to do not too little and not too much, or ther will be no benefits. He describe in detail how much Joule / cm2 is needed for different purposes. But IDK how to calculate the lamp I have, and also it is just a single bulb and it emits the light very uneven.. some places stronger and other places weaker.
What I do now is, I do only once a day, and about 5 minutes face, 5 minutes neck and 5 minutes decollete and I move my face and the body part I radiate the light on, to try to get a bit even light dose. Not easy.
I am so happy I ordered the Firewafe light! It just takes so long to arrive because of extreme weather conditions.. I need to be patient! Then I can finally do it the right way   And get a larger body part in, yay

So well, I realized I am perimenopausal!
I get my period every 3 months mostly, for maybe a year? And lately I have been waking up early, and I feel so warm.... and sometimes... I feel a bit moody? Well, thought lots of it was from stress left over from my last relationship but I should have healed by now from that and I have worked tons with myself doing healing, which have helped so much  . Still I some days feel a little "off", like I can get a bit anxious for a minor thing. I learned this is a sign of perimenopauseee too... ahhh. . I was so relieved to learn about the signs because then I know it is all fine, and it is my hormones that are adjusting themselves.
First I though it was too early to enter that stage, but hey, I am going on my 43th year now! And I never was preggo and my period started pretty early and I never missed a single one my whole life so maybe it is time. It is kinda cool . Time to grow up....  So weird because often I still feel a bit like a kid..lol

I guess I really need more sun now! And I felt it... I missed it soo much. And then, yesterday it came out, and I was off work..
I tanned 4 hours nude! Most of the time, around 3 hours, I even did "vaginal tanning"... It felt soooo awesome! Sadly clouds then came in so I could not tan longer, but I can tell those 4 hours did something to me.
I had a lunch of raw Galloway heart from a local small farmer, fresh and nice - enjoyed it as carpaccio. It was soo yummy. I did it on purpose as I read that Q10 helps absorb the redlight of the sun... and I had a smoothie made of Wakame seaweed and water.Also went for a walk after the tanning, and ate a few handful og green edibles that grows here in winter... with a little sand/dirt on them to get optimal benefit for my gut
For dinner I had 10 oysters shucked by me, and raw organic salmon tartar
and all this.... made for the most awesome sleep I have had since fall!! I slept like a baby! Woke up and felt like I was in Nirvana...lol My whole body was deeply relaxed and somehow a feeling like all my body from head to toes, were laughing...
I also dreamed so funnily.. and in one part of my dream I got such a sweet soft sensual kiss...lol

The road to the shore I pick them is closed i winter, but guess what, The worker that works in the house at the beginning of then road is so kind he said he will be there and open the road for me so I can drive to the beach to get my oysters   Wow people are so nice I just cant believe it!

### Inger — 2019-02-07T16:02:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-262483>

I literally burned my butt off

I got me the Vitalux UV lamp 300Watt, and decided to bask in front of it yesterday.... First, I kept in on as I sat on my desk on the computer, and then I lied down on the couch reading a book and palced the lamp over my butt. Yes. I had no clue it was so strong! Later I started to feel warm on my face and neck and butt.. and I got a sunburn  but only on half of my face, man! Because I had the lamp on the left side of me. I have attended the lamp to a standing lamp so I did not keep it that close.. maybe 30 - 60 cm away or so.
I must have been way too long in front of it

So this is a warnig for anyone using that lamp, it is mega strong. Way stronger than my Exo Terra bulbs that I have used previously. I never burned from them...

My poor butt

I have put Aloe Vera on it, and honey. It is all sticky ... I hope it heals fast. What do do. Lesson learned

I still feel great tho, I guess I got some vit D in too.. I slept great even if my butt does burned a bit in the night.....

Now.. I wanted to sun the other half of my face today to get a balanced tan  and then... after a minute the lamp broke!!!!  That was a short lived fun! Sending the lamp back to get it replaced.

Then I had to run some errands so decided to visit the Zoo store to get another Reptile Solar lamp today.. it was really expensive, 59 euro, and only 160 watt.. but I still bought it. I came home and wanted to bask in the "sun".. and the lamp does not even go on, not once! Broke! Ehh... what a mess
I need to go change it tomorrow. Not easy, this hunting for sun.....

I hope my Redlight comes soon. It seems to be stuck at the customs in Germany...

### Christina Gagnon — 2019-02-19T14:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-263536>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=260494)
>
> ..and tonight I flied in my dream... sooo high, over green mountains and valleys. Amazing!
> Still dreaming so much and clearly every night, wow. And I never have to pee in the night anymore! I feel DDW helped with this. Because I sometimes had to pee once in the night. Not since my DDW batches I had during summer/fall. Qlarivia, 1/4. Nice!
>
> I was in Finland over Christmas, visiting my parents. It was really nice. Went dipping in my moms ice-hole with my brother, did snow angels... talked and talked. So much love!
> My mom had saved a raw deer heart for me! I ate that divided up on two breakfasts, raw, as carpaccio. It was so delicious! The best Christmas food I had all the time there   Made me feel really good! Raw, wild animal hearts always gives me somehow a magic feeling, very subtle, but it is there.
>
> We measured our ages on my dads "True Age" machine, and laughed so much! I still had the best results, being 14 yo  (I am 42 yo) My mom (67) and dad (72) both were around 10-20 years younger than their age so that was great too, but they are really healthy I guess! At least no complaints and high functioning. But my uncle that live in my parents house too, did the test, it was horrible. He has Hepatitis C, and was really really sick, he is now doing a bit better as he got some new medicine from Norway that helps kill the virus. But he has high blodpressure too and many other issues, Reynauds etc. His real age is 60, and the "True Age" machine said he was 82 yo. Crazy! But he looks old too..
>
> I have a new hack I am doing!
> And this is to see what I can do for my skin - I just quit using nicotine gums, after using them for almost 5 years..uh! I realized I had got addicted.. not to the nicotine that much, but to the combination of sweetener I guess? and the chewing, and the nicotine! But I was not able to stop because my life had too much stress.. and now... this winter, I have made enough space for me to clear my head. And that is when I was able to stop using them. Yay!
> But I guess all that nic.gum was not too great for my skin (in addition to tanning salong in winter) I guess it makes more wrinkles? So I wanted to see if I can do some good for my face
>
> I am not doing tanning salon anymore either, instead I really try go outside more, and I am planning a vacation to the sun if I can afford it this winter.
>
> So my hack is very easy and cheap, 10 euro
>
> That is the cost of a cheap Philips infrared lamp 100 watt. I want a better one, but I have to save a little bit to get that. So I have started with this cheap lamp.
>
> I do cold face dunks for a few minutes, then I use the red light about 3-4 minutes, and then I dunk in the cold water again, and do red light again. It all takes about 15 minutes max. I do this every morning and every evening, I try to do it near sunset if I can.
>
> I want to know if this combination cold and red light does my skin any good . So far it feels great! I have just done it a week but I feel my skin already is improving a little?
> I have not been doing real face dunks for long time! I do dunk my face a little when I sit in the rainbarrel every morning, but it is not so much.
>
> Cant wait until I get the real red light of Alex from the facebook group!  I guess that will be a wise thing to invest in
>
> Otherwise I am doing really well, enjoying the dark and cozy evenings. Not too many weeks of darkness left as it gets lighter every day now, so I really need to enjoy the dark silent time I have left before they days get long again!

I don't watch TV but was at my brother's home and they were wathing a show called The Vikings where one of the main characters routinely jumped into a hole in the ice "because that was their way." It made me think of you and your ice-hole in Finland - I really admire your stamina to be able to do something like that.

Try harder Katie...lol Sing another song! You can do it
That said...
I just broke my favorite oyster knife I got as a gift from my boss! It was sooo sad, this knife lasted about 4 years..... it was awesome! My boss is a star chef and he has opened many thousand oysters in his life and he knows what a good knife is.
I have ordered a few new ones... lets hope they are good too.

My red lamp adventures are doing AWESOME!
I already am sure my wrinkles have improved    And I have used it just about 2 weeks I think! The Firewaves. It is a great lamp for that money, really, I do not regret buying it. I ordered another for my dad
I use it for skin, thyroid, ovaries, brain.... so many things. I have a feeling it improves my energy, and also really helps mood issues. Sooo good to have on hand when it is rainy stormy and gray   (which it is right now)

I also radiate my hair/head, and in just a week I feel my hair has gotten more "alive"! (or is it my imagination that has become more alive...?  ) It is very hard to describe precisely what it has done to my hair but I really like it
I have a few white hair on the top of my head and I wanted to see if the red light can reverse the white, but now I am so happy with how my hair feels I do not care if it is all white...lol
I had no idea the redlight can have such an impact on the hair quality - and so fast...

### Inger — 2019-03-09T18:20:30+0200

That is cool Christina  Yes, in Scandinavia it is very common, really. 2 % of the population in Finland do it all the time, also the government have many places there in winter, you can go and swim in a whole in the ice, they keep it open with a pump that makes the water move all the time. And they have heated, small houses at the beach where you can change your clothes after  For everyone to benefit from

You do not really have to have much stamina... you just do it.. jump in... enjoy the euphoria

### Inger — 2019-03-09T18:53:15+0200
