Liesel, what light did you get? I really recommend a book to go with it... so you know how to use it better! I have Ari Whittens book, I like it very much.

I also have started using grean tea on my face together with the redlight sessions I do for my skin. I use organic Sencha, a powder that I mix with hot water and just put it over my face and chest several times during the use (my own version...lol)

Check out this:
**Green tea and red light--a powerful duo in skin rejuvenation**
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed...Dc7pff2-Zjdg\_2B3PX4xuhuLaBGwwjipYMqhgZF0IT7ww](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19817517?fbclid=IwAR2t0VvT7i54aODc7pff2-Zjdg_2B3PX4xuhuLaBGwwjipYMqhgZF0IT7ww)

it says;

"Rejuvenated skin, reduced wrinkle levels, and juvenile complexion, previously realized in 10 months of light treatment alone were realized in 1 month."

I think this is really worth a try

Kris, the guy who has designed and sells the Firewave, I talk to him on facebook and he recommended doing it to me, he said it should give even faster result  I have done it just a few days and I am thinking it is true!

### LieselK — 2019-03-09T19:45:50+0200

I think it's a great investment unless you live at the equator. I plan my life around the sun here in NC. If it comes out I drop all my plans and put on my kiniki to sit outside. But it's been so dreary and rainy and cold lately. My thyroid also won't let me do CT and Sun in winter- now summertime with my cold trough in the sun is different! It has to be warm enough with the sun for me to be able to get enough of my body out long enough.

I think it's cool that the Fire Wave says that if you buy the smaller one and decide to get the larger unit later they will deduct the cost of the smaller from the price of the larger! That's a great deal to get 2 sizes then for $1,000.

### Inger — 2019-03-10T18:51:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-265031>

> [Christina Gagnon said:](/goto/post?id=264928)
>
> Do you know if the Firelight has the flicker problem that other LED red lights do?

Christina, it does have flicker but very minimal, iirc Jack wrote about the Firewave and also Jasons redlight, they have minimal flicker at 3%, but Jasons light has "harmonic" flicker which is better than not harmonic flicker that the Firewaves have according to Jack and their testing. But other IR lamps have about 50% flicker, so they are way way better.

It also feels really good to sit in front of it. It feels silent to me, somehow.
It is small enough to take with you on trips, I did on a trip to Hamburg last week, I used it in the hotel bathroom, it was awesome

Liesel,
around Christmas, after the flight to Helsinki, I have had some weird fluttering of my thyroid on and off. I had no clue what to do.. I started to use selenium again because I stopped using that (I took it because i take Lugols too) eating so many oysters that are very rich in selenium. I though maybe I have gone too low in selenium because of the iodine. I had 2 months sans oysters this fall you see, I could not go pick any, it was stormy and I had to work too much.
I felt the selenium helped a bit, but did not take the flutter completely away. Once in a while I had it again, so annoying.
Now I realized, since shining the redlight on my thyroid it is gone! Totally gone.
IDK if it is that, or the fact that I eat tons of oysters again (stopped taking selenium when I got my hands on oysters again too), or a combination of the two, but I am so happy about it!

Redlight is awesome for thyroid, you need to hold it very close and about 3-7 minutes. I have only been doing one minute every morning on my thyroid, but I do 10 minutes chest and face also, and also 1 minute forehead etc.
Oh man I could sit all day in front of that light...lol But apparently more is not better with IR! So I have to be careful not use it too long....

### LieselK — 2019-03-10T21:50:57+0200

I though maybe I have gone too low in selenium because of the iodine.

I tried taking Lugol's (3-6 drops) and also tried B Complex while eating at least 6 brazil nuts a day for selenium. I got horrible reflux! I think it's from the B Complex, but I stopped both it and the Lugol's because of the reflux. I plan to try Lugol's again now that it's been a couple weeks. How do you take it? I think it tastes pretty bad! I can only get oysters a few times a month here which is better than nothing I guess.

Jack suggested that much of the red light for me because I need the consistency of the sunlight to reprogram my T cells so they quit telling my B cells to attack my thyroid. As we move into summer and I can get real sun then I expect I won't need much at all of the red light- save it for dreary days and evening or times where I am at the computer to combat the negative effects of the bluelight and emfs. We got a bit of sun through clouds today and it was SO nice.

I ordered that book you suggested! It should be here tomorrow or Tuesday! Yay!

### Inger — 2019-03-11T11:00:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-265081>

Liesel, I take Iodine just in a little water, like about 5 drops 5%, and 1/6 glass of water.. and drink some pure water on top of it. The taste does not bother me at all.
But I am pretty weird about tastes I guess... I like lots of things that most do not like... I am not picky at all. Also I could eat the same food every day and I like it every time, like oysters, meat and seafood... i do not need any much cooking or spicing up anything to enjoy it.

I also paint my feet soles every night with Iodine. And once in a while on my belly above my ovaries, and my breasts too. My breasts are so healthy and soft

I do not take any co supplements to Iodine anymore regularly. In the beginning as I started taking Lugols I took them all for some months. But I really try to eat nutrient dense food, like tons of oysters, and heart, tongue, liver, other seafood, eggyolks, seaweed, wild greens I find in nature and very good quality meat. Also sometimes good quality nuts, like a treat .

Iodine supplementation needs enough iron and vit D too, so getting sun, and eating oysters or organs is a great thing.
Tongue, heart and oysters all have at least 4 times more iron in them as has red meat! Imagine

I just had a few pieces of my fermented lamb heart that has been sitting for a few months in my fridge... and it had a very strong flavor of Roquefort/ammonia.. and I loved it soooo much, uh! Too bad I just have a tiny bit left.

Off to pick some oysters now  Weather is okay for that today, not too much wind (that is the most important factor) and should not rain either, hopefully

### Inger — 2019-03-13T16:23:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-265346>

I just basked on my couch in front of my vitalux 300 watt UV lamp and Firewaves redlight for 1 hour and 40 minutes. I could have gone on, it deeply relaxed me I can still feel that tingeling relaxing feeling in my body. it felt just so so good! I am turning around every 15 minutes, sitting, laying on my belly, laying on my back and so on... so I do not burn. Keeping both lights about 80 cm away. I am going to repeat this tomorrow. We have really stormy and rainy weather right now.. all week, so I need some treatment now.
I am so so thankful for my lamps.

I picked oysters on monday, it was the only possible day this week, because of the storm and rain, so now I have my fridge full again!
I have bought 2 oyster cookbooks, just to experiment a little . I have never cooked oysters really, I love them raw, but I thought trying would not hurt, I want to try something similar than what I got at the Hilton hotel in New Orleans after the cruise! They were so awesome, charcoal grilled oysters..... but I would just do them in my oven. The cookbooks have lots of raw oyster recipes too. Could be nice to serve to guests to make it a bit more special . That said I never get tired of my simple freshly shucked oysters with a squeeze of lemon on top.

We have had some beautiful sunrises lately;

[\[URL=https://www.pic-upload.de\]](https://www.pic-upload.de/view-36383541/Foto0255.jpg.html)  \[/URL\]

[](https://www.pic-upload.de)

[](https://www.pic-upload.de)

I got a huge very fresh Galloway heart today at the market.... feel like Christmas eve
Galloway heart is my next favorite heart after wild game like Elch. It is sooo delicious, to eat raw, as carpaccio.Those Galloway cattle have been on the pasture right around the corner where I live. The women who owns the farm with her husband reserves every heart for me, always when they slaughter one, I am soo lucky no one else wants them

### Phosphene — 2019-03-15T20:19:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-265586>

I’m guessing part raw, sliced thin as carpaccio with olive oil and raw onion and part stuffed into a jar and fermented. Inger’s food is legendary...I’m only now starting to really get why.

I’m ordering a quarter or half cow soon and will make sure to get the heart! It has tons of natural coenzyme Q10.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/this-breakfast-gives-crazy-energy.4497/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/this-breakfast-gives-crazy-energy.4497/)

### Inger — 2019-03-16T14:59:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-265627>

> [Ronald Waters said:](/goto/post?id=265581)
>
> I have to see what i can find locally. I am able to get grass fed beef liver but I have never tried heart.
> How will you prepare it to eat?

Pretty much what Phosphene said

I normally make carpaccio out of it, just had this for lunch, it is now in my belly  took a pic so you can see what it looks like. I am not slicing it really thin at all, because i like it thicker too, and it goes way faster to make this way . I just put the sliced raw heart on the top of organic rucola, some sliced onion on top...
If I do not get organic rucola I just do without, works perfectly fine too.
Yesterday I has raw mushrooms sliced too on top of it. And then I sprinkle sea salt and freshly ground black pepper over it all, and add liberal amounts of organic balsamic vinegar and some good quality organic olive oil
Takes like just one or two minutes to prepare, super easy
And it tastes better than many great meal at great restaurants!

[](https://www.pic-upload.de)

If I have lambs hearts, I sometimes fry them quickly on gentle heat in my cast iron pan, add some curcumin and black pepper and salt. Especially the chewy pieces with blood vessels and such. And the hard fat that surrounds the heart muscle. It is kind of hard to eat raw because it is very dry.
So I save those "hard to eat" leftovers from the heart as I have finished the tender muscle meat, cut into pieces and fry gently just quickly. I like that a lot too

What you see on my plate is about 300 grams of raw heart.

I had to eat a little bit of meat (rare steak) as dessert because I was still a little hungry, huh!
I guess because I just came home from 2 hours in the sauna..
First time I tried the sauna in the nearest town. It was so awesome, I did finnish sauna and the most of the time steam sauna... I just could have gone on all day.
I guess doing so much sauna makes you hungry!

### Ronald Waters — 2019-03-16T22:28:20+0200

It looks amazing and sounds quite tasty but I must confess that I have never tried anything like that. I will certainly want to find a heart to try or just drop by for lunch.

### Inger — 2019-03-17T20:09:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-265733>

Yes definitely try it out Ronald, I bet you will love it

Todays dinner - want to come over?

[](https://www.pic-upload.de)

I had way more oysters than that but they did not fit on my plate . Organic salmon chevice with ginger and Dulse seaweed, marinated mushrooms (i make them myself), and wild oysters with about one spoon on each, of a kind of vinaigrette with onion, raspberry vinaigrette, ACV and lemon juice. It was nice!

And as a started i had ( an hour before this meal...lol) quite a bit of this delicious fatty meat of Galloway;

[](https://www.pic-upload.de)

The Galloway cattle are all year on the pasture in a neighbor town of mine, and just eat what they find in nature. The meat and fat is soo good, this fat was nicely yellow and soft.. it was just a dream - to eat raw. Thin slices of it... I almost could not stop. It really was divine. You need nothing on it, not even salt or pepper. The taste is so good!

### Phosphene — 2019-03-17T22:06:05+0200

Also wondering if freezing meat changes anything in the fat? Seems like it can’t be a bad thing with deuterium.

That fresh chunk does look good though—I’ll see what I can find at the farmers market Wednesday.

### Inger — 2019-03-20T13:46:34+0200

I do not eat much Parmesan, only if I happen to eat in a restaurant and it is on the carpaccio. I do not do pasteurized dairy, and Parmesan normally is pasteurized.
But do I like it? Sure, yes!  I love aged cheeses  But they are like candy for me, instead of buying an ice cream I buy a piece of Roquefort and eat the whole thing..lol but it can be weeks/months in between that I touch any cheese.

### Inger — 2019-03-20T13:54:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-265989>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=265736)
>
> What cut of beef is that? I’ll bet the farmer will be puzzled when I request the fattiest cuts in the quarter or half cow that I order, instread of the leanest. Usually you get to pick some specific cuts. Is there such a thing as cow belly?
>
> Also wondering if freezing meat changes anything in the fat? Seems like it can’t be a bad thing with deuterium.
>
> That fresh chunk does look good though—I’ll see what I can find at the farmers market Wednesday.

The cut is Querrippe in German, on google it translated to cross rib?
I have no idea if it is the same.. I tried to look at picture of the English version of it but it looked different than when I put the German word into google. Hmmm..

I just came home from the market, and had more of that fatty cut. She told me this Galloway with the huge heart I got and these fatty cuts, it is a female one. And she told me females have more fat! I cant get enough of this fat. It is so yummy. So soft, I cant even describe it.
Must be because these Galloways can survive on anything in winter, tree branches, dry grass.. whatever they find. They do not need anything, not even hay. It is a very old brand of cattle surviving very well in the north. Maybe that is what makes the taste so exquisite?
They do not get any antibiotics, not even anything against parasites. I know, because when I asked for the liver she said it is almost always tossed because it has parasites. So she is not allowed to sell it.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-03-20T17:26:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-266020>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=265989)
>
> The cut is Querrippe in German, on google it translated to cross rib?
> I have no idea if it is the same.. I tried to look at picture of the English version of it but it looked different than when I put the German word into google. Hmmm..
>
> I just came home from the market, and had more of that fatty cut. She told me this Galloway with the huge heart I got and these fatty cuts, it is a female one. And she told me females have more fat! I cant get enough of this fat. It is so yummy. So soft, I cant even describe it.
> Must be because these Galloways can survive on anything in winter, tree branches, dry grass.. whatever they find. They do not need anything, not even hay. It is a very old brand of cattle surviving very well in the north. Maybe that is what makes the taste so exquisite?
> They do not get any antibiotics, not even anything against parasites. I know, because when I asked for the liver she said it is almost always tossed because it has parasites. So she is not allowed to sell it.

**Querrippe**:

"The transverse or diaphragmatic rib is the front part of the ribs of the cow below the neck of the cow and the high rib. The moderately marbled meat is used for stews such as goulash and as cooked meat for stews, soups and broths.

Cross rib is offered without and with bone, then also called ladder piece (after the ribs arranged like ladder rungs). Once the meat is cooked, the ribs can be easily removed."

Translated with [www.DeepL.com/Translator](http://www.DeepL.com/Translator)

I am turning around every 15 minutes, sitting, laying on my belly, laying on my back and so on... so I do not burn. Keeping both lights about 80 cm away. I am going to repeat this tomorrow. We have really stormy and rainy weather right now.. all week, so I need some treatment now.
> I am so so thankful for my lamps.

Any thoughts?

### Inger — 2019-03-24T19:19:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-266354>

Mjau, to be honest IDK. I just know that it feels really good. I do not get burnt, and it feels so relaxing and energizing at the same time. I turn around under the lamp, I do not radiate one place for 1 hour 40 minutes, Maybe 15 minutes and then I turn around, on the side, my legs, back, belly.. and so on. And I only do it when weather is bad and no sun, and I have time off work to do it in the middle of the day when UV is highest.
And.. I use my firewaves together with it, from a distance so I get red light all over my body.

### Anya — 2019-03-26T04:51:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-266455>

aww no
have you made icecream?
love roquefort too

### Inger — 2019-03-26T10:40:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-266462>

Anya, yes I have made ice cream too  or Parfait is more correct I guess. It is so easy, just whisk egg yolks with a tiny bit honey into foam, whisk some heavy cream into foam, mix both and add... wild berries, or cacao/dark chocolate, vanilla or whatever else you like for taste  and freeze. I use more eggyolks than I use cream. But you can vary as you like it.

My mom use to make this ice cream, we loved it as kids too. But she used sugar in it. I use almost no sweetener, just a tiny bit of honey. And I do it just a couple time/year if even that. Did not do it even once last year.... But it tastes really good. Had some at Christmas at my parents home, my brother had made lots of it for Christmas. Also with honey as sweetener. Was a little bit too sweet for me but very tasty .

### Inger — 2019-03-26T10:50:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-266464>

My landlords cat got injuries on his back legs - pretty ugly ones. Yesterday as I sat in front of my redlight in the morning he came into my living room through the open terrace door and placed himself between my butt/back and my couch. I sit on the floor always in the mornings when I do my redlight session, and have my light in the front of me on a chair.
When I was ready I raised and let the light on, and as I had placed it on the floor it shined right on him, he seemed to like it. I guess it will help his injuries. At least he is doing way better now! Poor cat. Normally I want let him come into my home, but now I have let him be here as much as he want to help him heal. Funny he wants to be at my place so much.

[](https://www.pic-upload.de)

Also the daughter of my landlord want to be here all the time. She has now learned to open oysters, she is only 6 yo. She was SO proud when she first time managed to do it all by herself! It was really hard to let her try it and not help her too much, she pushed and pushed and trembled and fought with the oyster knife and oyster...lol so cute! Now she wants to open oysters for me all the time....and serve them to me with a squeeze of lemon.
(okay, the ones she opens are often a bit full of shell pieces and stuff... but who cares... )

ohh.. and she loves my redlight too!!!
One evening she was here and I said I have to do my evening session, which is, I shine the light on my back for a few minutes. She asked if she can do it too with me together. I said, sure! So she took off her shirt too and so there we sat both in front of the lamp, shining it on our naked backs. side by side.
Suddenly she started to take off her clothes and soon she danced naked in front of the light....LOL
I asked if she liked it and she said - yes! She lied down in front of it and sunned her naked butt in the light.... so funny!

### Inger — 2019-04-09T21:22:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-267724>

Awesome day off work today. Blue sky all day. Spring is fully here
And I sunned around 8 hours in my Eve´s dress today!    Then had oysters 3 times outside in the sun too, around 50 oysters alltogether.. I just craved them so bad today, I could not get enough. I could have had more!
Used my new favorite sauce on them, so yummy. The vinaigrette I described above. I added fresh ginger to it too today. Awesome.
Then I went for a walk in the woods and sat down in the grass and earthed in the evening sun. Walked barefoot in the grass. Ate lots of wild greens on the way home,. What an awesome day   . Got enough energy I guess, for work the rest of the week.

### Veg — 2019-05-13T20:14:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-270350>

Inger,
These are Plant Grow Lights, which are very cheap and starting from 30 Euros. Why can't they be used as red light therapy?

For example, this one:

Cost: 25 USD

[https://www.ebay.de/itm/Newest-Red-...hash=item36364096bb:m:mbxwGr1qK6fL6K6Hgqz6LVw](https://www.ebay.de/itm/Newest-Red-Blue-45w-Led-Grow-Light-Indoor-Garden-Hydroponics-System-Plants-bloom/232838436539?hash=item36364096bb:m:mbxwGr1qK6fL6K6Hgqz6LVw)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Led Quantity: 225 leds
Led Configuration: 165Red+60 Blue
LED Wavelength: Red: 660nm; Blue: 460nm

There are many other such plant growing devices on Ebay, where spectrum goes from red to infra-red too.

Are the blue lights dangerous? I dont think so. And the blue lights could be covered too using black tape.

Question is why to pay 1000 USD for similar devices. It is too much money for such devices.

>
> Question is why to pay 1000 USD for similar devices. It is too much money for such devices.

You need to read a book about the subject to understand why cheap lights want do it.
If you are really interested, in deep, like "skin in the game-style" , get a book like the one I bought when ordering my light. Red Light Therapy by Ari Whitten. Others might be great or better, but that book made me understand fully why a cheap light is like trowing the money in the trash.
250-300 euro is still affordable, even for the poor ones.

### Inger — 2019-07-07T13:58:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-273800>

I got a lesson about how unpredictable life is

The evening before going to Munich, I hit my foot with a pitchfork, like quite bad...it almost went through. Looked like Jesus feet as he was crucified  I did not think it was that bad but in the night it hurt so much I could not sleep.. and next morning I was not able to step on the foot. It became painfully clear to me that I am not going to Munich with that wound... what a bummer
I just had to stay home to heal it. So I did. My heart did broke a little though
I had been seeing so much forward to meet Jack and others there.....
Told Ron he needs to tell me all about it and be my eyes and ears. So he promised that.
It was fun to have Ron here, he ate lots of oysters too, and raw beef heart carpaccio  but weather was not so nice, he came to see the windy, colder side of the coast here, which also is pretty nice and wild though

Life really is unpredictable. Better to get used to it sooner than later!
But it sure will not be the last gathering of members so next time I will stay away from pitchforks the days before, that I am sure of

My foot has healed absolutely wonderfully though. I took off the plaster the doctor put on it, and I just let it be open in the air and sun and light, being outside a ton, grounded, with lots of skin exposed.
Having oysters and raw beef heart to speed the healing.

Doctor wanted to give me a tetanus vaccine but I told him I do not want any vaccine, sorry.. but instead tell me how I know if I get that tetanus illness and how it starts so I will know.

It was a really funny situation, because I sensed my doctor did not really believe in the tetanus vaccine either...lol so he told me about the symptoms, also that he had never seen anyone suffering that illness in his career (he is pretty old) and then we both laughed...
Later I read about the vaccine and that illness a lot and decided as long as bloodflow is normal/good, the wound is bleeding and you keep it in the air and sun there will be zero concern.

### drezy — 2019-07-07T14:03:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-273801>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=273800)
>
> Looked like Jesus feet as he was crucified

Good clarification Inger, cuz I always default to thinking about baby Jesus (feet) first.

### Inger — 2019-07-07T14:10:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-273802>

Yesterday my uncle died. The uncle I had spent most time with of all my uncles.
It feels so weird, sad.

He got a huge stroke, my brother found him on the basement floor a couple days ago, unconscious. Helicopter and ambulance came and they did everything to save him but he went brain dead pretty fast. They just hold him alive until his daughters came to see him and then they took off the machine and in 15 minutes his heart stopped beating.

He had suffered from high blood pressure, he did not want to take the medication either.. and also he avoided the sun, he was very pale the last year.
I told him Christmas as I was there that he needs tons of sun on ALL his body to help the blood pressure issue but he said the sun is dangerous too. So what can I do, people do what they want.

The True AGE machine of my dad, as we tested it again at Christmas, showed him to be over 80 yo even if he was only 60. (my dad is 74 and the machine told him his true age is 45-50 and my dad does look and are way healthier as his younger brother was)

I am glad he did got a nice last time of his life living by my parents and having the son of my sister, little Jack, around him the last 6 months. Jack is just the cutest little boy.

### Inger — 2019-07-07T14:14:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-273803>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=273801)
>
> Good clarification Inger, cuz I always default to thinking about baby Jesus (feet) first.

LOL. Drezy....

### drezy — 2019-07-07T14:17:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-273804>

FWIW your uncle's disease is exactly the bullet with my name on it too. I take account of in my every move and choice.

### Inger — 2019-07-07T14:21:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-273805>

yeah. Me too Drezy.
My moms mother died of a huge heart attack, and my grandpa her husband died of multiple strokes. My dads mother died of Alzheimer.
I am doing all I can to avoid that fate. Thus being naked outside whenever is possible... and eating the best foods I can afford and minding my environment etc.

### drezy — 2019-07-07T15:10:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-273807>

We've got similar cards in our hand.

### Katie Durham — 2019-07-07T16:29:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-273808>

Inger, I'm sorry about your favorite uncle. The relatives who didn't have to die so young, or from what they did die of, have been my inspiration too to take control of my life and do things differently. Also sorry you missed the Munich gathering, that would have been fun.

I'm about to have breakfast. Oysters! I always think of you as I'm shucking them. Still have all my fingers.

### Inger — 2019-07-07T17:16:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-273810>

[](https://www.directupload.net)

### Inger — 2019-07-07T17:17:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-273811>

Those oysters are for you Katie
found them at my oyster beach

Last week the landlords daughter who is now 6 yo, opened like 7 oysters for me all by herself without me assisting her at all, and no protection on her hands, just with her bare hands and my sharp oyster knife!!

I was scared but I thought I will just trust her skills, I just told her to be careful or her mom will be angry with me if she goes home with a hole in her hand
I just lied down in the sun and did not even watch her doing it. She was sooo proud as she handed the oysters to me readily prepaired by her...lol
What a strong little lady

I am the "embarrassment-Queen"   but at least that makes for tons of laughing at work etc. and it is very healthy to laugh for sure

### Inger — 2019-07-18T21:05:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-274552>

I listened to the radio driving to work yesterday... and I got a shock!

The news came on, and the woman told that today Vodafone switched on 5G in Hattstedt, a small town just less than half an hour drive from where I live!!
And that they are now switching on 5G in many cities around Germany...

I would not have believed it if anyone told.. why in this small tiny town?? Om man, I so hope it will not come to my town fast. But if, I need to pack my stuff and go somewhere else. I am not going to live in 5G for sure.

I have one coworker who lives in that 5G town.
I am going to ask her to tell to me if she feel any changes in how she sleep, moods etc. since the switch onto 5G.

Jack was spot on with what he talked about at the Flowfest........

### caroline — 2019-07-19T01:40:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-274579>

I so hear what you are saying Inger. We go to computer classes in a very small town not far from us. I switched on my phone and it came up 5G. I am horrified. We sit in this class under fleuro lights and surrounded by computers ....we are getting zapped to the max!

### Saichi — 2019-07-20T20:10:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-274656>

Maintaining is only a few hours a day though he posted. Personally I need 90 to 120 minutes in sun and ideally sunrise and sunset ocean CT. So 2.5 to 3 hrs for daily maintenance.

And regarding 5G have y'all not been looking at the 3" thick horrendous power cabling installed throughout your town/city? I know in the cities their hidden better but in metro areas the cell repeaters are clear as day and every damn building crazy redundancy.

### Inger — 2019-09-24T17:25:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-278758>

Went to the tiny lake in the woods on my day off, and stayed there for hours. I was completely alone there for almost all the time, what a bliss    Only a few times some men passed by and said Hi  And a mom and her kid! Germans do not mind at all if you are naked - that I really love about this country
I took some oysters with me, some beef heart carpaccio and water. I got so charged

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

it was needed because we have had some crazy days at work where we got completely overrun with guests! What a chaos!!
But I kinda enjoyed it (and was able to stay completely focused at my work, which is incredible important when the capacity of the staff is at its limit - you cant afford to do not one single mistake!), and felt calm inside through all the chaos, I bet because I had the seldom luxury of a fish head smoothie that morning of the most chaotic day
I rarely get fresh herring or mackerel anymore, but last week they had really nice ones so I bought them of course, and did a smoothie again

### caroline — 2019-09-24T22:37:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-278791>

I think working in a restaurant gives us many special abilities. We learn how to be very focused and very sharp. We cannot have any wasted moments or movements.

We learn to be very calm under pressure ....and then we perform brilliantly!

Maybe being a surgeon is somewhat the same? But of course, not life and death for us restaurant folks.

I think restaurant people are ver special.....I don’t think many people understand how extreme the pressure can be.

### caroline — 2019-09-25T12:48:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-278827>

That is one skill I haven’t learned! Darn ....got to get my act together!

Do you remember the old days when waitresses were thought of as dumb blondes? That was the only job they could get?

In Actual fact it is a highly skilled occupation that not very many people are good at ....let alone excel at.

We were lucky enough to have some exceptional staff ....but there were many who just could not do it - no matter how well educated they were.

Like life ....it takes a lot of kindness and intuition and inspiration and an ability to care for and about others who cross our path.

We were so lucky to share our path with many wonderful caring people....they cared for us as much as we cared for them.

### caroline — 2019-09-25T12:50:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-278828>

Inger ....I am so proud of you for so many reasons. We have been connected for a long time now ....and your growth is simply amazing!

### drezy — 2019-09-25T15:34:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-278833>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=278827)
>
> That is one skill I haven’t learned! Darn ....got to get my act together!

I'm pretty sure there is some poet out there that wrote something to the effect that it's never too late to learn how to tie a cherry with your tongue. It must have been either Rumi or Maya Angelou.

Either way here is what you could do. Tie a cherry stem and put it in the back corner of your mouth. Just pantomime a bunch when you carry out the switcheroo and voila out comes the pre-tied stem.

### Phosphene — 2019-09-25T16:01:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-278837>

Yes I must admit I’m a bit intimidated by my new restaurant venture and not unhappy that the opening’s been delayed. My food service experience consists of the following:

-   Three years of part-time prison service in high school at Burger King. (Yes, three years!  Though I still crave their mayo.)
-   Living in the international house in college and being on the dining committee, which cooked and served gourmet (or semi-gourmet) meals to the house once a month.
-   Bussing at Valentino’s Italian restaurant, and, after I turned 18, cocktail waitressing in the ‘lounge’ upstairs with hideous live music and desperate love-seekers. Was once offered a $500 tip to head out in the parking lot with an older creep. (Can still feel his bony hand briefly grasping mine.) I much too politely refused.
-   24+ years of cooking for my husband and family, getting more and more adventurous through the years.
-   \[edit\] About eight months of cooking with my lover...yeah might as well include it
-   About 10 years of volunteering in sons’ various sports concession stands...swimming, baseball, lacrosse, soccer, very briefly football and basketball...
-   About six months (total over 10 years) of volunteer service in a yoga ashram commercial kitchen, buffet-style for 100-200. Vegetarian but made with much love. I miss that place so much sometimes...

I think that’s it. Think it’s enough? There shouldn’t be much (if any) waitressing, but lots of prep and sous-cheffing, and some amount of whatever-it-takes.

Yes I will handle it with ease, joy and aplomb. (And maybe just a few curses under my breath.)

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-09-25T21:36:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-278884>

> [Cuffy said:](/goto/post?id=278806)
>
> All true.
>
> Working for years in the restaurant industry taught me many valuable life lessons.
>
> Like using my tongue to tie a knot in a cherry stem (it was a dare for a $100 tip).

I like all the talk about restaurant work here. Brings back memories of my stint in that business.

Many years ago as young college kid still wet behind the ears I worked summer weekends as a busboy in a busy motel restaurant, and later on as a food preparer for a cafeteria chain.

Certainly it was not as 'glamorous' as wait staff but without me of course dirty dishes would pile up in a mountain and dining tables would look like a war zone. Worst part of job was washing down those nasty dirty dishes by hand to prepare for next step of putting them in washer. A thankless job but it gave me a behind the scenes look at restaurant trade.

I also served as general manual laborer, cleaning floors, and gofer chores. I still remember the stale alcohol smell of their separate restaurant lounge area. A lot of booze consumed and many livers were maimed there.

### Inger — 2019-09-25T22:01:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-278890>

Thank you for your funny and nice compliments!
To be a waitress... well.. I have no education about it (my eductaion is something like a nurse, in fact). But it is not needed at all. A good waitress is only born from the heart! You need to love taking care of your guests, that is all
And I do! I enjoy the people sooo much! Today again I had such awesome guests and I love to talk to them about stuff, today we talked about light pollution/darkness (with some astro-photograph-guests) and oysters and narcisissm (a dentist-guest)!

I just came home from work and should NOT be in front of the computer but I was thinking about something today that I needed to share.

So what popped up in my mind today was, that inner healing stuff.

I thought about what I have learned this last year and how I managed to start healing my own deep wounds and fears, through meeting the pain in its rawness. How painful it is ! but how it magically heals what is broken. And then I started to think about 5G and all the nnEMF and then it damned on me;

If we have ongoing inner stress, which unresolved trauma and hurt/fear etc. will create, it is just unconcsious but it is there all the time, it will be another stressor in addition to all the radiation!
And so are bad relationships that tear us down instead of helping us grow.

In these times where we can only partly escape the nnEMF and the stress from it (especially if you work, it will be hard)
it is even WAY MORE IMPORTANT to make sure to eliminate those inners stressors! Or stress from bad relationships!
Because that will just be TOO MUCH for our bodies to handle.
So in todays world more than ever before, everyone should heal their inner trauma to stay healthy.
It makes you feel that peace and joy that comes from meeting reality as it is - and not coloured through some trauma-glasses.
Because that is what will happen if you do not heal your unconscious. How you see the world will be tainted. Because of the own unhealed pain. One just cant see clearly....

I am still on this healing journey and I so do not regret it
I do not care if it sometimes is just freaking painful. It is so worth it.

### caroline — 2019-09-26T12:17:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-278925>

> [Cuffy said:](/goto/post?id=278892)
>
>
>
>
>
> My continual pet peeve is watching a customer ask a waitress for a coffee refill. She brings out the pot, tops off the cup, and then directly returns the pot to the burner.
>
> No! Scan the floor and fill every cup, even customers you're not waiting on. Bring both the regular and decaf pots. Carry little things in your half-apron pocket that people always ask for (cuz we've all had tables where every time you go there, they need something else). Is that too much to ask?

You’ve got that all nailed!
I have been thinking about this for a long time .....all of our truly great employees (male and female) were Doctors kids. They had a super work ethic. How do you account for that?

One of our favorite employees used to come for meals With her parents (oncologist) and she would beg Doug to let her come to work for us. She was 15 and had never had a job before. She was an outstanding worker but, more importantly, she was an outstanding person. She has gone on to do outstanding things in HIV. I have this huge smile on my face as I am remembering her. She made a difference to so many people.

And the best thing about her (sort of) everyone thought she was our daughter! At the time we had three girls working. with the same name ....one of them being our own daughter. Everyone thought they were all our daughters!

Wonderful memories of extra ordinary people.... I am so lucky and so grateful for all of it....

### Inger — 2019-09-28T19:55:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-279158>

Ron, I only walked at the waters edge up to my knees.
I so badly wanted to swim, as I got too hot in the sun! But Last time I swam there I got some weird spots/eksema on my butt (yeah  - it healed just fine in about week without me doing anything, but still...) because there has been poisonous blue green algae in the water this summer. I still saw a litte at one corner of the pond. It has rained way too little and peope have been swimming their horses in the water and that I guess was too much for it.
So I am waiting to swim in it until it cools way more down and it rains a lot agan. Then it should be fine .
You could swim there if you could shower after, but I cant because I want to stay there for a while, you know
Thankfully I could cool off by laying on my towel because the sand is all so wet and very cold! So towel get wet too just from the sand. Great grounding too

You might want to try fisheadsmoothie soon Ron

I had lots of roe today, at work yesterday the chef gave me all the roe from several Catfishes he was cleaning. It was big cup full! Wow was I lucky
I cooked it gently in salted water today morning, and ate it today at work - just plain, but with a delicious olive oil with lemon taste and some white balsamic vinegar. It was so yummy!! And wow did it make me feel good too

### Inger — 2019-10-01T20:09:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-279317>

Ron, are you letting your hair grow out..???
Looks cool!

I do know that my customers seems to benefit if I have excess energy and feeling very peaceful and joyful  I love when I sometimes am alone at work and I waitress the room alone, when the other waiters have gone home.. and I can create this really good feeling in the whole room.... it is like music of sort? I just bath in it..... Whats up with that!
Funny isnt it. How a stressed and unbalanced waitress can make the whole feeling in the room different! I love to observe these things. I think about it, feel it, it just amazes me
and then I realize how precious a healthy body and spirit is. Like, it is really really worth it to do the work/discipline of healing

### Ronald Waters — 2019-10-06T22:21:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-279710>

That's my Russian hair but I can't afford to pay for the herbs that make it grow so crazy . When my hair grows like that for real I think I will be famous on the forum and everyone will want to know what I do.
I love hearing about how you feel like you Effect the mood of the whole room. I sometimes feel the same way. It's a wonderful experience to feel better as my mind gets clearer and my energy higher. I'm becoming a real believer that it takes far more than good food but also a diet of good light, water, and low nnEMF exposure.
Without people like you and the forum the world would convince me I am crazy but thanks to everyone sharing their stories we can know the truth.
Fall just arrived this week in Iowa and now I'm aware of how difficult it will be to have a good light diet come December. Amazing how I was never aware of this before, maybe I had too much brain fog to realize it

Awesome Ron   When you start to feel the benefits, there is no going back

> [Ronald Waters said:](/goto/post?id=279710)
>
> Without people like you and the forum the world would convince me I am crazy but thanks to everyone sharing their stories we can know the truth.

Yes.. that is so true. It helps to hear that other have similar experience, because according to our society today we really are crazy...LOL
What helps too is to spend time with children. They still have those natural instincts and you will not feel crazy at all being with them
Yesterday my landlords girl come on my door again.. and it started to get dark outside. We sat on the couch and played UNO with a candle on the table... she wanted to so I agreed. I was about to answer messages on facebook after coming home from work as the doorbell rung- but I then shut my computer down and thought I might as well play with her  It felt like a way cleverer thing to do!
So as we sat there she said she likes it so much that I have it so dark! And they have sooo much light at home.. and she hates it...
I told her, okay Emilia, when you crave some darkness, just come over to my place!
And we sat there and played UNO again and again in the candle light.. sharing a wool blanket...and cuddled and talked. She never wants to go home.. her mom always have to get her.
She still feels it. The magic of the REAL

> [Ronald Waters said:](/goto/post?id=279710)
>
> Fall just arrived this week in Iowa and now I'm aware of how difficult it will be to have a good light diet come December.

Yes, I could not either if I worked much in winter. But I am so lucky to have a seasonal job so I can really explore the darkness in winter! I really really love that

> Amazing how I was never aware of this before, maybe I had too much brain fog to realize it

Maybe. I think the more you let yourself feel the sunlight and the darkness, the more you will be aware of how wonderful they are   (and how awful fake light is)
especially if you do it with awareness and being fully present in it.

### Inger — 2019-10-07T12:26:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-279728>

> [Marko Pollo said:](/goto/post?id=279719)
>
> If you are outside and watching for sunbreaks, its amazing how how often you can connect with the sun. I Shed clothes like its on fire when the sun comes out. I make wind breaks with hay bales and i can also open a shop window and get full sun for a bit around 2pm. Its always there waiting for us!

Yes!
I am off work today and spending the whole day naked in the sun   yay

I very often get a hour or more of sunlight bathing in the morning also now in fall, and when I ask my coworkers if they got to enjoy the sun too, they ask me, "What sun?? We did not see any... How comes you always have so much sun at your place? This is weird!"
And we all live in this same area...
If you start to enjoy every ray of sunshine between the clouds, you will get lots of sun

### Inger — 2019-10-07T13:45:16+0300

Thanks Christine  My boss knows everything about how I live, and everyone at work does. We have tons of fun about it....
I am a little bit in love with all of them

On my birthday it was stormy and rainy - the road to work was flooded at high tide. I guess some lambs/sheep at the pastures around the house drowned too . Our guests were so brave they just drove through the water! Wow.. it was scary. I was so proud of them that they came anyways!
it is a wild and crazy place to work at. That is why I love it so much. It is unpredictable. Nature is, you become so small and helpless in the storm.
The year I was born, 1976, was the highest flooding ever, at the restaurant. The water was covering the house inside too.

### LieselK — 2019-10-08T21:06:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-279852>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=278890)
>
>
>
> Thank you for your funny and nice compliments!
> To be a waitress... well.. I have no education about it (my eductaion is something like a nurse, in fact). But it is not needed at all. A good waitress is only born from the heart! You need to love taking care of your guests, that is all
> And I do! I enjoy the people sooo much! Today again I had such awesome guests and I love to talk to them about stuff, today we talked about light pollution/darkness (with some astro-photograph-guests) and oysters and narcisissm (a dentist-guest)!
>
> I just came home from work and should NOT be in front of the computer but I was thinking about something today that I needed to share.
>
> So what popped up in my mind today was, that inner healing stuff.
>
> I thought about what I have learned this last year and how I managed to start healing my own deep wounds and fears, through meeting the pain in its rawness. How painful it is ! but how it magically heals what is broken. And then I started to think about 5G and all the nnEMF and then it damned on me;
>
> If we have ongoing inner stress, which unresolved trauma and hurt/fear etc. will create, it is just unconcsious but it is there all the time, it will be another stressor in addition to all the radiation!
> And so are bad relationships that tear us down instead of helping us grow.
>
> In these times where we can only partly escape the nnEMF and the stress from it (especially if you work, it will be hard)
> it is even WAY MORE IMPORTANT to make sure to eliminate those inners stressors! Or stress from bad relationships!
> Because that will just be TOO MUCH for our bodies to handle.
> So in todays world more than ever before, everyone should heal their inner trauma to stay healthy.
> It makes you feel that peace and joy that comes from meeting reality as it is - and not coloured through some trauma-glasses.
> Because that is what will happen if you do not heal your unconscious. How you see the world will be tainted. Because of the own unhealed pain. One just cant see clearly....
>
> I am still on this healing journey and I so do not regret it
> I do not care if it sometimes is just freaking painful. It is so worth it.

So much yes! And I think dealing with the inner wounds are much much harder than all the other mitigation we do.

### Inger — 2019-10-09T20:30:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-279911>

Yes, it is _**Macrolepiota procera**_, the **parasol mushroom**

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macrolepiota\_procera](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macrolepiota_procera)

"The height and cap diameter of a mature specimen may both reach 40 cm, a size truly impressive for the fruiting body of an [agaric](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agaric)."

I never knew you can eat them and I never saw any either - but I learned it now, I am so lucky
Last week I saw them as I was walking through the woods and i wondered wow are those huge and beautiful, maybe they are edible? So I looked in my mushroom book, and they are!
I always look for edible stuff on the way
I am just a huge fan of wild foods...

### Inger — 2019-10-09T20:48:00+0300

wow Drezy, we are both 76ies
Children of The storm..... maybe that is why we are a bit wild?
My boss is too!
I know another friend of mine who is in my heart.. from my school years.. I used to lie down on the grass with her during breaks and we held hands and listened to the birds.. I cannot ever forget that. We loved each other in a special way. Like we were in another world? A beautiful world
And it had nothing to do with lesbians (the guys shouted to us that we were lesbians as we walked hand in hand through the school yard but no, it was not. It was something very different and beautiful)
I love men  always have.. they fascinate me and I just think the are so cool and beautiful!

### Inger — 2019-11-02T17:04:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-281700>

OMG this is so long ago ^^^
I cant believe I did that..uh. Jack is so right, it is NOT a wise thing to do. I learned that.
I have learned so many things, it is a little weird to look back lol

**My latest biohack is inclined bed therapy**
And so far it is really cool! I have just done it little more than a week, but noticing a few things already.. I think  Will write about it soon.

Oyster beach restaurant closed for the winter, my last workday was yesterday. A little weird and sad, because I love to work there, and also very nice to now have so much more time   5 months of lots of time, yeah
This fall I have no heartbreak, like I had last fall! I have healed!!   I am feeling like being creative again

### Inger — 2019-11-02T17:12:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-281702>

last day the restaurant was open, it was sunny and very calm weather, almost freezing temps though. ut beautiful! It was high tide, and I got this urge to go swim in the ocean the last time.. I asked my boss if I could take a short break, and he said, sure!

So I took a kitchen towel in my hand and run down to the beach, and jumped into the water in my undies! There was many guests walking at the beach so I did not dare go naked... But it was absolutely wonderful... oh man... I had to jump into it twice, it was that good. Water was not even cold, I guess 9 degree C. Way warmer that my rainbarrel!

I dried off with the kitchen towel, put on my working clothes again except not the wet undies, and run up to work again. I put my undies to dry upstairs where we have our stuff.
Later my boss came down and said, Inger, why dont you tell your guests you works sans undies? haha.. I asked how he knows and he said, yeah I saw them upstairs hanging to dry.. Ehhh
He thought it was too funny!

The guests that walked on the beach and saw me swim happened to be my guests later.. Because they said, hey, you are the swimming lady?? We just saw you jump into the water?! Nobody else was swimming.. they thought it was cold. But it really was not. We laughed so much and I told them I do my rainbarrel every morning and it is way colder.
People there are really nice to be honest  I am so lucky

### Inger — 2019-11-02T17:15:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-281703>

> [Christine\_L said:](/goto/post?id=279987)
>
> [@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) I love mushrooms with butter! I would love to learn how to identify mushrooms. That's on my bucket list and perhaps future career. Forager!

Oh yes, just start!  Buy a few mushroom books and just do it
I learned a new mushroom this year, the big one I showed pictures of
I am sooo happy for it   Every new mushroom I learn to know is very much worth it and adds to the variety and possibility to find edible ones
But be careful, never ever eat one you are not 100% sure of.
One single little mushroom can kill an adult! Thats how poisonous they can be.
And some kills you so slowly it can take weeks and weeks... before you even notice. They destroy your kidneys veeery slowly.
So be careful

### Inger — 2019-11-04T18:52:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-281803>

> [shah78 said:](/goto/post?id=281769)
>
> 6 inch incline ! been doing it
>
> since 1995!

wow that is so cool   do you remember if you felt any changes/felt better from the beginning?

I realize I feel really good when I wake up even if I have not done everything so perfect the day before.. Like, if I had some wine, too much food... I still feel awesome waking up. I do not get hot from too much food! I used to burn up in the night if I really overeat, but that does not happen at all when inclined.
Also no matter what I did, I do not have to pee at all in the night.
I am in pre-menopause and well, I have not had much hot flashes this summer probably because I get lots of sun, but occasionally I did have some. Minor ones. I have not had any since sleeping inclined.
I read several women that got completely rid of their hot flashes as they started sleep inclined.
That is pretty amazing! I wonder how that can happen! Really mystical to me

My grandma died of Alzheimers, so I always try to help my dad not end like that. So I called him and told him to try sleep inclined, and he was really interested. They say it is a great tool to prevent Alzheimer.
And it is such an easy thing to do, no need to change anything! If that can really be that powerful, it is just... wow

Cristine, this is how I did it;

First I just did books, because I started at maybe 8 cm or so. And then after a few days I added some more books, did this twice, until I was at 12 cm.
Next I went to a shop and bought me 4 stones, terrace-stones, I wanted one that was 15 cm high but they had none, so I took two different ones, that together made 14,5 cm. That must be good enough
It all cost me about 5 euros. Almost for free . This is how it looks;

I slept sooo good tonight, wow!
I could not be on my computer at all since yesterday morning because the guy from the computer shop, that fixed it for me over the day (to make it a little faster) and brought it back to me, dropped it on the floor as he entered my apartment! Huh I had it very dark, just candles, maybe he got so scared!
I felt more sorry for him as for my computer honestly.. he was so sad he did it! But now he has fixed it temporarly with another screen attached to it until he gets a new screen built in (laptop).
He is so kind, really
But yeah, I try not be later on my computer than 7 PM, sometimes it goes to 8. Bad I know! I do use blueblockers and Iris but still it is not good. So I need to improve that. But there is so much to learn...lol

Anyways my sleep was insane last night! went to bed shortly before 9 PM because I could no way keep my eyes open anymore. I dreamed absolutely cool stuff, lots of deep sweet love, and other funny things! Also a little scary! Very deep, real, vivid! And then as I slooowly woke up as it started to get a little more light outside, it felt like coming out of some drugged state. I was so deeply relaxed and my body felt like just singing from joy, it took forever to really wake up! hahaha.. it was soo cool! I wonder if my magnetico (I have a 10 gauss) also contributes to that deeply relaxed feeling. It really feels so sweet you want to stay in that state forever..

I have also dropped coffee again. As work season ended coffee went too
Now I only work a little bit, I have no issue weaning of that drug  yay  Also have had no headaches. Okay I did only 1-2 cups/day but still I wanted to quit because it is not good, I know. I have quit before and it always felt right. Just high stress makes it hard to quit.

Today I tried a new oyster recipe, chanterelle and parmesan oysters, from my book "The Hog Island - Oyster lovers cookbook".
I did Roquefort and Machego instead of parmesan though, both raw cheeses. And I left out creme fraiche. My favorite was Roquefort! But both were good. I had 10 really big ones. Wow I got so full

no sunrises lately though, it has been raining all the time! But Friday the sun will come out again! I cancelled my work at the vets because of that, uh! ### Inger — 2019-11-10T17:41:09+0200

wow thats a lot of mushroomstuff in my journal   love it!!

I have started running
Well... haha.. its more like half running half walking, and the running is very slow! But I am slowly training my body to run, and it is fun   I just need some type of exercise now that I am working so much less. My body craves to move, and I want to do it outside, so I thought why not learn to run. I never really have done that, only sprinted a little.
I only do about 30-40 minutes, and I slowly run longer with walks in between, just how it feels good. I make sure not to stress my body too much, I want it to be fun!  And on the way I come by the tiny lake (it had blue green algae and I got a rash from it in summer, so I stopped swimming there, but now it has rained and the water is very cold as we have had several nighty below freezing. So I guess it is fine, I have not gotten any skin issues yet, I do shower when I come home) and I jump in it and swim a few meters and air dry, wow it feels soooo good    and then I am cold and as I run I get warm again.

I had that guy (the same guy that dropped my computer on the floor as he entered my dark, candle-lit home -so it broke) from the computer store here to fix my computer, and as he did, we chatted quite a bit.
As we chatted and told our stores I told him my age on occasion and he also did as he told some stuff yeah. And it was really nice to chat, but then I wanted him to leave because I wanted to get naked in the sun!!! So I said uhhmm.. I might go for a walk now, so... yeah.. he got it and he was also ready with the work so he picked up the stuff and we said goodbye.
A couple hours later I got a message on my phone, that he had really liked my presence, it was so nice to talk, he felt this connection..he really did not wanted to leave and would have loved to stay longer (yeah I noticed that! ). and he wondered what I think about it and we could both have something to give each other. Well he was asking for a date.
I thought whaaaat.. he is exactly 20 years younger! I am going to tell him I am not dating now because I am healing a little more and I need some time for myself still. Yes, and that is the truth!!

Oh man.. whats up with that... as soon as you open up and tell about your life and what you do, even if it is really crazy, guys seem to get interested. I guess I am way too open but I have hard time change that. Just talked to my sister and she told me she has the same issues, uh! Well she is married so for her it is easier to kick the guys off..lol

My inclined bed therapy experiment is going great!
I have many night with absolutely awesome sleep! Very deep, very nice! Dreaming is crazy too..lol
I like it. If it is going to stay this way I am not going to stop sleeping inclined... this seems to have great benefits   But lets see what the next weeks will say

### Inger — 2019-11-10T19:04:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-282070>

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)
you wrote somewhere that you have had issues with cramps, have you tried to sleep inclined yet?
Look at the testimonials below, there is at least one story about cramps disappearing..
I do not have cramps so I cant say anything to it but I do can say it makes me sleep even deeper and also it makes me never have to go to the bathroom in the night.

[https://inclinedbedtherapy.com/testimonials/other-maladies](https://inclinedbedtherapy.com/testimonials/other-maladies)

"One of my patients recently told me an extraordinary story about 'inclined bed therapy' - something I'd never heard of before.

The anti-cancer drugs that she is receiving have some unpleasant side effects including joint pain and insomnia. Someone had told her about the benefits of raising the head end of her bed by about six inches with wooden blocks. Willing to try anything in order to get through her treatment, she tried it out.

The result was positive: she slept soundly through the night - and with no joint pain! However, it was the effect it had on her husband that really impressed me. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

He had suffered a life-changing major stroke over 30 years ago that had affected the right side of his body. **Every night for all those years, he has suffered from appalling cramps in the calf and ankle muscles of his leg - so badly that they forced him out of bed every couple of hours throughout the night. Since placing the blocks under their bed, he has not had another cramp!**

Traditional horizontal sleeping has been shown by some researchers to inhibit the ability of the body's circulatory system to function at an optimum level. This sleeping position causes restricted blood flow and the inability of the lymphatic system to flush out the toxins that accumulate in the many networks of vessels throughput the body.

By raising the head of your bed between 4 and 8 inches, you can increase the gravitational force on your body while you are sleeping. This posture creates the opportunity for your body to improve circulation without causing discomfort.

It's not a 'therapy' as such, but I've been impressed by what I've heard and read. It is now the subject of hospital trials which sounds good to me.

It can't cause any harm, and may just give you some extraordinary benefits. It's worth trying!"

### JanSz — 2019-11-11T16:42:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-282105>

[https://howirecovered.com/understanding-potassium/](https://howirecovered.com/understanding-potassium/)

**Understanding potassium**
[October 3, 2013](https://howirecovered.com/understanding-potassium/) [Eric](https://howirecovered.com/author/admin/) [methylation](https://howirecovered.com/category/methylation/), [supplements](https://howirecovered.com/category/supplements/)

Anyone following [Freddd’s methylation protocol](http://howirecovered.wpengine.com/active-b12-therapy-faq/) or any other high dose methylation support should know there’s a risk of creating a potassium deficiency which is potentially lethal. It’s called hypokalemia. Muscle cramping is one of the warning signs and I’ve experienced some of those cramps. I’m currently supplementing with 2.2 g of elemental potassium a day and suspect I still need more, so I’m trying to understand the potassium issue in more depth before taking more potassium.

It’s a complicated subject because both too little and too much potassium is dangerous. For this reason, Andy Cutler recommends you not exceed 1 g of supplemental potassium without “doctor supervision”. While I appreciate the warning and take it seriously, I’m saving this precious oxymoron ‘doctor supervision’ as the title for a new post about my pathetic experience with doctors.

The more I learn, the more I think potassium may be one of those cornerstone supplements for me and for many others. Most of the information on this page comes from Stephanie’s posts on [FDC](http://groups.yahoo.com/neo/groups/frequent-dose-chelation/conversations/messages) who just happens to be studying and experimenting at the same time as I am:

_These observations are all from the book “[Potassium Nutrition](https://howirecovered.com/understanding-potassium/www.amazon.com/Potassium-Nutrition-Rheumatoid-Arthritis-ebook/dp/B00BXIJ3MO/ref=sr_1_1)” ($4 kindle book on Amazon) unless otherwise noted – the author explores all the research relating to dietary potassium deficiency leading to a functional deficiency, and how it applies to many chronic disease processes (he essentially includes chronic fatigue and fibromyalgia as well). In some cases these correlations have not been examined before, in part because conventional medicine overlooks the role of potassium, and the labs that are typically used to assess potassium and electrolyte levels don’t give the entire picture._

**There really isn’t a good way to assess the overall level of our potassium status** (it is possible but the tests to determine this aren’t really available – it requires special equipment which is very expensive). Even measuring RBC potassium and sodium doesn’t give the complete picture – for one thing, our bodies will lose potassium from muscle cells at a far greater rate than blood cells- and this is why muscle cramps and weakness are common symptoms.

Potassium gluconate (one of the most available supplement forms) goes into solution easily to give potassium ions (which is the electrolyte), and the gluconate part is metabolized in the liver to create bicarbonates. Bicarbonates are also electrolytes. So this is similar to supplementing potassium bicarbonate.

**Potassium bicarbonate is alkalizing** and it helps to prevent calcium loss that can be caused by a functional potassium deficiency…. which are both desirable for most people – particularly given modern diets. Potassium bicarbonate is also the form most similar to what you get from eating vegetables. However potassium bicarbonate will tend to _lower_ blood pressure.

My sense is that salt cravings are a way to help raise blood pressure – it’s actually the chloride in salt that tastes salty and raises blood pressure. My blood pressure was definitely getting lower after cutting back hugely on salt and supplementing with potassium bicarbonate over the course of a few days. Note that [one teaspoon of potassium bicarbonate](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BENdKb9ROlo) contains roughly 2000 mg of potassium.

If you have low blood pressure, it’s preferable to supplement primarily with potassium chloride – and use potassium bicarbonate (or potassium gluconate) to help balance pH (if urine pH becomes acidic). Too much bicarb will be too alkalizing and will lower blood pressure so it needs to be balanced with the chloride – which does the opposite.

**Chloride is another important electrolyte** – it gives the salty taste to salt and tends to raise blood pressure (loss of chloride usually accompanies a potassium deficiency because chloride is secreted to maintain serum pH). So naturally, potassium chloride tends to raise blood pressure, and tends to be acid forming. Potassium chloride will also help to raise the cell levels of potassium faster than potassium bicarbonate. This seems to make sense given their relative effects on electrolyte balance and blood pressure and pH.

**An ideal ratio of total daily intake (diet and supplements) of potassium to sodium is said to be something like 5:1.** But, a low potassium to sodium ratio is the norm these days – especially if you compare that to the ratio that researchers believe humans evolved on… potassium sources were plentiful and the mainstay of diet, whereas sodium was very scarce. Paleo man probably had at least a 10:1 potassium to sodium ratio in his diet, and many put that estimate much higher. So even people who eat a relatively healthy diet these days aren’t necessarily immune to the effects of a lower ratio – given the availability of sodium, and the relative scarcity of high potassium foods from plant sources…, together with all the other influences that affect our acid-alkaline balance and electrolyte balance in modern life (i.e. toxicities).

**Now, even getting to a ratio of 2:1 or 3:1 is better than the average human who eats a lot of processed foods.** Of course, there are many other factors besides this ratio to consider – including the source of potassium in food and whether it forms bicarbonates and is therefore alkalizing (this would be the potassium from plant sources, especially leafy greens) – or whether it makes chloride and is therefore acidifying (from protein sources of potassium), so this relates to our pH balance.

**If a person is very deficient (at the cell level), then it can take a long time for general healing to occur**… maybe 6 months to a year or more. However, if you’re supplementing – say 1 gram with each meal (~3 grams/day) – then you should be able to correct the deficiency state in a matter of weeks, assuming you lower sodium appreciably and otherwise eat a decent diet and you’re getting enough magnesium, taurine etc (according to Wikipedia, “[In cells, taurine keeps potassium and magnesium inside the cell](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taurine), while keeping excessive sodium out” so, supplementing with taurine – up to 3 grams per day – may be helpful).

**Medications, and other things that might cause potassium loss** or create acidity (like toxicity from metals and chelating) have to be taken into account as to how much potassium is right for you, and whether you need extra bicarbonate to neutralize acidity. It’s ideal to get our potassium from food of course, but it seems that can be kind of hard to do, especially for someone dealing with toxicities.

Hence, supplementing with potassium chloride and potassium bicarbonate as indicated can help improve our K to Na ratio, and overcome the effects of “poor” dietary influences, medications, toxins etc. It’s worth considering that the author of this book suggests that, “our bodies don’t store potassium”…but we need to continually take in a lot of it for healthy metabolism, nerve function etc.

**What becomes pertinent is our ability to regulate the level of potassium in serum and in cells**… Which leads to the topic of aldosterone – which is essential for regulating serum potassium… The question being, is it possible one might be low in aldosterone at least in part because we are functionally deficient in potassium? And will slowly improving this ratio of K to Na allow our aldosterone levels to rise along with potassium intake?

The info in this book suggests this is generally this case (short of permanent atrophy of the part of the adrenal gland that produces aldosterone – or I suppose secondary adrenal insufficiency will prevent the appropriate signaling for the release of adrenal hormones, no matter how much potassium is available). Regardless, one shouldn’t drastically increase potassium intake without keeping an eye on our serum potassium to ensure that our body is handling it properly – as Andy has told us.

If we don’t have adequate aldosterone, we can go high on serum potassium… And there are other factors too, certain medications and kidney function factor in. By the way, the same could apply to cortisol – that is, secretion may rise as potassium is increased.

**Increasing methylation may have the effect of pulling more potassium into cells that are deficient**… at the expense of serum potassium – causing low serum potassium (this is [Rich Van K’s theory](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?threads/why-is-potassium-supplementation-needed-in-methylation-treatmt.18670/)). According to this theory, people with chronic fatigue caused by methylation block have fewer cells than normal. Ramping up their methylation cycle supplies the missing folate needed to make new DNA and suddenly the body can make new cells. This is what we call ‘healing’:

All of a sudden, the cells now have enough DNA to overcome the arrest of the cell cycle, and their rate of cell division goes up, making new cells more rapidly. These new cells require potassium, and their membrane pumps start pumping it in from the blood plasma. Unfortunately, since the existing cells, which contain 95% of the body’s potassium inventory, are already low in potassium, there is no cushion or buffer for the blood plasma potassium level, and if it is not augmented by increased potassium intake from the diet or supplements, the PWC’s blood plasma potassium level drops, resulting in hypokalemia.

While both the cell and serum levels of potassium are important, it’s the serum level of that can be very dangerous if it goes out of the so-called “normal” range. So, if you are ramping up your methylation cycle, keep a bottle of potassium around and know the symptoms of hypokalemia (weakness, lack of energy, muscle cramps, stomach disturbances, an irregular heartbeat, and an abnormal EKG) and hyperkalemia (muscle weakness, slowed heart rate, and abnormal heart rhythm).

**A few words from the author himself, Charles Weber, MS.** We asked the author of Potassium Nutrition, to review the summary above for accuracy and this is his response:

I did not see anything in the URL you sent that was obviously invalid. The body is a complicated soup, though, and it is easy to get things wrong. If a person’s kidneys have not been damaged, by poison for instance, that person can do some great nutritional blunders with respect to potassium and still stagger through. Healthy kidneys can handle some bizarre ratios since they have much more capacity than they need. The only circumstance that I know of that is desperately dangerous is the interaction between potassium and thiamin (vitamin B-1). If potassium is supplemented it is essential that **vitamin B-1 be adequate** (see [http://charles\_w.tripod.com/kandthiamin.html](http://charles_w.tripod.com/kandthiamin.html) ). And of course, if the kidneys have been damaged in such a way as to be unable to excrete potassium there is some danger from supplements. This can easily determined by blood analysis.
\===================================================================================
\======================

### Ronald Waters — 2019-11-12T04:15:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-282124>

> [Christine\_L said:](/goto/post?id=281894)
>
> Inger thank you for the encouragement! I will buy a mushroom identification book and pull a drezy by helping my mushroom friends out with some wood
>
> Your blog is always so happy and full of life. I love reading it.
>
> I'm also interested in inclined bed therapy! Anyone have suggestions for setting up?

Hi Christine,. I just used 2x4 blocks so that I could adjust up and down to find optimal height. I also thought about cutting 6 inches off the end of a log for greater stability. you could also get retaining wall blocks that would be very stable. These 2 blocks equal 3 inches so you can add more as your body adjusts.

And the healing might take some time, I give it as long as it takes

I do really love men, and I do love to be in relationship, but it can wait! I am just not looking for it right now.

I am really happy for you and your lady, that sounds great for both of you
