### Inger — 2019-11-17T19:04:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-282422>

I went for a walk in the woods today.. and at one point I stopped to just absorb the beauty around me, and I watched the sky and the treetops.. and all of a sudden I heard a loud sound of wings of a big bird over my head.. it was a big eagle, just above my head, it circled over me - very close. I could see its feet very well. it was so beautiful. Then it took off and disappeared behind the treetops...
wow

I also met my second boss, the lady vet and her son, they were riding their horses on a path in the woods, that was nice too

I am still amazed about the inclined bed hack.
I had tons of water yesterday evening before bed, and I was so curious if it would make me want to go to the bedroom early in the morning, but it did not. Not at all. Where does all that water disappear\`? I just do not get it...lol what makes it so different that sleeping flat... a mystery.
also zero overheating even with tons of meat.
I need to eat less now, uh! But I wanted to test it first. How overeating impacts sleeping inclined and the difference. And yes, there clearly is a difference.

### Theka — 2019-11-17T22:03:38+0200

Inger are you trying to cultivate mushrooms or just gather them?

### Inger — 2019-11-18T20:25:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-282460>

>
> Inger are you trying to cultivate mushrooms or just gather them?

oh yes Theka, please try it!!!
Did you read all the testimonilas from the MS suferers? If not, check it out;

[https://inclinedbedtherapy.com/testimonials/multiple-sclerosis](https://inclinedbedtherapy.com/testimonials/multiple-sclerosis)

Yes, adjustable bed is not the same at all. It must be straight, and inclined 5 degree, only the head.

You cant lose anything by trying, I guess, and it does not cost a cent, or well... mine cost me 5 euros of stones
I am SO glad I tried it, so far I love how it makes me feel  another powerful tool it seems

I am not cultivating mushrooms yet - just gathering them. But I would LOVE to do it, learn about it.
I have been working a lot again, huh! My boss needed my help this month... what to do, can not not help such a great boss
But as winter deepens I get more silent and I might look deeper into the mushroom stuff. I might as well buy me a book that I can study in candlelight during the dark winter evenings

### Christine_L — 2019-11-26T23:21:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-282808>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=282067)
>
> wow thats a lot of mushroomstuff in my journal   love it!!
>
> I have started running
> Well... haha.. its more like half running half walking, and the running is very slow! But I am slowly training my body to run, and it is fun   I just need some type of exercise now that I am working so much less. My body craves to move, and I want to do it outside, so I thought why not learn to run. I never really have done that, only sprinted a little.
> I only do about 30-40 minutes, and I slowly run longer with walks in between, just how it feels good. I make sure not to stress my body too much, I want it to be fun!  And on the way I come by the tiny lake (it had blue green algae and I got a rash from it in summer, so I stopped swimming there, but now it has rained and the water is very cold as we have had several nighty below freezing. So I guess it is fine, I have not gotten any skin issues yet, I do shower when I come home) and I jump in it and swim a few meters and air dry, wow it feels soooo good    and then I am cold and as I run I get warm again.
>
> I had that guy (the same guy that dropped my computer on the floor as he entered my dark, candle-lit home -so it broke) from the computer store here to fix my computer, and as he did, we chatted quite a bit.
> As we chatted and told our stores I told him my age on occasion and he also did as he told some stuff yeah. And it was really nice to chat, but then I wanted him to leave because I wanted to get naked in the sun!!! So I said uhhmm.. I might go for a walk now, so... yeah.. he got it and he was also ready with the work so he picked up the stuff and we said goodbye.
> A couple hours later I got a message on my phone, that he had really liked my presence, it was so nice to talk, he felt this connection..he really did not wanted to leave and would have loved to stay longer (yeah I noticed that! ). and he wondered what I think about it and we could both have something to give each other. Well he was asking for a date.
> I thought whaaaat.. he is exactly 20 years younger! I am going to tell him I am not dating now because I am healing a little more and I need some time for myself still. Yes, and that is the truth!!
>
> Oh man.. whats up with that... as soon as you open up and tell about your life and what you do, even if it is really crazy, guys seem to get interested. I guess I am way too open but I have hard time change that. Just talked to my sister and she told me she has the same issues, uh! Well she is married so for her it is easier to kick the guys off..lol
>
> My inclined bed therapy experiment is going great!
> I have many night with absolutely awesome sleep! Very deep, very nice! Dreaming is crazy too..lol
> I like it. If it is going to stay this way I am not going to stop sleeping inclined... this seems to have great benefits   But lets see what the next weeks will say

Amazing!!! Its always nice to know that others are attracted to you. Its very flattering. I love that you wanted him to leave so you could get naked LOLLLLLLLL

### Inger — 2019-12-01T19:06:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-282953>

> [Christine\_L said:](/goto/post?id=282809)
>
> Wow this is great! The inclined bed therapy will be my newest hack!

let me know how it goes!

I messed up a little.

First, I locked myself out of my car and I could not open the lock anymore, it was broken. It was not very funny because I had everything in my car, cellphone, phone-numbers, home keys etc. But people were sooo nice to me and helped all they could, and they called for help and then the guy got my car opened after trying for half an hour. Amazing  Waiting for that I talked with an old man and got to hear some deep stories about his life. I listened, deeply, totally present. and as I had to leave he took my hand and looked me in my eyes and thanked me with so much gratitude... for just listening. Somehow it all touched me very deeply.

Then I did a mistake of not keeping my own boundaries. Yeah. So I got stressed out because of that and it brought back some minor hot flashes again .... wow... Now I know how much stress also impacts it! Even if I sleep inclined, it could not prevent what the stress destroyed.
What I am trying to do through all mistakes and struggles, LISTEN TO MY BODY. To my Belly. And the more I listen, the better I can feel what it wants to say. And I feel all the pain, and it is not very fun but this is how I learn. I am not going to run away or try to lessen the pain. Instead I make a plan how to not have to suffer like that again! Sometimes life is quite confusing!!

I think I have gone pretty wild inside.. and feeling really not too well being imprisoned. I was in a city, and watched a huge church that was a "must" to watch, built around year 1200. It was so huge. But I got a bad feeling in my belly. I thought about all the workers and how hard it must have been to build it, probably many died or got injuries, and about the religious wars and ugliness of it all. I watched the other houses around and felt like I had to go home fast! I did not enjoy it at all. Felt like a prison to me. The city was like a huge prison! I did not care how luxurious or anything it was...

When I came home I went into the woods... and it struck me how different it was. So wild, so free, so much joy! So beautiful! I just cried my eyes out I was so glad to be back there! I could feel the raindrops on my face, I could hear them! It was so silent! It smelled so good! The trees just grew wherever they wanted, they were free! Grass, herbs, mud.... it was all so soft... so wild.
It was like it should be, it felt 100% right.
In the city it felt wrong, weird.

I told my boss about it later - how I experienced it and what a crazy difference it was to me_,_ and he said he understands. I said that is probably why we both love to work by the oyster beach, as there is nothing but the sea(and it can be pretty wild!) and the horizon around.. and sheep
He agreed. He also have a bit of this wild spirit, somewhere hidden inside....

### Bob Stirling — 2019-12-02T08:17:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-282979>

Thank you Inger. I have had a relationship with suffering for many years and just now learning what it has been about. Eckhart Tolle discusses some aspects of it in the video.

_"For anyone dealing with depression or emotional pain, Eckhart discusses the importance of keeping our attention in the present and staying alert to the mind's tendency to create further suffering."

_

### Inger — 2019-12-03T18:45:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-283044>

Bob, I am so sorry.

Have you read the book, "The human magnet syndrome"? It helped me understand a lot too. And what Eckhardt says is so true.
At the most painful moments after he left me, I almost got kind of panic attacs in the night/evening, it was awful - it was like weaning of a drug!!
And what helped me the most was, when I just lied there in my bed in darkness and could not sleep, full of anxiety and then I thought, what if I eradictae the past and the future completely, what will be left?
So I did that in my mind.
And it became so peaceful all of a sudden. I figured I was safe! My bed was warm and cozy, I could watch the stars through the open window, I felt the air on my face... it was all nice, silent, peaceful.. safe. There was no worry "in the moment".
So the panic went away.

I used this trick often after that. Way better than taking any drugs, or alkohol, or doing anything else to escape the pain.

### Bob Stirling — 2019-12-03T22:18:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-283048>

Thanks Inger. I am learning about a lot of things I never would have expected a few years ago. Thank you for sharing your way of being in the present moment. That's so true when all is well, calm and still in the present... without the burdens of the past. I am also not using drugs or alcohol as an escape.

### Bob Stirling — 2019-12-04T17:28:16+0200

Thank you. For me, as far as I know, it is related to the relationship I have with myself... lack of self worth, self-judgement, belief in having to struggle and be limited, the need to do things "right", things like that. It is turning the other way... but it's not clear, and the causes have not been fully realized.

> So I did that in my mind.

Thank you Inger This is something I am going forward trying to learn how to do...... Eradicate the past and future completely..... What would be left?

I have got to figure out this stress thing. I can sleep so well some nights and then BAM something sets me off and I am awake all night. It is such a switch.

I read something in Inger's journal how she thought if she could erase the past and erase the future what would be left? The present. She tried to live in those moments and that is such a profound idea for me. Erase past and present and live in the NOW. Thank you Inger I need to learn how to do this.

### Inger — 2019-12-07T17:42:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-283252>

Yes Alyce,
just try it!
I mean... I did not use it to cut off my feelings, I do believe it is very important to feel them all, good and bad. Only so can we heal our trauma. To let it all come to the surface bit by bit.
But it does not make any sense anymore when you should get sleep but you cant, you get stuck in it, and you just go into panic mode and gets consumed by fear....

It is a really really useful tool. It helped to make that what I perceived as unbearable, bearable

I guess if one can go to trauma therapy that would be the best thing to do, but I figured I could do it myself this way, as I had no possibility for help from outside. Okay well, I did not get the idea form me, but from listening to Amoda Maas youtube lectures and reading her books. That is how I got the idea to try it . I am SO thankful for all the help that is out there if we just look for it!

### Inger — 2019-12-07T17:53:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-283253>

I dreamed about Jack again! This is not the fist time BTW...
In the dream, we walked, and hold each others hand tightly all the way. There was this very deep feeling of love, connection, a very innocent, sweet, deep, very beautiful and strong feeling. Wow!! That feeling stuck to me the whole day after the dream!
These sort of dreams happens once in a while to me and wow are they amazing   pretty crazy

Then something very unexpected happened.
I got an email from a film team who wants to make a documentary about someone on a raw meat diet.

Someone I know who has a German raw food forum had told them about me. So they asked if I would be willing to do it. First I though, oh nooo I am not at all that sort of a person who likes to be on TV at all.
But then I thought, I might do it just because it might help someone? I want to try get some other information out too at the same time, like about the sun, seasonal eating etc.
So I told them yes, I will do it.
So now they have scheduled two days of filming in the very beginning of next year.....

Uh I am pretty exited now! It will be sent for Galileo, a sort of TV channel in Germany that makes documentaries and such, I guess? I am not sure because I do not have a TV or watch TV.

I have been reading Jordan Petersons book, 12 rules for life. It is great! I have been watching his speeches and his daughter too, who was terribly sick and healed it all - stopped all medications too - through eating only ruminant meat. Wow it is pretty amazing. He started eating like her too after she healed herself. Very encouraging to learn about.

### Christine_L — 2019-12-10T20:08:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-283405>

[@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) what do you make of the dream?

If they are going to make a documentary I'm glad you will be featured!  How are they framing the story? Skeptic in me thinking...

### Inger — 2019-12-11T17:13:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-283437>

Christine,
I dont know.. I guess I value Jack a lot, I care for him, you know? He has really helped me so much. So maybe the dream was about the connection I carry with him in my heart?
Another time I dreamed that me and Jack was sitting side by side.. we just sat there, and I felt this super strong feeling of love and connection between us it was so strong, just like in the dream I had a few days ago. Weird, I know..lol

Well I have been talking with the head of the film team a few times on the phone, and the want to film me in my life and how my days go along.. at work etc. That I tell why I eat this way and what made me change my eating.
Bad thing is that my work is closed for the winter. I only work part time by the vets in their home during winter months, and they do not want their home filmed...which I very much understand. They are also very much under pressure right now and their son is really in trouble.. yeah. So we cant to it.

He wants to film me picking oysters, and that is makeable! If there is not so much storm that the ocean floor is covered by low tide. That can happen. The water can be way way higher when it is stormy. So it depends on the weather
So he wants me to go for dinner at a restaurant with some friends... yeah.. and he wants to film that...(but people do not want to be in front of the camera here, so I am not sure this will work out..)

And he wants me to go to my doctor and that we talk to my doc! That is going to be fun.. if the doctor allows! He called my doc today so he will soon know.
And yes, I will have to show my food, how I eat, what I have in my fridge etc. I guess!
I think it is a little awkward to be filmed but I just need to think about how maybe I can help someone change their lives for the better. I am going to tell about what else I do too, if I get a chance.

I told him spring or summer would be way better to film as they then can film at the oyster beach restaurant and picking oysters is not a problem then at all, but he said yeas he would love that too but his boss want this documentary to be ready soon... So I said well, then you will get the story of cold, dark(I told them I do not use lights only candles etc) and boring me... which is also nice for sure! But very different from the summer-me

Yesterday I got my 1/8 cow, it was maybe 25 kg, now I have so much food uh  I had to buy a second fridge too, to get the meat fit! A small cute fridge. Nice to have 2. My other fridge is small too, and in summer it is sometimes hard to get all the oysters to fit in it!

### Christine_L — 2019-12-12T00:03:50+0200

[@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) have you met Jack in person? Sometimes I have very vivid dreams about people I have not met (online or whatever) and friends I have not seen awhile but its always only with those I've had a strong connection (either bad or good). I wonder what that means. Maybe that our minds are connected in some way? I wonder if they do feel it too?

As for the film, I hope they keep the documentary neutral and balanced. Maybe its me being overly protective or because I've seen so many shows on TLC where they spin the documentary to show the person as odd or wonky for entertainment. Some of what we do can be misconstrued, not that you dont know that, but I would never want that to happen to you. What is their main goal for the documentary? Just to show different trends in lifestyle or to credit/discredit reasons for pursuing raw/natural lifestyle?

>
> [@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) have you met Jack in person? Sometimes I have very vivid dreams about people I have not met (online or whatever) and friends I have not seen awhile but its always only with those I've had a strong connection (either bad or good). I wonder what that means. Maybe that our minds are connected in some way? I wonder if they do feel it too?
>
> As for the film, I hope they keep the documentary neutral and balanced. Maybe its me being overly protective or because I've seen so many shows on TLC where they spin the documentary to show the person as odd or wonky for entertainment. Some of what we do can be misconstrued, not that you dont know that, but I would never want that to happen to you. What is their main goal for the documentary? Just to show different trends in lifestyle or to credit/discredit reasons for pursuing raw/natural lifestyle?

Yes I have met Jack, it was on the Kruse cruise 5 years ago I think it was? It was a really nice experience   and I do believe there is something like.. a quantum connection

The film, well, I am not at all concerned if they show me as odd..lol I am odd anyways you see!
I am just going to be honest and tell them what I do. I have stopped having concerns about what other people think about me when it comes to keeping my health and sanity, huh!

So far I do not have the feeling they have some bad goals.. seems to me they just want to present a different way of eating. So far . But it is going to be interesting for sure.

It seems to be pretty impossible to get any other people here filmed though, like eating out etc(the filmmaker would like to film me going out to eat with friends etc). They all seem to have fear. I do understand. People do not want to be different. Yeah.  But they do like different!  At least I get very much love from people here and they are so friendly and kind to me. I do feel very lucky!

They cant go against the CW.
What if all my bloodwork is great.
If not, I will know what to work on..lol

### Inger — 2019-12-14T20:55:51+0200

She told me her new boyfriend has such a difficult time finishing the roof on the small hut in the garden that he build for her little girl.
And she likes to have everything neat and nice and now she is soo irritated about that hut that never gets finished. I guess they fight over this issue often.

So she said;

Inger, I have a great idea!! Please tell my boyfriend that a film team is coming soon and filming our garden, and the hut needs to be ready before they come!!

### Inger — 2019-12-21T17:16:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-283840>

The darkest day of the year... and I got to see the sun   yay!
Sunrise from my terrace today;

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I went for a walk in the woods
(took this pic a few days ago though but this is my regular round I always walk most days)

at it was sooo nice! The sun was about to set, I met a couple of women on my way and we all smiled and greeted each other, it was a happy feeling all around  People always greet here when they meet, I like that a lot

It is really dark now though, they days are so short! I go to bed at around 9:30 PM when I cant hold my eyes open anymore.. and then IO wake up way early! Maybe 4-5 AM? No idea since I do not have a clock in my bedroom. I just lie there and watch out the window on the dark sky until dawn(or until I fall asleep again, which also can happen), and I get soo many great thought, ugh! I can see things so clearly and write letters in my mind. But when day comes I forget it! I should write it all down, but then I would have to put on a candle, and the darkness-idea all gets destroyed. I want to have around 10 hours complete darkness every night in winter. Learned that from Sue .
So I do not write it down. But IDK maybe I should.
Or maybe some things are meant to only stay in my mind. But it is really cool how clear the brain works in the dark in the early mornings

Now it gets lighter fast, that is cool too. But I feel like really exploring and enjoying the reminding darkness before it is too late though.

I had a herring head smoothie yesterday, I got fresh herrings at the market again. Okay they were not THAT fresh IMO but what to do. I still did the smoothie, and it was not good.. uh. But... I can feel it worked something on my mood because I have felt like amazing today! I almost had got a little winter blues last days.. and today I felt like I had taken some mood-booster drug, seriously. This is not the fist time herring head smoothies work like that for me. Cool

### Inger — 2020-01-06T19:05:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284302>

Okay, my New Year update!

I saw the sun set the last day of the year... it was beautiful.... and then I went to work, to a huge New Years party, and worked until 4 AM, then I was at my limit. Told my boss, I cant do anymore, I need to go home. So I was home short before 5 AM. Could not sleep, I was overworked, after working in super loud music and more than thousand guests at the party. Maybe I slept an hour, very weird sleep(I had 2 espressos in the late evening to not be so sleepy). So I went up as it got light outside, and decided I am going to make a pajama day on the couch! Because I felt horrible. But it is fine if you can just rest...

Then my phone rung. It was my boss.. He asked how I was doing and I said, not good! So he said, maybe a little fresh air at the sea shore will help you feel better? Please come and work for me today because I am getting overrun with guests and I need you...
OMG I could not believe it. I asked him if he wants to kill me??

Okay.. as he is such a great boss, I decided to say yes - if he get me one of those nice oyster knives he gave me 5 years ago and it lasted 4 years! That was the best I have had. He said, of course he will
So I went to take a shower and I cried in the shower I was so tired. Then I showered cold, and went to sit under my UV lamp and IR lamp, both on. For a while, maybe 15 minutes as my hair dried. Oh man it helped so much, I got to feel way better. Went to work and it was very much fun   Finally got to work at my beloved restaurant at the oyster beach again  It is normally closed all winter but 1 January people walk over there (4km) and eat and drink something, it is tradition.

I got my blood work too. It all looks good, only my vit C was too low.. should be minimum 2 but I had 1,5. I had on purpose not eaten even onions for weeks before the test, only meat and fish and fat. But I have no symptoms of low it C? So I googled it and found out when you eat carnivore, your body needs way less of it and that is why it produces less too. No worry

My ferritin was 182. I thought that was a little high, but maybe because I am perimenopause? The reference is up to 300 ng/l for post menopausal. Hmm. I though I might start drinking some peppermint tea with my meat meals to prevent iron overload. Or go and donate blood once in a while. I do not want too much iron either. Hemoglobin was 13,9 (ref.12-16), Iron 79 (ref. 40-160) so Iron was not too high. But I think ferririn is what says more about iron overload? I am not so good at labs.

B12 was quite high, I think my highest so far, 1350 pg/ml. Should be at least 200.

Potassium was fine too, even without any veggies. 4,6 (ref.3,5-5,6) Animal foods must have enough of it.

Magnesium was 0,78 mmol/l (ref. 0,75-1) so I might take a epsom salt bath once in a while. I do not have cramps though.

Folic acid was 15,2 ng/ml (ref.> 4,5)

25-OH-Cholec. Vit D was 40,1 ng/ml(30 is recommended)

Cholesterol was sky high..lol But I always have that. 361 mg/dl
HDL was 100mg/dl
Triglycerides was 57 mg/dl
LDL 255 mg/dl

Liver enzymes and platelets etc were all in range. I though my liver values might be a bit out of wack as I have had some liver discomfort lately. But it all looked fine on paper. So I have been thinking about that. This is my conclusions;

First I thought it was detox from putting my bed inclined too fast and too high, which makes the body detox better. But then I read about the liver and menopause, Jack writes really cool stuff about it. And I talked with a friend of mine on the phone, and she told me too much butter can be hard on the liver if you cant tolerate dairy. Yep!!
I cant tolerate dairy well at all! But I thought butter was fine....
I have been eating tons of grassfed raw butter all this fall. at least 100 grams every day, in addition to fatty grassfed meat. Lots of fat. I guess my liver did not like it. Also my joints not. Because as I wanted to start running, my knees were not happy. WTF. I am not that old yet..lol

I have cut all dairy out now. No butter anymore for me And it feels right

Also I am narrowing my eating window to about 5 hours. Feels great too

### Inger — 2020-01-06T19:23:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284303>

My darkness experiment.

Yes, the Christmas holidays I tried extended darkness for several days.
This is how I did it. I put on some several hour long lectures on my computer to listen to, some deep stuff, and covered the computer completely so there came no lights out. Covered the windows to the street side where some lamp light get into the room, and blew out my candles at 6 PM. So I was in complete darkness until I got so tired I had to go to bed. I managed to close the computer very carefully so I got almost no light at all on my eyes.
I stayed in bed until it got light outside. So it was about 16 hours of darkness. I did this 3 days in a row. It was a very nice experience! Almost a little psychedelic?

I could concentrate so well on what was said in the lectures! Wow.. all became so crystal clear. There was enough moon/skylight from the window so I could do a foot bath. That was all I could do, except listen. I also have white furniture and carpet so it helped of course. But I had to be very slow and careful when moving.

I think the body makes tons of melatonin when you have it so dark, because I got very drowsy. also next day I felt a little drowsy somehow? Very relaxed. Maybe that is how we are supposed to feel when hibernating??

Then, I of course made sure to get about an hour of outside time in the daylight. So as I walked in the woods I realized something weird. I have never thought about that before. All of a sudden I felt like my sight was so narrow, so incomplete. I had to turn around to see what was behind me! And on the sides. I realized how different it was in the dark. I could see everything! It was just dark. But it was the same in front of me and behind me, all was pretty black. It felt like... freedom! So weird!

So I realized how limited my awareness was and my eyesight.
In the darkness I became so unlimited. It felt like freedom somehow, even if it was a little bit like laying in a coffin too...lol
Very cool. It is easy to get addicted to that stuff. The darkness has something very very nice with it!
I think it also makes you softer. Because it makes you feel more.
Too sad the world we live in does not appreciate darkness, really.- Imagine one would be at a party, and all would be in darkness, just sitting there, talking. How cool would that be

### Inger — 2020-01-10T22:02:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284395>

Guys, I am not supposed to tell this before the short movie is in TV, in 2 weeks. But I cant keep it. And you are far from here so it might not matter.

And the best came last. Today, at the doctors office. It was an awesome doctor!!!
He said my cholesterol levels were so high he could not even fit them into his cardiovascular-risk detector - the calculator did not allow for such high levels! But as he lowered them a tiny bit to the highest allowable, he got the readings that my risk was under 1 %. So he said I have no worries at all My HDL is so high, that is why.. and he took my blood pressure and he said it was so perfect

Then he did a sonography of my arteries on my neck and the ones up to my brain. I was soooo nervous they will be all clogged, as I have been a smoker for many years, ans used way too much nicotine for many years and had tons of stress etc.

I could see the arteries on the display at my feet.

As he checked them, he was totally surprised..
He said they were perfect!! No plaque at all, they were just textbook perfect. They were completely CLEAN! The blood flow was perfect too. WOW
He said, there is nothing I can do for this lady, no pills, no medications, nothing. She is all good
Wow.. what will people think. As in Germany now, Vegan is in, and people are afraid of animal fats. Or too much fats anyways.

After the filming, I and he had talks.. and it was so cool. We even talked about orgasms in our dreams... I love that doctor. He hugged me as we left. Hugged! No doctor does that here. I want him to be my doc, but he is far too far away, not in my town, We had to drive almost 2 hours.
It was the by far most pleasant experience I have ever had with a doc

I had SO MUCH FUN filming! The filming team were awesome. Yesterday after filming was over we sat and talked in the resturant for hours, had some beer and wine, and we talked about nnEMF, and how bad it is and other deep stuff. They were so open and honest. It was a bliss to be with them, with people who think. Beautiful hearts. It was sad to leave them. We all hugged and felt sadness to separate. And the lady who did the sound, she said, oh man, I cant believe you have such clear arteries! I might ave to start eating like you too!

I had to take Taxi home from the railway station as there are no buses here. And the Taxi driver and I had talks.
And he told me his stories and I sensed he is special too and he and his kids walk a lot, many many KM. And as I told him I dislike technology and he agreed.. and he was all about not having tech and being outside and not using cellphone!! Wow, it makes me so happy when people think and live like that... it gives me hope. Seems those people are everywhere!! You just have to start talking and you will see them. Yay

I am so glad I did the movie thing.

### Danica — 2020-01-11T03:51:42+0200

Hi Inger, I just came across your journal - thank you for sharing so much! It is very inspiring!!! When I was going through your latest thoughts, I came across how you used UV and IR light when you were exhausted because you were working until 4am in the restaurant and had to go back the next day. I have not been a member for too long and am quite confused about what light to use inside. ### Saichi — 2020-01-11T20:56:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284448>

Inger I'm very curious what your home RF exposure is, mind sharing?

edit: Looking for your raw food burger recipe I found this:

> The worst thing I experienced was a building in a small sweet town, the name of the building was "Gesundheitscentrum" and it means "the center of Health".. a hospital. And just a few feet away from it was a huge cellphone tower.. and as we drove past the building my meter showed like way more than 400 milliwatt RF. It was so unreal.... how can this be allowed?????? My meter went crazy and beeped and it was all red.....
>
> I realized there are a lot of towers I could not see but my meter went real high, like way more than 400 milliwatt RF (=400 000 microwatt) and even more... a few times it showed 0 (zero) because it could not go higher. And it was on places like small towns with lots of nature around... scary!!! Who would have known!!!
>
> **We all need a meter these days, I would say it is a MUST**. It cost less than 200 bucks and it has huge benefits so we can know _what we cant see and feel_.
> I have around 45 microwatt in my home, that is 0,045 milliwatt (RF). Some places even lower. But I cant go under 0,030 microwatt. I put many layers of tinfoil around the meter but it still measured around 0,030 microwatt so I guess it must be somehow calibrated that way?

You should be able to get under 30 mics with better shielding.

### Saichi — 2020-01-12T21:50:52+0200

I'm thinking better to make a fondue separate that way the burger doesn't have to go into the broiler at all.

I used fresh ground but need to get some 4 week dry age to dehydrate.

### Christine_L — 2020-01-15T00:32:14+0200

I love this experiment! Again, Inger you are my spirit woman! This statement resonates with me. Maybe that's why everything feels more romantic in the dark. You rely on your other senses because your eyes are limited. With artificial lights everything is harsh and looks ugly, even more so at night since that is what people rely on. Our culture likes to cover up more flaws and bumps because of "bad lighting". What if we did more to embrace night for what it is? Time has not been good to reality. Day is not day, night is not night. How does anything make sense?

"_It is easy to get addicted to that stuff. The darkness has something very very nice with it!
I think it also makes you softer. Because it makes you feel more.
Too sad the world we live in does not appreciate darkness, really."_

### Inger — 2020-01-15T12:08:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284617>

> [Danica said:](/goto/post?id=284419)
>
> Hi Inger, I just came across your journal - thank you for sharing so much! It is very inspiring!!! When I was going through your latest thoughts, I came across how you used UV and IR light when you were exhausted because you were working until 4am in the restaurant and had to go back the next day. I have not been a member for too long and am quite confused about what light to use inside. I use candles at night but would UV and IR light be helpful during a day when inside or just when you are very tired and then use it only for 10-20min? Also do you have red light instead of normal light in the house (as in lightbulbs)? How do you know what brand/type to pick for each?

Hi Danica,
I eat breakfast in front of my IR lamp every morning - except when weather allows me to do it outside. Nothing wins real morning sun/sunrise! I do make sure to get some morning light at sunrise in my eyes anyways though, in addition to the redlight session. I use it maybe 10-30 minutes, depends on how far away I keep it.
I also use my Vitalux UVA/UVB/IR once in a while around noon. Like today, it is very dark and gray and stormy, and I have it at the side of my desk to get a little bit light into my soul  I limit the use though, maybe 30 min. to around an hour. And not every day either.

Otherwise I have a old blacklight on at daytime, and after dark I use candles, and also sometimes I leave my red salt lamp 25 watts on for a few hours but not close to me(on the other side of the room), as it has a high magnetic/electric reading.
I do not use other lamps really. I have a 300 watt incandescent Edison light bulb that I wanted to use at daytime, but somehow I do not like it.... I rather just use the daylight that comes into my window. I have my desk by the window for that reason.

As soon as the weather gets warmer and better I am outside a ton. I do not really spend time inside much at all, except what is a must (stuff that needs to be done in the home.. and of course at work I have to spend time inside partly)

How do I know what brand to pick? Hmmm.. I just have researched, and try to do the best I can. It is a work i progress
You need to watch what is the light spectrum of the lamp. Candles are of course the only one I can recommend. But the rest are just helpers. So I try to choose the least damaging ones, that might have some benefits too.

[@Saichi](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23447/)

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=284448)
>
> Inger I'm very curious what your home RF exposure is, mind sharing?

Well, my ESI24 meter shows all green at the places I spend most time, and that is enough for me. I do get some of the wifi through my walls though. So it is not perfect. But it seems that I am able to tolerate this amount at this point of my life
Next celltower is about 2 km away. What I guess are the downsides are neighbors wifi and windmills. They are everywhere here. Closest to my home is a few km away. Still I do not like it. But as I said, I feel pretty good so for now I will take it. So many positives about this place and my work situation, so it balances it out right now  How long into the future, no one knows!

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=284472)
>
> The raw food burger's better than traditional black blue or rare! I did 105 deg for 12 hours and then broil melted a slice of pepper jack for a couple minutes on top and finished with wild apple sage jam.
>
> I'm thinking better to make a fondue separate that way the burger doesn't have to go into the broiler at all.
>
> I used fresh ground but need to get some 4 week dry age to dehydrate.

Yum, sounds delicious   I often buy ground meat in bulk, 3-4 kg, and let it sit in a glass bowl in my fridge, I use it up during a week time. At the end it gets a little fermented and really taste gets better every day  The time is too short to make it stink like high meat, which is cool.

Yes, Christine, I love it too.... Oh man, can you imagine when they were filming here. It was so awful as they had their LED lamps with the horrible light and I had to sit in front of that! THAT part I REALLY did not like about the filming. I was not allowed to use blueblockers either. Man it disturbed me.
In addition some weird feelings came onto the surface of past trauma from my last relationship, feelings about not being good enough, being ugly, worthless etc. I can tell you it was not easy for me to be in front of the camera. IDK why it triggered that, but maybe because I felt like being exposed? Not being able to choose my lightning, where I eat my food etc? And my ex saying stuff that hurt me deeply, like; look in the mirror how you look! You have changed. Have no one told you about it? In a way that I had to think I must be so ugly and full of wrinkles. He told me I was so wrinkly. Yeah stuff like that. I cant even believe I tolerated that abuse. But that is when they get you addicted, and then they can devalue you. I learned the lesson of my life though. Why it could happen in the first place.. very interesting and eye opening.

Today I though about it in bed at dawn.
Truth, turds and diamonds.

How I i my life have somehow always managed to turn turds into diamonds
And that is not a good thing. I thought it was, but now I see it is not. Because a turd is a turd. It cant turn into a diamond.
The only thing I managed to do was, running away from reality!! How useful is that??
Not useful at all but harmful, if you ask me. The reality is going to be all screwed up...and no good decisions can be made on another base than truth. Only the truth, the bare reality as it truly is, can help you choose wisely.

I know, it is painful....truth/reality is often very painful, there is no denying that.
I must have not wanted to feel the pain, so I was running away.. continuing making diamonds out of turds..ha.
I am aware of that now. I still struggle at time though, but I now see clearly where it leads when you live in a fantasy world. You will crash.. and hard.

Yeah, that was my thoughts about  today

### Saichi — 2020-01-15T21:42:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284660>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=284617)
>
> Well, my ESI24 meter shows all green at the places I spend most time, and that is enough for me. I do get some of the wifi through my walls though. So it is not perfect. But it seems that I am able to tolerate this amount at this point of my life
> Next celltower is about 2 km away. What I guess are the downsides are neighbors wifi and windmills. They are everywhere here. Closest to my home is a few km away. Still I do not like it. But as I said, I feel pretty good so for now I will take it. So many positives about this place and my work situation, so it balances it out right now  How long into the future, no one knows!

Over 100 mics is RED (high alert) on my meter.

I recommend continued monitoring as people add new stronger devices and of course watch for any new LED lampposts around your neighborhood which are operating at sub-gigahertz. Most people have been duped to believe 5G as mostly millimeter when it's actually just densification of 4G!

### Christine_L — 2020-01-15T22:04:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284663>

[@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) ugh again I feel like you wrote my thoughts. When I deal with difficult people and situations - and when I say must I mean forced without physical force - people like you and I become so tough and accustomed to the way of living that we do not see - as you say, a turd! Because one must stay positive for self preservation, it becomes second nature to allow things to pass over us that you would never accept in a better state. You always make the best of a situation because you have to... Perhaps a long way of talking about the slow boil?

My concern is I wonder how to truly define evolution of thought, wants and seeing others as good versus accepting a not ideal situation as not so bad. I used to want a lot of things that probably were never really real or valuable to begin with, like a swanky lifestyle, objects, vacations, prestige, etc. I experienced alot of negative and sad life situations and began not to care about those things... I became content with simplicity and one person truly showed me unconditional love and gave me simple happiness. I still wonder if I let go of a part of myself or maybe I let evolution take its course. I do wonder if I gave up too early or gave IN and let go of the previous mentality that actually would never let me be truly happy. Over-consumption is replacing fulfilling internal happiness with things... that's the wrong way.

As I wrote that I gave myself an answer - it would be that you feel more confident, healthier, and happier in the new way. Thriving versus survival. We are allowed to change beliefs and who we are to adapt to new situations. But if the person or situation causes us constant difficulty, sadness, stress, dislike of ourselves... then its a turd pretending to be a diamond...

This is so beautiful  I am so happy for you!

> [Christine\_L said:](/goto/post?id=284663)

Look, yes, you gave the answer yourself
Even if our experiences were maybe really painful and horrible, it helped us se what really matters  So indeed all the pain was a blessing after all

> [Christine\_L said:](/goto/post?id=284663)
>
> But if the person or situation causes us constant difficulty, sadness, stress, dislike of ourselves... then its a turd pretending to be a diamond...

Yes. The pretending "thing" is the problem. It would not be difficult if people were 100% honest about their intentions and who they are. But no all people are, some pretend to be the opposite of the real them. Time will tell though, and also our belly will.

But I did not listen to the tiny, uneasy feeling in my belly. The pretending was all too professional AND I wanted it to be true.
Yes, eh! That was the problem! I wanted something to be what it was not.

I have learned to examine my "wanting", why, and the deep reasons to it, and also my belly feelings more and more. It helps so much  and it is such an interesting journey. Might be very painful at times but growing always will hurt  Just accept it.

### caroline — 2020-01-18T03:25:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284768>

growing and changing when necessary ....that is what this journey is all about.

We need to push ourselves gently but firmly.

No snoozing on the job! our job of living our best life, putting ourselves out there and considering all the possibilities.

Never, ever settle! We deserve the best that Life has to offer....but we can't sit and wait - we have to actively search for our truth.

We have to reach and stretch and desire the fullness of life ....It can be scary and unsettling and sometimes lonely - but the rewards are enormous.

My Mother used to tell me that I always wanted too much. Why would you limit yourself that way?

Good thing I never listened!!!

### Danica — 2020-01-19T07:05:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284799>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=284617)
>
> Hi Danica,
> I eat breakfast in front of my IR lamp every morning - except when weather allows me to do it outside. Nothing wins real morning sun/sunrise! I do make sure to get some morning light at sunrise in my eyes anyways though, in addition to the redlight session. I use it maybe 10-30 minutes, depends on how far away I keep it.
> I also use my Vitalux UVA/UVB/IR once in a while around noon. Like today, it is very dark and gray and stormy, and I have it at the side of my desk to get a little bit light into my soul  I limit the use though, maybe 30 min. to around an hour. And not every day either.
>
> Otherwise I have a old blacklight on at daytime, and after dark I use candles, and also sometimes I leave my red salt lamp 25 watts on for a few hours but not close to me(on the other side of the room), as it has a high magnetic/electric reading.
> I do not use other lamps really. I have a 300 watt incandescent Edison light bulb that I wanted to use at daytime, but somehow I do not like it.... I rather just use the daylight that comes into my window. I have my desk by the window for that reason.
>
> As soon as the weather gets warmer and better I am outside a ton. I do not really spend time inside much at all, except what is a must (stuff that needs to be done in the home.. and of course at work I have to spend time inside partly)
>
> How do I know what brand to pick? Hmmm.. I just have researched, and try to do the best I can. It is a work i progress
> You need to watch what is the light spectrum of the lamp. Candles are of course the only one I can recommend. But the rest are just helpers. So I try to choose the least damaging ones, that might have some benefits too.
>

> Next celltower is about 2 km away. What I guess are the downsides are neighbors wifi and windmills. They are everywhere here. Closest to my home is a few km away. Still I do not like it. But as I said, I feel pretty good so for now I will take it. So many positives about this place and my work situation, so it balances it out right now  How long into the future, no one knows!
>
>
> Yum, sounds delicious   I often buy ground meat in bulk, 3-4 kg, and let it sit in a glass bowl in my fridge, I use it up during a week time. At the end it gets a little fermented and really taste gets better every day  The time is too short to make it stink like high meat, which is cool.

Thank you so much Inger! I use candles at night but my husband turns on the light where he is so thought maybe there was something 'bearable' that we could get. We have incandescent lightbulbs so probably the 'best' out there at this point. I am thinking that when kids start getting older and will want to stay up, they may not want to stay up by candlelight and will want to turn the lights on. I guess we will have to deal with it when it comes. So far 2 of them are listening to and doing the sun stuff and a bit of cold. I hope it stays as I can see eg my sisters children slowly developing all kinds of stuff and my sis doesn't know what to do with it. I think is completely exhausted to be able to do anything any more.

### Inger — 2020-01-19T14:19:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284803>

You are welcome Danica
Oh that is a bummer when the rest of the family turn on lights  I am not allowing it in my home except here is an "emergency" (like when they were filming. They said they had to have lights on) but then I am the only one who rules here too...
Then you just have to do the best you can, like using low wattage incandescent bulbs. It is far better than the energy saving lamps. Maybe gradually use lower and lower watt bulbs and end up with 25, then 15 watt? So they get used to the dimmer lights?
Might be after a while they will start appreciating the cozy warm candle lights, they are very romantic and soft for sure  Kids always use to love candle light. At least all the kids I have met in my life

Maybe you can ask your husband to stay in another room with his choice of lights on, because you are doing this darkness experiment to see if it helps your well being? In fact it should be pretty easy to understand that darkness is needed for us. There is a reason it is dark in nature too. Maybe you could show him some studies about melatonin needing darkness to be released. That should be pretty easy to find and understand . It sure is way easier if the partner is on board or at least lets you experiment without destroying it all the time.

### Inger — 2020-01-19T14:36:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284804>

I have to change things up now. I realize things do not always stay the same.....
So now as I do not lose much iron anymore having my period way less often, I cant eat that much raw meat anymore, I am going to get way too much iron I think. With all the oysters I am eating that gives me tons of zink, I guess it is not very wise. As zink helps iron absorb even better, from what I have read. No wonder my ferritine is more than good.
So from now on I am eating way more seafood, less red meat, and more organs like liver to get more copper for a while. Also I am going to add in lots of eggyolks that I have not been eating too much of this past year. I can get pretty good quality eggs so I guess it is a great idea to eat way more of them.
I am also going to donate blood in a week, and regularly after that. Blood supply is always needed here and doctor said my blood is great so I should consider donating. Even if he did not see my iron levels as an issue but I do. Because my body is telling me otherwise. Lately I have had this discomfort in my liver after vacuuming for longer time and I cant help but think I have some excess iron stored there that reacts with the nnEMF.
I am going to try this and see what happens

Honestly the past year I have mostly been eating raw lamb and galloway meat, raw butter and then oysters. I though wow is this easy, I love it and this is what I am always going to eat! Hmmm... I now see it was maybe not so optimal after all, long term. Some more variety is clearly needed.. It was easy, delicious, simple and cheap, yes!
But I am glad to try something different now and learn more about things I had no clue about....

### Inger — 2020-01-19T14:44:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284805>

so Friday I had raw salmon, seaweed, with a niacin pill 100mg and some nutritional yeast, and for dinner lots of fresh, rare cooked lambs liver.
Oh my good I slept soo deep again, and had such amazing dreams!
I dreamed about the doctor we went to with the film team(btw he called me and told the doku is a bit delayed and will be sent in the 6th week instead, between 3. and 9. february). It was an awesome doctor. He had this sign on his door, that he do not shake hands with his patients for security reasons. To not spread any pathogens.
So he and I did not shake hands in his office, he did gave me a hug as we left though
So in my dream he and I sat at a desk on each side of the desk, and we shake hands, our hands touched for a pretty long time, and all the time there was this crazy strong warm deep feeling of tender love between our hands!!
so weird, but so cool   This good feeling kind of spread everywhere then  so I just woke up feeling soooo good and deeply relaxed and happy

tonight I dreamed that I was sleeping in my ex husbands arms on his hairy chest! And it felt nice and safe. OMG why am I dreaming all this weird stuff....LOL I am not thinking about anything like this when I am awake, I swear.
Must be the Niacin

### Inger — 2020-01-19T16:23:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284808>

copied the screenshot above from this link;
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4766324/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4766324/)

I have tried to find information on bio-availability differences of iron in cooked and raw animal food, but I cant find anything except this one, and it is plants, not meat. Anyhow, it seems that cooking decreased the iron bio-availability significantly. If the same is true for meat, eating huge amounts of raw muscle meat might not be wise at all if you have already more than enough iron in your body.....

An awesome cure if you have too little iron though

### Inger — 2020-01-20T20:04:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284842>

[https://jackkruse.com/reality-8-women-gain-time-menopause/](https://jackkruse.com/reality-8-women-gain-time-menopause/)

reading that blog again. Seems I need more sun! Yeah. High ferritine around menopause is there because we need more sun.. makes totally sense to me. I can feel a magnetic charge around my chest when I stay in the tanning bed for more than about 12 minutes... it is amazing. Must be all the iron charging up!
But I still cant figure out why I have this minor liver discomfort come and go. Somehow feels like my liver is stressed.
I am so curious what I will feel when I go and donate blood next week monday.
I always happy to get my period, it just feels good, I always liked it. Maybe I always have collected quite a bit of iron and havin the period helped me feel better? So somehow I think it is going to be really cool to donate some blood now, huh!
Tonight I am going to sleep flat just to see if there is a difference. I am so sad, as I love to sleep inclined!! I am not even sure if the inclining my bed has anything to do with it, but it did appeared a few weeks into doing that. And I am sleeping on a magnetico. No idea how an incline affect the magnets.
If after a few days there is no difference, I might try take off the magnetico and sleep inclined without it...and see what happends. But the magnetico mattress is so heavy it is really hard to get off the bed properly alone, as you cannout fold it, it has to be flipped and rolled, uh.

Oh man. Maybe I will sweat again. Maybe it is good so I sweat out some excess iron from my liver? HA. I need to go to the sauna! Yeah. I could go tomorrow. I love sauna, but rarely go. I could do that.

Or my poor liver has a parasite?  But my B12 levels were really good, as high as ever, and I have read if you have a parasite the B12 levels go down. Doc said all labs looked good. So that seems unlikely too. I need to figure out this mystery.
I am so glad spring is on its way    Honestly, days get longer every day, and sun get stronger

Okay. Rant over
Lights out. It is way too late to be on the computer, 7 PM

### Inger — 2020-01-21T12:33:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284869>

You are right Saichi, I need to stop eating that organic salmon as long as I get other seafood.

I only have it very occasionally but I have to admit I love it as it is so yummy fatty... I love make tartar out of it.
Today I had raw fresh mackerel tartar for breakfast though, and yesterday too

That is really interesting about your lady friend, I am 43, so we are the same age, about. She might be in perimenopause too and get her period less often, and then the iron starts to accumulate easier. The weird thing is, all women around my age that are in my life seem to have the opposite issue, very heavy periods and way too low iron. One even needs to take iron infusions intravenously! She told me she would love to have some of my blood (she is a doctor BTW!)
My sisters have way too low iron/hemoglobin, and also heavy bleeding. I do not have had that, my periods were very easy and enjoyable, not too much bleeding. Maybe because of how I live and eat, idk. But sure that must be why I have more than enough iron too, and my sisters not.
Those sisters also are very sensible to nnEMF, maybe their bodies try to get rid of iron to help the issue? Or is the iron stored in organs/tissue and do not show up in blood? And that makes the sensibility to nnEMF?
I would love to know more about this. I observe many things but I really cannot connect the dots fully yet.

\- - - -

Okay, so I slept flat tonight! It was so weird, really.
It felt like my head now was lower than my feet, I had gotten so used to the incline that it felt pretty much flat to me. It took some more time for me to fall asleep. But I do slept pretty well, maybe a little more fragmented as otherwise.
I went to bed at 9 PM, woke maybe a hour or more before I had to go up at 7:30 AM - I had to bring my car to yearly checkup today. So it was still quite dark as I left home.
I did have some interesting feelings and sounds around my gallbladder tonight. Very subtle, but it was there.. something was happening. My bowel movements indicated increased gall bladder activity, seriously. So weird!

I might have not mentioned this but what I realized as a "side effect" from inclining my bed, was that my bowels seemed to "slow down". I did not have a bowel movement every day anymore... and sometimes I thought, man, I am constipated now.. but as I did not have any other discomfort and my belly was flat as always on this carnivore diet, I did not think too much about it. Though it might be a transition thing.

But now when I think about it more, it might have been that my gallbladder just did not function properly for some odd reason? And that is where the discomfort in the liver area came from? It is still early to tell, I have to give it a few more days because mostly later in the day and after vacuuming especially(yay, now I have a good reason to vacuum clean my home today to test this out! ), that the discomfort is to there to feel it, and also mostly only around certain movements I do.
But I have a sense it is already different today, better. Hmm. This is so weird.

My thoughts so far is, when sleeping on a magnetico, the inclining of the bed will have a different (much stronger??) effect on the body/organs.
So what I am going to do now is, AFTER I have resolved this issue first, of course - and the discomfort is completely gone, I am going to try incline my bed just a couple cm. Maybe 2-3 cm. Not more, and see what happens. Maybe a little bit is just what is needed in this case. Or not at all. Then I would have to remove my magnetico and try the incline without it. I might try all different variables I can think of, one after another, because I am so curious!

I wish I would be more into physics etc. but I am so clueless in this area. I really do not know what a difference a incline will have when the magnetic force is stronger. If someone of you who read this have an idea, please share it!

### Inger — 2020-01-23T19:47:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-284980>

Two days of sleeping flat went.. so so. First day I slept fine, next day not so great.
So I thought, I want to sleep inclined again! but my liver/gallbladder area discomfort seemed to diminish by flat sleeping though.
What to do.
Okay, so I thought I must make a compromize. Sleeping only a couple cm inclined. I did that tonight, just about max 3 cm. incline. It felt good.. I love the incline. I slept so deep and great again, getting that deeply relaxed feeling in my body again, yes! I am going to try out this for a while as see what happens.

Now my new hypothesis is, if you sleep on a magnetico, inclining the bed might be way more powerful, like too powerful, to incline it 15 cm. Just a couple cm is enough. I will be testing this hypothesis for a while.

I got me 60 eggs today. Organic pastured eggs from hens that have lots of outside space/pasture. Had 8 yolks today. Mmmm.. they are soo good. Dangerously good.. if you wisk them briefly cold from the fridge, with a tiny half teaspoon of honey... until it becomes like a sauce. It is sooo yummy, really. The texture and taste of it is just so good, huh!

### Inger — 2020-01-24T20:34:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-285072>

Jenny,
I am inclining my head. That is how it is supposed to be done

\- - - - -

I got 10 very beautiful, fat, fresh herrings at the market today, for only 12 euro. yay    Felt like a queen again!
And on the top of it, for free, several herring heads and herring roes that the lady wanted to trow out.
I had a herring roe and head smoothie as I came home from work. Nice  Feeling so good

### Inger — 2020-01-27T19:25:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-285219>

Okay, so I went to donate blood today. I was a little nervous, because I have only done it once before in my life, about 24 years ago
But it was so much fun, I laughed my ass off .... This is what happened.

I prepaired well. Wanted to make my blood as good as possible, so the sick people who might get it benefit the most. I slept really good tonight, woke up feeling like a queen! Had a really cool dream too.. It went like this; _I sat naked on the floor in mud, in an open garage, and happily shaved my legs. They were very hairy! My dad come outside of the garage and see me, he was smoking and drinking. He said to me, ugh, you sinful woman, what are you doing, being naked in the mud! It is so embarrassing!! I answered, but dad, why are you criticizing me? Look, you smoke and drink and destroy your body, it is way worse? I am just sitting in the mud naked and does no harm..._

I ate 6 raw eggyolks for breakfast, with raw herring, and had this snack-plate after coming home from work, before leaving;

Wild caught graved salmon, wild salmon caviar, raw sheepmilk cheese from Spain, 3 pastured eggyolks raw.
Also had a spoonful of organic nutritional yeast and 100 mg Thiamine with the morning meal.

Then I went to the 100% UVB tanning bed for 19 minutes, and as I came home I did 10 minutes redlight-therapy, and then went out to stand a couple minutes naked on my terrace grounded in the rain when it was still light outside.
Then I went to donate blood.
Thought now my blood is ready to go. In Germany it is rather complicated, the blood donation, first filling in papers (that is always the case) and then first to one doctor who took temperature (I had 37 degree C) and Hemoglobin (I had 14,5) and then I had to go to another room with another doctor who talked with me about the questionnaire and sher took my blood pressure and asked more questions. Then I was allowed to enter the room with the nurses and the beds. And there were quitr some people around . So As the nburse took my blood I asked her some stuff and also how fast the bag fills up and she said it is different, depends on weather, if you are dehydrated etc. Between 4 and 7 minutes perhaps.
I asked if eating caviar could help it flow faster, and told her what I had done before I came to optimize my blood.. We laughed so hard! She said she is going to put an X on my bag and write it is filled of sunshine and caviar!  She tought it ewas cool and a great idea! My bag was full in 4 minutes BTW. Yay
Somehow I just could not stop laughing. The man who lied next to me losing his blood started to laugh too and then I told him now our blood is real good quality, because laughing so hard must make the blood really happy and the people who get it will feel that

It was a really fun experience. Too sad is, here women are allowed to donate blood only 4 times / year or every 3 months. What to do. I sure am going to donate those 4 times.

After it you was taken to a room were it was served some nice bread with eggs, ham, liverpate, meat... tea coffee..sweets, water. They made me a separate plate with meat and eggs and no bread. I had to wait 20 minutes before I was allowed to leave. I did not feel dizzy at all.

### Saichi — 2020-01-27T20:56:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-285225>

Where do you get wild salmon and roe from?!

### Inger — 2020-01-28T12:22:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-285261>

They have it in the supermarket here. Okay, it is packaged stuff and sure fresh would be better - but anyways, I am so happy to have a quite easy time to source good quality foods
When I get whole fresh herring I for sure eat the roe too, it often has roe in it.

### Saichi — 2020-01-28T21:00:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-285278>

So you're eating Alaskan salmon?

### Inger — 2020-02-06T19:23:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-285696>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=285278)
>
> So you're eating Alaskan salmon?

Yes. Why?

Meat has the double amount of iron than salmon has. Wow. Salmon has way more B-vitamins too, and more copper, iodine etc. The minerals are more balanced.
It was not so wise to eat tons of Galloway meat. But live and learn. I now get it, how muscle meat can lead to iron overload if you eat it in exess. Of course it is a great thing if you are anemic!  I never was....
It is easy to get lazy... and just eat something because you enjoy it so much and it is simple.
I am thinking about my eating oysters for starter, with lemon juice (vitC) and then raw meat. This is the ultimate iron bomb.. because vit C increases iron absorption too. Hmmm.... I am going to eat my oysters with other condiments from now on.

I can get frozen pacific wild salmon here for very cheap -2,50 euro/250 grams -, I cant eat it raw though - it is kind of weird. So I gently cook it with some onion and herbs. Add some fat. Yum. I really like that, can eat it every day. That, and then fresh herrings from the market, raw, and oysters, I am happy
Some raw organic sheep cheese for dessert
My 1/8 Galloway cow that sleeps in my freezer, will have to wait being eaten by me until I have given blood one more time, next month.. then I will slowly eat it but very slowly. Main calories will be from seafood from now on.
Feeling so grateful to Jack for his flashlight

I was in Berlin Tuesday to get a new passport, at the Norwegian embassy. The trip took me almost 6 hours one way. I took the train, a crazy fast one! I felt really good all the trip, and met such lovely people!
I was a little nervous how I will tolerate all that nnEMF in those trains.. and Berlin has 5G too at places.. But I felt great - no issues at all. Maybe because I decided to travel with God
I know this sounds weird, but sometimes it really is scary to be all alone, and have to take care of everything and resolve all the issues in daily life alone. No family and friends here. So one day as I felt just afraid and tired I decided to just marry God now as I am single anyways  So it felt really good because then I am not alone at all anmore, and I took him with me to Berlin

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-285735>

Daiichi's the nuclear plant with the largest uncontrolled fuel ponds and melt-throughs in history and by quite a big factor. My family's hometown is several hours northeast of the plant. Alaska's in the Pacific. I gave up following the data on contamination years ago because no point following labs on a dead horse.

### Inger — 2020-02-09T10:54:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-285775>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=285735)
>
> Daiichi's the nuclear plant with the largest uncontrolled fuel ponds and melt-throughs in history and by quite a big factor. My family's hometown is several hours northeast of the plant. Alaska's in the Pacific. I gave up following the data on contamination years ago because no point following labs on a dead horse.

I am pretty sure the good outweighs the bad when it comes to this issue.. after all we are not talking about very big amounts, looked into it yesterday more in depth, and I did not get worried.
The Baltic Sea is the most radioactive sea(or at least was a few years ago when I read about it) in the world because of Russian dumping radioactive waste in it. Still, fishermen who are eating tons of fish from it, are some of the healthiest population in Finland.
It does not add up Saichi, does it?
Maybe there is something in fish that protects you. That is how it must be. That said I would not eat fish direct from Fukushima though. But I am not going to stop eating wildcaught fish from Alaska/Pasific ocean. I am mixing it up with herring and cod from the North Sea/atlantic, shrimp from Greenland.. and my own oysters I pick. I think I will be fine
Where are you getting your seafood from?

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=285771)
>
> Inger - what were you making ....I think it was an air fryer? Was it thin hamburger patties?

Caroline,
it is an air dryer, I dry the burgers over night at about 35-40 degree C. They get sooo delicious that way, eaten slightly warm.. yumm

### Inger — 2020-02-09T11:00:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-285776>

> [Bob Stirling said:](/goto/post?id=285768)

Thanks Bob.
It is a bit hard for me to look at, as I just see a wild, wounded heartbroken woman right there...uh
But so glad if it still could be to any help, I still felt too wounded somehow from my last heartbreak for being exposed like that but I do not regret doing it anyways. It was a great experience after all, the people were all really nice and we had great talks behind the camera.

### Inger — 2020-02-09T11:10:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ingers-journal.6472/post-285778>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=285742)
>
> love this Inger .....I wish it was in English!

Yes.... i know! I am just telling what I eat and how I feel good drinking the fishheadsmoothie and what a nice energy it gives me
And then they ask about animals and how I can eat them working by a vet. So I tell I really love animals and always make sure they do not suffer and had a good as natural as possible life.
And then they ask about the environment after I had my breakfast, and how eating so much meat can be bad for it. And I explain how I feel good about it because I produce very little waste with my choices, also how agriculture will destroy the nature probably way more than the pastured animas I eat because of processing/needing lots of energy/pesticides etc.

In the restaurant I order something special "outside of menu" as they have only cooked options, I ask for the chef to slice raw meat for me and that is all. Well... she comes with a carpaccio on salad....lol It was kind, but not really what I asked for but they were all so kind! It tasted great though  I ate the meat and the parmesano on the top

And at the doctors office he tells my bloodwork all looks fine and my arteries are all clean which was a bit unexpected. In the end he gives me a clean bill of healt, despite my meat diet
I and the doctor had a chat after the filming ended and it was really cool. He was so interested! I wish he would be closer and we could meet and chat. I might send him an email and say if he ever comes to the north sea we could meet up at the oyster beach and have a chat about our thoughts about health

/////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
**Radiotrophic fungus**

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radiotrophic\_fungus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radiotrophic_fungus)
'**Radiotrophic fungi** are [fungi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fungi) which appear to perform [radiosynthesis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radiosynthesis_\(metabolism\)), that is, to use the pigment [melanin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melanin) to convert [gamma radiation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gamma_rays)[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radiotrophic_fungus#cite_note-sciencenews_20070526-1) into chemical energy for growth.
'
**You Should Know About This Chernobyl Fungus That Eats Radiation**

[https://www.popularmechanics.com/science/a30784690/chernobyl-fungus/](https://www.popularmechanics.com/science/a30784690/chernobyl-fungus/)

/////////////////////////////////////////////////////
Radioactive decay (also known as nuclear decay, radioactivity, radioactive disintegration or nuclear disintegration) is the process by which an unstable atomic nucleus loses energy by radiation.A material containing unstable nuclei is considered radioactive.Three of the most common types of decay are alpha decay, beta decay, and gamma decay, all of which involve emitting one or more particles ...
