Maybe you should try a movnat with Erwan \[and your DH - LOL\] for your birthday???? That would seem like a new beginning to me......

### Hope — 2014-01-01T16:37:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-104558>

It is January 1, 2014 and I thought I should make an attempt at a brief update. Since my last post, I've learned a ton about myself. Most importantly that I have the mold glitch gene, and more, i.e. lyme and other biotoxins. Found out about it in October after reading one of Jack's blogs - Leptin meet Lyme. It wasn't the first time....but something clicked and I researched the work of Dr Shoemaker. I knew intuitively that I must be having the issues he talks about and when I looked at his biotoxin pathway diagram, I knew I would be low in aMSH. It would explain why my sleep over the years has been yucky, despite my using a CPAP for sleep apnea. So I did the required blood tests and sure enough, I was right with all my intuitions. It sucked but at least now I have more answers.

Also explains why I would be miserably sick after moving to an apartment in 2006 that smelled musty, only silly me I didn't let myself be bothered. I got sick immediately but never made the connection then. I did wonder about my work office as IT always had yucky air and just being there would make me tired. With our without being on a computer. I am also convinced that it explains a lot of the issues in my family, especially my mom's health issues. It is heart breaking. And yet I am thankful to know better...so I can do better.

I still am very hooked on my pitch dark bedroom, which I LOVE, and I am so accustomed to having dark now, it's awesome, I crave it at night even when I'm not home but a hotel. It's like my body knows what's coming and is asking for it. Sleep has been amazing this fall/winter. After a trip to Outer Banks in June I wasn't doing so well though I believe now it had to do with mold in our rental....worst sleep ever. It smelled a little funky but again I tried to ignore what my body clearly couldn't.

I have become a boring person in that I go to bed early every night at home, and it is awesome. I LOVE the morning hours. I used to love staying up at night for the quiet, now it's the morning. Though we still have some mold at home, the remedies and cleaning we have had done have helped for sure, and I cannot wait to move out of our current home...it's near major power lines which I have always hated.

I do think our 20G magnetico is making a huge difference as well, though sometimes when I am exhausted, or ill, I stay in it all day. I was sick only once this year, after a trip to see my mom that was very stressful.

I am so thankful for all I have learned here, working with Michael Neuert, and there is much more to come I know.

My biggest desire is to have a healthy home in 2014. We still live outside DC, which I hate, but my DH is finally open to moving further out, even if not as far out as I would like...but I am working on it. There was one house so far I really loved, much more in the country but it was too far for DH so I need to keep working on him. He said, he is afraid he might hate living further out as he did it when single and always regretted the days and months there (in another state). Sadly when I say, well I have hated it here 3 years and it's also making sick, what about that, he has no answer...makes me sad but I am not giving up.

He also recently said that 'we should find a location we both would like'. And that was a relief...bc it means he is open to moving out of the area. I am hoping he might consider Denver. Or Seattle. FL's west coast would be great....but not sure if he'd be open. He might be open to New Orleans area, mostly bc he is a musician by heart. I was amazed he let me talk him into doing 23andme. Right before they stopped running the health part. No results in yet but I am sure they will soon. I strongly suspect that he has similar mold issues to me, and am still working on him to get the HLA testing done, at least. So we shall see.

I have to say one of the greatest gifts for me in 2014 is clarity. Clarity on what is good for me, and not. What is important and what not. Ads have nothing on me anymore, I see right through them. Sometimes it's sad, like all those ads for my gadgets running on batteries and or wifi. Yuck. Also clarity on what I really need to live, and what not. We or I have given a ton of 'stuff' away this year, especially since the summer...and it feels amazing to live lighter, with less, and much more freedom. I haven't become a true minimalist yet, but I'm on my way. I will never need a big mansion to live in, just something clean, mold and wifi and crappy power free. It may take a while to truly get there but I'm OK with that.

So short term I want to get out of our current house, move to a better local one, recover from mold, shield the new house from wifi as much as possible, and then ultimately move out of the area. I am super thankful for friends in the Shenandoah Valley, who I love to visit as many times as possible. There is no cell phone coverage at all, only weak wifi and I feel amazing there - only 1 hour east of us. Trees and hills everywhere. That's my dream for some day. I know when I am fully better and able to help make up for all the financial loss my health issues have caused, my dream is to build a small, green, low or no EMF home somewhere in the country. Don't know where yet but I see it in my heart. I also see a dog in our future....and we won't rent anywhere that doesn't allow pets. I still dream of having a baby but though I am aging don't think about it anymore. Must recover from mold first. I'm also much more OK with not having any at all. I don't know what will happen there....but I am open to whatever. Above all, I want to be well. And spend much less time online, and more in nature, outside. SPeaking of which, I must get going. Taking the hubs out on a drive to the country....in hopes he might get more comfy with the idea of living further out.

I wish everyone on this board all the best for 2014. And a big thanks goes out not just to Dr K but Misty too....the humble glue who keeps this great board and community together!!! SO appreciate your work, Misty, your ideas and questions that so many of us have  I do still dream of a live, in-person meet up some day!

### caroline — 2014-01-01T23:43:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-104569>

Hope - I am wishing so many wonderful things for you in 2014. You have made so much progress and I am proud of all you have accomplishment ... and best of all .... you know where you need to go.

This mould stuff is so worrying ..... How do you test for mould? If you were to move to a new place - How could you be sure? The Shenandoah Valley sounds wonderful for you - and so close really!

Can your DH work somewhat from home?

I love how you are moving forward and making plans ...... Have you done drawings of a small cabin you could build?

You could be self sustainable and raise animals.....and take in guests......

I was just skyping with my Daughter in Oakville \[Ontario\] she just had a spa day in the blue mountains with her best friend. All the pools are outside - hot and cold! She had an amazing time. The cold pool was 14 degrees. I was quite proud of her!

You will change your life ... you have already.....I cant wait to see where we all are a year from now!

### Hope — 2014-01-02T00:56:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-104571>

Hi Audrey,

how cool is that about your daughter?!! I would love to go there. Finding great places for vacations is not so easy, especially ones without mold, though now that I KNOW, it does get a bit easier. We hope to spend a week in FL, depending on when we move. Blue mountains are glorious. Today we took a nice walk along the Potomac, I persuaded DH to do that rather than go to the movies. I am not sure if I went to the movies once in 2013. Don't miss it, but crave nature. The air smelled amazing there, truly.

DH cannot do a thing from home, except certain (very few) online trainings. He is on special projects that don't allow any at home work. But he loves it. Bummer is, he is in a bad EMF hell. I have been checking out the area where he works, and the # of antennas and towers is just awful. They are going up everywhere, it is awful, around here. I just wanna scream when I see it. Also the power lines. SO many major ones. We drove through nice, some luxurious neighborhoods northwest of us, and I am just staggering at all the major power lines right by million dollar homes. Crazy! I wouldn't buy if I had the money. I guess people are all asleep or something or so focused on making money...they will pay the price later.

No drawings yet, but I LOVE my friend's house in the mountains. It is not big but super cozy, no mold and I dream in a way of repeating it. Especially the screened in porch. I feel amazing there and will lie on the hammock in any weather. We go out tomorrow, weather permitting. But the idea of making drawings is amazing. I love that link Misty shared with building healthy homes. I want to learn a lot more about it. In fact I wish I could become an expert in that. So many dreams

You test for mold with a special company that does it for you. NOT air - that is not reliable - but in the dust. People like me get sick not just from live but dead mold so it's really about removing it all or as much as possible. The biggest clue is a smell when you walk in. I have it in this house and also the previous apartment. It always kinda bothered me but I never stopped and thought, hey this might be hurting me. And it does. Now I will not stay in musty or damp places, no matter where. I will walk right out. Makes it easy also looking at homes. I look even at the outside. If there are issues with the siding, there is some water damage, or anything. It must be dry looking, and clean. And same thing inside. No warping anywhere, window in a bathroom. Stains on ceilings, or anywhere. A moisture meter helps too. same thing in laundry area all must be clean, hot water heater etc etc etc. You sort of get a feel for it when you are there. Also your gut REALLY leads you. Or me. Some houses look good online but I get there and something will tell me something isn't right, beyond what I see. It's weird but it works. Also avoiding carpet if possible is big. Though I have been in homes that have no smell at all with carpet. I also always check where the power lines are, and look online for wifi antennas etc. and check on my iPhone how much wifi pops up. Sometimes many, sometimes just a few. One house near it had barely any cell phone coverage, that was great.

Also will take my body voltage meter from Michael, and might rent a unit also. I wish I could the full testing Dr K would do but am doing my best. Before we sign a new lease, I might test out the house with one of his meters that I will rent again. Trouble is things go fast and right now not much is out there, but I hope at least by Feb 1 there will be more....and then I'll move ahead with that. Hopefully with Michael on the line. He is so great with this stuff. I'm ready to move, have been for a long time, but finally ready to really do it.....I know the ultimate goal is a ways away, but that's OK. It took Dr K three years - if I remember right - to make his big move. So it's OK if it takes me time too  I sure wish my DH was on board with all of it. But I am so happy that he gets I gotta get out of the mold for now....and he is even willing to pay for testing in the new place!). Right now getting out of mold and healing more from it will be key for me, esp. since wifi and mold make it worse - even if wifi is around me but not in the house. Ugh.

So for me it's the next right thing....always gotta keep my eye on that. It feels empowering to do that, rather than cave in overwhelm (which I have done too). I am a bit bummed with vacations as finding rentals that I could get out of if they smell is hard. Some hotels will let you cancel - my mold expert has done so - but with rentals it's harder....and I used to love airbnb. But oh well. I have this other dream of creating a website where people can list places they have stayed that are not moldy. I have thought about asking Tripadvisor too to add a category for mustiness LOL. I just think it would be great to have that. One family I know of where the DH was ill, the wife had to fly out ahead of time to check potential places for her DH....he was super sick.

Anyway YOU Audrey are amazing...all the support you offer here, and the changes you make yourself. I think of you in the ocean. Enjoy summer....wish I could be with you there right now. Maybe some day....another dream. I would LOVE to be with like-minded folks...even for a weekend. We went to a party for a bit last night and everyone was making a BIG deal about my Blueblockers. I will NOT take them off. Wear them on overnight planes too. One steward asked me what it was about...it's a fun way to talk about all this sometimes, but sometimes I just also want to be free, without being asked questions or getting funny looks. Though I won't compromise anymore....my health is too precious to me. I'd rather have people look at me funny. LOL.

Big hug to you Audrey

### Hope — 2014-01-22T17:43:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-106628>

So we are looking at new places to live. Rentals again bc I don't feel comfortable buying, not around here, plus everything is so expensive. Plus my DH is finally more and more open to leave the area completely.....in the next few years so it will be easier to do that WHEN we are ready. He's actually interviewing with a major online retail company though I don't think he will take it. I don't think it's really a good cultural fit but I am excited he is open to even the process.

So we have been looking and today at lunch we are meeting the realtor. Today I'm not only bringing my swiffer sheet to do a Hertsmi 2 in case I like what we see, but also my body voltage meter. I am pretty sure she is going to be more frustrated with me than she already is, but I ain't letting anyone stop me. This is my health we are talking about.

I started finally major testing myself at home, and whoa it is shocking. The cool news standing outside, even on concrete, even with rubber shoes on, my load is still FAR lower than inside.....except on the couch. And we are within 300 yards of major yucky lines.

Gotta find more ways to be outside, even in the freezing cold. Spent nearly an hour shoveling snow in bright sun early this am. So great.

Here's to not giving up....and blazing my own trail no matter what the people think around me.

### Hope — 2014-01-23T22:55:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-106803>

Thanks, Fitness! I just can't compromise on this.

So we are looking at houses and had the chance to look a couple yesterday. sadly one we liked was sold (not rented) five minutes after we said we'd send in an application. Not meant to be. The other one, which I loved even more.....for different reasons, but I have realized I cannot move in to. The brand new carpet not only gave me a headache but made me feel awful all around, have bad sleep, and feel awful still today. I'm so bummed but it's silly to move to a place that would give me headaches and not function well for who knows how long.

I'm also still digesting Monday's webinar. I didn't catch everything but took notes for the first time and that for some reason helped me so much. I was on the phone more and just listening, not so focused on the chat, though that is so fun usually to read everything. But I really can't wait to hear more about the redox potential. Mine must suck. No, it does. I do still love my magnetico. But I wonder now how good it is for me. Though I believe it helps me tons, perhaps I am doing myself a disservice. I am definitely dehydrated despite my water intake. I have bad ADH, i.e. low...partly from my mold issues. Hard to really reduce EMFs where we live, so close to major power lines. And in EMF hell generally though I do all I can at home, for sure. I was excited about the body voltage meter saying that just being outside my house, even with rubber shoes, the voltage is nearly zero, like 0.02 which is much better than inside. I learned I really should be sleeping on the couch. Readings are lowest there, and sitting at the kitchen table. My desk in my home office is going to do nothing else than look pretty till we move. I haven't tested myself while using a grounding pad.

Definitely need to read up a whole lot more about water...and I wonder how I will heal while mold is still affecting me, and the toxins are still in me, though I'm doing a lot to try to detox them. My recent hair analysis says I'm making progress on the Hg and Mg front which is good, but there is still much more to do.

Found out I am C677T heterozygous and also need to learn about my 23andme results. from THEIR reports I don't look too bad - my DH looks worse than me in risk factors though I know it doesn't mean much. Can't wait to have Sterling's app process it all. Now I am not sure if I should stop my beloved high sulphur veggies especially the onions and garlic. I do so love them. LOL. It's funny how I am not really tempted by carbs....but giving up those veggies would be hard. Same with meat and eggs. I do love fish....but still. I don't miss cheese at all and find that eating low carb and no dairy but butter (not even raw cream which I cannot get around here anyway) really helps my sleep a lot.

I do get sad wishing I could move to the country tomorrow. I am so beyond ready. I caught some of a CBS Sunday morning show on future cars....and how they will be self-driving. I thought, wow, they will take our freedom to drive. Makes me miss the Autobahn more, where I can drive as fast as I want. I do love speed - responsibly of course. but worst of it? There will be radars and scans and sensors in every such car. So driving will be even worse than today. I so wish I had land and could buy an 1980s Mercedes and just store it till I need it and then have it restored. I really don't want to have to buy such a connected car. I get how bad this is. My friends when I share it all think I am cookoo.

One cool thing, after almost a month of having my car-grounding device thingie laying by the front door, I asked my mechanic to install it, and they did it for free. So nice of them. Now I am driving around with a funny looking piece of rubber hanging out behind my one rear tire. Good thing I'm not famous. LOL.

I do feel sad today about my lack of living choices. I Just want to find a place that has NO MOLD, LOW wifi, good lighting (as in natural light), some privacy, some trees nearby....and be in a residential neighborhood and no major power lines nearby.

In searching antennas and towers I learned that a private woman has registered an antenna in my desired area to live. Interesting. I am tempted to call the listed number and ask why she would do this and does she know what harm she is doing herself? Those are my crazy thoughts flying in my brain.

I know from Michael Neuert only a small number of these things are registered, it's a voluntary thing. Makes me wanna move to the country even more...not that I need a nudge for that, ha.

I will keep on looking, and hoping and praying our realtor won't hate me too much for not yet picking a place after looking at 10 homes. We did lose a few, but still. I did send in HERTSMI-2 for one home. I don't love it, but it could work if there is no mold. Parking will be a problem and the oven is yucky but we would try to negotiate something better. We shall see. I do try to remember to surrender all this.....and to trust that God and universe know what I need. I am definitely searching and seeking every day for new places I may have missed.

I do love the snow we have had. It is below freezing in the morning - single digits - but I love and treasure it. DH insists on keeping the heat on at night. I so want it off. But oh well. We are at 63 now....maybe some day I can get him to come lower....

### caroline — 2014-01-24T00:09:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-106832>

Dear Hope.... I went back in your journal a bit to refresh my memory - It seems to me you are worrying yourself into an early grave. You are putting so much stress/pressure on yourself all the time - that can't be good. Can you let go a bit? I know - easier said than done. Go back in your journal and have a read.

You are trying to fix everything all at once ... cut yourself some slack - take your DH out on a date and go dancing! have some fun! What about a couples massage? You are lovely and young and this is your one and only life .... we all only have the present. Please stop and smell the roses!

My takeaway from all this lately .... If we want to be healthy and suck up life - we have to improve our redox potential and then everything else will fall into place. Dr. K. has said many times lately ... if we fix that we can mitigate all the other less than optimal things in our life .... our body will take care of the housekeeping...

Hope - you have so much to offer ....please take a step back and find the joy....xoxo

### Hope — 2014-01-28T15:13:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-107148>

**CSM Started January 24
**
So I started on a one time dose of CSM Friday to help with my biotoxins. So far, no bad results..was tired over the weekend but it got really good yesterday. Maybe it's also from meditating more again, and from having greater hope that we can find a place where I can like it, and heal more..in ways I could not here. But yesterday especially I felt as if my brain was clearer......hard to explain. but I knew something was happening. Wish I could do some NFB again but it's just too expensive right now. My gut says a move is #1. And getting rid of a ton more possessions...though I am OK with that.

I have been wondering if it's better to live in an area with greater wifi but have a single family home - though not much space - or one with less wifi, like only 25% of the amount of wifi...but it's all townhouses and nearly everyone has those satellite dishes on their roofs, not far from the bedroom. Ugh. Must test I know....

So we shall see. This am I am fasting for a blood test, and it is so hard. Funny how I am so used to eating my BF early......so thankful for more good nights of sleep. I really think the no dairy except butter, lots of veggies and fats and good protein and no sugars/fruits/simple carbs except in veggies is really good for me. But I wonder what my BUN/C will be. I know I'm dehydrated.....my ADH/Osmo levels are stink-o bc my pit gland can't make enough ADH bc of the mold in me.....but oh well. Must keep plugging away.

### Hope — 2014-02-14T16:57:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-110190>

Update....

So I have to write a short update in here today.....if nothing else, to remind myself to stay away from all things sugar and carbs. not good for me. I indulged in some gummy bears, not a good idea, last night. They have been sitting in a special drawer..and no tempting since last June. well not last night and it's not a good idea.

This morning I stepped out on the snow for like 15 seconds. No CT, but it was just fun to be on the snow for a minute. I do love winter and now that i know I can't be on magnetico during the day on weekends, it makes me appreciate darkness even more. And I will stay in darkness in the morning from now although of course the sun is rising earlier and earlier and we are just a few weeks away from the time change in March.

last night's webinar was great as usual and i really need to read the monk that sold his ferrari though my reading list is huge by now. Haven't made much progress with the Body Electric.

I finally changed my avatar...or added one. I picked this one bc I want to remind myself when I am on here...to keep simplicity in mind. especially as we look for a new house, still. a couple slipped under our fingers. So much to do and learn.

My sleep has been great, I must say, and I am not sure if it's the CSM but somehow this month I have some moments where my mind is actually lucid and clear, which is a great feeling indeed. I do see how key it is for me to watch light cycles like a hawk. I really think the morning Happy Light I use is helping too.

Some things I need to work on...
\- increase cholesterol..my LDL is too low at 100
\- put my water more on the magnetico....
\- use my Heartmath on a regular basis. I want to use it at least 3 times a week for 5 minutes. I was so excited to see in the Redox Rx. Just gotta use it.
\- do my meditation also at least 3 times a week
\- keep to the low carb high fat diet. Though I am finding that I struggle eating coconut oil. I CRAVE butter, but not CO so much.
\- I need to get stricter with my bedtime. I was doing 9pm through Dec but since January it's been later which is not as good. Early sleep really works well. I do notice too that my body CRAVES darkness at night. It's so fascinating and I feel like it's a sign of my body maybe slowly rewiring itself and getting back to how things used to be in previous generations??
\- decrease my BUN/C ratio. It's the one that doesn't seem to budge at all. i CANNOT get it lower than 17, no matter what I try. It could be bc of the bio toxins. I know my ADH is low bc I am not making enough MSH, thanks to the biotoxins.

My biggest goal is still to move. Have to stay in this area, but...I have been finding pockets further out where there are mostly residents, and much less wifi antenna's and towers. Still only a short term solution of course but we gotta get out of here...away from major power lines as well as the mold remnants, which I smell every day, and more so when I leave a couple days and return. It's like a blast of mold greeting me.

### Hope — 2014-02-14T17:01:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-110192>

PS I think Jack's story about his son is AMAZING!!!!! Made me cry when Jack says he feels like he ruined him with Chicken McNuggets and turned him around. How amazing is that. Now he's a Navy Seal. Does anyone need anything more??

Leptinism as his religion...love it.

I would love to experience that kind of thing. Maybe when I am out of mold and have healed more. I hope so. Though my dream isn't weight loss..it's raising a child, adopted or our own still. But I gotta be strong. No way I could do it "now".

### Hope — 2014-02-16T03:20:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-110551>

Looked at a couple houses today, found one with way less antennas and towers than where we are here, much more residential and near major parks and the Potomac River. One even had all hardwood but the smell was kind of weird - not moldy, didn't even have a sump pump....but like glass cleaner. But I felt encouraged. In it we could even sleep in the basement as there is a bedroom down there, and for some reason it smells better and drier there than by the front door. Too weird.

Also felt very very tired, as I have all week, my fault stressing over the snow at first....and not sleeping enough. Last night it was 9 hours, which is unusual for me but not with acupuncture...which makes me relax incredibly deeply. Also needed a foot massage today after the house search. Just exhausting. So many things to consider.

### sooperb — 2014-02-16T19:21:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-110629>

I wonder if they had one or two of those odious plug in air-poisoners anywhere, maybe an old one that has been left in place but isn't so pungent any more?

### Hope — 2014-02-16T21:08:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-110652>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=110629)
>
> I wonder if they had one or two of those odious plug in air-poisoners anywhere, maybe an old one that has been left in place but isn't so pungent any more?

Hmmmm I did see one of those in the kitchen but didn't think anything of it at the time. So it's possible. I am not sure if it was maybe windex. Or some floor cleaners. But I went to my local Kmart to smell different floor and other cleaners this morning, and it only really smells like windex. None of the other things I smelled. I walked out of there thinking, that was kinda dumb, to smell all those chemical bottles. LOL. But, thank goodness I do not use ANY of that stuff, except the recommended glass cleaner by my mold man. Everything else I use is either Thieves Cleaner or plain stuff I make.

Funnily, the BASEMENT rooms 'smelled' like NOTHING, totally dry, I had a really good feeling there. LOL.

Can't wait to hear what they decide - not till Wednesday....the other option I liked a lot was carpeted, but the house quality was really good, and the layout great.....except it has 20 year old carpet that they want to replace. And I'd rather live with the old one. We were there 90 minutes and I felt totally fine...unlike some other places I have been.

### Hope — 2014-02-17T00:33:45+0200

So looks like we have to decide tomorrow....either hardwood and a layout I am not so keen on but is workable or a layout and good build I love but that has old carpet.

Thankfully, both allow carpet so we might actually get a dog soon....after I recover more. OH how I love puppy dogs.

Hope we make right decision.....

Sooperb, u are too sweet to share this poem.

And after checking antennas and towers....the hardwood is it. Still close, but a LOT less than the carpeted.....like 1/3 and half....the crazy part, the closest antennas are from the schools. ELEMENTARY school!!!!

### sooperb — 2014-02-17T19:40:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-110851>

It's certainly a difficult area. My daughter, who's always had chronic asthma, lived rurally in a stone built cottage, many years old, that had been updated to include oil fired central heating. She lived in it about 12 years. In that time she often struggled to heat it as the price of oil went through the roof and there were times when there was no heating or hot water. There had always been a smell of damp in one of the inner cupboards so she just kept the door shut. Over the years her depression was really bad and she was diagnosed as bipolar. At one point when she moved her bed, the carpet was mouldy underneath and it had to be thrown away. There was some very heavy rains too that filled up a sort of sunken area in front of the girls' bedroom window to the point where it reached the air brick and presumably drained under the property. Eventually she moved (thank goodness) and when she moved her daughter's bed away from the wall, the carpet was damp and mouldy as was the back of the bed.

She has moved to another old property but at least this one isn't on the ground floor and after a lot of pressure on the landlord he's fixed most of the problems apart from a leak that no one seems to be able to trace back to the source. Anyway, although her depression is much better (moving sorted that in more ways than one) her asthma is still a problem, one of the girls has had to sleep in a bedroom where the wall was damp from a different leak and she has some very odd issues that they haven't been able to resolve despite many blood tests. I linked her to Martin's journal, she said it could almost be hers! She took a lot of her stuff with her when she moved and I told her many times it smelt just like her old home. One day she smelled it herself and got rid of anything that smelled. So as her health issues continue, I suspect that she may also have lingering mould in her system. Trouble is, if you went to a GP and asked for a blood test for mould I think they'd be called for a strait jacket lol. She had already joined a few dots though about mould and how it can cause depression etc. It can't just have been a coincidence that's for sure.

She had already joined a few dots though about mould and how it can cause depression etc. It can't just have been a coincidence that's for sure.

OMG @[sooperb](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/8412/)...reading this gives me the chills. Having done all the reading, she sounds like a classic case. Of course Dr Shoemaker would say, testing is needed, but I wouldn't wait for that. I would do anything I could...and also try to move. Unless she has great genes and no HLA issues, she could be feeling a LOT betting getting out of mold and possibly treating it if necessary....which it sounds like it to me, if she shows it in testing. And, I wouldn't go to a GP and ask for mold testing..I'd go and say, "I want to make sure that I don't have inflammatory cytokines floating around my body and burdening my immune system more than it needs to be and contributing to my asthma. Until we test, please consider the hypothesis that this could be part of my health issues. If my tests come back negative, then we'll know where I stand" And then I'd find someone who will run them, or run them myself if I can - there must be a way?! And if she lives someplace where there is mold anywhere in the building, she would be better off to leave. I KNOW it is hard. So so hard.....but her health AND longevity is worth it.

Our realtor is kinda mad at me bc I cost her extra time, bc half the houses we have seen, I had to leave right away, no chance to even think to rent it. Even in some basement storage cabinet. no way. I have suffered and paid way way too much. It's sad and I will find a way to pay her back extra time, but it's important...for someone like me who gets sick from it. And BTW - I see myself in Martin's journal too. I just wish I read it right away, same as the Leptin Meet Lyme blog...it took me a year to find and also GET it. Now, things just peel off my eyes. Like my thyroid issues...at least partly mold related. Hypothalamic damage = thyroid can't work as well. I see things in me so clearly now, it's nuts. Maybe it's the CSM killing off toxins and my brain is having some comeback for a few moments. I am rooting for your daughter to get this tested.

Dr Shoemaker has Md's write him from Denmark, Germany.....there must be someone in the UK who also gets it!! That is my big wish for you!! Too bad we can't hire Morse or Lawrence Fox to help you find her the right guy. Whatever you do, don't give up. Has she don't the online visual contrast test?

PS Can you let us know how she is doing? I would love to hear!! Like what she decides to do etc. I so hope she will get tested.

Also you could help her increase her redox potential. I bet it is shot, like mine....though I think I see improvements, especially in the weeks I am strict. I can tell still that I had gummy bears on Thursday night.

### Hope — 2014-02-18T00:06:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-110884>

So our application to the hardwood rental looks like it will be approved. I stopped by there and another house today that was tested with carpet and came back OK that I just LOVE the energy in. I just feel good when I walk in. It is so inviting, cozy, sturdy, brick outside, like it could withstand a big bad storm. Love it. But carpet everywhere. Talked briefly to Dr Shoemaker who said if I spent 2 hours there OK as I did without any worsening symptoms and the testing is OK, I should be OK there. Love that response....lol

The other one - the hardwood we put in an application - he said we need to test, can't assume Windex smell is really just Windex and we don't know if the water damage from a side window was cleaned up properly/fixed, which is what one of the really good mold experts in VA also said. And I did see the ceiling under the master bath was painted over..looks good but i can tell something painted just there.

So I am sending a test for this house as well....and may be losing the money if the owner says, can't wait for it to come back. But I feel like this is important enough....it's a big reason for the move. Plus though I like the house, in my heart I feel drawn to the carpeted house bc of how it feels. LOL. I don't know why I like living in a really sturdy looking/feeling house that I also like the inside. I am so weird.

Thankfully both DH and I like both houses...wifi is slightly lower on the hardwood.

Audrey...if you catch this post.....I know I am over thinking. My frontal cortex and executive abilities are not on par these days, so forgive me.

I am partly sharing this here to look back a year from now to see what I decided, and how I feel about it all. In a way I feel I must go with the carpeted. My heart usually never leads me wrong. lol.....even though in this case, theoretically the other could be OK.

FUNNY thing - for 3 months there was nothing much...and now there are a couple houses I really like.

### caroline — 2014-02-18T00:23:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-110890>

My Dear Hope ..... I don't think I meant that you are over thinking - I just hate to see you so continually stressed out and worry about the impact on you and your DH and on your body.

Dr. K. tells us time and time again that the best Doctor is the one in our own head. I think you are really listening to your intuition now and that is probably the most important thing. Looking back over the years - I can see I always knew when something was right or wrong. The one stone cottage that I love the most and we were all very happy there......I walked to work a way I had never walked before -down a street I had never been on before and saw this stone cottage ...and it was all over. We briefly looked at the inside - that was immaterial to me - I needed that house! I was so frustrated with my Dh because he wanted to offer a little less than asking price - as any sane person does!

When I look back at that ....it was the original stone farm house in the area - you could see all the original tree lines - heaps of conifers .... rows and rows of them .....I knew from the moment I saw it that it was magic!

I am curious .... I would have thought that carpet wouldn't be good if you have mould issues?

Please have some fun in amongst all of this - I think that a place will reveal itself to you.......

### sooperb — 2014-02-18T00:35:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-110892>

Thanks for your interest Hope . As far as health goes, my daughter has had all sorts of issues, she had a varicose vein in her leg start when she was eleven! She had a squint, trouble with her wisdom teeth, pains in her legs and knees that they never really diagnosed, she grew out of them. Then there's her asthma, she's an allergic asthmatic and she used to take steroids and has suffered with roid rage. It's left her very reluctant to medicate and it's a bit of an ongoing battle trying to make her see she needs to keep on top of it. She's had food poisoning twice and was very sick the second time, she had a hernia op and the anaesthetic nearly killed her, the list is a long one. She has amazing fortitude and courage. She is doing so well at the moment, saving towards her own house so she wouldn't consider moving again. Her present place is tons better than her last but not perfect. She is a great believer in fate and that God will provide when the time is right. Ive been trying to encourage her to read Jack's pages but they are long and she is short on time, works 2 or three jobs and is raising her two girls on her own.

I love your approach to asking about testing, I can see it is nicely pitched. I've not heard of the online visual contrast test, sounds really interesting.

You sound as intuitive as she is, your house has chosen you, go with your gut as she does, she's seldom wrong.

### Hope — 2014-02-18T02:33:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-110923>

Lahelada and Audrey, thank you guys both so much! Audrey.....I do have leather boots but I also have that pooch Jack talks about. DH loves my legs though, LOL. Too cold for anything mini.  He would love a concert though.

Lahelada.....I actually have gone back to the carpet one. Can you believe it? After I talked to Dr Shoemaker, a mold expert. An older one that I know is mold-free. And it's a struggle to decide..head vs heart...sun versus trees....I would definitely have the carpet cleaned in a special way regularly besides the vacuuming we would do. I just love the FEEL of that carpeted house.....and it doesn't smell at all. Feels cozy....and so inviting. I will have to meditate and get still, really seek my heart. We have a couple days.....

So appreciate your input, and sooperb and fitness@home too....more than I can say.

I just meant - you don't want any regrets ...... regrets are a bitch! and you never know when the rug is going to be slipped out from under you.

I had a dear friend that died pretty suddenly at 55 .... I always remember him standing in front of me and saying "the last thing I will be saying on my deathbed is - I wish I had slept more" This beautiful man lived and loved to the absolute max - he rung every last bit of joy out of his life every single day. I don't now why he was like that - but he was. It was amazing to be in his company .......and I will always remember how much he loved his Mom and cared for her in her old age. He would always bring her for breakfast on Saturday mornings ...and she would always steal all the little jams ....and we would all just laugh! she had a cupboard full of them at home and never used them....too funny!

### Lahelada — 2014-02-20T00:29:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111268>

Hope,it is just a matter of getting used to it. You put the oven on while you are prepping whatever so it is not really an issue. I do think that old heavy ovens roast nicer. If you think that it is in good shape and there is not much that goes wrong with them then don't let it be a stumbling block. I used to be 100 % convinced that I love elctric but the charm of lighting a flame to cook is hard to beat.
You will need to to change some cookware possibly. Black pans often burn whatever you are baking in a gas oven . Additionally in a power outage you can still cook.

### Hope — 2014-02-20T02:35:59+0200

### Hope — 2014-02-20T03:05:31+0200

that is what I do.....outside of the ABC. Audrey, you are so sweet. And YES, I love puppies very, very much. I cannot even tell you how much I love doggies. The cool thing - the ONE strip mall in the area we'd move to, has a big animal medical center....and a doggie day care right there. I thought, HA! I wanna at least volunteer there.

I love dogs as much as some people love their kids...even if they are not even mine. I have even started cooking for them (my friend's dogs) as the one doesn't like the dry food (smartie pant!)...but always inhales what I make for him. but....I also think it's a big responsibility that I want to be ready for. Will I have energy? i don't know. I don't see them daily now, just a couple times a month and when I do, I give them tons of attention I wouldn't be able to on a daily basis probably. But you are totally RIGHT!! My friend's dogs are such treasures to me. They have such love and gratitude in their eyes when they look at me. Next week I will get to be with them a few days. They bring me incredible joy. But when I go to Germany, he or she'd be home alone all day.

THanks so much. So much to consider. Wish I was wealthy....could buy a place, or build it.....have more than one animal. Wish I hadn't lost so much over the last 10 years....but I know it does no good to think that. I am grateful for all I do have.

### sooperb — 2014-02-20T16:06:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111364>

If I had my way my house would be full of dogs and cats, especially those who have been tossed out by uncaring people. Dogs though are special. Who else gives you a reason to go out in the cold and wet, who is always pleased to see you when you come in, even if there's a shredded pillow or a pile of poo somewhere, who will come and lay a gentle head on your knee and look at you with eyes full of love, who will guard your bed at night and savage any ~cat~ strange person who might molest you as you sleep? I can think of a hundred reasons to have a dog and only about ~99~ a few not to. We are on dog number 3, a border collie rehomed by someone who could no longer give him the time he needed. He's not everything I'd like in a dog, poor chap is over trained and forever looking humble and worried. He's got big brown eyes and a lovely big nose though. Go on Hope, you know you want to.

### Hope — 2014-02-21T00:39:00+0200

### Hope — 2014-02-21T20:03:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111575>

**Housing Drama - wish me luck!! LOL
**
So all week I have been struggling with what to do about the housing situation because for some reason I never had a peace in my heart about the application we put in. Just always had a bad feeling......like we wouldn't get this house, or that it just wasn't right for me somehow.

Well, this morning the realtor said they had rented it to another family already. Quite sad. So now my DH is willing to put in an application to the house I loved all along...trouble is suddenly another family is looking at it too. Been on market 4 months.

The cool part? I knew something was not right, even if we had an appt to talk to the owner tomorrow. All week I wanted to put in an app into the other house....and DH didn't want to have two out there, because of the deposit and he was afraid he might not get it back.

So now I have to run to drop off an application to the other one..and hope that the family who is looking at it today is not going to take it. LOL.

Crazy part is...I am not at all sad that the hardwood house is gone.....it is more like a relief to me. The other one, the one we will apply to now, I love, and I will be sad to lose that one if we do for sure. But oh well. There is nothing I can do.....if they choose another family too.

I know I need to accept whatever happens, I was just really eager to leave here...leave the mold behind. But I think I might just double pay one month's rent somewhere. I was really hoping to be out of here by April 1....but if we find nothing now, we will have to wait...and I can't. My health is worth more to me, for sure. Even one month is too long...I have wanted to leave since November. oh well.

### fitness@home — 2014-02-21T22:06:42+0200

### Hope — 2014-02-21T22:30:15+0200

Dear Fitness, thanks for the sentiments...I really appreciate it a lot!  I know you are correct....

I rushed to submit the other application...but no idea till Monday (this is for Inger - if she reads this on Saturday)....that's what the office says. Although it's been on the market....four months and suddenly, some family looked at it this morning....not sure if they will put in an application.

### Hope — 2014-02-22T17:44:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111697>

So I am feeling really lousy...today marks the end of the worst sleep I have had in many months. I guess it's good that it was so good before...the stress with the housing situation this week. Last night I got maybe 3.5 hours of sleep, and all week it's been less than my usual 8 hours. Ugh. I feel like my RP has vanished, deep into the center of the earth nowhere to be found. My brain and emotions = weak. I can tell my frequency is low. Mostly I am terribly tired, and I wanted so much to get done today. But oh well. I do think that a visit to a chiropractor, where I hadn't been in months, contributed...not the adjustment but the air in there. Just awful, for me. I feel like I can smell mold more and more now, in places, though I always could before.

I hope to listen to the Tim Jackson replay. I might not take in much. But I was so encouraged that he has the dreaded genotype too....not bc I wish it for him but bc he seems to be in amazing health.....despite the issues. So if he can, so can I.

Trying to let go of the house and just surrender and accept whatever happens, which is easier as long as I don't think about it much....I know if not this, there will be another house. I just would love to move on...quite literally

### sooperb — 2014-02-22T19:30:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111708>

Hope, if that's your real name then it's a very inspiring one. My inner sense tells me that you are down but not out  Nothing happens without reason and whilst some people do drift and become at the mercy of the tide, you aren't one of them. I think once we decide to start stitching ourselves a new skin, we have to be aware that as we change we have to adjust the fit. We might snip the wrong bit out or decide a different colour would fit better so we alter again and again. Your house is on the pattern for your new skin too, you know you need to snip a bit out and replace it, it will happen because you will drive it instinctively and only commit when you are certain it will fit. When you do, that will be another piece of the pattern fixed in place until or if you need to change it again. Take heart, you're doing just fine, the new you is worth the effort and the journey

I love the photo, talking to a house seemed just a bit wierd lol.

### Hope — 2014-02-22T20:43:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111717>

We might snip the wrong bit out or decide a different colour would fit better so we alter again and again. Your house is on the pattern for your new skin too, you know you need to snip a bit out and replace it, it will happen because you will drive it instinctively and only commit when you are certain it will fit. When you do, that will be another piece of the pattern fixed in place until or if you need to change it again. Take heart, you're doing just fine, the new you is worth the effort and the journey
>
> I love the photo, talking to a house seemed just a bit wierd lol.

Sooperb, you are too kind. Thanks for those beautifully crafted words of encouragement. I appreciate them more than I can express on this day where I feel so weary....we have been looking for some time, or shall I say, I have....well it seems long to me  Just two months really, and part of the delay was that I was scoping out areas, on my own, to see where it would work for us both.....close enough to the office for DH (but not close so I would be exposed to the hundreds of towers and antennas in the area he works in, ugh) and yet far enough away for me to feel like we have some nature, more of a residential area. It took a while and searching but I think I've found it and then waiting for houses to open up, inspect them on my own from outside and then see the ones we really liked....it feels like a little bubble up there and is close to a big river....I dream of learning to kayak there. Get really good at it. Lots of trees too....sadly it's all very expensive. But we shall see what happens. I so love what you wrote to me, I must print it and read it regularly. I shall put it in my paper calendar .

Anyway, Hope isn't my real name but easier for the board...and when I thought what my name should be on here, Hope popped up. Oh, I am glad you don't mind the switch. The house is actually the summerhouse of Albert Einstein outside Berlin, as it is today (they keep it pretty much the same). I got to visit there last summer, very inspiring. He lived in such a small home, almost all year round, and it was quite small, the bedrooms, the kitchen, only the common/living room was a bit bigger. He really lived simply. Didn't like telephones even then but loved his baths. It is said it was his refuge....until he left Germany for good in 1932, to be safe from.... I wanted it to remind myself of his simplicity....in our house search. But today I needed a break from focusing on houses of all kinds. LOL.

I feel so tired today, it's hard not to want to be in bed, just watching the Inspector Lewis DVDs I borrowed. Love imagining myself in Oxford....smart people....beautiful building (minus the wifi). Thankfully DH is being very kind today, he peeled my brussels sprouts now roasting in the oven. Sadly last night we had a big fight... about a few things, and also about water, and how I should drink the tap as he is, ugh. Not the first time this week.

I had him do a taste test this morning of two waters. One was the regular spring water from the fridge and the other was the spring water I had vortexed and set outside last weekend in a gallon glass jar for a few hours in the sun. There is a distinct difference in how they fell in my mouth. Even he said there was a difference.

We've talked about getting water delivered in the past. Right now I pick up gallons once a week at the grocery store. Maybe it's time to look into delivery.

### sooperb — 2014-02-22T21:57:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111728>

It's certainly challenging living with someone who isn't on the same page isn't it ? I've always been the peace keeper at home in the sense that I have given in over many things because it was easier. I have had some successes though, especially with food, our diet has changed a lot in the last 10 years, we may not have lost weight that much weight but we eat a whole lot healthier. Plus hubby has been willing to try some things like giving up carbs because I've linked to numerous articles that prove they're bad for diabetics (he's type 2). If he can see proof then he will concede I could be right. Of course he isn't always convinced if it doesn't fit with what he thinks so there are plenty of areas (EMF for example) where I'm not winning yet lol. So I sit in my blue blockers while he doesn't.

That's where I've grown though, I now find that where once I would have given in or given up, now I just think, well, if it's not for him that's his decision, I'm going to try it anyway. He tends just to either poke fun for a while or pretend he hasn't noticed. I have to say he was mortified at my walking barefoot in the supermarket because they had a marble floor, there was a fair bit of hissing until I put my shoes back on. I didn't really mind because it was just a small thing, barefoot on the beach he didn't win

I've mentioned RO water, he's poo-pooed it, his basic science says it doesn't make sense. I'll leave it for now because I haven't got round to finding an explanatory article as yet to link him to. We drink filtered water and our tap water isn't fluoridated so I'm happy to wait for a while. I think perhaps I'm lucky in that I have a small amount of income that I can spend as I like. I'm just waiting on a shower filter (for MY bathroom lol) so I can shower in un-chlorinated water. He'll think it's another potty notion, do I care? Nope  As Jack has said many times, you're important so do it for you and let the mob fall in if they want to. If they don't well, that's their loss.

### Lahelada — 2014-02-22T22:46:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111733>

I think I am rather lucky that he now accepts that my way is rather eccentric. You should have sen him though when I let him in on the secret of eating fat to lose weight..(???)...and again..(.???).. New Year´s eve we had a guest who is a doc and while we were talking about motivation to do ,to change in patients ( my partner is a Chinese medicine practioner ) he admitted that I beat his expectations . Bide your time ladies . The general rule applies that the man will have the last word and they are universal : "yes,dear! "

### Hope — 2014-02-22T22:46:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111734>

Oh soo-perb (my autocorrect keeps switching to superb, though that fits you)....that is so kind of you to share. I feel alone, and more so when not well/tired/drained from a stressful week and housesearch. I so long to be out of mold..start over. but oh well. when we moved to the area, I couldn't find a thing for months. Went to Germany, and two days after my return, we found something and were chosen over another couple...moved in a week later.

Oh I love that

As Jack has said many times, you're important so do it for you and let the mob fall in if they want to. If they don't well, that's their loss.

That is so great too.....I am going to apply that to the search for a house too. So wish they'd accept that application. Must stay surrendered.

### Hope — 2014-02-22T23:37:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111746>

Soo and Lahe, wow, thank you so much for sharing those precious pictures of your precious pies  as my friend calls hers...

you have made me smile once again.

I am to be off to pet- and house-sit for a friend's house in the mountains...but so tired I have to wait till tomorrow, unless i go later.

### Inger — 2014-02-24T17:52:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111942>

Pet and house sit in the mountains.... mmmm... sounds lovely  you are one lucky girl

I am so exited about your house hunt Hope...! If you have to wait longer could you probably do some camping or live by a friend in a not moldy place for some weeks? Or maybe in a tent in the yard

Nut butters are no good for me either Hope... I never buy them. I also do not feel well from regular "unroasted" nuts from the supermarket. I need special quality, I buy truly raw organic nuts from Orkos in Germany.

When I lived in Hamburg I always got me the Fachinger water and the St Leonards Quelle. Their sunwater. I loved it! If I ever move there again these are the waters I am going to drink  I wish I could send you some of my own well and magnetized water, it is so yummy! I pour it from the bottle in a huge glass and stir with a wooden spoon to make a vortex. Very easy.. and very yummy. For free. Oh man, your DH can drink whatever he wants and so can you

If you want a pet I recommend a cat! They are so little work and so much love, I love how independent they are... and I love when my cat sleeps in my bed too! She is just so funny and clever. And eats only animals foods, mostly shrimps. You might be allowed a cat anywhere, they do not make much dirt or destroy the floors. They are very clean and easy. You could just promise to replace anything that your cat might destroy but so far my cat have never done anything but scratched my furniture lol

I love your pic, Hope...! Very beautiful

@ Sooperb, your cat has such a shiny fur... an pretty colors. wow!
@ Lahelada your dog is such a sweetie too...

### Hope — 2014-02-24T19:15:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111953>

Dear Inger, thanks so much....you are too kind to share. Yes I love those waters as well, when I am there. Amazing taste. When you only drink water, pretty much, your taste buds change eh?

So we are waiting for a response, turns out they just now got all our info to the owner, though the agent said we have a strong app and it's been on the market for 4 months, too. Still concerned with the oven, takes so long to heat, but they will clean it and said it is meant to be in working condition. It is 20 years old so definitely not energy efficient, which is good.

We can't have pets there, sadly. But it's not a forever place, just a place for me to heal. It is beautiful. Not fancy but inviting, clean, well built, you can tell. This helps avoid mold too and no basement, just a ground floor next to the garage.

I am a bit scared. Partly from all the work coming my way, but also because it seems so unreal to get to a place where there is less mold.....hard to describe. I hope I will have the strength indeed. But I feel good about it, too...though I don't like the terms....as in you have to resign a lease every year...it's not month to month like now, which is great.

I am also sad to leave our homeowners...they have been the kindest, most amazing landlords ever. Some LL are cheap or bossy, but not them. But oh well. I still love that house too, the layout etc, but the mold is bad. And the MAJOR power line too. This one, if we get it, will have underground wiring. Must test. We could actually put the bedroom in the bottom floor, though there is no door. We could add our own. Though I think DH would prefer it for himself, and use it as a man cave with his guitars, amps and speakers. It will also be a lot of work to keep vacuuming, but when I get well, it will be good exercise.

Inger, I am so glad you like the nuts from germany! I only do organic too. Already lowered my intake, only eat raw now. I also wanted to ask you....do you still use a facial moisturizer, or Argan oil? I just got some....want to play with it.

Oh and thanks for the kind words on the pic

Yes the puppies are awesome. They cannot wait for me to play with them. One of them, the one with the harder life who just visits here all the time....she grounds herself all the time, on the ground, in the creek, the pond. She is really teaching me so much. From her I knew all that Jack is teaching us is so true. I need to write a short story about her life as I see it when I am here. She vanishes into the forest...pines.....chasing all manners of things, even if she'd never catch or hurt them (I think), like the super fast bunny this morning. It is magical, indeed. And my dream is to have a vacation home out here too, like a refuge when things go crazy or if they do in the capitol of the country....I feel incredibly different here. Just one cellphone tower anywhere near. Internet barely functional....my whole body = calm. Except when I stress emotionally

### Inger — 2014-02-24T20:19:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111956>

Hope  if the oven is no good you could buy a gas fire cooking plate? I have seen those here, they are on my wish list... that way you can cook totally radiation free   who needs a oven? ;-) You know.. you are going to make delicious steaks everyday anyways for you and hubs

I use destilled Rose Water and Argan Oil on my face every morning and evening. I love it!!! And once in a while I do a peeling with ground coffee + natural soap or mud and after that a mask with raw egg yolk. Makes you a baby face

You really need to try the rose water! But take care it is the real destilled one, many fake rose waters out there. You will know it from the higher price and that it says destilled pure rose water only on the bottle. like this

[http://www.amazon.com/Organic-Bulga...1393265844&sr=8-6&keywords=organic+rose+water](http://www.amazon.com/Organic-Bulgarian-Water--250ml-organic/dp/B001JHZ6W0/ref=sr_1_6?ie=UTF8&qid=1393265844&sr=8-6&keywords=organic+rose+water)

Oh Inger, you are so brilliant. Thanks so much! Had not heard of that kind of oven  but will look into it for sure. Thanks a million....

### Hope — 2014-02-24T22:06:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-111972>

The house we didn't get....too much mold.

Wow, so I have to post this here..because I got another confirmation that my gut always knows best. So weird, eh?

But the house we didn't get, where I had some kind of SMELL, even if it was just windex, and I lacked a true peace the whole week we were waiting, and where my gut said I had to wait for the test anyway......well it was too high in mold. Weird eh? So lessons:

1) gut knows best
2) a hardwood floor house could be worse with mold than a carpeted one.
3) building materials, even to the lay person and what they look like, quality inside etc, does matter too. this hardwood house we lost.....it just didn't have the same quality as the one I am now waiting for.

Not sure what will be, but I am so excited for this. LOL. I am so crazy....but my gut led me right. Or maybe, I should say, my yucky, ueber sensitive genes and sense of smell.

Crossing fingers for the other house....

### Hope — 2014-02-25T23:44:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-112163>

So they accepted our offer...now we have to figure out the lease etc. I am so thankful, and scared at the same time. So much to do. Must make a list again, and use the one i made last time too. Moving is a big undertaking indeed. But I hope it all works out...the new place has a lovely bathtub, with a window in it (which I dreamed of) so I can let the air flow through....and put a candle in too. Telling you guys before I could even reach DH.

I want to try to clean as much stuff as possible, and toss our current couch too...or donate it.

yahooo Hope! Great news.

Thanks Audrey and Seahorse. I am equally happy and overwhelmed. And amazed we found a house with 20 year old appliances  I will miss our backyard to ground in, but we will have a small yard there and I can just sit on the front side of the house too...or better yet in a park nearby......

### Inger — 2014-02-26T08:45:43+0200

Hi Inger, thank you so much. I love love love your picture....really, you should be a model. Not that you want all that EMF in photoshoots but you could do country shoots? You are just so pretty!

We hope to move April 1.....though I will ask if we can stay in our current place several days longer paying rent there so I can clean and decontaminate as much stuff myself and we can move a lot of boxes on our own...and only hire movers for the big stuff. like the magneticos! They are so so heavy...and they will have to be carried two floors up, ugh.

yes I am excited, too...can't wait to take a bath with a window in the room  though I hope all will be well with the stove.

A bathroom with a window, magical, it sounds just lovely, you have chosen wisely and fate has agreed. The stove is not important, no one needs to eat cooked food, we just do because we like to. I wish I could come and help you move, I could benefit from the LHT  Throw your mouldy stuff away and manage until you can afford new.

Oh thanks Sooperb. I sure hope all comes through well.  I can imagine you doing the cartwheels. It's been a long, hard process.

The moving and packing I hope I will get through OK. My DH, who likes to keep as many papers as possible, may not be willing to trash a lot of his papers...even if he doesn't need them or could scan them...or I could for him. I will see what we do. Definitely losing the couch we have now and a nearly 18 year old loveseat - the first piece of furniture I got when I moved here for grad school.

I wish I could get away with non-cooked food. I don't digest raw well, but maybe when my MSH comes up and my leaky gut and leaky BBB function better, I can. who knows?

how are you doing??

### sooperb — 2014-02-26T17:46:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-112280>

See my journal, ha flying now I am. Well, better anyway  I didn't mean raw exactly Hope, there's plenty you can eat that you don't have to cook, tinned samon, tuna and sardines, some raw cheeses, smoothies that you can chuck an avocado in and butter and EVCO, even raw eggs if you don't mind living dangerously lol. At a pinch cooked meats with a good provenance if you can source them. Or you could even cook your own and freeze some before you move to eke out until you resolve the oven issue. You know in some countries many a century ago, the bakers would put peoples joints in their ovens once the bread was made, communities offered so much to each other then. How about a barbecue stove? Might fit the bill for a while. There are no problems, only solutions. I was a girl guide in a previous life lol.

### Hope — 2014-02-26T17:57:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-112282>

Oh how neat! I am sure it will be OK. The stove part works, but I love my roasted cauliflower or brussel sprouts, it's like eating chips. Or kale chips, YUMM!!  But I am thinking I could use it as a dehydrator. It is quite filthy which I hate but oh well. They said they'll clean it.

Can't do cheese...my RP is too low. I do way better without it. It REALLY helped my sleep....

### Hope — 2014-02-26T19:50:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-112313>

So I am sitting here crying.....I don't know why completely. Maybe it's release or the pressure coming off, or feeling like maybe SOMEONE recognizes that I am doing something right, and doesn't think I am not a crazy or bad person the way my husband often does because of the way I want to live, or eat, or drink which some days it is way harder than others (right now being harder) (even if the landlord doesn't know my diet etc) ? Or because I want to move to have a better chance to heal? Maybe because he recognizes that I was doing a good job taking care of the house (It's mostly me)....I don't know. I guess it's just nice to hear something NICE said about you. Because our realtor kept complaining about the fact that I wanted to do HERTSMI2 testing for three houses and she wanted us out of her hair (which I understand), and talking about how we needed good landlord references even though I knew we'd have them because I have always tried so hard to keep everything in good shape. I may be a crazy or boring person but I always try to do right, or my best. Or maybe because it's rare to be dealing just with someone NICE and now we won't anymore. Just being nice is such a gift. Or maybe it's because I have no idea if the new people will be nice too? Or maybe because they were so willing (and did) give us a new stove before we moved in..and actually offered, after asking if I liked to cook? And then they did? I don't know. Maybe I am just crazy and really struggling with the lack of my husband's support.....even my mold support group leader said, it sounds like we are not on the same page.

here's what he says:

Hi ....,

Thanks for the prompt notice and good luck in the new place. I hope it works well for you both. I'll give you a call soon so we can discuss the details more specifically. Also, I received a phone call this morning regarding your tenancy and I provided them with a very accurate account of your rental history at xxxx. In other words, I had nothing but stellar praise for your time there. I'll be tied up in meetings most of the day, but I will try to give you a call this evening.

What a nice thing to say indeed. I am not a total failure....even if my husband thinks I'm really mostly a burden and he hates the money he's lost because of my health issues.

### Hope — 2014-02-26T19:52:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-112315>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=112310)
>

Thanks...will have to check it out after we move.....

### Gagnrad — 2014-02-26T20:05:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-112318>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=112276)
>
> I wish I could get away with non-cooked food. I don't digest raw well, but maybe when my MSH comes up and my leaky gut and leaky BBB function better, I can. who knows?

How about dried meat?

This is additive free, though fairly expensive per pack:

[http://britishbeefjerky.co.uk/shop/index.php?main\_page=product\_info&cPath=1&products\_id=4](http://britishbeefjerky.co.uk/shop/index.php?main_page=product_info&cPath=1&products_id=4)

Or there's Sussman's:

[http://www.biltong.co.uk/Category/beef](http://www.biltong.co.uk/Category/beef)

I like the genuine air-dried Italian ham, too. There's Spanish air-dried ham around, too, these days, although the Spanish stuff sometimes has additives in, whereas the Italian prosciutto is usually very clean.

When I think about it I do eat more raw protein and fat than it seems like I do at first take, since I tend to eat cooked beef or lamb very rare and also eat various dried meats, smoked salmon, raw egg yolks, raw oysters, and sometimes take the unheated fermented cod liver oil.

I've cut the unpasteurised cheese, even goat's and ewe's milk cheese, because I'm not sure I do well on dairy, but the rest isn't bad to be going on with, if hardly in Inger's class.

I bet most people could do all right on biltong and air-dried prosciutto. And how about smoked salmon?

### sooperb — 2014-02-26T21:51:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-112336>

I bought some S.African biltong from a place I know in Edinburgh, brought it home and my son, who doesn't even live here any more, found it and ate all but a couple of scraps, he's like a mobile dustbin sometimes. The bit I tried tasted lovely, I liked it a lot. Other biltong I've seen locally has all sorts of grot in it.

Aw Hope, cheer up, it's probably relief that you're moving toward where you want to be but sad to be leaving and in a way, heading into the unknown. We're all on a journey and it can sometimes be rocky and a bit frightening and we have to make hard decisions. Your health is important to you more than it is to anyone else, it's your life and you only get one crack at it, if your husband can't see that, try explaining it and if he still doesn't get it .........

Your health is important to you more than it is to anyone else, it's your life and you only get one crack at it, if your husband can't see that, try explaining it and if he still doesn't get it .........

Thanks for the reminder, Sooperb. I guess I feel stressed or whatever. Sometimes I wish I had a more supportive DH. I have tried many times to help him understand, he was even on a consult with Dr Shoemaker (mold expert MD) but he puts it all down. He knows better than all the experts we learn from, and has for years. The fact that I have gotten worse in the last couple years, since we lived where we do, doesn't faze him somehow. I can't rely on him, at all. And you are correct - no one will care but me. It's just sometimes, just a little encouragement would be nice. Because I work so hard...and yet he mostly complains...like the bottled water I buy. I will look for a cheaper source post move. yet, he is unwilling to give up the cable TV I never watch, really anymore....because "he does use it." 10 minutes each morning....and I don't know when else. Weekends.

Anyway. Thank you for the encouragement. And gagnrad, I will have to look at dried beef. I do eat smoked salmon, mackerel and herring too. lots of canned sardines.

### Hope — 2014-03-03T20:04:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-113192>

Rough day. DH stayed home bc of snow. I feel tired today, started packing yesterday. So much work. I need to sit down and write a plan for each room in an Excel spreadsheet. Go through, what I can do now - take it out of the home, clean, pack in an airtight container. 2 more boxes to giveaway too but my belongings seem to have no end. I have way too much. Trying to use up my candles too. had a fight with DH too bc he doesn't get it, about the cleaning etc. So sad. He puts down whatever Dr Shoemaker recommends if it's not convenient. Ugh. It breaks my heart that old books that can be bought again are more important than me. At least it feels like it. I am sure my brain isn't up to par today either. But he will have to replace the pillows that I will toss that I had before the wedding. They will go. I do have a lot more stuff than he. I wanna be a minimalist. No. I am becoming a minimalist. In my heart, I am closer and closer to being a minimalist.

I did go in the snow a bit. Knees faired much better than feet. Want to get all my legs into the snow. So nice, and gentle and beautiful. I just love snow. I hope maybe some day I can handle humidity as well as I do cold temps now. I do love cold temps. Oh another fight about that too....he wants it at 70, I at 60. his compromise? 66.

In this morning's affirmation, I was encouraged (and said out loud),

Today I will resist nothing that occurs.

Eh. Maybe I can manage that tomorrow.

### sooperb — 2014-03-03T23:56:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hope-rising.6536/post-113219>

I am sad for you Hope and I endorse what Inger says, you just do your thing, there are two people in a partnership and neither one is more important than the other. Stand your ground, it's important to you and for you. Quite honestly, even if the whole thing was phooey, you have a right to try if it's what you want to do. Why not? You aren't about to tip your union into financial ruin I'm sure. Just as I'm sure that your DH has his belief system and would be defensive about it if you were to challenge it, so you have the same right to hold on to yours.

My husband isn't at all supportive about EMFs. Years ago there was a lot in the UK media about the effect pylons had on people who lived under and/or near them. It led to research which found there was no evidence to suggest it was harmful. Despite the complaints of headaches and illness and the higher than average incidence of child cancers, it was declared that there was no link. That was quite a few years ago, there's a wealth of new research which has established causal links yet he hasn't read any of it, he just refers back to the old stuff and says they never found any evidence, it's a load of nonsense. You know what, I don't care if he believes it, I will still work towards minimising my exposure and if he wants to fry his mitochondria while I'm not there, he can

Fortunately, for the sake of our relationship, he has embraced the bits that would be more awkward and difficult if he didn't, the dietary side, some supplements and the quality of what we eat. I'm not the strongest willed person going but sometimes, if you're passionate, you find a way. I can roll with being called whacky, peculiar and being subjected to the rolling eyes treatment but more importantly whatever he thinks, he doesn't stand in my way too much.

I so hope your DH will be able to widen his views, he doesn't have to agree but neither should he actively seek to prevent you doing what you believe is best for you, you wouldn't do it to him I'm sure. Maybe once the move is done and there is less stress you can come to some sort of agreement

Oh.. dear Hope..
maybe try these things as soon as your energy is too low;

open all the doors and windows when your hubs is away and enjoy the fresh and cool air breeze that touches you when you are working... it is amazing! Especially if you have little clothes on and you feel it on the skin

Sing.... play that you are a singer if it helps, if you like put on you favorite music loud and sing to it while working (very loud singing works the best!)- and make the whole packing thing to a play too, I do this if I feel something is not too funny.. Think beautiful thoughts

Drink lots of water

### Hope — 2014-03-22T19:29:02+0200
