# Thread: can anyone explain why watches won't work on my Dad
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-explain-why-watches-wont-work-on-my-dad.6591/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### agatha — 2013-04-02T23:26:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-explain-why-watches-wont-work-on-my-dad.6591/post-82643>

My Dad doesn't wear a watch - they won't go on him. He used to try have the old fashioned wind up analogue kind but after a couple of months the watch would stop working. After he took it off his wrist it would start to work again after a while. I don't think he's tried battery or digital watches.

Can anyone explain why the watches would not work? Could it be something to do with energy fields?

### Kiera2 — 2013-04-03T00:27:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-explain-why-watches-wont-work-on-my-dad.6591/post-82649>

I can't explain it, but my grandfather was the same. Any watch he wore would slow to a stop, but start up again once he took it off. He was also a water dowser; he could find underground water just by walking through a paddock with a stick in each hand. I'm sure the two are related, but I don't know how it works.

### shilohman — 2013-04-03T00:42:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-explain-why-watches-wont-work-on-my-dad.6591/post-82653>

> [agatha said:](/goto/post?id=82643)
>
> My Dad doesn't wear a watch - they won't go on him. He used to try have the old fashioned wind up analogue kind but after a couple of months the watch would stop working. After he took it off his wrist it would start to work again after a while. I don't think he's tried battery or digital watches.
>
> Can anyone explain why the watches would not work? Could it be something to do with energy fields?

Just guessing here, but maybe your dad is just a well grounded guy. If I remember right and I may be way off on this, but the analogue wind up type of watches used a crystal which grounding may interfere with. Anyone else know for sure?

### lioness7 — 2013-04-03T01:14:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-explain-why-watches-wont-work-on-my-dad.6591/post-82656>

Well this is an interesting question similar to what I wanted to post. My husband goes through watch batteries several times a year. The wind up types need repairing often as well. I have seen him burn out light bulbs when he touches switches , remotes for the tv don't "listen" and his secretaries won't allow him near their computers for fear of them crashing. He hates electronics otherwise. I would love to hear anyone's take or experience as well.

### paleo-bunny — 2013-04-03T02:55:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-explain-why-watches-wont-work-on-my-dad.6591/post-82662>

I kill any replacement watch batteries after about two months, as does my father with his watches. This applies both to cheap and relatively expensive watches.

I tried an imported analogue wind-up vintage soviet watch, which I killed after a few months, admittedly probably through over-winding.

I blow a lot of lightbulbs, especially near or just after a full moon - I also trip switches and break various other electronics. It can get expensive.

### Hope — 2013-04-03T04:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-explain-why-watches-wont-work-on-my-dad.6591/post-82665>

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-03T06:34:42+0300

# Thread: Hammer Toe

### caroline — 2013-04-03T13:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hammer-toe.6598/post-82690>

Does anyone have any experience with hammer toe? I have had one for many years that hasn't been too much of a problem \[except for buying shoes\] but now I have another one beside the first one and it is pretty painful. I think it is from all the hours of barefoot walking at the ocean.
The first one was from 35 years of standing/working on my feet. orthotics were helpful.

My physio thinks I probably need surgery. I understand that they release the tendon and put in a metal pin......that doesn't seem like such a good idea giving what we are learning about EMF's! I don't particularly want my toes to become a lightning rod!

Any ideas?

### nonchalant — 2013-04-03T15:45:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hammer-toe.6598/post-82697>

Do you wear flip-flops?

[http://www.alignedandwell.com/katysays/open-toes-open-hearts/](http://www.alignedandwell.com/katysays/open-toes-open-hearts/)

I think surgery would be a mistake.

### caroline — 2013-04-04T02:03:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hammer-toe.6598/post-82764>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=82697)

>
> [http://www.alignedandwell.com/katysays/open-toes-open-hearts/](http://www.alignedandwell.com/katysays/open-toes-open-hearts/)
>
> I think surgery would be a mistake.

No - I don't wear flip flops ...I find them very uncomfortable - I need some arch support. I do wear "Fit Flops" however - they do have an arch and I love them and have been wearing them for years. I just tried them on after your post and I don't need to grip with my toes to keep them on. I will monitor that tho - thanks so much for the imput!

Why do you think surgery would be a mistake? other than the obvious?

### Destiny — 2013-04-04T02:33:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hammer-toe.6598/post-82772>

My father had a surgery for his hammer toe 3 years ago and he is happy with the result. However, I don't have any details and doubt my father was even told how it was done.

### Rachelle — 2013-04-04T16:52:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hammer-toe.6598/post-82837>

I had a friend who had surgery also and is happy. They only did one foot at a time and there is quite a bit of down time but she is very happy she did it.

### Pamela — 2013-04-04T17:32:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hammer-toe.6598/post-82841>

I had hammer toes as a young person and at about 20 yrs old had surgery on both feet. The Dr. said instead of breaking the bones, let's just stretch the tendons, it will be easier and not a problem. I said ok and it was HORRIBLE. I was waitressing at the time, Dr. says I'll be able to go back within a week to work, yea right. I was literally crawling in my house for several days. To this day I get cramps in my feet that I believe are from those stretched tendons. I can't say if breaking the bone and resetting it would have been any better. The only plus side to it all is that the toes are somewhat flatter but I still have a bump so have to be careful on shoes.

### caroline — 2013-04-05T01:52:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hammer-toe.6598/post-82902>

# Thread: Switching off pain
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/switching-off-pain.6599/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Linz — 2013-04-03T14:25:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/switching-off-pain.6599/post-82692>

I don't recommend trying this at home, but I would love to know if others have noticed a changed perception of pain.

I dropped a heavy weight on a toe a few weeks ago (black nail). Soaking in very cold water without ice really helped the pain but it came back soon after going to sleep. Soaking eased the pain but the second time I was too tired and took an otc painkiller. No relief at all after 1/2 hour. Then I discovered just dipping my foot in the water briefly seemed to completely switch off the pain for about an hour at a time.

Since then I have read Dr K's book and the fascinating detail about his experience of pain when he did his surgical hack. I live quite well grounded, often outside in the cold, with a long term good diet but have only dabbled with CT by JK standards and really thought the ct would have to be quite extreme to make a big difference. In the past I can remember soaking hurt fingers and toes in water but only achieving some relief while I was actually doing it.

This week (most definitely careless and not an N=1!!) another squashed and rather lacerated toe. This time has been almost pain free. Cold water and barefeet turns off the pain (not just dulling it). I used a grounding mat under my legs at night and only woke a few times. The worst pain was when I had to go shopping for an hour two days after the injury and I came home with all my toes hot and red rather like chillblains.

### nonchalant — 2013-04-03T15:38:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/switching-off-pain.6599/post-82693>

Yes, Linz, I've experienced a reduction in the perception of pain from CT. Wasp stings, bacon grease splatters, stubbed toes, and one memorable encounter with a bull nettle plant.
I was not CT'ing directly before or after those experiences, but I had been CT'ing regularly. My skin registered the pain, but it was 'shut off' after a few minutes. (Just like the 'pain' sensation when stepping into an ice bath gets turned off in a minute or two.) Actually, for the bacon grease, I don't register much pain at all.

I used to think ice would make it feel better only when the area was numb, but it's like my body has learned a new trick.  My experience with the wasp sting and bull nettle were before I started grounding. I that would make an additional difference. I have seen videos where grounding electrodes were placed on either side of a wound, to promote healing. Same for TENS.

Glad your toes are surviving.

### Dali Dula — 2013-04-03T15:38:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/switching-off-pain.6599/post-82694>

I noticed changes in pain sensitivity after about six months of paleo. i attributed it to changes in O3/O6 ratio. Dental sensitivity to both pain and cold is what made me notice it. Another observaton- sense of smell has greatly improved over the last 20 months. I can now smell things like ammonia in fish products that I'm pretty sure I was missing before. Kind of annoying actually, I discard a lot more fish than I used to.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-28T02:04:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/switching-off-pain.6599/post-86979>

Success story today! DH has been experimenting with water for headaches, and today, while he was tending the lawn, he asked for something to drink "because I have a big headache". Woot!! He gets it! And sure enough, in about 30 minutes he was feeling fine. I make sure his coffee & tea also are made with quality water now. He also complains a lot less now when we go get water from a local spring. (Guess I can be aggressive too, Dr K!)

The science may be complex, but the application can be quite simple.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-05-28T03:56:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/switching-off-pain.6599/post-86995>

This is how CT improves your semiconductors........realize that cold temp's increase electrical currents in superconductors and not in a electric circuit or an ionic circuit. This is why.........it works. Then read todays blog post on how current is determined and marry it with this information here. You might begin to see where the magic of CT really is. It is not hormetic. It is built into us by design.

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-04-12T18:12:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schumann-generator.6621/post-83908>

> [Rachelle said:](/goto/post?id=82832)
>
> I was thinking about getting the earthsafe whole house but have no experience with them. I hope someone chimes in here.
>
> Here is the site. [http://www.lessemf.com/schumann.html](http://www.lessemf.com/schumann.html)

I have the EarthSafe Whole House unit in my bedroom, and it is plugged into the grounding connection on one of the wall power sockets. I'm running it 24/7. I noticed a difference almost immediately upon powering it on. Also, when I walk into the house after work, it feels like a weight has been lifted from my shoulders...uncanny. In conjunction with earthing, and a Magnetico 10 gauss sleep pad, I'm experiencing a full night's sleep with no awakening and no need for bathroom breaks.

I started out just using an earthing strap attached to the K-1 kidney meridian on my foot, and noticed that my sleep seemed more refreshing. I did this for about a month and then decided to purchase a magnetic sleep pad. I then had a week's experience using the grounding strap/magnetic pad combo, and there was significant sleep improvement but I still woke up every once in a while. When I added the resonator, there was yet another significant improvement, and my sleep has been uninterrupted ever since. Every night feels like a small but noticeable improvement over the previous one, so I'm still moving toward some sort of equilibrium.

So...this has been my n=1 experience so far. As always, your mileage may vary. ;-)

Just wanted to mention that all of this is in addition to ditching the wi-fi, turning off the cell phone and other emf-generating appliances at night, and practicing light 'hygiene' after sundown. A meter for testing yourself is absolutely necessary, IMHO. My home environment appears to be relatively low emf, and I have a newer house with low-e windows which block a lot of external radiation. I do have a smart meter, though... :-( fortunately it's on the other side of the house away from the living areas.

Seems like all of the puzzle pieces that Jack is giving us are coming together. I sure am learning a lot!

### [email redacted] — 2013-05-17T03:51:40+0300

### Trinity — 2013-05-18T15:33:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schumann-generator.6621/post-86406>

Hi Guys,

I recently purchased the Earth Safe Whole House unit to put in my bedroom. I have been struggling with chronic insomnia and high nighttime cortisol for years now so I was hoping it would help my sleep! I've only had it plugged in 2 nights. Subjectively, I've noticed 2 things:

1) I noticed feeling a bit more relaxed in my bed (high nighttime cortisol seems to cause a lot of muscle achiness and tension)
2) My sleep has been a bit better the past couple nights although there is definitely room remaining for improvement

I can't say for sure if the Schumann unit is solely responsible for those improvements because there are obviously a lot of variables (plus the placebo effect , but I do find it interesting and intend to continue my experiment.

\--Trinity

There are EMF's being emitted from these units, but at 7.83 that would seem to be a good thing. Not as good as going back a hundred years and having NO microwaves blasting around the planet, but we are trying to do the best we can.

I'd like to see some more trials and testing for effect on these Schumann generators. I'm intrigued.

### JackFrake — 2013-05-22T23:43:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schumann-generator.6621/post-86684>

What's the difference between sleeping on a Magnetico vs. sleeping with a Schumann Generator under your mattress or next to your bed?

Aren't they basically doing the same thing? Is there a noticeable difference b/w sleeping with a Magnetico compared to adding a Schumann Generator, or is it marginal?

A Magnetico vs. Schumann for DUMMYs summary would be greatly appreciated!

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-05-23T04:22:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schumann-generator.6621/post-86701>

> [JackFrake said:](/goto/post?id=86684)
>
> What's the difference between sleeping on a Magnetico vs. sleeping with a Schumann Generator under your mattress or next to your bed?
>
> Aren't they basically doing the same thing? Is there a noticeable difference b/w sleeping with a Magnetico compared to adding a Schumann Generator, or is it marginal?
>
> A Magnetico vs. Schumann for DUMMYs summary would be greatly appreciated!

Magnetico Pad = produces a static (not a wave) magnetic field...augments the Earth's field which has diminished over the decades.

Shumann resonator = generates electromagnetic wave resonating at 7.83 Hz which mimics the field in the cavity between the Earth's surface and the ionosphere. The presence of man-made EMF has greatly diminished its natural effect, hence the need to augment it with the resonator.

Two different things are going on... our bodies evolved directly exposed to the Earth's magnetic field and the Schumann wave. The present condition in most of our living environments is such that we don't 'see' these fields very well, if at all, and hence our bodies are not optimal as a result.

My personal experience... started off sleeping grounded, and that made a noticeable difference in my sleep, but I was still getting up at night and the sleep wasn't deep. With the addition of the Magnetico pad, the sleep quality jumped to the next level, and as time has progressed it has gotten better and better. I'm asleep no longer than 5 minutes after I crawl into bed, and the next thing I know it's time to get up. It's awesome, and I feel so good in the morning. The Schumann generator has provided a noticeable but more subtle effect. When I first turned it on I became aware of feeling very calm, yet alert, within 30 minutes of activation. I haven't turned it off since, so I may have acclimated to its effects. I think that the combination of these three technologies has produced my result, and I'm amazed and thrilled with it.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-07-22T04:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schumann-generator.6621/post-90547>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=90520)
>
> Thanks ATL\_Paleo... This is great....
> Do you have to have the "electronics" UNPLUGGED for them to stop emitting EMFs or is it OK to just have them turned off... if turned off and they still emit, does it make the distance you need to be away from them lower?
> I'm definitely going to get that computer grounding wire... I was just afraid it's the same concept as the grounding mats and by using it I will actually be getting MORE dirty electricity??? Any truth to that?

In order to know how much EMF you are exposed to, you really need to measure it. There are a number of EMF meters available ... see Michael's website for more info. I have used both a gauss meter and body voltage (BV) meter. Without testing (measuring) I would not have known how much EMF was in my life.

Based on the test results, I have rearranged my computer desk area, turned off a motor that was emitting unreal amounts of EMF, unplugged some seldom used electric devices, and turn off my bedroom circuit breaker every night before bed. In addition, I no longer use WiFi (hard wire from cable modem to computer), and ditched my cordless phone for plain, old boring corded landline.

Michael stated in the boot camp, that focusing on your sleeping area is an excellent starting point since your body is in "repair" mode as you sleep. As an experiment, after more than a month of sleeping with the bedroom circuit breaker off, I tried going to sleep w/ the breaker on. After about 10-15 minutes of tossing and turning, I turned the breaker off, and quickly fell asleep. So in my case, I am sensitive enough to ELF fields from the wiring in my bedroom walls that it impacts the quality of sleep.

As Dr. Kruse says ... test, test, test ....... this advice also applies to EMF.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-12T05:07:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/schumann-generator.6621/post-137624>

Colder temperatures increase the strength of a magnetic field. Why? Cold can have an effect on magnetism, because cold can have an effect of how fast or slow electrons move. This links the sun’s power to the magnetic field of the Earth. It also links the **_Schumann resonance_** strength to the sun. When the sun is shining the Schumann frequency is strongest. When the sun is shining the Earth’s magnetic field is weakest. This implies that the Schumann resonance strength varies inversely with the magnetic field’s strength. _**At night time,**_ **_the magnetic field is stronger on Earth._ So when you use a fake generator better be well thought out.**

# Thread: 23 year old toe nail fungus spontaneously resolving with full on CT and BAB
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23-year-old-toe-nail-fungus-spontaneously-resolving-with-full-on-ct-and-bab.6651/>

**Posts:** 34

### curves64 — 2013-04-05T22:25:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23-year-old-toe-nail-fungus-spontaneously-resolving-with-full-on-ct-and-bab.6651/post-83012>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/imag0626-2-jpg.83/)

I've had toe nail fungus for 23 years. I know exactly when I got it because I was pregnant with my now 22-year-old daughter. I have tried just about everything to resolve it. I've tried every topical and oral medication conceived of, and even had my toe nails removed (ouch!). Nothing worked for very long. What I came to realize after discovering Dr. Kruse a year ago, was that my toe nail fungus was most likely an outward representation of internal inflammation (hsCRP last October was 2.15). When I decided to go full out CT early this year, I never would have guessed that it would have an impact on my long-standing problem. If you look at my pic you can see that right below the ugly part of my toe nail is a clean, brand-spankin new clear nail. Now, I don't know how this will turn out but I'm really encouraged. It takes about 18 months to grow out a big toe nail so I'm keeping my fingers crossed!!

Along the lines of gross things fixing themselves.... I have had a mole under my left breast for about 2 yrs. Kind of rapid growing odd shape....the kind you "keep an eye on" and "mean to get checked". I read in the iodine thread, I think it was Grizz that just started putting iodine on any random growth on herself, her husband, her dog....so i figured what the hell??

Put some on it....damned if that thing isnt shrinking! Could be diet, CT, or the iodine. Whatever it is I'll take it!

Keep us posted on the toe

### curves64 — 2013-04-08T00:12:44+0300

Lovelife, no you didn't miss a blog on the photoelectric effect. I asked Dr. Kruse about it on the members QA last month and he told me to google it as well.  -- Turns out I had already googled it and read about it from more than one site but I didn't get how it all tied in. On that call he said something about it's dual function as a particle and a wave.

I'm not sure this will help but here's a bit on it from Wikipedia: "Study of the photoelectric effect led to important steps in understanding the quantum nature of light and electrons and influenced the formation of the concept of waveâ€“particle duality.\[1\] Other phenomena where light affects the movement of electric charges include the photoconductive effect (also known as photoconductivity or photoresistivity), the photovoltaic effect, and the photoelectrochemical effect. It also led to Max Planck's discovery of quantized energy and the Planck Relation (E = hv), which links a photon's frequency with its energy. The factor h is known as the Planck constant."

Don't get me wrong. I'm completely fascinated by the science, but honestly, I don't understand most of it. I know this will sound odd, but I just "feel" my way around and let my feelings/intuition guide me. I'm not driven to act by the science, i'm driven to act by my "inner knowing." If you find a layman's description of the photoelectric effect please share

As for the iodine, it is definitely accelerating the healing of my fire ant bitten toes -- next stop, skin tags and moles!!

I will

I live in a small city 30 km away from Helsinki (biggest city in Finland, where I also work, part time). there is lots of EMF for sure.. I use the train to get there, about 40 minutes - and I have measured it and it was not too bad actually if sitting in the mid of the wagon! I do have looked for another workplace for summer and I got the job too. It is right by the sea! A restaurant where I get to work outside too.. I am sure there are way less EMF than the other place I use to work. That is why I picked it, it is a bit outside the big city too. I am only working there 1-2 days / week as that is what I want, it is enough to do here in summer - I take care of the yard and animals so I need to pay no rent. But yes, I do am concerned about my work place too so that is why I changed. I do loved my old place but I still can work there once in a while if I want.

This small city I live in here has about 30 000 people living on a quite large area. Not too much EMF but sure, here are 3G too.. sadly. And some towers and stuff... and smart meters. And people have wifis and all this stuff.. a lot. But closest neighbours are more than 100 yards away so no wifi signals to catch in this house, thanks God.

When my Lugol's arrives I am going to make my N=1.
I am going to paint some moles, and I am going to paint my small lipoma too for sure! Ha. It does not really bother me, and I tried some halfhearted CT on it last winter.. but it is still there. Maybe Iodine helps? It won't hurt to try!

I've tried every topical and oral medication conceived of, and even had my toe nails removed (ouch!). Nothing worked for very long. What I came to realize after discovering Dr. Kruse a year ago, was that my toe nail fungus was most likely an outward representation of internal inflammation (hsCRP last October was 2.15). When I decided to go full out CT early this year, I never would have guessed that it would have an impact on my long-standing problem. If you look at my pic you can see that right below the ugly part of my toe nail is a clean, brand-spankin new clear nail. Now, I don't know how this will turn out but I'm really encouraged. It takes about 18 months to grow out a big toe nail so I'm keeping my fingers crossed!!

Congradulations  you will have a beautiful nail again...after all these years..
I had the same problem (but for shorter period of time)... my nail fungus lasted for about 3 years..I used many kinds of methods and only laser treatment helped my. Of course, it costed a lot for me...but it was worth.

# Thread: What the heck happened to my sleep?!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-heck-happened-to-my-sleep.6661/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-04-06T15:31:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-heck-happened-to-my-sleep.6661/post-83114>

I am beyond baffled right now. My latest labs (posted in another thread, but I'll post them below too) are the best I've had in years and yet two days after having these drawn my sleep totally tanked! That was over 2 weeks ago and it hasn't improved yet. I am so exhausted and just frustrated, confused, angry . . . my labs are still far from perfect, but they do show a huge improvement, so what the heck happened to my sleep?!

When we started cutting wifi and the breakers to the bedrooms at night several weeks ago my sleep was awesome! I noticed a difference immediately. A few weeks after that we got a 10 gauss Magnetico pad and I was sleeping on that for about a week before my sleep tanked this time . . . could it really just be a detox effect??
We've tested and tested for EMF and I'm not finding any hidden/terribly high sources beyond things we can't control like the refrigerator/oven, etc. I cut back use of my laptop to maybe an hour a day and even that I've been doing outside while barefoot on the ground. I'm getting at least 2-3 hours a day of earthing in.

I tried increasing my Progesterone to 300 mg and while it did help my sleep for the few nights I did it, it also ended up making me extremely nauseous on the fourth night and I woke with major muscle weakness, which I later read is due to high Progesterone. So I cut back down to 200 mg last night and sleep was crap again. I wake in the middle of the night and feel wide awake and HOT for several hours before I can finally fall back to sleep again for a short while.

Considering the magnetico and my labs below, any thoughts on what may be causing this?? The only other thought I have is with my DHEA being a little high. I don't think Jack ever mentioned this could be a problem, BUT I know in the past when I was taking a higher amount of DHEA my sleep tanked even though I expected the opposite to happen. I'm thinking about skipping or lowering my DHEA the next couple of days to see what effect, if any, that has, but if anyone has any other thoughts/suggestions I'd love to hear them! Thanks

Current Supplements
\- 5 mcg T3
\- 1 gr Naturethroid
\- 200 mg Compounded Progesterone (I did start 100 mg Prometrium vaginally 3 days before testing, but I donâ€™t think that would have really impacted the results yet, right?)
\- 65 mg DHEA (Iâ€™m now decreasing that to 50 mg since my testosterone was high and Iâ€™m assuming the DHEA is converting to T; thinking about switching to 7-keto??)
\- 25 mg Pregnenolone (Iâ€™m now increasing to 50 mg)
\- 12,000 IU Vitamin D (going to increase this to 50,000 IU/day and hit tanning hard)

### BJK77 — 2013-04-08T15:31:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-heck-happened-to-my-sleep.6661/post-83344>

> [Joann said:](/goto/post?id=83264)

I was wondering about that too. I know before when I first started takign DHEA I was using a much higher dose. I didn't get testing done, but my sleep did get crappy and I ended up cutting back on the DHEA and sleep then improved. I have been cutting back the last 2 nights now though with no change at all.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=83265)
>
> did the expert check you car and work and not just your house?

He did check my vehicle, which I already knew wasn’t great, but I rarely drive it. At most, it’s 20 minutes a day to get to and from work. I didn’t have the EMF Engineer go into my workplace, but I took the meters in and I sit in front of a computer, so obviously it wasn’t great either, but it wasn’t as bad as I expected. I do use a laptop, but I have it in a docking station with an extra monitor, wired keyboard and wired mouse and the laptop itself is a good 3’ or more away from where I sit. I moved all the cables as far away from where I sit as possible and I brought in a huge Himalayan salt lamp to help as well. I also don’t have wifi in my office, thankfully. Plus, I only went into the office one day last week so I was mostly home and we have very low levels at home, not to mention the fact that I spent most of every day outside earthing last week.

I know I could be wrong, but my gut tells me this can’t be EMF. I 100% believe in the dangers of it, but we have really been doing so much over the last several weeks to minimize it as much as we possibly can. Like I said above, short of cutting out the refrigerator and oven I don’t see much more we can do. And to that regard, I have even started doing my long-term cooking like broths and roasts outside on the patio or in the garage in a crockpot or small convection toaster oven.

My neighborhood consists of 1 to 3-acre lots, so we’re fairly well spread out. I can see 2 of my neighbor’s wifi’s, but not at a strong enough signal to ever connect to them.

What doesn’t make sense to me is that my sleep greatly, HUGELY improved when we started cutting wifi and the breakers at night, so I know we are on the right path, but something else is obviously at play here.

Hope,

I do remember reading on their site that it may take some individuals 2-3 months to really notice an improvement and that there could be some detox effects, but I never expected to not sleep for 2-3 months!! It’s been just over 2 weeks now and I’m really feeling it . . . I can’t imagine going on like this for several more weeks  I did have a total of 9 amalgams removed about 2 years ago. 7 were done properly, but the first 2 were, unfortunately, done before I really knew better and though the dentist told me he took proper precautions I know it wasn't all done exactly like it should have been.

Thanks for the encouraging words! I hope to figure it out soon!!

We used to have a grounding sheet on the bed, but we took that off when learning that it may actually have been acting as an antenna. I am anxiously awaiting the video we've heard so much about to learn to ground my laptop for the limited times I now use it. Other than that, I guess I'm not really doing anything else. What am I missing? I'd love any suggestions.

I really haven't been wearing my blue blockers, but we have a pretty consistent evening routine where we just use all natural light until sunset when we turn on our lanterns. We're either outside until then playing with the kids or we're inside reading - absolutely no electronics in my house past 6:30! It used to be 7:00, but when we all got used to that I started even earlier. I even cut the breakers to the bedrooms at that time. I'm a Drill Sergeant when it comes to that

I agree about the work environment. It's by far the toughest, but I'm so thankful I normally only have to go into the office 3 days a week. Last week it was only 1 day and my sleep still sucked, so I don't think I can blame it on any EMF at work.

Thyroid is actually decent. Well, Free T3 is anyway - for the first time since I've been paying attention to my labs it's actually in range. Free T4 is a bit low still and TSH is high, but it's at least better than it was.

I do have a lot of muscle weakness and fatigue and definitely don't feel even remotely rested when I wake in the morning. It's beautiful outside today and I did sit on the grass for my lunch, but I'm trying to force myself to get out and take a few breaks and walk, but honestly my legs don't even want to move! Thyroid, lack of sleep, detox, hormones?!?! I have no clue . . .

Ok if you think its detox - can you try sleeping somewhere else - to see if its? trying to think of ways to crack this. For me what your describing is how I feel when my allergies get bad. My n=1. However, you've got something completely different going on. How's your Pg dosing looking? How's your E? I forget if you're E-dominate, or just low on Pg? has anything changed in your diet recently? Keep thinking, keep writing/talking about this - eventually it will come to you.

I'd start with testing sleeping w/o the magenitico sleep pand and your thyroid first, then go from there to see what the rest of your hormones look like.

### Joann — 2013-04-08T22:01:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-heck-happened-to-my-sleep.6661/post-83379>

BJK, reread your post....Waking up in the middle of the night hot.....sounds hormonal to me. I don't know what dhea numbers were before, but what they are now, doesn't seem like there is a need to supplement.
When do you take it? morning or night. P can disturb sleep in some people. Thyroid too.
I would try skipping dhea and or P to see what happens.

### BJK77 — 2013-04-08T22:54:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-heck-happened-to-my-sleep.6661/post-83385>

You all are awesome! Thanks so much for helping me brainstorm. Just knowing I've got some support here and others to help me think through this makes me feel light years better than I did

Gretchen - Funny you should mention about sleeping somewhere else since that's exactly what I was thinking of last night when I couldn't sleep! I'm just not sure where to go. The basement seems like it would be a good option, but the last couple of times I slept down there with the kids my sleep was crap too . . . more from an uncomfortable air mattress than anythign else! I need to find something comfy to sleep on and then I can give that a try. Otherwise, it will be pointless.

We were also supposed to head to the beach this weekend which would have been a great experiment to see how things worked out, but that's now been put on hold until next weekend . . . if nothing works before that though I'll at least have it to look forward too.

I just can't think of a single thing I changed in my diet. It's tight as always. I am super strict about what I eat; carbs are always very low; all organic, grass-fed meats; tons of seafood; no sugar/grains/dairy/eggs, etc.

Hmmm . . . looking at my labs estrogen seems a bit low. It was really high back in December at 331, but now is only 63 with a range of 35-400.

Joann - Initially I kept thinking hormones too, but then I look at my labs and they look better than they have in years! I know I shouldn't go by numbers on a piece of paper though. How we feel is so much more important . . . it's just hard not to want that stinkin' gold star for having perfect numbers! I did cut DHEA the last 2 nights and I think you're right - I should continue to cut that until I get new labs done, which I'm scheduled for in about 2 weeks. I've been taking it in the morning.

Pg I take in the evening right before bed and it has always helped me sleep before. In fact, when I upped from 200 mg to 300 mg last week to see if it would help my sleep it did the first couple of nights I did it, but then I got super nauseous and woke with even worse muscle weakness. I later read that's a sign of high Pg, so I backed down to 200 mg again. I have 100 mg capsules, so last night I tried splitting it up and taking 250 mg, but that didn't seem to do anything at all.

I don't want to mess around with all the hormones willy nilly, but at this point I'm getting somewhat desperate!

# Thread: Amazing results in 3 months!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amazing-results-in-3-months.6662/>

### kleinb87 — 2013-04-06T17:22:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amazing-results-in-3-months.6662/post-83119>

So it has been 3 months from getting first lab tests and fully implementing all of mitochondria rx and emf rx. My labs were trashed to say the least even while eating paleo. Zero on all hormones, inflammation high, and my only saving grace was a decent vit. D at 60. Digestion was horrible and my body was not optimal at all.

Yesterday, I got my DHEA, Vit. D, hs-crp, and homocysteine levels.

DHEA-s: 235 (98-340)
Vit.d: 96.5 (100)
Hs-crp: 0.96
Homocysteine: 7.8 (7-12)

Biggest improvement for me was hormones and hs-crp. Hs-crp before was 5.2. Body composition is slowly improving, and my menstrual cycle has returned after not having it for two years (no kids, 26 yrs old).

Photoelectric effect, grounding, CT and epipaleo work.

I am so thankful for Jack and his insight. Am I optimal? Not yet, but I have already made a 180 degree turn around from January. Another couple of months and I can guarantee I will be even better. Thanks for all of the support from the forum and Jack!

### kleinb87 — 2013-04-07T17:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amazing-results-in-3-months.6662/post-83228>

Thanks everyone! I may not live by the ocean, but moving there soon. After everyone thought I was crazy for laying outside in the snow with shorts and a tank top, it has proven to work plus minimizing all sorts of EMF. when you know better, you do better.

What has also improved immensely is my level of thinking, normal is no longer my standard. I am rising above and quickly seeing how "normal" people think. Roadblocks have been present especially with my new methods for teaching curriculum in Spanish from my department, but the students' response has been incredible. They are truly learning and not going through the motions.

My writing has improved as well as my thoughts on life. The impossible is now what I am challenging and I know that it can happen as long as the work is put forth.

Lets all keep moving forward and change the world.

### diane — 2013-04-07T23:21:34+0300

### kleinb87 — 2013-05-20T05:31:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amazing-results-in-3-months.6662/post-86511>

1 month later and feeling better than ever. I have now found that my BUN/ creatine ratio has improved and is now 12! Na is 142 mmol/L (a little high). Body composition is slowly going down with increased CT and photoelectric effect (until I move to the ocean in the late Spring it is 2- 22 pound bags of ice while I am laying out in the sun). But best of all, my thinking has improved 100%, brain fog is gone from the fall, and I feel like I can accomplish anything. I have since quit a job that was not fulfilling and went all in working on my own business full time and rebranding it to do not only what I am good at, but what I am passionate about (creativity and making the impossible possible). Full launch of new rebranding is tomorrow, and I'm not worried at all. Scary, right? Without Dr. Kruse's help this would have just still been a dream because my thinking would still have been lacking and clouded with fear. This is no longer the case.

"When you know better, you do better." -Dr. Kruse

Results are possible when you put forth the effort.

# Thread: Love some insight...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-some-insight.6679/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-04-07T23:45:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-some-insight.6679/post-83263>

Posed a question to Jack....thinking perhaps I didn't word it well. I guess what I am trying to figure out is...if we unleash all kinds of bacteria and pathogens with EMF exposure then do we need to treat them once they have been unleashed or just deal with the EMF exposure and let the body fix itself.

Does that make sense? I don't want to keep treating all kinds of illnesses with various meds, but if once they are able to dominate in the body through the EMF destruction must they be treated into submission while the body is strengthened through diet, CT, earthing, and of course limiting EMF exposure.

Jacks reply was that I didn't get it and should move.

It's extremely possible that I don't get it....there is a lot to "get". I have moved. Maybe not as remotely as he did, but not everyone has the means and ability to do what he has done and retain gainful employment. I am on over 8 acres in an agricultural area, surrounded by farms where people have shit for internet and cell reception. We have well water and copper pipes, have begun turning everything off in the house. Were just waiting for our tax bill which we got yesterday...grrrr...to see if there was money left to get some other goodies. There is not. But we are getting the magneticos anyway, because I feel like I do "get" the importance of addressing anything I have the power to.

My kids are all being treated for some serious crap and their little bodies are a mess. This is why we moved. A primal urgent need to protect ourselves. I'm just wondering if I can protect them from more pharmacological onslaught or if it has become a necessary evil.

Appreciate any insight and education y'all can offer.

Christi

### cantweight — 2013-04-08T00:38:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-some-insight.6679/post-83275>

We've only been here since last summer, so I believe it will take time. As bad as the EMF situation was in our old place I will admit total ignorance about our day to day carrying on. The ipods, cell phones, wi-fi etc. So the losnig those things has been very recent.

I noticed big improvement quite soon after moving and the funny thing is we were all forced to sleep in the basement (slab) from June to Sept as the house is older and was undergoing some renovations. We slept great!! My husband wants to move us all back down there. I want magneticos..lol.

Organic and whole food since 2009 Gluten free and very low grain has been over a year. Really pushing the seafood has just been this year (2013) and the response from the kids is excellent.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-09T21:14:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/safer-career-options-for-our-kids.6689/post-83513>

I have a friend who works as an artist. She has a multi-pronged approach to keep her dream alive and still earn a living. She teaches art/drawing at the community colleges to seniors, art classes in her home, and she displays art in a couple of galleries. She also donates her art for a banner that lines the street of her small town during an annual festival (20 artists volunteer art and all different banners are hung...it's very cool).

I think if he wants to pursue art or anything for that matter he needs to think outside the box on how to make this an income bearing business but not in the normal sense. It can be done.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-04-08T20:56:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-83374>

So I'm thinking I want to add minerals to my RO water I buy. I buy 6 5 gallon jugs for my water dispenser at home and was thinking about using either Concentrace trace mineral drops and/or Himalayan/Real Salt.

So whats the better plan and how much do I need to add per gallon. I thought I remembered reading 1/4 teaspoon per liter which would be 4 and 3/4 teaspoons of salt? Sounds like a ton.

I don't remember where I read that so I'm not sure it was 1/4 teaspoon per liter.

Any recommendations on how much Concentrace/Salt to use?

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-09T03:16:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-83419>

We have always added minerals to our RO water but just a couple of days ago decided to add Real Salt to the water. I love the taste of the "salt water". It tastes sweet. We are only adding about 1/4 tsp/40 oz so that may not be enough but it sure does make the water taste good!

### Ariel8100 — 2013-04-12T20:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-83921>

If the body is properly charged with minerals it will be able to deal with the minute amounts of those heavy metals. Currently I am using 1/4 teaspoon per40 oz. It tastes sweet. I think accurate quantity per40 oz.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-04-14T02:36:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-84009>

> [Ariel8100 said:](/goto/post?id=83921)
>
> If the body is properly charged with minerals it will be able to deal with the minute amounts of those heavy metals. Currently I am using 1/4 teaspoon per40 oz. It tastes sweet. I think accurate quantity per40 oz.

That's around 4 teaspoons of salt per 5 gallon jug. I can do that. Would there be any problem with adding the concentrated minerals and salt to the 5 gallon jugs before going to the place to get them filled? I figure that's the easiest way to do it.

Rather than going in there and adding my Concentrace and Salt to the bottles while they are filling them I can take off the caps and put the minerals/salt in the bottles put the caps on and than get them filled at the place.

The minerals/salt should mix in the water just fine. I wonder how much plastic if any leaches into the water. I don't really have a better option than my 5 gallon jugs though. I can't afford a whole whose filter or anything at this point. I hope that a good eating plan in Epi-Paleo and water with plenty of minerals will deal with the toxins including any leaching plastic...if that's even a concern. Do plastics like those in a 5 gallon jug leach in room temperatures? I suppose when they are in the car/trunk they might get warmer and perhaps leach some..don't know enough about the leaching of plastics.

Also does anyone know what kind of plastics is generally in these bottles? Does it have BPA?

### Celeste — 2014-07-04T08:25:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-133116>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-151204>

I was following Dr. Robert Cassar and his protocols until then. Everything that he says is so different and as always the science and explanations behind it makes so much sense. He is all about being vegan, except honey I believe. He talks greatly about eliminating parasites from pork, beef and fish(especially sushi). He's very big on hydrating, especially with blue-green algae water, with Himalayan sea salt added. Also can you finally straighten out once and for all, what is the best water to drink? So tired of the bullshit science behind all of the theories. One week I'm drinking distilled, the next RO, the next Spring, then alkaline. Much appreciate any feedback and keep the crusade going. The work you do is amazing and the fact that you afford everyday people the opportunity to take control of their own health will pay back in dividends!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-31T15:17:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-151233>

salt destroys the EZ formed in water.........so if you add salt to water you must restructure it and that costs you energy. You dont need salt in your water.......you can use it on your food.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-12-31T15:18:27+0200

### yewwei.tan — 2014-12-31T18:03:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-151248>

> [jim demetz said:](/goto/post?id=151204)
>
> Jack, can you elaborate why you wouldn't add salt? Does it make a difference if it's on food or in water? I have pretty much changed everything diet wise since I came across your information. I was following Dr. Robert Cassar and his protocols until then. Everything that he says is so different and as always the science and explanations behind it makes so much sense. He is all about being vegan, except honey I believe. He talks greatly about eliminating parasites from pork, beef and fish(especially sushi). He's very big on hydrating, especially with blue-green algae water, with Himalayan sea salt added. Also can you finally straighten out once and for all, what is the best water to drink? So tired of the bullshit science behind all of the theories. One week I'm drinking distilled, the next RO, the next Spring, then alkaline. Much appreciate any feedback and keep the crusade going. The work you do is amazing and the fact that you afford everyday people the opportunity to take control of their own health will pay back in dividends!

**The recommendation**: RO water or Northern Hemisphere Spring Water is the best

Can you get away with other types of water? Yes, if your redox potential is good.

Jack is right that adding salt to water decreases the EZ of said water. Gerald Pollacks book is the go-to source of info -- [http://www.amazon.com/dp/0962689548](http://www.amazon.com/dp/0962689548)

I asked before if drinking "good water" actually leads to more EZ water in the cell. I suspect the answer is Yes. This is probably due to Onsager's Reciprocity Relationship, whereby higher-energy, lower-density water that is in the GI tract naturally moves towards areas of lower-energy, higher-density water.

\----

Is getting good water absolutely important? I don't know, but if you're in a bad environment, you don't want the extra handicap of bad water.

For me, getting good water from the Northern Hemisphere didn't move the needle on health when I was in Melbourne (37 degrees south of the equator). I move to Cairns, which is 17 degrees south of the equator, and the lousy tap water didn't stop my recovery at all.

.....

### ssj3 — 2015-01-03T01:38:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/remineralization-of-water-concentrace-salt-and-how-much.6690/post-151417>

Would there be certain situations where salt is favourable??? Just a few thoughts... we have EZ on both sides of cell membrane, if salt exists on outside = diminished EZ, but a more negative EZ. Will this pull more hydronium ions from inner cell thus reducing its side? A high salt concentration can also create a crystalline structure that excludes, so we eat a fresh oyster, lots of salt (water), will this then increase this salt concentration outside the cell and prevent any 'toxic' material from entering the inside of the cell naturally??? Could this then be exploited, could this salt concentration then be removed and draw nutrients inside the cell?

# Thread: Is there a human contact blog?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-04-09T16:35:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/post-83474>

Jack's facebook post about affection has the wheels in my head turning....

I am an admitted slacker when it comes to keeping up with blogs. I am in school and it takes up 95% of my reading time. Summer is my time to catch up.

Is there a blog I have missed that covers the phenomenon of human contact. Why we crave it? Why we need it? Why the baby that has it thrives and the baby that does not fails?

Oxytocin and hormone production being an obvious...I am curious about electricity. Do we share electrons? What about when we pet animals? It's not just the nicety of it all, it never is. There is always biology. My husband and I are different people when we are connected, skin touching skin is much more constant in summer months or less clothed due to cold adaption winter months. The more disconnected we become from any aspect of life Earth or human the closer we get to death. Loneliness I would bet rivals many other neolithic diseases. Where does this tie in?

If I missed it please point me to the blog as my curiosity is piqued. Again.

Thanks,
Christi

### diane — 2013-04-09T16:42:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/post-83475>

There are references to oxytocin - but also sharing of electrons in the EMF blogs. I don't recall one being dedicated to that - but it's definitely a fascinating topic!

### Hope — 2013-04-09T17:02:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/post-83478>

Loneliness is the cause of a lot of this....you may want to check out (when you have finished all the Jack Kruse stuff!) - if you haven't - the Molecules of Emotions by Candace Pert, or get her CD set Your Body is Your Subconscious Mind. She touches on this stuff a little there, too. And she said loneliness is a huge contributor to disease. And I see that. Some people are lonely in marriage, too. We were literally, actually MADE for connection. Dr Pert also talks about the environment, like how we are disconnected from nature etc...not nearly the way Jack does, but it's still great stuff.

I read somewhere that you should hug for 30 seconds. Even smiling at another person is a good thing.....

You are going to be an awesome NP!!!!!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/post-83536>

>

> Christi

some really good thoughts there Christi ...Dr. K. has talked about sharing electrons. Someone who is weaker can benefit from someone who is stronger.
Loneliness is a terrible and debilitating thing .......

### cantweight — 2013-04-10T04:03:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/post-83567>

I was just thinking on this so much recently went I went to my uncle bobs 85th bday party. My kids asked if he was going to be on oxygen or if he could do anything and I realized they are thinking of my grandmom that at that age had already run her race so to speak.

Uncle Bob was the life of his party Jim Beam in hand. I thought about him and how he lived.....he always had love in his life, happiness, and contentment. Human contact and strong circle of friends and family. It has to be a piece of the puzzle

### caroline — 2013-04-10T04:08:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/post-83569>

Absolutely - are those things you speak of are critical .....

### Rachelle — 2013-04-10T13:56:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/post-83591>

I must be from another planet, though I came from a, close knit, family of party animals with many loving friends surrounding us. While on the outside I appear outgoing, the opposite is true and I let very few in my life. I am far from being antisocial but really tired of people. It takes a person who has a degree of self-sufficiency and autonomy to be secluded, but I am much happier since I made this decision. Do i miss hugging and the freedom to expresss myself? you damn right I do, but I can't focus on that now.

The life of a person in seclusion is voluntary. The breakthrough to other people, it seems to me, is partly to understand that they, like oneself, are largely creatures of their upbringing, experience, and personality type. Add to this their social and cultural ambiance, which few people resist, especially when it is itself hateful, aggressive, and self-aggrandizing.

Most people thus are still living major issues of childhood, echoing the falsities of their culture, responding in their subconscious minds and in their bodies the pain of forgotten hurts, and giving in to instincts deeply embedded in our psyches. All of these have to be examined critically, or at any rate opened up so that they can be let go. Letting these go opens space for self to fill with better things, though one must be in that place where better things are. It seems I am in a physical place right now where others are resentful and therefore appear hostile and angry. I am not able to fully get away from this so I had to create this sanctuary within. This does not totally isolate me, only steel me for daily life, until something better does appear. Maybe I am a bit over protective of myself right now. but I just can't be slapped in the face constantly with the reality many do not wish to better themselves. I am much less negative since being isolated and my health is on the upside. What does that say? Guess I can't worry about it now.

### cantweight — 2013-04-10T17:30:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/post-83616>

I couldn't agree more. It takes so much strength to distance yourself from those society says you should keep close, that you are obligated to. I used to think I was weak for cutting these people out, I didn't have the strength to fight their battles and be their shoulder to cry on constantly when they had no intention of changing themselves and becoming whole and happy. They need someone to enable that behavior and I no longer wanted to be that. To be drained by that lack of consciousness is exhausting and for someone trying to become optimal it is not an option. Carrying those people does no one any good, it hurts both parties as neither can find their strength in that.

I have often wondered if there is an evolutionary component, a survival of the fittest that occurs when we no longer carry the weight of those that don't want to change, and seek out the energy of those that do and those that have. There is a spark that says this person gets it, they are going the same place I am. The positive energy.

### caroline — 2013-04-10T23:30:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/post-83668>

I completely agree ...I can't be around negative people any more - I won't. I need to connect with positive people who want to really live life to the fullest and be the healthiest version of themselves that they can be. I think there is an instant attraction/spark to positive people for sure.

Dr. K. has said .... you sometimes have to cut people out of your life ...it isn't that YOU don't care - it is that THEY don't care.
That was so important for me to hear....

### cantweight — 2013-04-11T03:43:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/post-83695>

I wonder if when I become optimal i will have the strength to go back and lift those I love and have left behind. Or perhaps when they see us at optimal it will ignite the change in them that awakens them. I hope so!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-11T05:53:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-human-contact-blog.6700/post-83709>

Only they can lift themselves.......you only provide the nudge. If they need more than that move on.......The Sex Rx will be in webinar format.....I am toying with doing it this month.....not sure yet.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-30T17:43:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/factor-x-strikes-again.6702/post-85195>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=83509)
>
> [http://science.nbcnews.com/\_news/20...ve-in-a-flash-to-survive-growing-acidity?lite](http://science.nbcnews.com/_news/2013/04/09/17672170-purple-sea-urchins-evolve-in-a-flash-to-survive-growing-acidity?lite)

Those scientists are trying to have their cake and eat it too. They intersperse terms like "natural selection", "adapt", "evolve", "only the fittest survive", "those with only the most advantageous genes survived". The scientists then go on to say there were changes in the abundance of certain genes in larvae one week old. That last bit sounds like epigenetic expression as opposed to a natural selection process (which perhaps would have killed off most of the larvae that were unfit - my words). Maybe there are two camps of researchers growling at each other from different sides of the table.
